PRODUCT: Cardiwin (Atenolol)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">CardiWin(Atenolol) is a medication that belongs to the <b>beta-blocker</b> drug class. It is a cardio-selective beta-1 adrenergic antagonist, meaning it primarily targets and blocks beta-1 receptors in the heart.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Here is a description of how it works and what it is used for:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atenolol works by blocking the effects of epinephrine (adrenaline) and norepinephrine on the heart. By doing this, it:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 36.0pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Slows down your heart rate.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 36.0pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Reduces the force of your heart's contractions.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 36.0pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Decreases blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This makes it easier for your heart to pump blood, which reduces the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications (What it is used for)</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atenolol is primarily used to treat several cardiovascular conditions, including:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 36.0pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is a common medication used to lower and control blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 36.0pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Angina Pectoris (Chest Pain):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It helps prevent chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart muscle.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 36.0pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Myocardial Infarction (Heart Attack):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is used to reduce the risk of further damage and improve survival after a heart attack.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Some other uses may include treating certain types of irregular heartbeats and, in some cases, preventing migraine headaches.</span></span></span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Vildrozon
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vildagliptin is an oral medication for type 2 diabetes. It belongs to the class of drugs called <b>dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitors</b>. It works by targeting an enzyme in the body to improve blood sugar control.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vildagliptin is used to improve glycemic control in adults with <b>type 2 diabetes mellitus</b>. It is typically prescribed:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As a monotherapy when metformin is considered inappropriate due to intolerance.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In combination with other oral anti-diabetic medications, such as metformin, thiazolidinediones, or a sulfonylurea, when diet and exercise alone, or a single agent, do not provide adequate glycemic control.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In combination with insulin (with or without metformin) when insulin plus diet and exercise do not provide adequate control.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vildagliptin works by enhancing the body's own ability to regulate blood sugar levels. Its mechanism is focused on the <b>incretin system</b>.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">DPP-4 Inhibition:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Vildagliptin selectively inhibits the enzyme <b>dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4)</b>. This enzyme is responsible for breaking down a group of hormones called <b>incretins</b>, specifically <b>glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1)</b> and <b>glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP)</b>.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Increased Incretin Levels:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By inhibiting DPP-4, vildagliptin prevents the breakdown of these incretin hormones. This leads to higher circulating levels of active GLP-1 and GIP.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Glucose-Dependent Insulin Release:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Incretins are secreted by the gut in response to food intake. When their levels are increased, they stimulate the pancreas to release <b>insulin</b> in a glucose-dependent manner. This means that insulin is released only when blood sugar levels are high, which reduces the risk of hypoglycemia (low blood sugar).</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Reduced Glucagon Secretion:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> The higher levels of active incretins also suppress the release of <b>glucagon</b> from the pancreas. Glucagon is a hormone that signals the liver to produce and release glucose, especially between meals. By reducing glucagon, vildagliptin helps to lower blood glucose levels, particularly after a meal.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In essence, vildagliptin's action is to make the body's natural blood sugar regulation system work more effectively after meals without the risk of causing a severe drop in blood sugar.</span></span></span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Atenowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atenolol is a <b>beta-blocker</b> medication that is commonly used to treat cardiovascular conditions. It is specifically a <b>cardioselective</b> beta-blocker, meaning it primarily targets and blocks receptors in the heart. The maleate salt form is used to improve the drug's stability and bioavailability for oral administration.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atenolol is prescribed for several conditions, often used alone or in combination with other drugs. Its main indications include:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It lowers blood pressure, reducing the strain on the heart and arteries.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Angina Pectoris (Chest Pain):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It helps prevent chest pain associated with heart disease.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acute Myocardial Infarction (Heart Attack):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is used to improve survival and reduce the risk of a second heart attack after a patient has experienced one.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Other Uses (Off-label):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It can also be used to treat certain irregular heart rhythms, prevent migraine headaches, and manage symptoms of anxiety or alcohol withdrawal.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atenolol's therapeutic effects are a result of its ability to block β1-adrenergic receptors. Here's how it works:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Selective Receptor Blockade:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Atenolol selectively binds to <b>beta-1 adrenergic receptors</b>, which are found in high concentrations in the heart. This prevents the natural stress hormones, like <b>epinephrine</b> (adrenaline) and <b>norepinephrine</b>, from binding to these receptors.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Decreased Heart Rate and Contractility:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By blocking the beta-1 receptors, atenolol inhibits the stimulating effects of these hormones on the heart. This leads to a decrease in heart rate and the force of heart muscle contractions.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Reduced Blood Pressure:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> The slower heart rate and reduced force of contractions lead to a decrease in cardiac output. This, in turn, lowers blood pressure and reduces the workload on the heart. The effect of atenolol on blood pressure is also linked to its ability to inhibit the release of renin from the kidneys.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Improved Oxygen Supply:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> For conditions like angina, the reduced workload on the heart means it requires less oxygen, which helps relieve chest pain.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Samicx (Tranexamic Acid)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tranexamic acid (TXA) is an antifibrinolytic medication, which means it helps to prevent the breakdown of blood clots.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a synthetic derivative of the amino acid lysine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including oral tablets, intravenous injection, and topical solutions, allowing for its use in a wide range of medical situations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tranexamic acid is used to treat or prevent excessive bleeding in a variety of contexts.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Heavy Menstrual Bleeding (HMB):</b> It is a common and FDA-approved treatment for cyclic heavy menstrual bleeding in women.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Trauma:</b> It is used in the management of severe bleeding after major trauma, especially if given within the first three hours of the injury, as it has been shown to reduce mortality.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Surgical Procedures:</b> It is frequently used to reduce blood loss during and after surgeries, particularly in orthopedic, cardiac, and spinal procedures, which can reduce or eliminate the need for blood transfusions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dental Procedures:</b> It is used to control or prevent bleeding following tooth extractions, especially in patients with bleeding disorders like hemophilia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Bleeding Disorders:</b> It can be used to manage bleeding in conditions like hereditary angioedema and to treat nosebleeds.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">To understand the mechanism of tranexamic acid, it's helpful to know about the body's natural process for breaking down blood clots, which is called <b>fibrinolysis</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>The Fibrinolytic System:</b> After a blood clot is formed to stop bleeding, the body initiates a process to gradually break it down.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A key enzyme in this process is <b>plasmin</b>, which is responsible for dissolving the fibrin meshwork that holds the clot together. Plasmin is formed from an inactive precursor called <b>plasminogen</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>The Role of Plasminogen:</b> Plasminogen binds to the fibrin clot at specific sites called lysine-binding sites.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This binding is a necessary step for the activation of plasminogen into plasmin.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Tranexamic Acid's Action:</b> Tranexamic acid is a synthetic lysine analog.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because of its similar structure, it acts as a <b>competitive inhibitor</b> of plasminogen activation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It reversibly binds to the lysine-binding sites on the plasminogen molecule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By occupying these sites, tranexamic acid prevents plasminogen from binding to the fibrin clot.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> By preventing plasminogen from being activated on the clot's surface, tranexamic acid inhibits the formation of plasmin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This, in turn, prevents the breakdown of the fibrin clot, thereby stabilizing it and promoting hemostasis (the process of stopping bleeding).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In essence, while blood thinners like Rivaroxaban work to prevent a clot from forming in the first place, tranexamic acid works to preserve and strengthen a clot that has already formed, which is why it is used to control existing or anticipated bleeding.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p>Sildenafil Tablet</p>

PRODUCT: M-Bec (Montelukast Sodium)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Montelukast sodium is an oral medication that belongs to a class of drugs called leukotriene receptor antagonists (LTRAs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly potent and selective agent that targets the inflammatory pathways involved in asthma and allergic rhinitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not a bronchodilator for the immediate relief of asthma attacks but is used for long-term maintenance and prevention of symptoms. It is available in various oral formulations, including tablets, chewable tablets, and granules.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Montelukast is indicated for the treatment of several conditions, often as part of a long-term management plan:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Asthma:</b> It is used as a maintenance treatment for the prevention and long-term control of asthma in adults and children (typically 12 months of age and older).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It helps to prevent symptoms like wheezing and shortness of breath and to reduce the frequency of asthma attacks.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Exercise-Induced Bronchoconstriction (EIB):</b> It is indicated for the prevention of EIB in adults and children who are at least 6 years of age.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Allergic Rhinitis:</b> It is used for the relief of symptoms of seasonal allergic rhinitis in adults and children (2 years of age and older) and perennial allergic rhinitis in adults and children (6 months of age and older).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Montelukast's mechanism of action is based on its ability to block the effects of leukotrienes, which are potent inflammatory mediators released during an allergic or asthmatic reaction.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Leukotriene Synthesis:</b> During an allergic or asthmatic trigger (e.g., an allergen, exercise), mast cells and eosinophils in the airways and nasal passages produce and release leukotrienes, specifically cysteinyl leukotrienes (LTC4​, LTD4​, LTE4​).</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Receptor Antagonism:</b> These leukotrienes then bind to and activate <b>cysteinyl leukotriene receptors (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>CysLT1</b><b>​</b><b>)</b> on various cells, including smooth muscle cells in the airways.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This activation triggers a cascade of inflammatory responses, including:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bronchoconstriction:</b> The smooth muscles in the airways contract, leading to a narrowing of the airways.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Mucus Secretion:</b> Mucus production increases, further obstructing the airways.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Airway Inflammation and Edema:</b> The airways become inflamed and swollen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Montelukast's Action:</b> Montelukast is a potent and selective <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>CysLT1</b><b>​</b><b> receptor antagonist</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to these receptors with high affinity, effectively blocking leukotrienes from binding and activating them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptom Prevention:</b> By blocking the <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>CysLT1​ receptors, montelukast prevents the inflammatory and bronchoconstrictor effects of leukotrienes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to reduced airway inflammation, decreased bronchoconstriction, and less mucus production, thereby helping to prevent asthma symptoms and control allergic rhinitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Ranocin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<h3>Terazosin HCl is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>alpha-1 adrenergic blockers</b> (or alpha-1 antagonists). It is used to treat two distinct conditions: benign prostatic hyperplasia and hypertension. The "HCl" in its name stands for hydrochloride, which is a salt form used to make the drug stable and soluble for oral administration.</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:16px;"><span style="font-family:Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif;">Indications</span></span></p>

<p>Terazosin HCl is primarily indicated for the treatment of:</p>

<p><b>&nbsp; &nbsp;Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH):</b> It is used to relieve the symptoms of an enlarged prostate, such as a weak urine stream, urinary urgency, and frequent urination. It helps to relax the smooth muscles in the prostate and bladder neck, improving urinary flow.</p>

<p><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</b> It is used to lower blood pressure, either alone or in combination with other antihypertensive agents.</p>

<hr />
<p>Mechanism of Action</p>

<h3>Terazosin's therapeutic effects are a result of its ability to selectively block <b>alpha-1 adrenergic receptors</b> in two key locations in the body.</h3>

<ol start="1">
	<li>
	<p><b>In Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH):</b></p>

	<ul>
		<li><b>Alpha-1a Receptor Blockade:</b> Terazosin specifically targets and blocks alpha-1a adrenergic receptors, which are abundant in the smooth muscle of the prostate gland, prostatic capsule, and bladder neck.</li>
		<li><b>Muscle Relaxation:</b> By blocking these receptors, terazosin prevents the binding of norepinephrine, a hormone that causes muscle contraction. This leads to the relaxation of the smooth muscles in the prostate and bladder neck.</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>Improved Urinary Flow:</b> The muscle relaxation reduces the obstruction of the urethra, thereby improving the flow of urine and relieving the bothersome urinary symptoms of BPH.</p>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>In Hypertension:</b></p>

	<ul>
		<li>
		<p><b>Alpha-1b and Alpha-1d Receptor Blockade:</b> Terazosin also blocks alpha-1b and alpha-1d adrenergic receptors found in the smooth muscle of the peripheral blood vessels.</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>Vasodilation:</b> By blocking these receptors, terazosin prevents the vasoconstricting (narrowing) effect of norepinephrine on the arteries. This leads to <b>vasodilation</b> (widening of blood vessels).</p>
		</li>
		<li><b>Lowered Blood Pressure:</b> The vasodilation reduces the peripheral resistance that the heart has to pump against, leading to a decrease in blood pressure.</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p><b>Dual Mechanism:</b> Terazosin's ability to block alpha-1 receptors in both the prostate and blood vessels makes it a useful medication for men who have both hypertension and BPH, as it can treat both conditions simultaneously.</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Oteby 40mg(Otilonium Bromide)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Otilonium Bromide is a synthetic drug that acts as a <b>smooth muscle relaxant</b> and <b>antispasmodic agent</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a quaternary ammonium compound, which means it carries a positive charge that limits its absorption from the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This poor systemic absorption is a key feature of the drug, allowing it to exert a localized effect on the smooth muscles of the gut with minimal systemic side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Otilonium Bromide is primarily used for the symptomatic treatment of painful conditions of the gastrointestinal tract characterized by spasms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS):</b> It is used to relieve symptoms of IBS, such as abdominal pain, cramping, bloating, and irregular bowel habits.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Functional Bowel Disorders:</b> It is also used to treat other painful conditions where spasms of the digestive tract are a prominent symptom.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Otilonium Bromide's mechanism of action is based on its ability to directly affect the smooth muscle cells of the gastrointestinal tract, primarily by modulating the movement of calcium ions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Calcium Channel Antagonism:</b> Otilonium Bromide acts as a <b>calcium channel blocker</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It primarily inhibits the influx of extracellular calcium ions (Ca2+) into the smooth muscle cells of the gut. Since calcium is essential for muscle contraction, by blocking its entry, the drug effectively relaxes the smooth muscles and prevents spasms.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Tachykinins:</b> The drug has also been shown to have an inhibitory effect on tachykinins, which are neurotransmitters like Substance P that are involved in stimulating smooth muscle contraction and pain signaling in the gut. By interfering with the action of these neurotransmitters, otilonium bromide provides an additional antispasmodic and pain-relieving effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="3">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Localized Action:</b> Due to its quaternary ammonium structure, otilonium bromide has very low systemic bioavailability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means that after oral administration, it remains highly concentrated in the gastrointestinal lumen and exerts its effects directly on the smooth muscles of the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This localized action minimizes the risk of systemic side effects, such as those associated with anticholinergic drugs (e.g., dry mouth, blurred vision, and drowsiness).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, otilonium bromide works by inhibiting the entry of calcium ions into intestinal smooth muscle cells, thereby preventing muscle contraction and relieving the painful spasms associated with functional bowel disorders like IBS.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its lack of systemic absorption makes it a safe and effective treatment option.</span></span></p>

	<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
	</li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Ferhem( Iron Sucrose)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Iron sucrose is an iron replacement product administered intravenously. After injection, it dissociates into iron and sucrose. The iron is then transported by a protein called transferrin to red blood cell precursors, where it is incorporated into <b>hemoglobin</b> to produce new red blood cells.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indications:</b> It is indicated for the treatment of <b>iron deficiency anemia</b>, particularly in patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD) who are on dialysis or not.</p>
	</li>
</ul>

HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>

	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Lignocaine is a local anesthetic. It works by reversibly blocking nerve signals in a specific area of the body. It prevents the influx of sodium ions into nerve cells, which stops the transmission of pain signals to the brain.</p>

PRODUCT: Betapime(Cefepime (USP))
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Cefepime is a fourth-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. It works by interfering with the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall, leading to the death of the bacterial cell. This mechanism makes it effective against a broad range of bacteria.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indications:</b> It is used to treat a wide variety of bacterial infections, including <b>pneumonia</b>, urinary tract infections, and severe skin infections. It is also used in patients with a weakened immune system who have a fever due to a suspected infection.</p>
	</li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Wynat 60mg (Artesunate)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Artesunate is a semi-synthetic derivative of <b>artemisinin</b>, a compound isolated from the sweet wormwood plant (<i>Artemisia annua</i>).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a water-soluble derivative, which makes it suitable for both oral and injectable formulations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Artesunate is known for its powerful and extremely rapid effect against the blood-stage of the malarial parasite, leading to a swift reduction of the parasite load and a fast resolution of symptoms.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Artesunate is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>severe <i>Plasmodium falciparum</i> malaria</b>, including cases with multi-drug resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often the first-line treatment for severe malaria due to its rapid action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> For uncomplicated malaria, artesunate is almost exclusively used as part of a fixed-dose combination therapy with another longer-acting antimalarial, as this strategy is the current standard of care to prevent the development of drug resistance.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of artesunate is unique and involves the parasite's own metabolic processes to generate its lethal effect.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Activation by Heme:</b> The malarial parasite digests hemoglobin from red blood cells to obtain amino acids.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process produces <b>heme</b>, a highly reactive and toxic iron-containing compound.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Free Radical Generation:</b> The defining feature of artesunate, like other artemisinin derivatives, is the <b>endoperoxide bridge</b> within its molecular structure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When artesunate is taken up by the parasite, the heme iron cleaves this endoperoxide bridge.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This cleavage generates highly reactive and toxic <b>free radicals</b> and other cytotoxic compounds.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Protein and Membrane Damage:</b> These free radicals quickly and indiscriminately damage essential parasite proteins, enzymes, and membranes. This widespread damage leads to the rapid death of the parasite within the red blood cell.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rapid Action:</b> This mechanism allows artesunate to kill the malarial parasites very quickly, leading to a rapid reduction in the total parasite biomass.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action is responsible for the fast clinical response observed in patients, with fever and other symptoms resolving rapidly. The short half-life necessitates its use in combination with a longer-acting drug to ensure complete eradication of the infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, artesunate works by being activated by the parasite's own metabolism, which leads to the generation of free radicals that cause widespread damage and kill the parasite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its potent and rapid action is why it is a cornerstone of modern malaria treatment, especially for severe cases.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Alvostan (Amlodipine + Valsartan)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Amlodipine as Besylate and Valsartan is a single-tablet medication that contains two distinct and complementary drugs for the treatment of high blood pressure (hypertension).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This fixed-dose combination leverages the strengths of both a <b>calcium channel blocker</b> (Amlodipine) and an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker</b> (Valsartan) to provide a more effective and comprehensive approach to blood pressure management than either drug alone.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination therapy is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b>. It is typically prescribed in two main scenarios:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>For patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled</b> by a single medication (either amlodipine or valsartan) on its own.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>As an initial therapy</b> for patients who are likely to require more than one medication to reach their blood pressure goals, particularly those with more severe hypertension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By lowering blood pressure, this medication reduces the risk of serious cardiovascular events, including stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different and vital pathways that regulate blood pressure:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Amlodipine (Calcium Channel Blocker)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amlodipine is a calcium channel blocker that works by inhibiting the influx of calcium ions into the smooth muscle cells of the blood vessel walls.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vasodilation:</b> The relaxation and widening of the arteries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Peripheral Resistance:</b> This makes it easier for the heart to pump blood, which in turn lowers blood pressure.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Valsartan (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Valsartan works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a crucial role in regulating blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its specific mechanism is:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Angiotensin II Receptor Blockade:</b> Valsartan selectively blocks the binding of a powerful hormone called <b>angiotensin II</b> to its receptors in various tissues, including blood vessels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II normally causes blood vessels to constrict (tighten).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking its effects, valsartan prevents this constriction, leading to further vasodilation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Decreased Aldosterone Release:</b> It also reduces the release of aldosterone, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water, which would otherwise increase blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of these two drugs provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Amlodipine's vasodilation</b> reduces peripheral resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Valsartan's blockade of the RAAS</b> prevents blood vessel constriction and fluid retention.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual mechanism of action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure compared to monotherapy, while also mitigating some of the side effects of each drug. For example, valsartan can help counteract the peripheral edema (swelling in hands and feet) that is a common side effect of amlodipine.</span></span></p>

The combination helps to relieve pain while protecting the stomach from the ulcer-causing effects of naproxen.</p>

PRODUCT: Olamex 40mg (Olmesartan Medoxomil)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<p>Olmesartan Medoxomil is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs)</b>. It is a potent and selective antihypertensive agent used to treat high blood pressure. The "Medoxomil" portion of the name indicates that it is a prodrug—an inactive compound that is converted into its active form, olmesartan, after being absorbed by the body.</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3>Indications</h3>

<p>Olmesartan Medoxomil is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>. It can be used alone or in combination with other antihypertensive medications to achieve blood pressure control.</p>

<p>By effectively lowering blood pressure, olmesartan medoxomil helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and kidney damage.</p>

<h3>Mechanism of Action</h3>

<p>Olmesartan's therapeutic effect is a result of its ability to block the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a key role in regulating blood pressure. Its mechanism is as follows:</p>

<ol>
	<li><b>Conversion to Active Drug:</b> After oral administration, olmesartan medoxomil is rapidly and completely converted by enzymes in the gastrointestinal tract to its active form, <b>olmesartan</b>.</li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li><b>Blockade of Angiotensin II Receptors:</b> Olmesartan acts as a competitive antagonist at the <b>angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors</b>. It binds to these receptors with high affinity, preventing the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> from attaching to them.</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor, meaning it causes blood vessels to narrow. By blocking its receptors, olmesartan prevents this narrowing, leading to <b>vasodilation</b> (the widening of blood vessels). This decreases the peripheral resistance, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.</li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li><b>Reduced Aldosterone Release:</b> The binding of angiotensin II to its receptors also stimulates the release of <b>aldosterone</b>, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water. By blocking this action, olmesartan helps to decrease the body's fluid volume, which further lowers blood pressure.</li>
	<li><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced fluid retention leads to a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure, easing the workload on the heart and helping to protect blood vessels and vital organs like the kidneys.</li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Olamex-H(Olmesartan Medoxomil + Hydrochlorothiazide)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> This is a combination of two antihypertensive drugs. <b>Olmesartan</b> is an angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB) that prevents a hormone called angiotensin II from tightening blood vessels. <b>Hydrochlorothiazide</b> is a diuretic ("water pill") that helps the body get rid of excess water and salt. The combined effect lowers blood pressure more effectively.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indications:</b> It is used to treat <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>.</p>
	</li>
</ul>
