HTML_DESCRIPTION: 
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Maxten
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Irbesartan is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <span style="font-weight:normal">angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs)</span>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a selective and potent antihypertensive agent used to treat high blood pressure and other cardiovascular conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Irbesartan is a non-peptide compound that works by selectively blocking the effects of the hormone angiotensin II.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></h3>

	<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications</span></span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Irbesartan is primarily indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></span></h3>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</b> It is used alone or in combination with other antihypertensive medications to lower blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diabetic Nephropathy:</b> It is used to delay the progression of kidney disease in patients with Type 2 diabetes who also have high blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively managing blood pressure and protecting the kidneys, irbesartan helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular and renal events, such as stroke, heart attack, and end-stage renal disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt">Mechanism</span></b> <b><span style="font-size:13.5pt">of Action</span></b></span></span></p>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Irbesartan's therapeutic effects are a result of its ability to block the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a key role in regulating blood pressure and fluid balance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blockade of Angiotensin II Receptors:</b> Irbesartan acts as a specific and competitive antagonist at the <b>angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to these receptors with high affinity, preventing the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> from attaching to them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="2">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor, meaning it causes blood vessels to narrow. By blocking its receptors, irbesartan prevents this narrowing, leading to <b>vasodilation</b> (the widening of blood vessels).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This decreases the peripheral resistance, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Aldosterone Release:</b> The binding of angiotensin II to its receptors also stimulates the release of <b>aldosterone</b>, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water. By blocking this action, irbesartan helps to decrease the body's fluid volume, which further contributes to lowering blood pressure.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced fluid retention leads to a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure. This eases the workload on the heart and helps to protect blood vessels and vital organs like the kidneys from the damaging effects of chronic hypertension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="5">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Similar to other ARBs, irbesartan does not affect the breakdown of bradykinin, which means it is less likely to cause the common side effect of a dry cough associated with ACE inhibitors.</span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Winstate(Febuxostat)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Febuxostat is a xanthine oxidase inhibitor. It works by blocking the enzyme xanthine oxidase, which is responsible for converting purines into uric acid. By inhibiting this enzyme, it lowers the amount of uric acid in the blood.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indications:</b> It is used for the chronic management of <b>hyperuricemia (high uric acid levels)</b> in patients with <b>gout</b>.</p>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Meglotate(Megestrol Acetate)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Megestrol acetate (MA) is a synthetic progestin, a derivative of the naturally occurring steroid hormone progesterone. It is an oral medication that exhibits both progestational and anti-estrogenic properties. Megestrol acetate is known for its dual therapeutic roles: as a hormonal agent in the treatment of certain cancers and as a potent appetite stimulant. It is available in various oral formulations, including tablets and an oral suspension.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Megestrol acetate is indicated for a variety of conditions, with a focus on its hormonal and appetite-stimulating effects. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Appetite Stimulation:</b> It is widely used to manage anorexia, cachexia (severe weight loss), or unexplained significant weight loss in patients with chronic diseases such as AIDS or cancer.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Breast Cancer:</b> For the palliative treatment of advanced breast cancer, particularly in postmenopausal women.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Endometrial Cancer:</b> For the palliative treatment of advanced endometrial carcinoma.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Endometriosis:</b> In some cases, it may be used to treat endometriosis.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of megestrol acetate is multifaceted and depends on the condition being treated.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Antineoplastic (Anti-Cancer) Mechanism</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Progesterone Receptor Agonism:</b> Megestrol acetate acts as a potent <b>agonist at progesterone receptors</b> in target tissues, including the breast and endometrium.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anti-Estrogenic Effects:</b> It has a strong anti-estrogenic effect, which is particularly relevant in hormone-dependent cancers like breast and endometrial carcinoma. It is believed to downregulate estrogen receptors, reducing the cancer cells' ability to respond to estrogen.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Tumor Growth:</b> By altering the hormonal environment and potentially having a direct cytotoxic effect, megestrol acetate helps to slow or stop the growth of hormone-sensitive tumors.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Appetite Stimulant Mechanism</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hypothalamic Action:</b> The exact mechanism by which megestrol acetate stimulates appetite is not fully understood, but it is believed to be related to its effects on the hypothalamus, the part of the brain that regulates appetite and metabolism.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Impact on Neuropeptides:</b> It is thought to increase the release or activity of <b>neuropeptide Y (NPY)</b> and other orexigenic (appetite-stimulating) hormones, while simultaneously reducing the activity of anorexigenic (appetite-suppressing) hormones like leptin.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anti-Cytokine Effects:</b> Chronic illnesses often lead to the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that cause a loss of appetite and muscle wasting. Megestrol acetate may exert its effect by counteracting the catabolic effects of these cytokines.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The ability of megestrol acetate to induce significant weight gain, primarily from an increase in fat mass, makes it a valuable treatment for managing severe weight loss.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Zelwist(Azilsartan Medoxomil)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan Medoxomil is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent and selective antihypertensive agent used to treat high blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "Medoxomil" portion of the name indicates that it is a prodrug—an inactive compound that is converted into its active form, azilsartan, after being absorbed by the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan Medoxomil is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It can be used alone or in combination with other antihypertensive medications to achieve blood pressure control.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, azilsartan medoxomil helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and kidney damage.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button><hr< source-inline-chip=""> size=3 width="100%" align=center&gt; </hr<></span></span></span></div>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan's therapeutic effect is a result of its ability to block the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a key role in regulating blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to Active Drug:</b> After oral administration, azilsartan medoxomil is rapidly and completely converted by enzymes in the gastrointestinal tract to its active form, <b>azilsartan</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="2">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blockade of Angiotensin II Receptors:</b> Azilsartan acts as a competitive antagonist at the <b>angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to these receptors with high affinity, preventing the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> from attaching to them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="3">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor, meaning it causes blood vessels to narrow.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking its receptors, azilsartan prevents this narrowing, leading to <b>vasodilation</b> (the widening of blood vessels).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This decreases the peripheral resistance, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="4">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Aldosterone Release:</b> The binding of angiotensin II to its receptors also stimulates the release of <b>aldosterone</b>, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water. By blocking this action, azilsartan helps to decrease the body's fluid volume, which further lowers blood pressure.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced fluid retention leads to a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure, easing the workload on the heart and helping to protect blood vessels and vital organs like the kidneys.</span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan is noted for its potent and long-lasting blood pressure-lowering effect compared to other ARBs, which is a key reason for its once-daily dosing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
	</li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Tyzonix
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;<span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span>
	<ul>
		<li>
		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Nitazoxanide is a synthetic, broad-spectrum antiparasitic and antiviral drug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the first drug of its class, known as thiazolides, which are synthetic nitrothiazolyl-salicylamide derivatives.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After oral administration, nitazoxanide is rapidly metabolized into its active form, tizoxanide.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in both tablet and oral suspension forms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

		<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Nitazoxanide is primarily indicated for the treatment of diarrhea caused by specific protozoal infections in adults and children:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diarrhea caused by <i>Giardia lamblia</i>:</b> A common parasite that causes giardiasis, an infection of the small intestine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>

		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diarrhea caused by <i>Cryptosporidium parvum</i>:</b> A protozoan that causes cryptosporidiosis, a diarrheal disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>

		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">While it is FDA-approved for these specific indications, nitazoxanide has demonstrated a broad spectrum of activity against a wide range of other parasites (including various helminths), anaerobic bacteria (e.g., <i>Clostridium difficile</i>), and certain viruses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because of this, it is sometimes used off-label for other conditions, such as:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intestinal parasites:</b> Infections caused by <i>Entamoeba histolytica</i>, <i>Ascaris lumbricoides</i>, and others.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>

		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Viral infections:</b> It has shown some activity against influenza viruses, hepatitis B and C viruses, and noroviruses, but more research is needed to support these uses.</span></span></span></li>
		</ul>

		<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Nitazoxanide's mechanism of action is unique and multi-faceted, allowing it to act on various pathogens.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary antiprotozoal effect is related to its interference with anaerobic energy metabolism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

		<ol>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Pyruvate Ferredoxin/Flavodoxin Oxidoreductase (PFOR):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Nitazoxanide and its active metabolite, tizoxanide, work by inhibiting a key enzyme in anaerobic metabolism called <b>pyruvate ferredoxin/flavodoxin oxidoreductase (PFOR)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> PFOR is essential for the energy metabolism of many anaerobic bacteria and protozoa.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ol>

		<ol start="2">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Electron Transfer:</b> The drug interferes with the PFOR-dependent electron transfer reaction.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking this process, it effectively starves the pathogen of energy, which prevents it from growing, multiplying, and performing vital functions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the death of the microorganism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ol>

		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In addition to this primary mechanism, nitazoxanide also has other actions that contribute to its broad-spectrum activity:</span></span></p>

		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Glycoprotein Maturation:</b> For viruses like coronaviruses, nitazoxanide has been shown to interfere with the maturation of viral glycoproteins, such as the spike protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This can impair the virus's ability to replicate and assemble.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>

		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Other Enzymes:</b> Studies suggest that nitazoxanide may also inhibit other enzymes and pathways in various pathogens, including quinone oxidoreductase and glutathione-S-transferase, which are involved in drug detoxification and other essential processes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>

		<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
		<br />
		<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Olamex-H(Olmesartan Medoxomil + Hydrochlorothiazide)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> This is a combination of two drugs used to treat high blood pressure. <b>Olmesartan</b> is an ARB that widens blood vessels, while <b>Hydrochlorothiazide</b> is a <b>thiazide diuretic</b> that increases the excretion of salt and water by the kidneys. The dual action provides a more powerful blood pressure-lowering effect than either drug alone.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indications:</b> It is used to treat <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>, especially in patients who are not adequately controlled by a single medication.</p>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Vizta
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;<span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span>
	<ul>
		<li>
		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rosuvastatin Calcium is a potent medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>statins</b> or <b>HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a synthetic lipid-lowering agent used to reduce high cholesterol and triglyceride levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Rosuvastatin is considered one of the most effective statins at lowering LDL ("bad") cholesterol. The "calcium" in its name refers to the salt form used to ensure the drug's stability and solubility for oral administration.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

		<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rosuvastatin Calcium is used for a variety of conditions related to high cholesterol and the prevention of cardiovascular disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>
		</li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Hypercholesterolemia:</b> It is a first-line therapy to lower high levels of total cholesterol and LDL ("bad") cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		<li><sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hypertriglyceridemia:</b> It is effective at lowering high levels of triglycerides.</span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cardiovascular Risk Reduction:</b> It is prescribed to reduce the risk of heart attack, stroke, and the need for revascularization procedures in patients with or without existing heart disease, but who have multiple risk factors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<ul>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Atherosclerosis:</b> It is used to slow the progression of atherosclerosis (the buildup of plaque in arteries).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

		<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rosuvastatin's therapeutic effect is primarily a result of its ability to inhibit a key enzyme in the cholesterol synthesis pathway.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<ol>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enzyme Inhibition:</b> Rosuvastatin works in the liver by competitively inhibiting the enzyme <b>HMG-CoA reductase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is the rate-limiting step in the mevalonate pathway, which is the body's primary way of synthesizing cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ol>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<ol start="2">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Cholesterol Production:</b> By blocking this enzyme, rosuvastatin significantly reduces the amount of cholesterol the liver produces.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ol>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<ol start="3">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased LDL Receptor Activity:</b> The liver, in response to the reduced internal cholesterol production, increases the number of <b>LDL receptors</b> on the surface of its cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are responsible for binding to and removing LDL cholesterol from the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ol>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted">
		<ol start="4">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Decreased LDL and Triglycerides:</b> The increased number of LDL receptors leads to a significant reduction in the amount of circulating LDL ("bad") cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Rosuvastatin also causes a modest increase in HDL ("good") cholesterol and a decrease in triglycerides.</span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By reducing the amount of circulating LDL cholesterol, rosuvastatin helps to prevent the formation of <b>atherosclerotic plaque</b>, which is the primary cause of heart attacks and strokes. Its high efficacy and long half-life allow for potent cholesterol reduction with once-daily dosing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
		</ol>
		<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"> </source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Vocina( Voriconazole)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Voriconazole is a synthetic triazole antifungal medication, part of the azole class of drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a broad-spectrum antifungal agent with enhanced activity against a wider range of fungi compared to older azoles like fluconazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Voriconazole is available in both oral (tablets and suspension) and intravenous formulations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly potent drug and is a cornerstone in the treatment of invasive fungal infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Voriconazole is a powerful antifungal used to treat serious and life-threatening fungal infections, particularly in immunocompromised patients. Its primary indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Invasive Aspergillosis:</b> It is considered a first-line treatment for invasive aspergillosis, a severe fungal infection of the lungs and other organs, especially in immunocompromised individuals.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Invasive Candidiasis:</b> It is also indicated for the treatment of invasive candidiasis, including infections in the esophagus, bloodstream, and other deep-seated sites.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fungal Infections Caused by Other Pathogens:</b> Voriconazole is effective against other serious infections caused by fungi such as <i>Scedosporium apiospermum</i> and <i>Fusarium</i> species, which are often resistant to other antifungal drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prophylaxis:</b> It may be used for the primary prevention of fungal infections in high-risk patients, such as those with neutropenia following chemotherapy or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Voriconazole's mechanism of action is primarily fungicidal (it kills the fungus) for certain species and fungistatic (it inhibits growth) for others.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Like other azoles, its core mechanism involves inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, a vital component of the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process can be broken down into these steps:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of 14-α-demethylase:</b> Voriconazole potently inhibits the fungal cytochrome P450-dependent enzyme called <b>lanosterol 14-alpha-demethylase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is crucial for converting lanosterol into ergosterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Cell Membrane Integrity:</b> By blocking this key enzymatic step, voriconazole prevents the production of ergosterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the accumulation of toxic intermediate sterols in the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Permeability and Cell Death:</b> The defective cell membrane has altered structure, increased permeability, and impaired function. This disruption leads to the leakage of essential cellular components and ultimately results in the death of the fungal cell.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Voriconazole's potent and broad-spectrum activity is attributed to its high affinity and specific inhibition of fungal cytochrome P450 enzymes. However, this same mechanism can lead to significant drug-drug interactions, as human cytochrome P450 enzymes are also inhibited, requiring careful monitoring.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Hexla
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clopidogrel (as Bisulphate) is an oral medication that acts as an <b>antiplatelet agent</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is used to prevent the formation of blood clots, which can lead to serious cardiovascular events.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a prodrug, meaning it is converted into its active form in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The bisulphate salt form is used for stability and bioavailability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clopidogrel is primarily indicated for reducing the risk of thrombotic events in patients with a history of:</span></span></p>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Recent myocardial infarction (MI)</b>, also known as a heart attack.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Recent stroke</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Established peripheral arterial disease (PAD)</b>.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS):</b> It is often used in combination with aspirin to prevent blood clots in patients with unstable angina or a recent heart attack.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-stenting:</b> It is a crucial part of dual antiplatelet therapy after the placement of a coronary stent to prevent blood clots from forming on the stent, which could lead to a heart attack.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clopidogrel's antiplatelet effect is a result of its ability to inhibit a specific receptor on the surface of platelets.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Metabolic Activation:</b> Clopidogrel is a prodrug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After it is absorbed, it is metabolized by a specific liver enzyme system (primarily cytochrome P450 2C19, or CYP2C19) into its active metabolite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="2">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Receptor Blockade:</b> The active metabolite of clopidogrel then <b>irreversibly binds to the P2Y12 receptor</b> on the surface of platelets.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="3">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Platelet Aggregation:</b> The P2Y12 receptor is a crucial part of the pathway that causes platelets to aggregate (clump together) and form a blood clot.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking this receptor, clopidogrel prevents the activation of the GPIIb/IIIa complex, which is the final common pathway for platelet aggregation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<ol start="4">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sustained Effect:</b> Because the binding is irreversible, the antiplatelet effect lasts for the entire lifespan of the platelet (approximately 7-10 days). This means that for the drug's effect to subside, new, uninhibited platelets must be produced by the body.</span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In essence, clopidogrel makes the platelets "slippery" and less likely to stick to each other or to the walls of blood vessels, thereby preventing the formation of dangerous blood clots.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Axabin 5mg (Apixaban)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Apixaban is a direct factor Xa inhibitor. It works by blocking Factor Xa, a key clotting protein in the blood coagulation cascade. By inhibiting this factor, it prevents the formation of thrombin, which is essential for clot formation.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indications:</b> It is primarily used to prevent blood clots that can lead to stroke in people with atrial fibrillation (an irregular heartbeat). It is also used to prevent deep vein thrombosis (DVT) and pulmonary embolism (PE) in patients who have had knee or hip replacement surgery.</p>
	</li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Dydrowin 10mg (Dydrogesterone)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dydrogesterone is a synthetic progestogen that is structurally and pharmacologically similar to natural progesterone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike many other synthetic progestogens, dydrogesterone has a unique stereoisomeric structure that gives it a high selectivity for progesterone receptors while having minimal androgenic or estrogenic activity.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is administered orally and is used to treat conditions associated with a deficiency of endogenous progesterone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dydrogesterone is indicated for the treatment of various conditions caused by or associated with progesterone deficiency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Luteal Phase Support:</b> It is used in assisted reproductive technologies (ART) to support the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle and help establish a pregnancy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Threatened and Habitual Abortion:</b> It is used to prevent miscarriage in women with a history of recurrent miscarriage or in cases of threatened miscarriage due to luteal phase defect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Endometriosis:</b> It is used to relieve the symptoms of endometriosis by inducing a decidualization of the endometrial tissue.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dysfunctional Uterine Bleeding:</b> It is used to regulate the menstrual cycle and control abnormal uterine bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT):</b> It is used in combination with estrogens to prevent endometrial hyperplasia and reduce the risk of endometrial cancer in postmenopausal women receiving estrogen therapy.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Premenstrual Syndrome (PMS):</b> It may be used to treat symptoms associated with PMS.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of dydrogesterone is based on its ability to act as an agonist at progesterone receptors, thereby mimicking the effects of natural progesterone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to Progesterone Receptors:</b> Dydrogesterone binds with high affinity to the <b>progesterone receptors</b> in the uterus, breast, and other target tissues. This binding activates the receptors and triggers a cascade of intracellular events that lead to the physiological effects of progesterone.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Maintaining the Uterine Lining:</b> In the uterine endometrium, progesterone plays a crucial role in preparing the lining for implantation of a fertilized egg.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It promotes the transformation of the proliferative endometrium into a secretory endometrium.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When used to prevent miscarriage, dydrogesterone helps to maintain the integrity of the uterine lining, which is essential for the survival of the embryo.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Uterine Contractions:</b> Dydrogesterone also has a tocolytic effect, meaning it helps to relax the smooth muscles of the uterus. By reducing uterine contractions, it can help prevent the expulsion of the embryo or fetus, which is a key mechanism in preventing threatened and habitual abortion.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Counteracting Estrogen's Effects:</b> In hormone replacement therapy, progesterone's role is to counteract the proliferative effect of estrogen on the uterine lining. Dydrogesterone ensures that the endometrium does not become excessively thick, thereby reducing the risk of endometrial hyperplasia and cancer.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dydrogesterone's unique stereoisomeric structure means that, unlike some other progestogens, it is not converted into androgens or estrogens and has very little effect on metabolic and endocrine parameters, which contributes to its favorable safety profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: LevetiCam 500mg
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levetiracetam, sold under brand names such as Keppra®, is an antiepileptic drug (AED) used for the treatment of seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a unique anticonvulsant due to its distinct and novel mechanism of action, which is different from most other AEDs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Levetiracetam is often favored by clinicians because it has a favorable safety profile, minimal drug-drug interactions, and does not require dose titration for many of its indications, allowing for rapid treatment. It is available in oral forms (tablets, extended-release tablets, oral solution) and an intravenous (IV) formulation.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levetiracetam is a broad-spectrum AED, meaning it is effective for a variety of seizure types. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Partial-Onset Seizures:</b> Used as monotherapy or adjunctive therapy in adults and children 4 years of age and older.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Myoclonic Seizures:</b> Used as adjunctive therapy in adults and adolescents 12 years of age and older with juvenile myoclonic epilepsy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li><sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Generalized Tonic-Clonic Seizures:</b> Used as adjunctive therapy in adults and children 6 years of age and older with idiopathic generalized epilepsy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The exact mechanism of action of levetiracetam is not fully understood, but its primary effect is believed to be related to its selective binding to a synaptic vesicle protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Synaptic Vesicles:</b> Neurons communicate by releasing neurotransmitters from small sacs called synaptic vesicles.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These vesicles travel to the presynaptic terminal (the end of the sending neuron) and fuse with the membrane, releasing their contents into the synaptic cleft. This process, known as synaptic transmission, is essential for brain function.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ol start="2">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>The SV2A Protein:</b> A key protein involved in this process is <b>synaptic vesicle glycoprotein 2A (SV2A)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The precise function of SV2A is still under investigation, but it is known to be involved in regulating the fusion of vesicles with the presynaptic membrane and the subsequent release of neurotransmitters.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Levetiracetam's Action:</b> Levetiracetam works by binding with high affinity and selectivity to the SV2A protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to this protein, levetiracetam is thought to modulate the release of neurotransmitters.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ol start="4">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This modulation is believed to reduce the synchronous, high-frequency firing of neurons that characterizes a seizure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By dampening the excessive release of excitatory neurotransmitters (like glutamate) and potentially promoting inhibitory neurotransmission, levetiracetam stabilizes neuronal activity and prevents the spread of seizure discharges.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Because levetiracetam's mechanism of action is distinct from most other AEDs, it does not interact with the GABAergic, glutamatergic, or sodium channel pathways that are targeted by other anticonvulsants. This unique profile explains its broad spectrum of activity and its low potential for drug-drug interactions.</span></span></p>

	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Labor and Delivery:</b> It is a commonly used medication for epidural and caudal blocks during childbirth to manage labor pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Peripheral Nerve Blocks:</b> It is injected around a specific nerve to numb the area it supplies, for example, for dental procedures or limb surgery.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ul>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Spinal Anesthesia:</b> It can be injected into the subarachnoid or epidural space of the spine to produce anesthesia for lower body surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bupivacaine works by blocking the generation and conduction of nerve impulses, thereby preventing the transmission of pain signals to the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism of action is centered on its interaction with voltage-gated sodium channels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sodium Channels and Nerve Impulses:</b> Neurons (nerve cells) transmit electrical signals, or action potentials, by the rapid influx of sodium ions (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>Na+) into the cell through voltage-gated sodium channels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This influx of positive ions causes the nerve cell to depolarize and propagate the electrical signal along the nerve fiber.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ol start="2">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bupivacaine's Action:</b> As a local anesthetic, bupivacaine enters the nerve fiber and binds to the intracellular portion of the voltage-gated sodium channel.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to this site, it physically blocks the channel, preventing sodium ions from entering the cell.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> With the sodium channels blocked, the nerve cell cannot depolarize and generate an action potential.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This effectively stops the initiation and conduction of the pain signal. Since the signal cannot be transmitted, the person does not feel pain in the area that has been affected by the drug.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key feature of bupivacaine is its high lipid solubility and its slow rate of dissociation from the sodium channel, which is why it has a longer duration of action compared to other local anesthetics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This prolonged effect makes it an excellent choice for procedures where extended pain relief is necessary.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
	</source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></li>
</ul>
