PRODUCT: Olamex 20mg (Olmesartan Medoxomil)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Olmesartan Medoxomil is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent and selective antihypertensive agent used to treat high blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "Medoxomil" portion of the name indicates that it is a prodrug—an inactive compound that is converted into its active form, olmesartan, after being absorbed by the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Olmesartan Medoxomil is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It can be used alone or in combination with other antihypertensive medications to achieve blood pressure control.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, olmesartan medoxomil helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and kidney damage.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Olmesartan's therapeutic effect is a result of its ability to block the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a key role in regulating blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to Active Drug:</b> After oral administration, olmesartan medoxomil is rapidly and completely converted by enzymes in the gastrointestinal tract to its active form, <b>olmesartan</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blockade of Angiotensin II Receptors:</b> Olmesartan acts as a competitive antagonist at the <b>angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to these receptors with high affinity, preventing the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> from attaching to them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor, meaning it causes blood vessels to narrow. By blocking its receptors, olmesartan prevents this narrowing, leading to <b>vasodilation</b> (the widening of blood vessels).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This decreases the peripheral resistance, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Aldosterone Release:</b> The binding of angiotensin II to its receptors also stimulates the release of <b>aldosterone</b>, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking this action, olmesartan helps to decrease the body's fluid volume, which further lowers blood pressure.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced fluid retention leads to a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure, easing the workload on the heart and helping to protect blood vessels and vital organs like the kidneys.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefepime is a fourth-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a broad-spectrum, semi-synthetic, bactericidal drug, meaning it kills bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is administered parenterally, either through intravenous (IV) injection or intramuscular (IM) injection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cefepime is a zwitterion, a molecule that has both a positive and negative charge, which enhances its ability to penetrate the outer membrane of Gram-negative bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This unique feature contributes to its broad-spectrum activity and makes it a key antibiotic for treating serious hospital-acquired infections.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">

	</ul>

	<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefepime, like all beta-lactam antibiotics, works by disrupting the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall, a crucial structure for the bacterium's survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The mechanism involves the following key steps:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<ol>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Penicillin-Binding Proteins (PBPs):</b> Cefepime's beta-lactam ring binds to and inhibits penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These are a group of enzymes located in the bacterial cell membrane that are essential for the final stage of cell wall synthesis.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Peptidoglycan Cross-linking:</b> PBPs catalyze the transpeptidation reaction, which cross-links peptidoglycan chains to form a strong, rigid cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to and inactivating PBPs, cefepime prevents this cross-linking.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<ol start="3">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osmotic Lysis and Cell Death:</b> The disruption of the cell wall's structural integrity makes the bacterium unable to withstand its own internal osmotic pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the activation of autolytic enzymes within the cell, causing the cell to swell and eventually rupture (lyse), resulting in the death of the bacterium.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ol>
	<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted">
	<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A significant advantage of cefepime is its enhanced stability against a wide range of beta-lactamase enzymes, including those produced by Gram-negative bacteria that can degrade other cephalosporins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This stability, along with its ability to rapidly penetrate the outer membrane of Gram-negative bacteria, gives it a broader and more potent spectrum of activity compared to earlier generations of cephalosporins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
	</source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Ampine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amlodipine as Besylate is a widely used medication belonging to the class of drugs known as <b>dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers</b>. The besylate salt is used to make the active drug, amlodipine, more stable and soluble, which improves its absorption and efficacy. Amlodipine is a long-acting calcium channel blocker, which allows for once-daily dosing.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amlodipine as Besylate is prescribed to treat several cardiovascular conditions. Its primary indications include:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is a first-line agent used to lower blood pressure, either alone or in combination with other antihypertensive drugs. By lowering blood pressure, it reduces the risk of serious complications such as stroke, heart attack, and kidney failure.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Angina Pectoris (Chest Pain):</span></span></b></span></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Chronic Stable Angina:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Used for the symptomatic treatment of chronic stable angina, which is chest pain that occurs with physical exertion.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vasospastic Angina (Prinzmetal's Angina):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is particularly effective in treating this type of angina, which is caused by a spasm of the coronary arteries.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Coronary Artery Disease (CAD):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> In patients with angiographically documented CAD and without heart failure, it is used to reduce the risk of hospitalization due to angina and the need for coronary revascularization procedures.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amlodipine's therapeutic effect is primarily due to its role as a <b>calcium channel blocker</b>. Here is a step-by-step breakdown of its mechanism:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Blockage of Calcium Channels:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Amlodipine specifically inhibits the influx of calcium ions into vascular smooth muscle cells and cardiac muscle cells by binding to <b>L-type voltage-gated calcium channels</b>.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vascular Smooth Muscle Relaxation:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> The inhibition of calcium influx is more pronounced in the smooth muscle cells of the arterial walls than in the heart muscle. Since calcium is essential for muscle contraction, blocking its entry causes the smooth muscles in the arteries to relax.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vasodilation and Reduced Peripheral Resistance:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> This relaxation leads to <b>vasodilation</b>, or the widening of blood vessels. As the blood vessels widen, the total peripheral resistance—the resistance the heart has to overcome to pump blood—decreases.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lowered Blood Pressure:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> The reduced peripheral resistance leads to a significant drop in blood pressure, making it easier for the heart to pump blood throughout the body and reducing its overall workload.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Improved Oxygen Supply to the Heart:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> In patients with angina, amlodipine dilates the coronary arteries, which increases the blood and oxygen supply to the heart muscle. This helps to relieve chest pain and prevent future angina attacks.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Alvostan DS
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Amlodipine as Besylate and Valsartan is a single-tablet medication that contains two distinct and complementary drugs for the treatment of high blood pressure (hypertension).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This fixed-dose combination leverages the strengths of both a <b>calcium channel blocker</b> (Amlodipine) and an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker</b> (Valsartan) to provide a more effective and comprehensive approach to blood pressure management than either drug alone.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination therapy is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b>. It is typically prescribed in two main scenarios:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>For patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled</b> by a single medication (either amlodipine or valsartan) on its own.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>As an initial therapy</b> for patients who are likely to require more than one medication to reach their blood pressure goals, particularly those with more severe hypertension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By lowering blood pressure, this medication reduces the risk of serious cardiovascular events, including stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different and vital pathways that regulate blood pressure:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Amlodipine (Calcium Channel Blocker)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amlodipine is a calcium channel blocker that works by inhibiting the influx of calcium ions into the smooth muscle cells of the blood vessel walls.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vasodilation:</b> The relaxation and widening of the arteries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Peripheral Resistance:</b> This makes it easier for the heart to pump blood, which in turn lowers blood pressure.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Valsartan (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Valsartan works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a crucial role in regulating blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its specific mechanism is:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Angiotensin II Receptor Blockade:</b> Valsartan selectively blocks the binding of a powerful hormone called <b>angiotensin II</b> to its receptors in various tissues, including blood vessels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II normally causes blood vessels to constrict (tighten).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking its effects, valsartan prevents this constriction, leading to further vasodilation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Decreased Aldosterone Release:</b> It also reduces the release of aldosterone, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water, which would otherwise increase blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of these two drugs provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Amlodipine's vasodilation</b> reduces peripheral resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Valsartan's blockade of the RAAS</b> prevents blood vessel constriction and fluid retention.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual mechanism of action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure compared to monotherapy, while also mitigating some of the side effects of each drug. For example, valsartan can help counteract the peripheral edema (swelling in hands and feet) that is a common side effect of amlodipine.</span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Alvostan Plus
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This single-tablet combination medication contains three distinct antihypertensive drugs: <b>Amlodipine as Besylate</b> (a calcium channel blocker), <b>Valsartan</b> (an angiotensin II receptor blocker), and <b>Hydrochlorothiazide</b> (a thiazide diuretic). This triple-drug therapy is used to provide a potent and multi-faceted approach to controlling severe hypertension.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is specifically indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b>, particularly in the following scenarios:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>For patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled</b> by a dual-combination therapy (such as amlodipine/valsartan, or any other two-drug regimen).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>As a more aggressive initial therapy</b> for patients with severe hypertension who are likely to require three medications to reach their blood pressure goals.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By targeting three different mechanisms that regulate blood pressure, this medication provides a robust reduction in both systolic and diastolic blood pressure, thereby significantly lowering the risk of life-threatening cardiovascular events like stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The medication works by simultaneously addressing three different pathways that contribute to high blood pressure:</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Amlodipine (Calcium Channel Blocker)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Amlodipine inhibits the entry of calcium ions into vascular smooth muscle cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> This causes the blood vessels to relax and widen (vasodilation), which decreases the resistance to blood flow and lowers blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Valsartan (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Valsartan blocks the effects of <b>Angiotensin II</b>, a powerful hormone that causes blood vessels to constrict and stimulates the release of aldosterone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking these receptors, valsartan prevents blood vessel constriction, leading to further vasodilation. It also reduces aldosterone's effect, which helps to decrease the body's retention of sodium and water.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">3. Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide Diuretic)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Hydrochlorothiazide, often referred to as HCTZ, acts on the kidneys to increase the excretion of sodium and chloride.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> As sodium and chloride are excreted, water follows, leading to a reduction in the body's total fluid volume. This reduced blood volume lowers blood pressure and further decreases the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The three-drug combination provides a synergistic effect that addresses different physiological aspects of hypertension:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amlodipine and Valsartan work together to relax and widen blood vessels.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hydrochlorothiazide reduces the total fluid volume in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combined action leads to a powerful and comprehensive reduction in blood pressure, often achieving control in patients for whom monotherapy or dual therapy has been insufficient. The inclusion of a diuretic also helps to counteract the peripheral edema (swelling) that is a common side effect of amlodipine.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Vodesta
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Valsartan and Hydrochlorothiazide is a medication that includes an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB)</b> and a <b>thiazide diuretic</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This fixed-dose tablet is used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension) by combining two different and complementary mechanisms of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination is often more effective at lowering blood pressure than either drug used alone.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This medication is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is typically prescribed to:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with either Valsartan or Hydrochlorothiazide alone.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As an initial therapy for patients with moderate to severe hypertension who are likely to require more than one drug to achieve their blood pressure goals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, this combination therapy helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.</span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different systems that regulate blood pressure and fluid balance in the body.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Valsartan (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Valsartan works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal pathway that controls blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It specifically blocks the binding of a powerful hormone called <b>angiotensin II</b> to its receptors in the blood vessels and other tissues.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking these receptors, valsartan prevents angiotensin II from causing blood vessels to constrict (narrow).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to <b>vasodilation</b> (widening of blood vessels), which decreases peripheral resistance and lowers blood pressure. It also reduces the retention of sodium and water in the body.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide Diuretic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Hydrochlorothiazide, often called HCTZ, acts on the kidneys.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It inhibits the reabsorption of sodium and chloride ions from the distal convoluted tubule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> As sodium and chloride are excreted, water follows them out of the body through urine. This process reduces the total fluid volume in the bloodstream, which in turn lowers blood pressure and decreases the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Valsartan and Hydrochlorothiazide provides a powerful synergistic effect:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Valsartan's action on the RAAS causes vasodilation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hydrochlorothiazide reduces the body's blood volume.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure than either drug could achieve on its own. The combination can also help to counteract potential side effects. For example, the diuretic effect helps manage the body's fluid balance, which is important in managing blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Radilna
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Diltiazem HCl is a medication classified as a <b>non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker</b>. Unlike its dihydropyridine counterparts (like amlodipine), diltiazem has a significant effect on both vascular smooth muscle and cardiac muscle. The "HCl" in its name stands for hydrochloride, which is a salt form used to improve the drug's stability and solubility for administration.</p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<hr />
<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3>Indications</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Diltiazem HCl is used to treat a variety of cardiovascular conditions. Its primary indications include:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</b> It is effective in lowering blood pressure by dilating blood vessels, which reduces the heart's workload.

	<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>
	</li>
	<li><b>Angina Pectoris (Chest Pain):</b>
	<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

	<ul>
		<li><b>Chronic Stable Angina:</b> It helps prevent and manage chest pain by increasing oxygen supply to the heart.

		<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>Vasospastic Angina (Prinzmetal's Angina):</b> It is highly effective in treating this form of angina, which is caused by coronary artery spasms.</p>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li><b>Supraventricular Tachycardia (SVT):</b> It is used to control the ventricular rate in patients with atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter, and to terminate episodes of paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia.
	<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>
	</li>
</ul>

<hr />
<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3>Mechanism of Action</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Diltiazem's mechanism of action involves the inhibition of calcium ion flow into muscle cells, but it affects both the heart and blood vessels.</p>

<ol start="1">
	<li>
	<p><b>Calcium Channel Blockade:</b> Diltiazem specifically blocks the entry of calcium ions through <b>L-type voltage-gated calcium channels</b> on the membranes of both vascular smooth muscle cells and cardiac muscle cells.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Effects on Cardiac Muscle:</b></p>

	<ul>
		<li><b>Decreased Myocardial Contractility:</b> By reducing calcium influx into heart muscle cells, diltiazem weakens the force of heart muscle contractions (a negative inotropic effect).</li>
		<li><b>Slowed Heart Rate:</b> It also slows down the electrical conduction through the sinoatrial (SA) and atrioventricular (AV) nodes of the heart. This leads to a slower heart rate (a negative chronotropic effect).</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Effects on Vascular Smooth Muscle:</b></p>

	<ul>
		<li><b>Vasodilation:</b> By blocking calcium in the smooth muscle cells of the arteries, diltiazem causes these muscles to relax. This leads to the widening of both coronary arteries (which supply blood to the heart) and peripheral arteries throughout the body.</li>
		<li>Combined Effect:The combined action of diltiazem provides multiple therapeutic benefits:</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul>
	<li><b>Lowered Blood Pressure:</b> The dilation of peripheral arteries reduces the resistance the heart has to pump against, lowering blood pressure.</li>
	<li><b>Reduced Cardiac Workload:</b> The reduced heart rate and force of contraction decrease the heart's oxygen demand, which is beneficial for managing angina.</li>
	<li><b>Improved Oxygen Supply:</b> The dilation of coronary arteries increases blood flow and oxygen supply to the heart muscle, directly helping to relieve chest pain.</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Rate Control:</b> The effect on the heart's electrical conduction system helps to control and normalize abnormal heart rhythms.</p>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Aventor
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>This is a single-tablet combination medication that pairs a <b>calcium channel blocker (Amlodipine)</b> with an <b>HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor (Atorvastatin)</b>. This combination is designed to treat two co-existing and often related conditions: high blood pressure (hypertension) and high cholesterol (dyslipidemia). The use of a single pill simplifies the medication regimen for patients who require both therapies.<button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<p>INDICATION<br />
This combination is indicated for:</p>

<ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Patients with hypertension and dyslipidemia</b> who are not adequately controlled with either amlodipine or atorvastatin alone.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Patients who require both medications</b> and can benefit from the convenience of a single-pill regimen to improve adherence.</p>
	</li>
</ul>

<p>By effectively managing both high blood pressure and high cholesterol, this medication reduces the overall risk of major cardiovascular events, including heart attacks, strokes, and the need for revascularization procedures.</p>

<p>Mechanism of Action</p>

<p>This combination medication works through two distinct and complementary mechanisms to address both conditions simultaneously.</p>

<p>1. Amlodipine (Calcium Channel Blocker)</p>

<h4><b>Action:</b> Amlodipine works by inhibiting the influx of calcium ions into the smooth muscle cells of the blood vessel walls.</h4>

<p><b>Effect:</b> This causes the blood vessels to relax and widen (vasodilation), which decreases the peripheral resistance to blood flow and lowers blood pressure. This action reduces the workload on the heart and improves blood circulation.</p>

<ul>
	<li>2. Atorvastatin (HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitor / Statin)
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
</ul>

<h4><b>Action:</b> Atorvastatin works in the liver by inhibiting the enzyme <b>HMG-CoA reductase</b>. This enzyme is the rate-limiting step in the body's synthesis of cholesterol.</h4>

<p><b>Effect:</b> By blocking this enzyme, atorvastatin significantly reduces the amount of cholesterol the liver produces. This leads to a decrease in LDL ("bad") cholesterol, which is the primary driver of atherosclerosis (the buildup of plaque in arteries). It also causes a modest increase in HDL ("good") cholesterol and a decrease in triglycerides.</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h4>Combined Effect</h4>

<h4>The combination of these two drugs provides a synergistic approach to cardiovascular risk reduction:</h4>

<ul>
	<li><b>Amlodipine</b> directly lowers blood pressure, reducing the strain on the heart and arteries.</li>
	<li><b>Atorvastatin</b> lowers cholesterol levels, preventing the formation of atherosclerotic plaque that can lead to heart attacks and strokes.</li>
</ul>

<p>By treating both hypertension and high cholesterol, the medication addresses two of the most significant modifiable risk factors for cardiovascular disease, leading to a substantial reduction in a patient's overall cardiovascular risk profile.</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ezmitan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Ezetimibe and Atorvastatin is a single-tablet medication that includes two drugs with different, yet complementary, mechanisms for lowering cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It pairs <b>Ezetimibe</b>, a cholesterol absorption inhibitor, with <b>Atorvastatin</b>, an HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor (statin).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This fixed-dose combination provides a powerful dual-action approach to reducing blood cholesterol levels more effectively than either drug alone.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>For patients whose cholesterol levels are not adequately controlled</b> by a statin alone (Atorvastatin).</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>For patients who need to lower their cholesterol aggressively</b> and can benefit from the convenience and increased efficacy of a single-tablet regimen.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>To reduce the risk of major cardiovascular events</b> such as heart attack, stroke, and the need for revascularization procedures in patients with coronary heart disease or other risk factors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination works by targeting two different sources of cholesterol: the liver's internal production and the absorption of cholesterol from the diet.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Atorvastatin (HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitor / Statin)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Atorvastatin works in the liver by inhibiting the enzyme <b>HMG-CoA reductase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is a critical and rate-limiting step in the body's synthesis of cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking this enzyme, atorvastatin significantly reduces the amount of cholesterol the liver produces.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to a substantial decrease in LDL ("bad") cholesterol, which is the primary driver of atherosclerosis (the buildup of plaque in arteries).<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Ezetimibe (Cholesterol Absorption Inhibitor)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Ezetimibe works in the small intestine, where it specifically inhibits the absorption of cholesterol from the diet and bile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It does this by blocking the <b>Niemann-Pick C1-Like 1 (NPC1L1)</b> protein, a key transporter for cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking this protein, Ezetimibe reduces the amount of cholesterol that enters the body from the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This forces the liver to use more of the cholesterol already in the bloodstream, leading to a further reduction in blood cholesterol levels.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of these two drugs provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Atorvastatin</b> blocks the body's primary internal source of cholesterol production.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ezetimibe</b> blocks the external source of cholesterol from food.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual-inhibition approach leads to a more comprehensive and potent reduction in LDL cholesterol compared to using a statin alone. It is particularly effective for patients who need an aggressive reduction in their cholesterol levels to minimize their cardiovascular risk.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Wozide
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<h3>Losartan Potassium is a medication classified as an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB)</b>. It is a potent and selective antihypertensive drug that works by blocking the effects of the hormone angiotensin II. The "potassium" in its name refers to the salt form used to improve the drug's stability and bioavailability for oral administration.</h3>

<p>Indications</p>

<p>Losartan is primarily used for the treatment of various cardiovascular and renal conditions. Its main indications include:</p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</b> It is a first-line agent used to lower blood pressure, either alone or in combination with other antihypertensive drugs.</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Diabetic Nephropathy:</b> It is indicated to slow the progression of kidney disease in patients with Type 2 diabetes who also have hypertension.</li>
	<li><b>Stroke Risk Reduction:</b> It is prescribed to reduce the risk of stroke in patients with high blood pressure and an enlarged heart (left ventricular hypertrophy).</li>
</ul>

<hr />
<p>Mechanism of Action</p>

<p>Losartan's therapeutic effects are a result of its ability to block the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a key role in regulating blood pressure. Here's a step-by-step breakdown of how it works:<button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ol start="1">
	<li><b>Blockade of Angiotensin II Receptors:</b> Losartan acts as a competitive antagonist at the <b>angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors</b>. It binds to these receptors, preventing the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> from attaching to them.</li>
	<li><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor, meaning it causes blood vessels to narrow. By blocking its receptors, losartan prevents this narrowing, leading to <b>vasodilation</b> (the widening of blood vessels). This decreases the peripheral resistance, making it easier for the heart to pump blood</li>
	<li><b>Reduced Aldosterone Release:</b> The binding of angiotensin II to its receptors also stimulates the release of <b>aldosterone</b>, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water. By blocking this action, losartan helps to decrease the body's fluid volume, which further lowers blood pressure.</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced fluid retention leads to a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure, easing the workload on the heart and helping to protect blood vessels and vital organs like the kidneys.</p>
	</li>
</ol>

<p>Unlike ACE inhibitors (another class of blood pressure medications), losartan does not interfere with the breakdown of bradykinin, which is why it is associated with a lower risk of causing the side effect of a dry cough.</p>

HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Losartan Potassium-Hydrochlorothiazide is a single-tablet combination medication that pairs an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB)</b> with a <b>thiazide diuretic</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This medication is used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension) by combining two different and complementary mechanisms of action to provide a more effective and comprehensive blood pressure control than either drug alone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">For patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with either losartan or hydrochlorothiazide alone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As an initial therapy for patients with moderate to severe hypertension who are likely to require more than one medication to achieve their blood pressure goals.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, this combination helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.</span></span></p>

<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different systems that regulate blood pressure and fluid balance in the body.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Losartan Potassium (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Losartan works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It specifically blocks the binding of the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> to its AT1 receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking these receptors, losartan prevents angiotensin II from causing blood vessels to constrict (narrow).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to <b>vasodilation</b> (widening of blood vessels), which decreases peripheral resistance and lowers blood pressure. It also reduces the retention of sodium and water in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide Diuretic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Hydrochlorothiazide, often called HCTZ, acts on the kidneys.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It inhibits the reabsorption of sodium and chloride ions from the distal convoluted tubule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> As sodium and chloride are excreted, water follows them out of the body through urine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process reduces the total fluid volume in the bloodstream, which in turn lowers blood pressure and decreases the workload on the heart.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Losartan and Hydrochlorothiazide provides a powerful synergistic effect:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Losartan's action on the RAAS causes vasodilation.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hydrochlorothiazide reduces the body's blood volume.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure than either drug could achieve on its own, making it a highly effective treatment for hypertension.</span></span></p>
