PRODUCT: Ivabro
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<h3>Ivabradine HCl is a unique medication that is classified as a <b>hyperpolarization-activated cyclic nucleotide-gated (HCN) channel blocker</b> or, more simply, an <b>If​ channel inhibitor</b>. Unlike beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers, ivabradine lowers heart rate without affecting blood pressure or myocardial contractility (the force of the heart's contraction). The "HCl" in its name denotes the hydrochloride salt form, which is used for stability and solubility.</h3>

<h3>Indications</h3>

<p>Ivabradine is primarily indicated for the treatment of two conditions, both related to its ability to slow the heart rate:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Symptomatic Stable Angina Pectoris:</b> It is used to treat chronic stable angina in adults with a normal sinus rhythm who have a heart rate of at least 70 beats per minute. It is typically used for patients who cannot tolerate or have contraindications to beta-blockers, or in combination with beta-blockers if their angina is not adequately controlled.

	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Chronic Heart Failure:</b> It is used in adults with chronic systolic heart failure (NYHA class II to IV) with a normal sinus rhythm and a heart rate of at least 75 beats per minute, in combination with standard therapy, including beta-blocker therapy, or when beta-blocker therapy is contraindicated or not tolerated.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3>Mechanism of Action</h3>

<p>Ivabradine's mechanism of action is highly specific and differs from other cardiovascular drugs. It works by selectively inhibiting the <b>If​ channel</b>, also known as the "funny current" channel</p>

<p><b>If​ Channel Inhibition:</b> The If​ channel is found in the <b>sinoatrial (SA) node</b>, the natural pacemaker of the heart. These channels are responsible for generating the slow inward current that drives the spontaneous depolarization of the SA node cells. This depolarization is what sets the heart's rhythm and rate.</p>

<ol start="1">
	<li><b>Slowing the Pacemaker:</b> By selectively blocking these If​ channels, ivabradine slows down this spontaneous depolarization process.</li>
	<li><b>Heart Rate Reduction:</b> This results in a reduction in the firing rate of the SA node, which directly translates to a slower heart rate.
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>No Effect on Blood Pressure or Contractility:</b> Because ivabradine's action is confined to the If​ channels in the SA node, it does not affect the calcium or potassium channels responsible for myocardial contractility or vascular smooth muscle tone. This means it can lower the heart rate without causing a drop in blood pressure or a decrease in the force of the heart's contraction, which are common effects of beta-blockers and calcium channel blockers.</p>
	</li>
</ol>

<p>This unique mechanism makes ivabradine an excellent option for patients who need heart rate control but have conditions where a drop in blood pressure or reduced heart contractility would be undesirable.</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Nabiz
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Nebivolol is a medication that belongs to the class of <b>beta-blockers</b>. It is considered a "third-generation" beta-blocker due to its unique pharmacological profile. Nebivolol is a highly <b>cardioselective</b> beta-1 adrenergic receptor antagonist, meaning it primarily targets and blocks receptors in the heart. What sets it apart from many other beta-blockers is its additional ability to induce vasodilation (the widening of blood vessels), which contributes significantly to its blood pressure-lowering effects. The hydrochloride salt form is used to make the drug stable for oral administration.</p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3>Indications</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Nebivolol is used for the treatment of several cardiovascular conditions, most notably:<button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</b> It is used alone or in combination with other medications to lower blood pressure.

	<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>
	</li>
	<li><b>Chronic Heart Failure:</b> It is indicated in Europe and other regions for the treatment of chronic heart failure in elderly patients.
	<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>
	</li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3>Mechanism of Action</h3>

<p>Nebivolol's therapeutic effects are a result of a dual mechanism that combines beta-blockade with vasodilation:<button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ol start="1">
	<li><b>Selective Beta-1 Receptor Blockade:</b> Nebivolol is a highly selective antagonist of beta-1 adrenergic receptors, which are found primarily in the heart. By blocking these receptors, it prevents the action of stress hormones like norepinephrine and epinephrine. This results in:

	<ul>
		<li>A decrease in heart rate (a negative chronotropic effect).</li>
		<li>A decrease in the force of heart muscle contractions (a negative inotropic effect).</li>
		<li>
		<p>A reduction in cardiac output and the heart's workload.</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>Nitric Oxide-Mediated Vasodilation:</b> This is the unique aspect of nebivolol's mechanism. It stimulates the release of <b>nitric oxide (NO)</b> from the endothelium (the inner lining of blood vessels). Nitric oxide is a potent vasodilator, meaning it causes the blood vessels to relax and widen. This effect is thought to be mediated through an agonistic action on beta-3 adrenergic receptors.</p>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Combined Effect:</b> The combination of these two actions provides a comprehensive approach to lowering blood pressure. The beta-1 blockade reduces the heart's workload, while the nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation reduces the peripheral resistance that the heart has to pump against. This dual mechanism contributes to its high efficacy and can lead to improved outcomes for patients with hypertension and heart failure.</p>
	</li>
</ol>

Nebivolol is a highly <b>cardioselective</b> beta-1 adrenergic receptor antagonist, meaning it primarily targets and blocks receptors in the heart. PRODUCT: Reztin SR
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><span style="font-size:18px;">Description</span></p>

<p>Ranolazine is a medication used to treat chronic angina (chest pain). It belongs to a class of drugs known as <b>anti-anginals</b>. It is a unique medication because its mechanism of action is different from other commonly used anti-anginal drugs like beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, and nitrates. Ranolazine works by targeting specific ion channels in the heart, and importantly, it does so without significantly affecting heart rate or blood pressure.</p>

<p><span style="font-size: 18px;">Indications</span></p>

<p>Ranolazine is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>chronic stable angina pectoris</b>. It is typically used:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>As a second-line therapy</b> in combination with other anti-anginal drugs (such as beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, or nitrates) when those medications alone do not adequately control symptoms.</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>For patients who cannot tolerate other anti-anginal medications</b> due to side effects like low heart rate or low blood pressure.</p>
	</li>
</ul>

<p>While it is approved for chronic angina, ranolazine is not used to treat acute angina attacks.</p>

<p>Mechanism of Action</p>

<p>Ranolazine's anti-anginal effects are a result of its unique ability to selectively inhibit the <b>late sodium current (INa​) in cardiac muscle cells</b>.</p>

<p><b>Inhibition of Late Sodium Current:</b> Under normal conditions, a small, persistent inward sodium current flows into heart cells during the repolarization phase (Phase 3) of the cardiac action potential. In ischemic conditions (when the heart muscle is not getting enough oxygen), this "late" or "persistent" sodium current is significantly increased.</p>

<ol start="1">
	<li><b>Prevention of Calcium Overload:</b> This increased sodium influx leads to an accumulation of intracellular sodium (Na+). This elevated sodium level, in turn, disrupts the function of the <b>sodium-calcium exchanger (NCX)</b>, a protein that pumps sodium out of the cell in exchange for calcium (Ca2+) coming in. The disruption of this exchanger leads to an overload of calcium inside the heart muscle cells.</li>
	<li><b>Reduced Myocardial Wall Tension:</b> This calcium overload can cause a number of problems, including reduced diastolic relaxation and increased ventricular wall tension, which increases the heart's oxygen demand and can worsen angina. By inhibiting the late sodium current, ranolazine prevents this chain of events
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Improved Myocardial Function:</b> The reduction in intracellular sodium and calcium overload improves the relaxation of the heart muscle and reduces ventricular stiffness, which ultimately decreases the heart's oxygen consumption and improves blood flow. This effect alleviates the symptoms of angina without affecting heart rate or blood pressure.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Secondary Effects:</b> By stabilizing the ionic balance in heart cells, ranolazine also has been shown to have antiarrhythmic properties, as it can reduce the electrical instability caused by late sodium current overload.</p>
	</li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Clowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3>Description</h3>

<p>Clonidine is a medication that belongs to a class of drugs known as <b>central alpha-2 adrenergic agonists</b>. It works primarily in the central nervous system (brain and spinal cord) to regulate blood pressure and heart rate. It is available in various forms, including oral tablets and a transdermal patch.</p>

<p><span style="font-size:16px;">Indications</span></p>

<p>Clonidine is used for a variety of conditions, with its primary indications being:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure):</b> It is used alone or in combination with other antihypertensive agents to lower blood pressure. It is often considered a second- or third-line agent for this purpose.</li>
	<li><b>Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD):</b> The extended-release form of clonidine is approved to treat ADHD, particularly in children and adolescents, either alone or in combination with stimulant medications.</li>
</ul>

<p>In addition to these primary uses, clonidine is also used off-label for several other conditions, including:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Managing withdrawal symptoms</b> from opioids, alcohol, or nicotine.</li>
	<li><b>Treating menopausal flushing</b>.</li>
	<li><b>Prophylaxis of migraine headaches</b>.</li>
	<li><b>Managing certain types of neuropathic pain</b>.
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
</ul>

<hr />
<p>Mechanism of Action</p>

<p>Clonidine's mechanism of action is centered on its ability to stimulate alpha-2 adrenergic receptors in the brain.<button _ngcontent-ng-c641761624="" cdkoverlayorigin="" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ol start="1">
	<li>
	<p><b>Central Alpha-2 Agonism:</b> Clonidine acts as a direct agonist (a stimulator) of <b>presynaptic alpha-2 adrenergic receptors</b> in the brainstem, particularly in the vasomotor center.</p>
	</li>
	<li><b>Reduced Sympathetic Outflow:</b> When these receptors are stimulated, they act as a negative feedback mechanism, reducing the release of the neurotransmitter <b>norepinephrine</b> from nerve terminals. Norepinephrine is a key chemical messenger of the <b>sympathetic nervous system</b> ("fight or flight" response)
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Reduction:</b> By reducing the sympathetic nervous system's outflow from the central nervous system, clonidine decreases sympathetic stimulation to the heart and blood vessels. This leads to:</p>

	<ul>
		<li><b>Peripheral Vasodilation:</b> Blood vessels relax and widen, reducing peripheral resistance.</li>
		<li><b>Decreased Heart Rate:</b> The heart beats more slowly.</li>
		<li><b>Reduced Cardiac Output:</b> The amount of blood the heart pumps per minute decreases.</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation, a slower heart rate, and reduced cardiac output results in a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure.</p>
	</li>
</ol>

<p>In the context of <b>ADHD</b>, the mechanism is thought to involve the modulation of norepinephrine in the prefrontal cortex, which can improve attention, reduce impulsivity, and manage hyperactivity.</p>

PRODUCT: Azil
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan Medoxomil is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent and selective antihypertensive agent used to treat high blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "Medoxomil" portion of the name indicates that it is a prodrug—an inactive compound that is converted into its active form, azilsartan, after being absorbed by the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Azilsartan Medoxomil is primarily indicated for the treatment of <span style="font-weight:normal">hypertension (high blood pressure)</span>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It can be used alone or in combination with other antihypertensive medications to achieve blood pressure control.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, azilsartan medoxomil helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and kidney damage.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan's therapeutic effect is a result of its ability to block the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal system that plays a key role in regulating blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to Active Drug:</b> After oral administration, azilsartan medoxomil is rapidly and completely converted by enzymes in the gastrointestinal tract to its active form, <b>azilsartan</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blockade of Angiotensin II Receptors:</b> Azilsartan acts as a competitive antagonist at the <b>angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to these receptors with high affinity, preventing the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> from attaching to them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Vasoconstriction:</b> Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor, meaning it causes blood vessels to narrow.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking its receptors, azilsartan prevents this narrowing, leading to <b>vasodilation</b> (the widening of blood vessels).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This decreases the peripheral resistance, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Aldosterone Release:</b> The binding of angiotensin II to its receptors also stimulates the release of <b>aldosterone</b>, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water. By blocking this action, azilsartan helps to decrease the body's fluid volume, which further lowers blood pressure.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Overall Effect:</b> The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced fluid retention leads to a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure, easing the workload on the heart and helping to protect blood vessels and vital organs like the kidneys.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azilsartan is noted for its potent and long-lasting blood pressure-lowering effect compared to other ARBs, which is a key reason for its once-daily dosing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Maxten Plus
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Irbesartan + Hydrochlorothiazide is a single-tablet combination medication that includes an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB)</b> and a <b>thiazide diuretic</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This medication is used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension) by combining two different and complementary mechanisms of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This fixed-dose combination often provides a more significant and comprehensive blood pressure reduction than either drug used alone.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold"><span style="font-weight:normal">This combination therapy is primarily indicated for the treatment of </span><span style="font-weight:normal">hypertension</span><span style="font-weight:normal">.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span><span style="font-weight:normal"> It is typically prescribed to:</span></span></span></span></h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with either irbesartan or hydrochlorothiazide alone.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As an initial therapy for patients with moderate to severe hypertension who are likely to require more than one drug to achieve their blood pressure goals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, this combination helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different systems that regulate blood pressure and fluid balance in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Irbesartan (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Irbesartan works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It specifically blocks the binding of the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> to its AT1 receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking these receptors, irbesartan prevents angiotensin II from causing blood vessels to constrict (narrow). This leads to <b>vasodilation</b> (widening of blood vessels), which decreases peripheral resistance and lowers blood pressure. It also reduces the retention of sodium and water in the body.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide Diuretic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Hydrochlorothiazide, often called HCTZ, acts on the kidneys.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It inhibits the reabsorption of sodium and chloride ions from the distal convoluted tubule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> As sodium and chloride are excreted, water follows them out of the body through urine. This process reduces the total fluid volume in the bloodstream, which in turn lowers blood pressure and decreases the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Irbesartan and Hydrochlorothiazide provides a powerful synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Irbesartan's action on the RAAS causes vasodilation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hydrochlorothiazide reduces the body's blood volume.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure than either drug could achieve on its own, making it a highly effective treatment for hypertension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Nobrat
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fenofibrate is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>fibrates</b> (or fibric acid derivatives).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a lipid-regulating agent that is used to treat various types of dyslipidemia (abnormal levels of lipids in the blood).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Fenofibrate works primarily by activating a key nuclear receptor that regulates lipid metabolism.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fenofibrate is used to manage several lipid disorders, with its main indications being:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Severe Hypertriglyceridemia:</b> It is used to reduce very high levels of triglycerides in the blood, which can be a risk factor for pancreatitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Hypercholesterolemia and Mixed Dyslipidemia:</b> It is used to lower high levels of LDL ("bad") cholesterol and total cholesterol, and to raise low levels of HDL ("good") cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often used in combination with other lipid-lowering therapies or as an alternative when statins are not tolerated.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Atherosclerosis:</b> By improving the lipid profile, fenofibrate helps to reduce the progression of atherosclerosis (the buildup of plaque in arteries), which in turn lowers the risk of cardiovascular events.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fenofibrate's mechanism of action is primarily mediated through its activation of a nuclear transcription factor called <b>Peroxisome Proliferator-Activated Receptor Alpha (PPARα)</b>.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>PPARα Activation:</b> Fenofibrate binds to and activates PPARα, which is a key regulator of gene expression involved in lipid and lipoprotein metabolism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Lipolysis:</b> Activation of PPARα leads to an increase in the synthesis and activity of <b>lipoprotein lipase (LPL)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> LPL is an enzyme that breaks down triglycerides in lipoproteins (like VLDL and chylomicrons) into free fatty acids.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This increased breakdown of triglycerides is the primary reason fenofibrate so effectively lowers triglyceride levels.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Decreased VLDL Production:</b> Fenofibrate also reduces the hepatic synthesis of the apolipoprotein C-III (apo C-III), an inhibitor of LPL.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By reducing apo C-III, it further enhances LPL activity. It also reduces the liver's production of very-low-density lipoprotein (VLDL), which is the primary carrier of triglycerides in the blood.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased HDL Cholesterol:</b> By increasing the synthesis of apolipoprotein A-I and apolipoprotein A-II, fenofibrate leads to an increase in HDL ("good") cholesterol levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> HDL helps remove cholesterol from the arteries and transport it back to the liver for disposal.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced LDL Cholesterol:</b> While its main effect is on triglycerides, fenofibrate can also cause a modest reduction in LDL cholesterol. This is achieved by promoting the conversion of dense LDL particles into a more benign form and by increasing the catabolism (breakdown) of LDL.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, fenofibrate works by revving up the body's natural processes for breaking down triglycerides and increasing the good cholesterol, while also reducing the production of the bad lipoproteins that contribute to heart disease.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Lipotrex
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atorvastatin Calcium is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as <b>statins</b> or <b>HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is one of the most widely prescribed drugs in the world and is used to lower high levels of cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "calcium" in its name refers to the salt form used to ensure the drug's stability and bioavailability for oral administration.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atorvastatin Calcium is used for a variety of conditions related to high cholesterol and the prevention of cardiovascular disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Hypercholesterolemia:</b> It is a first-line therapy to lower high levels of total cholesterol and LDL ("bad") cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hypertriglyceridemia:</b> It is also effective at lowering high levels of triglycerides.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cardiovascular Risk Reduction:</b> It is prescribed to reduce the risk of heart attack, stroke, and the need for revascularization procedures in patients with or without existing heart disease, but who have multiple risk factors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Secondary Prevention:</b> It is used in patients who have already experienced a heart attack or stroke to prevent future events.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Atorvastatin's therapeutic effect is primarily a result of its ability to inhibit a key enzyme in the cholesterol synthesis pathway.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enzyme Inhibition:</b> Atorvastatin works in the liver by competitively inhibiting the enzyme <b>HMG-CoA reductase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is the rate-limiting step in the mevalonate pathway, which is the body's primary way of synthesizing cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Cholesterol Production:</b> By blocking this enzyme, atorvastatin significantly reduces the amount of cholesterol the liver produces.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased LDL Receptor Activity:</b> The liver, in response to the reduced internal cholesterol production, increases the number of <b>LDL receptors</b> on the surface of its cells. These receptors are responsible for binding to and removing LDL cholesterol from the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Decreased LDL and Triglycerides:</b> The increased number of LDL receptors leads to a significant reduction in the amount of circulating LDL ("bad") cholesterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Atorvastatin also causes a modest increase in HDL ("good") cholesterol and a decrease in triglycerides.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By reducing the amount of circulating LDL cholesterol, atorvastatin helps to prevent the formation of <b>atherosclerotic plaque</b>, which is the primary cause of heart attacks and strokes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Telmirox
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><button _ngcontent-ng-c448890458="" cdkoverlayorigin="" data-hveid="0" data-ved="0CAAQvoAQahcKEwi5rbbMoIyQAxUAAAAAHQAAAAAQMg" decode-data-ved="1" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><br />
</button></p>

<p>Description</p>

<h3>Telmisartan is a medication that belongs to a class of drugs known as angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs). It is taken orally, typically as a tablet once a day. It is often used as a standalone treatment for high blood pressure but can also be used in combination with other medications, such as diuretics or calcium channel blockers, to enhance its effect.</h3>

<p>Indications</p>

<p>Telmisartan is prescribed for various conditions, including:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>High Blood Pressure (Hypertension):</b> It is a primary treatment for high blood pressure, as it helps to lower and control blood pressure, reducing the risk of serious health problems like strokes, heart failure, and kidney disease.</li>
	<li><b>Cardiovascular Risk Reduction:</b> It is used in adults aged 55 or older who are at high risk for major cardiovascular events (like heart attack or stroke) and who are unable to take ACE inhibitors. This includes individuals with a history of coronary artery disease, peripheral arterial disease, stroke, or high-risk diabetes.</li>
	<li><b>Other uses:</b> In some cases, it is also used to treat heart failure and diabetic nephropathy (kidney disease in people with diabetes and high blood pressure).
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3>Mechanism of Action</h3>

<p>Telmisartan works by targeting the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS), a complex hormonal system that regulates blood pressure and fluid balance. Here is a breakdown of its mechanism:<button _ngcontent-ng-c448890458="" cdkoverlayorigin="" data-hveid="0" data-ved="0CAAQvoAQahcKEwi5rbbMoIyQAxUAAAAAHQAAAAAQOA" decode-data-ved="1" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ol start="1">
	<li><b>Blocking Angiotensin II:</b> The body produces a substance called angiotensin II, which is a powerful vasoconstrictor. This means it causes blood vessels to narrow, which in turn increases blood pressure.</li>
	<li><b>Angiotensin II Receptor Antagonist:</b> Telmisartan is an "angiotensin II receptor antagonist." It works by selectively blocking the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors.
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Relaxing Blood Vessels:</b> By binding to these receptors, telmisartan prevents angiotensin II from tightening the blood vessels. This leads to the relaxation and widening of the blood vessels, which lowers blood pressure and allows blood to flow more smoothly.</p>
	</li>
	<li><b>Reduced Aldosterone:</b> Blocking the action of angiotensin II also reduces the secretion of aldosterone, a hormone that causes the body to retain sodium and water. This leads to a decrease in blood volume, further helping to lower blood pressure.</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Potential Metabolic Effects:</b> Some studies suggest that telmisartan may also have an additional effect as a partial agonist on peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-gamma (PPAR-γ). This unique property may offer benefits related to glucose and lipid metabolism, such as improved insulin sensitivity.</p>
	</li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Telmirox-H
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telmisartan + Hydrochlorothiazide is a single-tablet combination medication that includes an <b>angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB)</b> and a <b>thiazide diuretic</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This medication is used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension) by combining two different and complementary mechanisms of action to provide a more effective and comprehensive blood pressure control than either drug alone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination therapy is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b>. It is typically prescribed to:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with either telmisartan or hydrochlorothiazide alone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As an initial therapy for patients with moderate to severe hypertension who are likely to require more than one drug to achieve their blood pressure goals.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, this combination helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different systems that regulate blood pressure and fluid balance in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Telmisartan (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker - ARB)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Telmisartan works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It specifically blocks the binding of the powerful hormone <b>angiotensin II</b> to its AT1 receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking these receptors, telmisartan prevents angiotensin II from causing blood vessels to constrict (narrow). This leads to <b>vasodilation</b> (widening of blood vessels), which decreases peripheral resistance and lowers blood pressure. It also reduces the retention of sodium and water in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide Diuretic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Hydrochlorothiazide, often called HCTZ, acts on the kidneys.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It inhibits the reabsorption of sodium and chloride ions from the distal convoluted tubule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> As sodium and chloride are excreted, water follows them out of the body through urine. This process reduces the total fluid volume in the bloodstream, which in turn lowers blood pressure and decreases the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Telmisartan and Hydrochlorothiazide provides a powerful synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telmisartan's action on the RAAS causes vasodilation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hydrochlorothiazide reduces the body's blood volume.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure than either drug could achieve on its own, making it a highly effective treatment for hypertension.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Mopa
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Methyldopa is a centrally acting <b>antihypertensive</b> drug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a prodrug that is converted to an active compound in the brain, where it works to lower blood pressure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Methyldopa has been in clinical use for many years and is particularly notable for its safety profile, especially in pregnant women with hypertension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Methyldopa is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its use has become more limited due to the development of newer, more convenient, and often more effective drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> However, it remains an important medication in specific patient populations, particularly for:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hypertension in Pregnancy:</b> It is considered one of the safest and most well-studied antihypertensives for use during pregnancy.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Patients with chronic kidney disease</b> where other agents may not be suitable.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Methyldopa's therapeutic effect is due to its conversion into a false neurotransmitter that mimics the action of a natural one, leading to reduced sympathetic nervous system activity.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to a "False" Neurotransmitter:</b> After oral administration, methyldopa is transported into the central nervous system (brainstem) and is converted by the enzyme DOPA decarboxylase into <b>alpha-methyldopamine</b>, and then further converted by the enzyme dopamine beta-hydroxylase into <b>alpha-methylnorepinephrine</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Alpha-2 Agonism:</b> Alpha-methylnorepinephrine then acts as a potent agonist (activator) of the <b>presynaptic alpha-2 adrenergic receptors</b> in the brainstem.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Sympathetic Outflow:</b> When these receptors are stimulated, they act as a negative feedback loop, reducing the release of the primary sympathetic neurotransmitter, <b>norepinephrine</b>, from nerve terminals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This decreases the overall sympathetic nervous system outflow from the central nervous system.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Reduction:</b> The reduced sympathetic stimulation to the heart and blood vessels leads to:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Peripheral Vasodilation:</b> Blood vessels relax and widen, reducing peripheral resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Decreased Heart Rate and Cardiac Output:</b> The heart beats more slowly, and the amount of blood it pumps per minute decreases.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:11.0pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri&quot;,sans-serif">The combined effect of vasodilation and reduced cardiac output results in a significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure.</span></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Alezko
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aliskiren (as Hemifumarate) + Hydrochlorothiazide is a single-tablet combination medication used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This medication includes <b>Aliskiren</b>, a direct renin inhibitor, and <b>Hydrochlorothiazide</b>, a thiazide diuretic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination provides a unique and powerful way to lower blood pressure by targeting two different and complementary systems.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination therapy is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b>. It is typically prescribed to:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with either aliskiren or hydrochlorothiazide alone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As an alternative for patients who require a multi-drug approach to reach their blood pressure goals.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By effectively lowering blood pressure, this combination helps to reduce the risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as stroke, heart attack, and heart failure.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination works by targeting two different and crucial systems that regulate blood pressure and fluid balance in the body.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Aliskiren (Direct Renin Inhibitor)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Aliskiren works on the <b>renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS)</b>, a hormonal pathway that is central to blood pressure regulation. Unlike ACE inhibitors or ARBs, which act further down the pathway, aliskiren works at the very beginning by directly inhibiting the enzyme <b>renin</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Renin is responsible for converting angiotensinogen into angiotensin I.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking this initial step, aliskiren prevents the entire cascade that leads to the formation of the powerful vasoconstrictor <b>angiotensin II</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This ultimately leads to vasodilation (the widening of blood vessels) and reduced fluid retention.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide Diuretic)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> Hydrochlorothiazide, often called HCTZ, acts on the kidneys.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It inhibits the reabsorption of sodium and chloride ions from the distal convoluted tubule.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1279182064="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1279182064="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c641761624="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Effect:</b> As sodium and chloride are excreted, water follows them out of the body through urine. This process reduces the total fluid volume in the bloodstream, which in turn lowers blood pressure and decreases the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Combined Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Aliskiren and Hydrochlorothiazide provides a powerful synergistic effect:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aliskiren blocks the RAAS pathway at its origin, preventing the creation of vasoconstricting hormones.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Hydrochlorothiazide reduces the body's blood volume.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual action leads to a more significant and sustained reduction in blood pressure than either drug could achieve on its own, making it a highly effective treatment for hypertension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2427743028="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
