PRODUCT: Regrosil
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Prasugrel, sold under the brand name Effient, is a potent antiplatelet agent belonging to the thienopyridine class of drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a "prodrug," which means it is inactive when administered and must be metabolized by the body into its active form to have an effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is used to prevent blood clots in the arteries and is typically prescribed in combination with aspirin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Prasugrel is known for providing a more rapid and consistent antiplatelet effect compared to older drugs in the same class, like clopidogrel (Plavix®).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Prasugrel is used to reduce the risk of thrombotic cardiovascular events, such as heart attack, stroke, and stent thrombosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications are for patients with <b>acute coronary syndrome (ACS)</b> who are undergoing <b>percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This includes patients who have experienced:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Unstable angina (UA)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Non-ST-elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In these conditions, a clot has either partially or completely blocked a coronary artery. PCI, which involves using a balloon to open the artery and a stent to keep it open, is a common treatment.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Prasugrel is prescribed to prevent new clots from forming on the stent or elsewhere in the coronary arteries.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Prasugrel's mechanism of action involves the inhibition of platelet activation and aggregation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It specifically targets a key receptor on the surface of platelets.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Platelet Activation:</b> When a blood vessel is damaged, platelets become activated. One of the main activators is adenosine diphosphate (ADP), which binds to receptors on the platelet surface, including the <b>P2Y12 receptor</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>The Role of the P2Y12 Receptor:</b> The binding of ADP to the P2Y12 receptor is a critical step in a signaling pathway that leads to a change in the platelet's shape and the activation of the GPIIb/IIIa receptor complex.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This complex allows platelets to stick to each other, a process known as aggregation, which forms a platelet plug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prasugrel's Action:</b> As a prodrug, prasugrel is rapidly metabolized in the liver to form a pharmacologically active metabolite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This active metabolite then <b>irreversibly binds to and blocks the P2Y12 ADP receptors</b> on the surface of platelets.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> By blocking the P2Y12 receptor, prasugrel prevents ADP from binding and activating the platelet.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This inhibits the activation of the GPIIb/IIIa receptor complex, thereby preventing the platelets from aggregating and forming a clot.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because the binding is irreversible, the antiplatelet effect of prasugrel lasts for the entire lifespan of the platelet (about 7 to 10 days).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Teglore
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ticagrelor, sold under the brand name Brilinta®, is a P2Y12 platelet inhibitor used to prevent blood clots.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike other drugs in the same class, such as clopidogrel and prasugrel, ticagrelor is not a prodrug and is active in its original form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It also has a distinct mechanism of action in that it binds to its target receptor reversibly, meaning its effect is not permanent for the life of the platelet.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This property allows for a more rapid onset of action and a quicker reversal of its antiplatelet effects if discontinued, which can be beneficial in case of bleeding or before surgery.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ticagrelor is primarily used to reduce the risk of cardiovascular events, such as heart attack, stroke, and cardiovascular death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications are:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS):</b> It is prescribed for patients who have had a recent ACS event (unstable angina, NSTEMI, or STEMI), often in combination with aspirin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This includes patients who undergo percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with stenting, as it helps prevent the formation of clots on the stent (stent thrombosis).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>History of Myocardial Infarction (MI):</b> It is also used in patients with a history of heart attack to reduce the risk of a future cardiovascular event.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>High-Risk Coronary Artery Disease (CAD):</b> It may be used to reduce the risk of a first heart attack or stroke in patients with stable coronary artery disease who are at high risk for such events.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Ischemic Stroke or High-Risk Transient Ischemic Attack (TIA):</b> It is used in some cases to reduce the risk of a recurrent stroke.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ticagrelor works by inhibiting platelet activation and aggregation through its interaction with the P2Y12 receptor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Here is a detailed breakdown of its mechanism:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Platelet Activation Pathway:</b> Platelet aggregation is a key step in forming a blood clot.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> One of the main molecules that activates platelets is adenosine diphosphate (ADP).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> ADP binds to and activates the <b>P2Y12 receptor</b> on the surface of platelets.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>The Role of the P2Y12 Receptor:</b> When activated by ADP, the P2Y12 receptor initiates a signaling cascade that ultimately leads to the activation of the GPIIb/IIIa receptor complex. This complex is crucial for cross-linking platelets and forming the stable platelet plug.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ticagrelor's Action:</b> Ticagrelor is a <b>direct-acting, selective, and reversible antagonist</b> of the P2Y12 receptor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to a different site on the receptor than ADP, but this binding prevents ADP from activating it.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Key Differences:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Direct-Acting:</b> Unlike prasugrel and clopidogrel, ticagrelor does not require metabolic activation to become effective.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means it has a faster and more consistent onset of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reversible Binding:</b> The binding of ticagrelor to the P2Y12 receptor is not permanent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When the concentration of the drug in the blood decreases, the drug dissociates from the receptor, and the platelet's function returns to normal. This contrasts with the irreversible binding of other P2Y12 inhibitors, which permanently inactivate the platelet for its entire lifespan.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This reversible property allows the antiplatelet effect to wear off more quickly, which is an important consideration in a patient who may need emergency surgery.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Vigetra
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vigabatrin is an antiepileptic drug (AED), also known as an anticonvulsant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a structural analog of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the central nervous system (CNS).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Due to its mechanism of action and significant potential for side effects, particularly irreversible vision loss, its use is restricted and it is not typically a first-line treatment for seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral forms, such as tablets and powder for solution.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vigabatrin is used for specific, difficult-to-treat seizure disorders where the benefits are considered to outweigh the risks.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Infantile Spasms (IS):</b> It is approved as a monotherapy (used alone) for infants aged 1 month to 2 years with infantile spasms, particularly when the spasms are associated with Tuberous Sclerosis Complex (TSC).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In these cases, it is often considered a first-line treatment.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Refractory Complex Partial Seizures (CPS):</b> It is indicated as an adjunctive therapy (used with other medications) for adults and children 2 years of age and older who have refractory complex partial seizures, meaning their seizures have not responded adequately to several other anti-seizure medications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Because of the high risk of permanent vision loss, Vigabatrin is only available through a special program called a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program in many countries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This program ensures that patients are monitored regularly for visual field defects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vigabatrin works by increasing the concentration of GABA in the brain, thereby enhancing inhibitory neurotransmission and reducing the excessive neuronal firing that causes seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It achieves this through a very specific and irreversible action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>The GABA System:</b> In a healthy brain, GABA acts as a "brake" on neuronal activity.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is released into the synapse, where it binds to receptors on the receiving neuron and makes it less likely to fire an electrical signal.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After it has done its job, GABA is removed from the synapse and broken down by an enzyme called <b>GABA transaminase (GABA-T)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vigabatrin's Action:</b> Vigabatrin is an analog of GABA, and it acts as a <b>"suicide inhibitor"</b> of GABA-T.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means it mimics GABA and is taken up by the GABA-T enzyme.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> However, instead of being broken down, Vigabatrin permanently binds to and inactivates the GABA-T enzyme.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> By irreversibly inhibiting GABA-T, Vigabatrin prevents the breakdown of GABA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to a significant and sustained increase in GABA levels within the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated GABA levels enhance the inhibitory signaling, which helps to suppress the abnormal electrical activity that characterizes seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because the inhibition of GABA-T is permanent, the duration of Vigabatrin's effect is dependent on the body's rate of new GABA-T enzyme synthesis, which is why a single dose can have a long-lasting effect despite the drug's relatively short half-life in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Agozit
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Agomelatine, sold under brand names such as Valdoxan® and Thymanax®, is a unique antidepressant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is considered an atypical antidepressant because its mechanism of action is distinctly different from conventional classes like SSRIs (Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors) and SNRIs (Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitors).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a structural analog of the hormone melatonin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Agomelatine is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>major depressive episodes in adults</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often used as a second- or third-line option in patients who have not responded to or have not tolerated other antidepressants.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In some regions, it may also be approved for the treatment of <b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key benefit of agomelatine is its ability to address the sleep disturbances that are a common symptom of depression, such as difficulty falling asleep or waking up early.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This makes it a valuable option for patients with prominent circadian rhythm disruptions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Agomelatine's antidepressant effect is attributed to a dual mechanism of action that targets both the melatonergic and serotonergic systems in the brain:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Melatonin Receptor Agonist:</b> Agomelatine acts as a potent agonist at the <b>melatonin MT1 and MT2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are found in the brain's suprachiasmatic nucleus, which is the body's central "body clock" and regulates circadian rhythms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By stimulating these receptors, agomelatine helps to resynchronize disrupted sleep-wake cycles, promoting a more natural and restorative sleep pattern.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action is believed to be crucial for its therapeutic effect, as circadian rhythm disruption is a known factor in the pathophysiology of depression.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>5-HT2C Serotonin Receptor Antagonist:</b> Agomelatine also acts as an antagonist at the <b>serotonin 5-HT2C receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are located on neurons that regulate the release of other key neurotransmitters, specifically norepinephrine and dopamine, in areas of the brain associated with mood, such as the prefrontal cortex. By blocking the 5-HT2C receptors, agomelatine effectively disinhibits these neurons, leading to an increase in the release of norepinephrine and dopamine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This increase in mood-regulating neurotransmitters contributes to its overall antidepressant effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The unique combination of these two actions—resynchronizing circadian rhythms and increasing norepinephrine and dopamine levels—is considered to be synergistic, meaning their combined effect is greater than the sum of their individual effects. This is what distinguishes agomelatine's pharmacological profile from other antidepressants.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Amrida
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amisulpride is an atypical antipsychotic medication belonging to the benzamide class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a selective antagonist of dopamine D2 and D3 receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike many other antipsychotics, it has a distinct dose-dependent effect and a high affinity for receptors in the limbic system, which is believed to contribute to its specific therapeutic effects and a lower risk of certain side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Amisulpride has a range of indications depending on the dose, but it is primarily used for psychiatric conditions.</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Schizophrenia:</b> It is indicated for the treatment of both acute and chronic schizophrenic disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Positive Symptoms:</b> At higher doses (typically 400-800 mg/day), it is effective in treating "positive symptoms" of schizophrenia, such as delusions, hallucinations, thought disorders, and hostility.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Negative Symptoms:</b> At lower doses (50-300 mg/day), it is used to manage "negative symptoms," which include blunted affect, emotional and social withdrawal, and lack of motivation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative Nausea and Vomiting (PONV):</b> In some regions, it is approved for the prevention and treatment of postoperative nausea and vomiting, often administered intravenously.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Depression:</b> In some contexts, low doses of amisulpride are used to treat depressive symptoms, particularly those associated with dysthymia or major depressive disorder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of amisulpride is centered on its selective and dose-dependent antagonism of dopamine receptors, primarily D2 and D3.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine System:</b> Dopamine is a crucial neurotransmitter involved in a wide range of functions, including mood, motivation, and thought processes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Overactivity of the dopaminergic system, particularly in the mesolimbic pathway, is thought to cause the positive symptoms of psychosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dose-Dependent Action:</b> Amisulpride's unique effect is tied to its concentration in the brain, which is controlled by the dose.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Low-Dose Action (50-300 mg/day):</b> At low doses, amisulpride preferentially blocks <b>presynaptic dopamine D2 and D3 autoreceptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These autoreceptors normally act as a "brake" on dopamine release, inhibiting the neuron from releasing more dopamine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these receptors, amisulpride removes this inhibitory feedback, leading to an increase in dopamine release into the synapse. This enhancement of dopaminergic transmission is thought to be responsible for its effectiveness in treating the negative symptoms of schizophrenia and depressive symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>High-Dose Action (400-800 mg/day):</b> At higher doses, amisulpride occupies and blocks the <b>postsynaptic D2 and D3 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This prevents dopamine from binding to and activating these receptors, which reduces excessive dopaminergic activity. This blockade is the primary mechanism by which amisulpride alleviates the positive symptoms of schizophrenia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Selectivity:</b> Amisulpride has a high selectivity for D2 and D3 receptors and a very low affinity for other receptors like serotonin, histamine, or adrenergic receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This selectivity is what gives it an "atypical" profile, as it often has a lower risk of side effects like sedation, weight gain, and extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS) compared to many older antipsychotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Buspowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Buspirone, sold under the brand name Buspar®, is a non-benzodiazepine anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is structurally unrelated to benzodiazepines, barbiturates, or other sedative-hypnotic drugs, which gives it a different pharmacological profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A key characteristic of buspirone is that it does not have sedative, muscle-relaxant, or hypnotic effects, and it is not associated with the same risks of physical dependence, tolerance, and withdrawal as benzodiazepines.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Buspirone is specifically indicated for the management of <b>generalized anxiety disorder (GAD)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is effective in treating both the emotional and physical symptoms of GAD. Unlike benzodiazepines, which provide immediate relief for acute anxiety, buspirone's therapeutic effects build up gradually over several weeks (typically 2 to 4 weeks) and it must be taken consistently to be effective.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not an appropriate medication for treating acute panic attacks.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The exact mechanism of buspirone's anxiolytic effect is not fully understood, but it is primarily thought to be related to its modulation of both the serotonergic and dopaminergic neurotransmitter systems.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT1A Receptor Agonist:</b> Buspirone has a high affinity and acts as a <b>partial agonist at the serotonin 5-HT1A receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are found in both the presynaptic and postsynaptic membranes in various brain regions, including the limbic system (which is involved in emotion) and the raphe nuclei (where serotonin-producing neurons are located).<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Presynaptic Action:</b> By acting as a partial agonist on the presynaptic 5-HT1A receptors, buspirone reduces the firing rate of serotonin neurons.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is a form of negative feedback that initially reduces serotonin release.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postsynaptic Action:</b> Over time, the sustained partial agonism at postsynaptic 5-HT1A receptors is believed to desensitize them, leading to a more regulated and balanced serotonin system. This long-term modulation of serotonin signaling is believed to be responsible for its gradual anxiolytic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine Receptor Modulation:</b> Buspirone also acts as an antagonist at <b>dopamine D2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This activity is less pronounced than its effect on serotonin, but it is thought to contribute to its overall therapeutic profile.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, buspirone's anxiolytic action is slow and progressive. It is thought to work by subtly and gradually altering the brain's serotonin system to a more stable and less reactive state. This contrasts sharply with benzodiazepines, which work on the GABA system to produce an immediate and powerful sedative effect.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Termipram
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Citalopram, typically formulated as Citalopram Oxalate, is an antidepressant medication that belongs to a class of drugs called Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors (SSRIs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a racemic compound, meaning it contains two mirror-image forms (enantiomers), designated as S-citalopram and R-citalopram.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A more potent version, escitalopram, is a single-enantiomer drug containing only the S-enantiomer.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Citalopram is known for its high selectivity for the serotonin transporter and has a relatively mild side-effect profile compared to older antidepressants.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Citalopram is primarily used for the treatment of mental health conditions related to an imbalance in brain chemistry.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> Citalopram is FDA-approved for the treatment of depression in adults.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Off-label Uses:</b> It is also used off-label to treat various other conditions, including:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">

	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">

		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Panic Disorder</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Social Anxiety Disorder<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

	</li>

	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Like other antidepressants, the full therapeutic effect of citalopram may take several weeks to become apparent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Citalopram's mechanism of action is centered on its ability to increase the concentration of serotonin in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin System:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) is a key neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in regulating mood, emotion, sleep, and appetite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After a serotonin molecule is released into the synaptic cleft (the space between two neurons), it transmits a signal to the receiving neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> To end the signal, the serotonin is typically reabsorbed by the releasing neuron via a protein called the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Citalopram's Action:</b> As a Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor, citalopram works by blocking the SERT.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to and inhibiting this transporter, citalopram prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to an increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated serotonin levels allow the neurotransmitter to bind more frequently to the receptors on the postsynaptic neuron, enhancing serotonergic activity and improving communication between neurons.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain that are responsible for the drug's antidepressant and anxiolytic effects.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key feature of citalopram is its high selectivity for the serotonin transporter.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It has very little to no effect on other neurotransmitter systems, such as dopamine, norepinephrine, acetylcholine, or histamine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This selectivity is what contributes to its favorable side effect profile compared to older antidepressant classes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Thymin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Escitalopram, typically formulated as Escitalopram Oxalate, is an antidepressant medication that belongs to the Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor (SSRI) class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the single S-enantiomer of the racemic compound citalopram. The S-enantiomer is the pharmacologically active component responsible for the therapeutic effects of citalopram, making escitalopram a more potent and specific SSRI.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enhanced selectivity is believed to contribute to its efficacy and a generally well-tolerated side-effect profile.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Escitalopram is used to treat various mental health disorders associated with an imbalance of serotonin in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> It is a first-line treatment for depression in adults and adolescents aged 12 and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD):</b> It is also a first-line treatment for GAD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Off-label Uses:</b> Escitalopram is also used to treat a number of other conditions, including:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Panic Disorder with or without agoraphobia<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">

	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Similar to other antidepressants, the full anxiolytic and antidepressant effects of escitalopram are typically not felt immediately and may take up to 2 to 4 weeks to become fully effective.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Escitalopram's mechanism of action is centered on its highly selective and potent inhibition of the serotonin transporter.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin System:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) is a key neurotransmitter involved in mood regulation, sleep, and various cognitive functions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When released from a neuron into the synapse, serotonin transmits a signal to the next neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> To regulate this signal, the excess serotonin is normally reabsorbed back into the releasing neuron through a protein called the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Escitalopram's Action:</b> As a highly selective SSRI, escitalopram works by potently and allosterically inhibiting the SERT. It binds to both the primary binding site and an allosteric (secondary) site on the transporter protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual binding action is thought to make it a more effective inhibitor of serotonin reuptake compared to its racemic parent compound, citalopram. By blocking the SERT, escitalopram prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to a rapid and significant increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft. The elevated serotonin levels allow for a greater and more prolonged stimulation of the serotonin receptors on the postsynaptic neuron. Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin activity is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain's neuronal circuitry, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects on mood and anxiety.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The high selectivity of escitalopram for the serotonin transporter means it has very little effect on other neurotransmitter systems (like dopamine, norepinephrine, or histamine).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This specificity is often cited as the reason it has a relatively favorable side effect profile and a lower risk of drug-drug interactions compared to some other antidepressant medications.</span></span></p>

-------------------------------------------

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> It is a first-line treatment for depression in adults and adolescents aged 12 and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD):</b> It is also a first-line treatment for GAD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Off-label Uses:</b> Escitalopram is also used to treat a number of other conditions, including:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Panic Disorder with or without agoraphobia<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Social Anxiety Disorder (Social Phobia)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Similar to other antidepressants, the full anxiolytic and antidepressant effects of escitalopram are typically not felt immediately and may take up to 2 to 4 weeks to become fully effective.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Escitalopram's mechanism of action is centered on its highly selective and potent inhibition of the serotonin transporter.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin System:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) is a key neurotransmitter involved in mood regulation, sleep, and various cognitive functions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When released from a neuron into the synapse, serotonin transmits a signal to the next neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> To regulate this signal, the excess serotonin is normally reabsorbed back into the releasing neuron through a protein called the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Escitalopram's Action:</b> As a highly selective SSRI, escitalopram works by potently and allosterically inhibiting the SERT. It binds to both the primary binding site and an allosteric (secondary) site on the transporter protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual binding action is thought to make it a more effective inhibitor of serotonin reuptake compared to its racemic parent compound, citalopram. By blocking the SERT, escitalopram prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to a rapid and significant increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft. The elevated serotonin levels allow for a greater and more prolonged stimulation of the serotonin receptors on the postsynaptic neuron. Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin activity is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain's neuronal circuitry, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects on mood and anxiety.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The high selectivity of escitalopram for the serotonin transporter means it has very little effect on other neurotransmitter systems (like dopamine, norepinephrine, or histamine).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This specificity is often cited as the reason it has a relatively favorable side effect profile and a lower risk of drug-drug interactions compared to some other antidepressant medications.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Zectam
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Escitalopram, typically formulated as Escitalopram Oxalate, is an antidepressant medication that belongs to the Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor (SSRI) class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the single S-enantiomer of the racemic compound citalopram. The S-enantiomer is the pharmacologically active component responsible for the therapeutic effects of citalopram, making escitalopram a more potent and specific SSRI.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enhanced selectivity is believed to contribute to its efficacy and a generally well-tolerated side-effect profile.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Escitalopram is used to treat various mental health disorders associated with an imbalance of serotonin in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin activity is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain's neuronal circuitry, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects on mood and anxiety.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The high selectivity of escitalopram for the serotonin transporter means it has very little effect on other neurotransmitter systems (like dopamine, norepinephrine, or histamine).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This specificity is often cited as the reason it has a relatively favorable side effect profile and a lower risk of drug-drug interactions compared to some other antidepressant medications.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
