PRODUCT: Rox
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Paroxetine, typically formulated as Paroxetine HCl (hydrochloride), is an antidepressant medication that belongs to the Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor (SSRI) class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent SSRI with a relatively short half-life, which can lead to withdrawal symptoms if not tapered correctly.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Paroxetine is also known to have some anticholinergic activity, which can contribute to certain side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Paroxetine has a broad range of indications for mental health conditions, and it is considered one of the most potent SSRIs for treating anxiety disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> Treatment of depression in adults.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Panic Disorder:</b> With or without agoraphobia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Social Anxiety Disorder (Social Phobia).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Premenstrual Dysphoric Disorder (PMDD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Menopausal hot flashes</b> (low-dose formulation).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Similar to other SSRIs, the full therapeutic effect of paroxetine may take several weeks to become apparent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Paroxetine's mechanism of action is centered on its highly potent and selective inhibition of the serotonin transporter.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin System:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) is a key neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in regulating mood, emotion, sleep, and appetite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When a serotonin molecule is released into the synaptic cleft (the space between two neurons), it transmits a signal to the receiving neuron. To end the signal, the serotonin is typically reabsorbed by the releasing neuron via a protein called the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Paroxetine's Action:</b> As a potent SSRI, paroxetine works by blocking the SERT.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to and inhibiting this transporter, it prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to an increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated serotonin levels allow the neurotransmitter to bind more frequently and for a longer duration to the receptors on the postsynaptic neuron, thereby enhancing serotonergic activity and improving communication between neurons. Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain that are responsible for the drug's antidepressant and anxiolytic effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key feature of paroxetine's pharmacology is its strong affinity for the SERT, making it a very effective inhibitor of serotonin reuptake.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Additionally, unlike some other SSRIs, paroxetine has some mild anticholinergic (inhibits acetylcholine) and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitory effects, which can contribute to some of its side effects (e.g., dry mouth, constipation, sedation) but may also play a minor role in its therapeutic profile.</span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Paroxetine's mechanism of action is centered on its highly potent and selective inhibition of the serotonin transporter.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin System:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) is a key neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in regulating mood, emotion, sleep, and appetite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When a serotonin molecule is released into the synaptic cleft (the space between two neurons), it transmits a signal to the receiving neuron. To end the signal, the serotonin is typically reabsorbed by the releasing neuron via a protein called the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Paroxetine's Action:</b> As a potent SSRI, paroxetine works by blocking the SERT.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to and inhibiting this transporter, it prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to an increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated serotonin levels allow the neurotransmitter to bind more frequently and for a longer duration to the receptors on the postsynaptic neuron, thereby enhancing serotonergic activity and improving communication between neurons. Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain that are responsible for the drug's antidepressant and anxiolytic effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key feature of paroxetine's pharmacology is its strong affinity for the SERT, making it a very effective inhibitor of serotonin reuptake.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Additionally, unlike some other SSRIs, paroxetine has some mild anticholinergic (inhibits acetylcholine) and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitory effects, which can contribute to some of its side effects (e.g., dry mouth, constipation, sedation) but may also play a minor role in its therapeutic profile.</span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Lovan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluoxetine Hydrochloride (HCl) is an antidepressant medication that belongs to a class of drugs known as selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is one of the most widely prescribed antidepressants globally and is available in various forms, including tablets, capsules, and an oral liquid solution.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The most common brand name for fluoxetine is Prozac.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluoxetine HCl is used to treat a wide range of mental health and other medical conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c2317158386="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c2317158386="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c1218284679="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Used for both acute and long-term treatment of depression in adults and children.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Approved for treating obsessions and compulsions in both adults and pediatric patients.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bulimia Nervosa:</b> The only antidepressant specifically approved to treat binge-eating and purging behaviors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Panic Disorder:</b> Used to manage panic attacks and associated symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Premenstrual Dysphoric Disorder (PMDD):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A severe form of premenstrual syndrome (PMS) with symptoms of depression and anxiety.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c2317158386="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c2317158386="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c1218284679="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bipolar Depression:</b> Often used in combination with other medications like olanzapine to treat depressive episodes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of fluoxetine is primarily attributed to its mechanism as a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Here's how it works:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c2317158386="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c2317158386="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c1218284679="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin's Role:</b> Serotonin is a neurotransmitter in the brain that plays a key role in regulating mood, emotion, sleep, and appetite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In many people with depression and anxiety disorders, serotonin levels are thought to be low.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reuptake Process:</b> Normally, after a nerve cell (neuron) releases serotonin into the space between neurons (the synaptic cleft), a reuptake pump on the presynaptic neuron reabsorbs the serotonin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fluoxetine's Action:</b> Fluoxetine works by blocking this reuptake pump.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting the reabsorption of serotonin, it allows the neurotransmitter to remain in the synaptic cleft for a longer period.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Serotonin Levels:</b> The resulting increase in serotonin concentration enhances its ability to bind to postsynaptic receptors, which helps to improve mood and emotional balance over time.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Long-Term Effects:</b> This process leads to adaptive changes in the brain's serotonin system, which are believed to be responsible for the long-term therapeutic benefits of the medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Fluoxetine has a long half-life, meaning it stays in the body for an extended period, which contributes to its sustained effect.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Olabex
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Olanzapine and Fluoxetine HCl is a medication that brings together two distinct classes of psychotropic drugs: an atypical antipsychotic (Olanzapine) and an SSRI antidepressant (Fluoxetine).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination is available as a single-pill formulation under brand names like Symbyax®.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The rationale for combining these two drugs is to treat conditions that involve a combination of both mood symptoms and psychotic features.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination medication is specifically indicated for the treatment of two major conditions:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bipolar I Disorder:</b> It is approved for the acute treatment of depressive episodes associated with Bipolar I Disorder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike a sole antidepressant, the inclusion of an antipsychotic helps manage the mood swings and psychotic features that can be part of bipolar depression.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Treatment-Resistant Depression (TRD):</b> This combination is also indicated for the treatment of depression in patients who have failed to achieve an adequate response to at least two different antidepressant treatments.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The combination of an antipsychotic and an antidepressant can be more effective than monotherapy for this challenging condition.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of the Olanzapine/Fluoxetine combination is a result of the synergistic action of its two components on different neurotransmitter systems.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Olanzapine (Atypical Antipsychotic):</b> Olanzapine's primary mechanism involves the modulation of dopamine and serotonin systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ol>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine Antagonism:</b> Olanzapine is a potent antagonist at <b>dopamine D2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these receptors, it reduces the excessive dopaminergic activity that is believed to contribute to the psychotic symptoms of conditions like schizophrenia and bipolar mania.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin Antagonism:</b> Olanzapine is also a potent antagonist at several <b>serotonin receptors, particularly 5-HT2A</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Antagonism of the 5-HT2A receptor is believed to contribute to its antipsychotic and mood-stabilizing effects. It may also lead to a more favorable side-effect profile regarding extrapyramidal symptoms compared to older antipsychotics.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fluoxetine (SSRI Antidepressant):</b> Fluoxetine's mechanism is focused on the serotonin system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin Reuptake Inhibition:</b> Fluoxetine is a Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor (SSRI).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by blocking the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>, which prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron. This leads to an increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft, enhancing serotonergic activity and alleviating depressive symptoms.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Synergistic Effect:</b> When combined, olanzapine and fluoxetine are believed to work synergistically.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Sabert
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sertraline, typically formulated as Sertraline HCl (hydrochloride), is an antidepressant medication that belongs to the class of Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors (SSRIs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is one of the most widely prescribed SSRIs due to its effectiveness in treating a broad range of conditions and its generally favorable side-effect profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sertraline is approved for the treatment of several psychiatric and mood disorders, making it a versatile option for clinicians.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> It is a common and effective treatment for depression in adults and adolescents.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD):</b> It is a first-line treatment for OCD in both adults and children over the age of 6.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Panic Disorder:</b> With or without agoraphobia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Social Anxiety Disorder (Social Phobia).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Premenstrual Dysphoric Disorder (PMDD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sertraline's therapeutic effect is due to its potent and selective inhibition of the serotonin transporter.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin System:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) is a key neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in regulating mood, emotion, sleep, and appetite.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When a serotonin molecule is released from a neuron into the synaptic cleft (the space between two neurons), it transmits a signal.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> To end the signal, the serotonin is reabsorbed back into the releasing neuron via a protein called the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sertraline's Action:</b> As a Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor, sertraline works by potently and selectively blocking the SERT.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to and inhibiting this transporter, it prevents the reabsorption of serotonin back into the presynaptic neuron.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to an increase in the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated serotonin levels allow the neurotransmitter to bind more frequently and for a longer duration to the receptors on the postsynaptic neuron, thereby enhancing serotonergic activity and improving communication between neurons. Over time, this sustained increase in serotonin is thought to lead to adaptive changes in the brain that are responsible for the drug's antidepressant and anxiolytic effects.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">While sertraline is primarily a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, it also has some weak inhibitory effects on dopamine and norepinephrine reuptake, although this is much less pronounced than its effect on serotonin. This high degree of selectivity for the serotonin system contributes to its reputation as a well-tolerated and effective SSRI.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Lafix ER
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Venlafaxine, sold under brand names such as Effexor®, is an antidepressant medication that belongs to the class of Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitors (SNRIs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by increasing the levels of both serotonin and norepinephrine in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> At lower doses, its effect is primarily on serotonin, but as the dose increases, its effect on norepinephrine becomes more pronounced.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Venlafaxine is also available in an extended-release (XR) formulation, which is commonly used to minimize side effects and improve tolerability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Venlafaxine is used to treat a variety of mood and anxiety disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> It is a common and effective treatment for depression.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Social Anxiety Disorder (Social Phobia).</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Panic Disorder.</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In some cases, Venlafaxine is also used off-label for other conditions like neuropathic pain and to manage menopausal hot flashes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Venlafaxine's therapeutic effect is a result of its dual inhibition of the reuptake of serotonin and norepinephrine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Neurotransmitter Systems:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) and norepinephrine (NE) are two crucial neurotransmitters in the central nervous system. Serotonin is heavily involved in mood, emotion, and sleep, while norepinephrine plays a key role in alertness, energy, and the body's stress response.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After being released into the synaptic cleft, these neurotransmitters are typically reabsorbed by their respective transporter proteins: the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b> and the <b>norepinephrine transporter (NET)</b>.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Venlafaxine's Action:</b> Venlafaxine acts by potently and selectively inhibiting the reuptake of both serotonin and norepinephrine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It has a significantly higher affinity for the serotonin transporter than the norepinephrine transporter.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">At <b>low to moderate doses</b>, venlafaxine primarily acts as a <b>Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor (SSRI)</b>, with its effects largely confined to blocking SERT.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">At <b>higher doses</b>, the inhibitory effect on the <b>norepinephrine transporter (NET)</b> becomes clinically significant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effects:</b> The dual inhibition of SERT and NET leads to an increase in the concentration of both serotonin and norepinephrine in the synaptic cleft.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated levels of these neurotransmitters are believed to enhance signaling in the brain regions involved in mood and emotion, thereby alleviating symptoms of depression and anxiety. This dual action is thought to provide a broader spectrum of activity than SSRIs alone and may be particularly effective for patients who have not responded to a single-acting antidepressant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
