<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lacosamide, sold under the brand name Vimpat®, is an anticonvulsant medication used to treat certain types of seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is classified as an antiepileptic drug (AED) and has a unique and novel mechanism of action that is distinct from most other AEDs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral forms (tablets, oral solution) and an intravenous (IV) formulation, which allows for its use in hospital settings for patients who cannot take oral medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lacosamide is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>partial-onset seizures</b> in patients with epilepsy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Monotherapy:</b> It is used as a single medication for the treatment of partial-onset seizures in adults and children 4 years of age and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Adjunctive Therapy:</b> It is also used as an add-on medication for partial-onset seizures in adults and children 4 years of age and older who are not fully controlled on other antiepileptic drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lacosamide's mechanism of action is unique among antiepileptic drugs and involves a selective enhancement of sodium channel inactivation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action helps to stabilize hyperexcitable neuronal membranes and prevent the rapid and repetitive firing of neurons that leads to seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sodium Channels:</b> Voltage-gated sodium channels are crucial for the generation and propagation of action potentials (electrical signals) in neurons.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When a neuron is excited, these channels open, allowing sodium ions to rush into the cell, which causes depolarization and the firing of an action potential.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After firing, the channels enter a state of inactivation, which prevents them from opening again immediately. This allows the neuron to return to its resting state.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hyperexcitability in Epilepsy:</b> In patients with epilepsy, neurons can become hyperexcitable, meaning they fire excessively and repetitively.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is often due to a disruption in the normal functioning of ion channels, including sodium channels.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lacosamide's Action:</b> Lacosamide selectively enhances the <b>"slow" inactivation of voltage-gated sodium channels</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike many other AEDs that affect "fast" inactivation, lacosamide's action is unique. By promoting this slow inactivation, it stabilizes the neuron's membrane and prevents the sustained, repetitive firing that is characteristic of a seizure.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Key Distinction:</b> The unique feature of lacosamide's action is that it selectively affects neurons that are in a depolarized or active state, which are the ones involved in seizure activity. It does not significantly affect sodium channels in resting neurons, which is thought to be the reason for its favorable side-effect profile compared to some other sodium channel-blocking AEDs.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, lacosamide works by a targeted and selective action on voltage-gated sodium channels to reduce the hyperexcitability of neurons, thereby preventing the spread of seizure activity without causing widespread central nervous system depression.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Gabizam
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Pregabalin, sold under brand names such as Lyrica®, is an anticonvulsant and analgesic medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a structural analog of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), but like gabapentin, it does not act directly on GABA receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Instead, it has a distinct mechanism of action and is primarily used to treat neuropathic pain, seizures, and anxiety. Pregabalin is a Schedule V controlled substance in the U.S. due to its potential for abuse and dependence.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Pregabalin is a versatile medication with several FDA-approved and off-label uses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy (DPN):</b> Management of nerve pain associated with diabetes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postherpetic Neuralgia (PHN):</b> Management of the chronic nerve pain that can persist after shingles.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fibromyalgia:</b> Management of the widespread pain, stiffness, and fatigue characteristic of this condition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Adjunctive Therapy for Partial-Onset Seizures:</b> Used as an add-on medication for the treatment of certain types of partial seizures in adults with epilepsy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD):</b> In some countries, it is approved for the treatment of GAD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism by which pregabalin exerts its therapeutic effects is centered on its high-affinity binding to a specific subunit of voltage-gated calcium channels.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to the Alpha-2-Delta Protein:</b> Pregabalin works by binding with high affinity to the <b>alpha-2-delta (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>α2</b><b>​</b><b>δ</b><b>) subunit of voltage-gated calcium channels</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These channels are found on the surface of neurons, particularly in the presynaptic terminal.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Neurotransmitter Release:</b> By binding to the <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>α2​δ subunit, pregabalin reduces the influx of calcium into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This reduction in calcium flow decreases the release of several key excitatory neurotransmitters, including <b>glutamate</b>, norepinephrine, and substance P.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Analgesic and Anxiolytic Effect:</b> This reduction in excitatory neurotransmitter release is believed to be the primary mechanism for its pain-relieving and anti-anxiety effects. By dampening the release of pain-signaling molecules, pregabalin modulates and suppresses the transmission of pain signals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In anxiety, it reduces the hyperexcitability of neurons in brain regions associated with fear and worry.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anticonvulsant Effect:</b> By reducing the release of excitatory neurotransmitters, pregabalin helps to stabilize neuronal membranes and prevent the abnormal, rapid electrical firing that causes seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Similar to gabapentin, it is important to note that pregabalin does not directly bind to GABA receptors, nor does it affect GABA uptake or metabolism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary action is through its modulation of calcium channel activity, which in turn reduces the release of excitatory neurotransmitters.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Topister
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Topiramate, sold under brand names such as Topamax®, is an anticonvulsant and nerve pain medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a broad-spectrum antiepileptic drug (AED) with multiple mechanisms of action, making it effective for a variety of seizure types as well as other neurological conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral forms, including tablets and sprinkle capsules.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Topiramate has several approved indications for both seizure disorders and migraine prevention.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main uses include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Epilepsy:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Monotherapy:</b> Used as a single medication for the treatment of partial-onset or primary generalized tonic-clonic seizures in adults and children 10 years of age and older.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Adjunctive Therapy:</b> Used as an add-on medication for partial-onset seizures, primary generalized tonic-clonic seizures, and seizures associated with Lennox-Gastaut syndrome in adults and children 2 years of age and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Migraine Prophylaxis:</b> It is widely used for the prevention of migraine headaches in adults and adolescents aged 12 and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is important to note that it is not used for the acute treatment of a migraine attack.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Off-label Uses:</b> Topiramate is also used off-label for several other conditions, including:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bipolar disorder<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Essential tremor<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cluster headaches<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Weight loss (due to its appetite-suppressing side effect)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Topiramate's therapeutic effects are attributed to a complex and multi-faceted mechanism of action that involves several distinct pathways in the brain.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Voltage-Gated Sodium Channel Blockade:</b> Topiramate works by a use-dependent blockade of <b>voltage-gated sodium channels</b>. By inhibiting these channels, it reduces the rapid, sustained firing of action potentials in neurons, which helps to suppress the spread of seizure activity.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>GABA Enhancement:</b> Topiramate enhances the activity of the inhibitory neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It does this in two ways:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It acts as a positive allosteric modulator of some GABA-A receptors, which increases the frequency with which the chloride channel opens when GABA binds, leading to greater neuronal inhibition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It also inhibits the enzyme that metabolizes GABA, which leads to an increase in GABA concentration.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Glutamate Receptor Antagonism:</b> Topiramate is an antagonist at <b>AMPA and kainate receptors</b>, which are types of glutamate receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Glutamate is the primary excitatory neurotransmitter in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these receptors, topiramate reduces the excitatory signaling that contributes to seizures and neuronal hyperexcitability.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Carbonic Anhydrase Inhibition:</b> Topiramate is a weak inhibitor of the enzyme <b>carbonic anhydrase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action may contribute to its anticonvulsant effects and is also thought to be responsible for some of its side effects, such as metabolic acidosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of these actions—blocking excitatory signals (from glutamate and sodium channels) and enhancing inhibitory signals (from GABA)—makes topiramate a potent and broad-spectrum AED.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This complex mechanism also explains its efficacy in a wide range of conditions beyond just epilepsy.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: L-pride
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levosulpride is an atypical antipsychotic and prokinetic medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the levorotatory enantiomer of sulpiride, meaning it is a mirror image of the sulpiride molecule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Levosulpride is a substituted benzamide and is known for its dual action on both the central nervous system and the gastrointestinal system, making it a versatile drug for a variety of conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not available in the United States or the United Kingdom, but it is marketed in many other countries, including those in Europe, Asia, and Latin America.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levosulpride has a range of indications that vary by country, with its primary uses falling into two main categories: psychiatric disorders and gastrointestinal disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastrointestinal Conditions:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Functional Dyspepsia:</b> It is widely used to treat symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, belching, and nausea, particularly when accompanied by delayed gastric emptying.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It helps reduce symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS):</b> It is used to manage symptoms like abdominal pain and bloating by improving gut motility.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Psychiatric Conditions:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Schizophrenia:</b> It is used to treat both acute and chronic forms of schizophrenia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Depression:</b> It can be used as a treatment for depression, sometimes in combination with other medications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anxiety Disorders:</b> It has anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Conditions:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vertigo:</b> It is used to treat symptoms of dizziness.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Premature Ejaculation:</b> It has been explored as a treatment for this condition due to its effects on the central nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of levosulpride is primarily based on its ability to selectively block dopamine D2 receptors, but it also has other effects that contribute to its therapeutic profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its action is dose-dependent and location-specific.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Levosulpride is a selective antagonist of <b>dopamine D2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by blocking these receptors, which are found in both the brain and the peripheral nervous system, including the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Psychiatric Effect:</b> In the brain's mesolimbic pathway, the blockade of D2 receptors helps to reduce excessive dopaminergic activity, which is thought to be the cause of psychotic symptoms like delusions and hallucinations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prokinetic Effect:</b> In the gastrointestinal tract, dopamine D2 receptors normally inhibit the release of <b>acetylcholine</b>, a neurotransmitter that promotes muscle contractions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these D2 receptors, levosulpride removes this inhibition, leading to an increase in acetylcholine release.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated acetylcholine levels enhance the strength and frequency of contractions in the stomach and intestines, improving gastrointestinal motility and relieving symptoms of indigestion and reflux.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT4 Receptor Agonism:</b> Some research suggests that levosulpride may also act as a moderate agonist at <b>serotonin 5-HT4 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action would further contribute to its prokinetic effects, as 5-HT4 receptor stimulation also promotes gastrointestinal motility.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Depression:</b> It can be used as a treatment for depression, sometimes in combination with other medications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anxiety Disorders:</b> It has anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Conditions:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vertigo:</b> It is used to treat symptoms of dizziness.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Premature Ejaculation:</b> It has been explored as a treatment for this condition due to its effects on the central nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of levosulpride is primarily based on its ability to selectively block dopamine D2 receptors, but it also has other effects that contribute to its therapeutic profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its action is dose-dependent and location-specific.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Levosulpride is a selective antagonist of <b>dopamine D2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by blocking these receptors, which are found in both the brain and the peripheral nervous system, including the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Psychiatric Effect:</b> In the brain's mesolimbic pathway, the blockade of D2 receptors helps to reduce excessive dopaminergic activity, which is thought to be the cause of psychotic symptoms like delusions and hallucinations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prokinetic Effect:</b> In the gastrointestinal tract, dopamine D2 receptors normally inhibit the release of <b>acetylcholine</b>, a neurotransmitter that promotes muscle contractions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these D2 receptors, levosulpride removes this inhibition, leading to an increase in acetylcholine release.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated acetylcholine levels enhance the strength and frequency of contractions in the stomach and intestines, improving gastrointestinal motility and relieving symptoms of indigestion and reflux.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT4 Receptor Agonism:</b> Some research suggests that levosulpride may also act as a moderate agonist at <b>serotonin 5-HT4 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action would further contribute to its prokinetic effects, as 5-HT4 receptor stimulation also promotes gastrointestinal motility.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Xepeno
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clozapine, sold under brand names such as Clozaril®, is an atypical antipsychotic medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is considered the first of the second-generation antipsychotics and is known for its superior efficacy in treating treatment-resistant schizophrenia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> However, due to its significant and potentially life-threatening side effects, particularly agranulocytosis (a severe drop in white blood cell count), its use is heavily restricted and requires mandatory monitoring of blood cell counts.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clozapine is typically reserved for patients who have not responded to other antipsychotic medications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Treatment-Resistant Schizophrenia:</b> Clozapine is the only medication approved for the treatment of patients with schizophrenia who have not responded adequately to at least two other antipsychotic drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also the most effective medication for this condition.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduction in Recurrent Suicidal Behavior:</b> It is approved to reduce the risk of recurrent suicidal behavior in patients with schizophrenia or schizoaffective disorder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Schizophrenia with Parkinson's Disease Psychosis:</b> It is sometimes used off-label to manage psychotic symptoms in patients with Parkinson's disease because it has a lower risk of worsening motor symptoms compared to other antipsychotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clozapine's unique efficacy and side-effect profile are attributed to a very complex and "dirty" mechanism of action, meaning it interacts with a wide variety of neurotransmitter receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary actions are on dopamine and serotonin systems, but it also affects many others.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine Receptor Antagonism:</b> Clozapine is an antagonist at several dopamine receptors, but its action is distinct from other antipsychotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Unlike first-generation antipsychotics that potently block D2 receptors, clozapine has a relatively weak and transient antagonism of these receptors. This "loose" binding is thought to be the reason for its low risk of causing extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS) and tardive dyskinesia, which are common side effects of potent D2 blockers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>D4 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Clozapine has a very high affinity for the <b>dopamine D4 receptor</b>, an action that is unique among antipsychotics and is believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects, although the exact role of this receptor is still being studied.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin Receptor Antagonism:</b> Clozapine has a very high affinity for <b>serotonin 5-HT2A receptors</b>, and it is a more potent antagonist at this receptor than at the D2 receptor. This potent 5-HT2A antagonism is believed to be a key reason for its superior efficacy in treating both positive and negative symptoms of schizophrenia and its low risk of EPS.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Receptor Interactions:</b> Clozapine is a very non-selective drug and interacts with a wide range of other receptors, which contribute to its side-effect profile:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Alpha1-Adrenergic Receptor Antagonism:</b> This can cause orthostatic hypotension (a drop in blood pressure upon standing).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Histamine H1 Receptor Antagonism:</b> This is responsible for its strong sedative effects and can also contribute to weight gain.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Muscarinic M1 Receptor Antagonism (Anticholinergic):</b> This can cause side effects like dry mouth, blurred vision, constipation, and sedation.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT1A Receptor Agonism (Partial):</b> This may contribute to its anxiolytic and antidepressant effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, clozapine's superior efficacy in treatment-resistant schizophrenia is believed to be due to its unique and balanced antagonism of both dopamine and serotonin receptors, particularly its potent action on 5-HT2A and D4 receptors and its weak, transient action on D2 receptors.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Mytodone
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Risperidone, sold under brand names such as Risperdal®, is an atypical antipsychotic medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It belongs to the class of second-generation antipsychotics and is known for its potent antagonism of both dopamine D2 and serotonin 5-HT2A receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various formulations, including oral tablets, oral solution, and a long-acting injectable (LAI) form, which is used for maintenance therapy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">

		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Treatment-resistant depression (as an add-on therapy)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Risperidone's therapeutic effects are attributed to its strong and balanced antagonism of both dopamine and serotonin receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Risperidone is a potent antagonist at <b>dopamine D2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these receptors, it reduces the excessive dopaminergic activity that is believed to contribute to the positive symptoms of psychosis, such as hallucinations and delusions. Its affinity for the D2 receptor is strong, which contributes to its efficacy but also carries a dose-dependent risk of extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS), similar to first-generation antipsychotics.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT2A Receptor Antagonism:</b> Risperidone also has a very high affinity and acts as a potent antagonist at <b>serotonin 5-HT2A receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its affinity for this receptor is even higher than for the D2 receptor. This potent 5-HT2A antagonism is believed to be a key reason for its atypical profile, as it helps to alleviate both positive and negative symptoms of schizophrenia and may contribute to a lower overall risk of certain side effects compared to older antipsychotics.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Receptor Interactions:</b> Risperidone also interacts with other receptors, which contribute to its overall effects and side-effect profile:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Alpha1-Adrenergic Receptor Antagonism:</b> This can cause orthostatic hypotension (a drop in blood pressure when standing).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Histamine H1 Receptor Antagonism:</b> This is responsible for its sedative effects.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, risperidone's balanced antagonism of dopamine D2 and serotonin 5-HT2A receptors is the key to its broad therapeutic efficacy in treating a range of psychiatric conditions. This dual action helps to manage both the positive symptoms of psychosis and the negative symptoms of schizophrenia.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Derby
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itopride HCl is a prokinetic agent and a substituted benzamide derivative.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not available in all countries (for example, it is not available in the U.S. or U.K.) but is used in many parts of the world. It is known for its dual action, which improves gastrointestinal motility without the significant central nervous system side effects of some other prokinetics, such as metoclopramide.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itopride HCl is primarily indicated for the treatment of gastrointestinal symptoms caused by reduced gastrointestinal motility and delayed gastric emptying.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main uses include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Functional Dyspepsia:</b> It is used to relieve symptoms of non-ulcer dyspepsia, often called chronic gastritis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This includes symptoms such as a sensation of bloating, early satiety (feeling full after eating very little), post-meal fullness, upper abdominal discomfort or pain, nausea, and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It can be used to treat symptoms of GERD, such as heartburn, by improving the function of the lower esophageal sphincter and accelerating gastric emptying.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other motility disorders:</b> It can be used to treat other conditions where delayed gastric emptying is a problem, such as gastroparesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itopride has a unique dual mechanism of action that enhances gastrointestinal motility:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Dopamine, a neurotransmitter, has an inhibitory effect on gastrointestinal motility when it binds to D2 receptors in the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these D2 receptors, itopride prevents dopamine from exerting its inhibitory effects. This removal of the "brake" on gastrointestinal muscle contractions allows for increased and more coordinated movement of food through the digestive tract.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acetylcholinesterase Inhibition:</b> Acetylcholine is a neurotransmitter that promotes muscle contraction in the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The enzyme acetylcholinesterase is responsible for breaking down and inactivating acetylcholine. Itopride inhibits this enzyme, which leads to an increase in the concentration of acetylcholine in the synaptic clefts of the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Elevated levels of acetylcholine then stimulate muscarinic receptors on smooth muscle cells, leading to stronger and more frequent contractions and enhancing peristalsis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of these two actions—D2 receptor antagonism and acetylcholinesterase inhibition—results in a significant increase in acetylcholine and an overall enhancement of gastrointestinal motility, leading to accelerated gastric emptying and symptom relief.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because itopride does not readily cross the blood-brain barrier, it has a lower risk of causing central nervous system side effects, such as extrapyramidal symptoms, compared to drugs like metoclopramide.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ziprowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ziprasidone, formulated as Ziprasidone hydrochloride monohydrate, is an atypical or second-generation antipsychotic medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is known for its unique pharmacological profile, including its dual antagonism of dopamine and serotonin receptors and its additional activity as a serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is distinct from other atypical antipsychotics in that it has a low risk of causing significant weight gain and metabolic changes, though it carries a risk of QT prolongation, which is an important consideration for its use.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral capsules and as an intramuscular injection for acute agitation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ziprasidone is primarily used to treat major psychiatric disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Schizophrenia:</b> It is used for the treatment of schizophrenia in adults.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The intramuscular injection is specifically indicated for the rapid control of acute agitation in patients with schizophrenia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bipolar I Disorder:</b> It is approved for the acute treatment of manic or mixed episodes associated with bipolar I disorder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also used as a maintenance treatment for bipolar I disorder, often as an adjunctive therapy with lithium or valproate.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of ziprasidone is complex and multi-faceted, involving a balanced and potent interaction with several key neurotransmitter systems in the brain.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Ziprasidone acts as a potent antagonist at <b>dopamine D2 receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these receptors, it reduces the excessive dopaminergic activity in the mesolimbic pathway, which is believed to be the primary cause of the positive symptoms of psychosis, such as hallucinations and delusions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT2A Receptor Antagonism:</b> It has a very high affinity for and acts as a potent antagonist at <b>serotonin 5-HT2A receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The ratio of its affinity for 5-HT2A to D2 receptors is very high, which is a characteristic of atypical antipsychotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This potent serotonin antagonism is believed to contribute to its efficacy in treating both positive and negative symptoms of schizophrenia and is associated with a lower risk of extrapyramidal symptoms.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Serotonin Receptor Modulation:</b> In addition to its action on the 5-HT2A receptor, ziprasidone also has other serotonergic effects:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is an agonist at the <b>5-HT1A receptor</b>. This action may contribute to its antidepressant and anxiolytic effects.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It acts as an antagonist at the <b>5-HT1D receptor</b>, which is also thought to play a role in its therapeutic profile.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin and Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibition:</b> Ziprasidone has a moderate inhibitory effect on the reuptake of both serotonin and norepinephrine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action increases the concentration of these neurotransmitters in the synaptic cleft, which may contribute to its antidepressant effects and its efficacy in bipolar depression.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Receptor Interactions:</b> Ziprasidone also has significant antagonistic activity against other receptors, which contribute to its side-effect profile:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Histamine H1 Receptor Antagonism:</b> This action is responsible for the somnolence and sedation seen with the drug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Alpha1-Adrenergic Receptor Antagonism:</b> This can cause orthostatic hypotension (a drop in blood pressure when standing up).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, ziprasidone's therapeutic effect is believed to be due to its powerful and balanced antagonism of D2 and 5-HT2A receptors, along with its unique agonist activity on 5-HT1A receptors and its reuptake inhibition of serotonin and norepinephrine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination of effects provides a broad spectrum of activity for treating both psychotic and mood-related symptoms.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
