PRODUCT: Dexbalt DR
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Duloxetine, typically formulated as Duloxetine HCl, is a Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitor (SNRI) antidepressant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This class of medication works by increasing the levels of both serotonin and norepinephrine in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike SSRIs, which are highly selective for serotonin, SNRIs have a dual-action mechanism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Duloxetine is also notable for its pain-relieving properties, which makes it useful for a range of conditions beyond mood disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Duloxetine has a wide range of approved indications, primarily for both psychiatric and chronic pain conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Major Depressive Disorder (MDD):</b> It is a first-line treatment for depression.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD):</b> It is also used to treat and manage GAD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathic Pain (DPNP):</b> It is used to manage the nerve pain associated with diabetes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fibromyalgia:</b> It is a key medication used to treat the widespread muscle pain and stiffness characteristic of this condition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> This includes chronic pain from conditions like osteoarthritis and chronic lower back pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Duloxetine's therapeutic effects are attributed to its dual inhibition of the reuptake of serotonin and norepinephrine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Neurotransmitter Systems:</b> Serotonin (5-HT) and norepinephrine (NE) are two crucial neurotransmitters in the central nervous system. Serotonin is heavily involved in mood, emotion, and sleep, while norepinephrine plays a key role in alertness, energy, and the body's stress response.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Both are involved in the regulation of pain. After being released into the synaptic cleft, these neurotransmitters are typically reabsorbed by their respective transporter proteins: the <b>serotonin transporter (SERT)</b> and the <b>norepinephrine transporter (NET)</b>.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Duloxetine's Action:</b> Duloxetine acts by potently and selectively inhibiting both the SERT and the NET.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these transporters, it prevents the reabsorption of both serotonin and norepinephrine back into the presynaptic neuron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effects:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antidepressant/Anxiolytic Effect:</b> The inhibition of SERT and NET leads to an increase in the concentration of serotonin and norepinephrine in the synaptic cleft.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated levels of these neurotransmitters are believed to enhance signaling in the brain regions involved in mood and emotion, thereby alleviating symptoms of depression and anxiety.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Analgesic Effect (Pain Relief):</b> The action on norepinephrine is particularly important for duloxetine's pain-relieving properties. By increasing norepinephrine and serotonin levels in the descending pain inhibitory pathways of the spinal cord, duloxetine helps to modulate and suppress the transmission of pain signals to the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This central mechanism of action is why it is effective in treating chronic pain conditions, including neuropathic pain.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Duloxetine also has a less potent inhibitory effect on dopamine reuptake, particularly in the prefrontal cortex, which may also contribute to its antidepressant effects. However, its primary and most significant actions are on the serotonin and norepinephrine systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The difference between Duloxetine and Duloxetine HCl is simply the salt form of the same active drug; they have the same description, indications, and mechanism of action.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Ayfoxit
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Etifoxine, typically formulated as Etifoxine HCl, is a non-benzodiazepine anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) and anticonvulsant medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is structurally distinct from benzodiazepines and, importantly, does not act on the classical benzodiazepine binding site on the GABA-A receptor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is known for having a lower risk of certain side effects common with benzodiazepines, such as sedation, anterograde amnesia, and dependence.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Etifoxine is used in many countries, particularly in Europe and Asia, but it is not approved for use in the U.S.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Etifoxine is primarily used for the short-term treatment of anxiety disorders, particularly those related to a stressful situation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anxiety Disorders:</b> Treatment of anxiety and psychosomatic symptoms, such as autonomic and cardiovascular manifestations, that are part of an adjustment disorder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Peripheral Nerve Damage:</b> Some research suggests it may also be used to promote peripheral nerve healing and to treat chemotherapy-induced pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of etifoxine is unique and dual, involving both a direct and an indirect effect on the GABAergic system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Direct GABAA Receptor Modulation:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>GABA System:</b> Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) is the main inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It acts by binding to GABA-A receptors, which are ligand-gated chloride channels. When GABA binds, the channel opens, allowing chloride ions to enter the neuron. This influx of negative ions makes the neuron less likely to fire an electrical signal, thereby having a calming, inhibitory effect.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Etifoxine's Action:</b> Unlike benzodiazepines, which bind to the alpha subunit, etifoxine binds directly to a different site on the GABA-A receptor, specifically at the interface of the <b>beta-2 and beta-3 subunits</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to this site, it acts as a positive allosteric modulator, enhancing the effect of GABA and leading to an increased influx of chloride ions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This results in a potent inhibitory effect that calms neuronal activity.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Indirect GABAA Receptor Modulation (Neurosteroid Synthesis):</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>TSPO:</b> Etifoxine also binds with high affinity to the <b>18 kDa translocator protein (TSPO)</b>, which is located on the outer mitochondrial membrane in both the central and peripheral nervous systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This protein was previously known as the peripheral benzodiazepine receptor (PBR).<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Neurosteroid Production:</b> When etifoxine binds to TSPO, it stimulates the synthesis of endogenous neurosteroids, particularly <b>allopregnanolone</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Allopregnanolone is a very potent positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors, and its action is similar to that of benzodiazepines.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This indirect pathway further enhances GABAergic neurotransmission, contributing to etifoxine's overall anxiolytic and anticonvulsant effects.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, etifoxine provides its anxiolytic and anticonvulsant effects through a unique dual mechanism: it directly enhances GABA-A receptor function by binding to the beta subunits and indirectly enhances it by stimulating the synthesis of neurosteroids via TSPO. This dual action and its different binding site give it a therapeutic profile with a lower risk of dependence and cognitive side effects compared to traditional benzodiazepines.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Inaxo
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Minocycline HCl is a semi-synthetic, second-generation <b>tetracycline antibiotic</b>. It is a broad-spectrum antibiotic, meaning it is effective against a wide range of bacteria, including both Gram-positive and Gram-negative organisms. Minocycline is also known for its enhanced <b>lipophilicity</b> compared to other tetracyclines, which allows it to penetrate tissues and barriers like the central nervous system (CNS) more effectively. In addition to its primary antibacterial properties, minocycline also exhibits anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indication</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Minocycline is used to treat a variety of infectious and non-infectious conditions. The indications for its use are extensive and include:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bacterial Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Respiratory tract infections (like pneumonia), urinary tract infections, skin and soft tissue infections, and certain sexually transmitted infections (like chlamydia and gonorrhea). It may be used as an alternative treatment for patients who are allergic to penicillin.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acne Vulgaris:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is widely prescribed to treat <b>moderate to severe inflammatory acne</b> due to its ability to kill acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rosacea:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Minocycline is used to treat the inflammatory lesions associated with this skin condition.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Other Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It can also be used to treat infections spread by ticks, lice, and mites, as well as more serious conditions like plague and anthrax.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Minocycline works by inhibiting <b>bacterial protein synthesis</b>. Its mechanism is primarily <b>bacteriostatic</b>, meaning it stops bacteria from growing and replicating rather than outright killing them. Here's a breakdown of how it works:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Binding:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Minocycline enters the bacterial cell.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Targeting:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Once inside, it specifically targets and binds to the <b>30S ribosomal subunit</b>.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibition:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By binding to this subunit, it prevents transfer RNA (tRNA) from carrying amino acids to the ribosome-messenger RNA (mRNA) complex. This effectively blocks the addition of new amino acids to the growing protein chain.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Result:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Without the ability to synthesize essential proteins, the bacteria cannot grow or reproduce, which ultimately clears the infection.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Losrene
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cycloserine is a broad-spectrum antibiotic that is a structural analog of the amino acid D-alanine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a second-line antitubercular agent used primarily for the treatment of drug-resistant strains of <i>Mycobacterium tuberculosis</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Due to its potential for serious central nervous system (CNS) side effects, its use is typically reserved for cases where first-line drugs are either ineffective or not tolerated.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cycloserine is a crucial component of multi-drug regimens for the treatment of tuberculosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications are:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Multidrug-resistant tuberculosis (MDR-TB):</b> It is used in combination with other antituberculosis agents to treat strains of <i>M. tuberculosis</i> that are resistant to at least isoniazid and rifampicin, the two most potent first-line drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis (XDR-TB):</b> It is also part of treatment regimens for this more severe form of tuberculosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary tract infections (UTIs):</b> In some cases, it can be used to treat UTIs caused by susceptible bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The antibiotic effect of cycloserine is due to its interference with bacterial cell wall synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is unique because it targets two key enzymes in the early stages of peptidoglycan formation, which is a critical component of the bacterial cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>D-Alanine Analog:</b> Cycloserine has a structure very similar to that of D-alanine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This structural similarity allows it to act as a competitive inhibitor of two crucial enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Alanine Racemase:</b> The first enzyme it targets is <b>alanine racemase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is responsible for converting L-alanine (a common amino acid) into D-alanine, which is a necessary building block for the bacterial cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting this enzyme, cycloserine prevents the formation of D-alanine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of D-Alanine Ligase:</b> The second enzyme it targets is <b>D-alanine:D-alanine ligase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme joins two molecules of D-alanine together to form a D-alanyl-D-alanine dipeptide, which is a key component of the peptidoglycan layer.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting this enzyme, cycloserine prevents this critical bond from forming.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The dual inhibitory action of cycloserine on two sequential steps in cell wall synthesis makes it a particularly effective antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By preventing the bacteria from building a functional cell wall, it compromises the structural integrity of the bacterium, leading to its death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A notable side effect of cycloserine is its ability to cross the blood-brain barrier and act as a partial agonist at the glycine site of the <b>NMDA receptor</b>, which can lead to neuropsychiatric side effects such as anxiety, depression, psychosis, and seizures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Texodine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cephradine is a first-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It belongs to the beta-lactam class of antibiotics, which are known for their ability to fight a wide range of bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a semi-synthetic drug that is administered orally or via intramuscular or intravenous injection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cephradine has a spectrum of activity that primarily targets Gram-positive bacteria and some Gram-negative bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cephradine is used to treat a variety of bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory tract infections:</b> Such as tonsillitis, pharyngitis, sinusitis, laryngo-tracheo bronchitis, acute and chronic bronchitis, lobar pneumonia, and bronchopneumonia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary tract infections:</b> Such as cystitis, urethritis, and pyelonephritis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and soft tissue infections:</b> Such as abscess, cellulitis, furunculosis, and impetigo.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Otitis media</b> (ear infections).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cephradine, like other beta-lactam antibiotics, works by inhibiting bacterial cell wall synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism of action is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bacterial Cell Wall Synthesis:</b> Bacteria require a rigid cell wall, made of a polymer called peptidoglycan, for structural integrity and survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The final stage of this synthesis involves a cross-linking process carried out by enzymes called <b>penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-Lactam Ring:</b> The key feature of cephradine is its beta-lactam ring.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This ring has a similar structure to the D-alanyl-D-alanine portion of the peptidoglycan precursor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of PBPs:</b> Cephradine acts by binding to and inhibiting these PBPs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to the active site of the enzymes, it prevents them from cross-linking the peptidoglycan strands.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to the formation of a weak and defective bacterial cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The weakened wall cannot withstand the internal osmotic pressure of the bacterium, causing the cell to swell and burst, a process known as lysis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to bacterial cell death, making cephradine a bactericidal (bacteria-killing) antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Atloma
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telithromycin, sold under the brand name Ketek®, is a semisynthetic antibiotic that belongs to the ketolide class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Ketolides are a novel subclass of macrolide antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Telithromycin is distinguished by its unique structure, which makes it effective against bacteria that have developed resistance to older macrolide antibiotics like erythromycin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> However, due to reports of serious side effects, particularly liver toxicity and the risk of exacerbating myasthenia gravis, its use has become highly restricted.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telithromycin's indications have been significantly narrowed due to safety concerns. Its current and primary approved indication is:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Community-acquired pneumonia (CAP):</b> It is used for the treatment of mild-to-moderate CAP caused by susceptible strains of bacteria, including multi-drug resistant strains of <i>Streptococcus pneumoniae</i>, which are often resistant to other macrolides.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In many countries, it is contraindicated in patients with myasthenia gravis, and its use is limited to situations where other antibiotics are not suitable.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telithromycin works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis, a process essential for the growth and survival of bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is similar to that of macrolides, but with key differences that make it more potent and effective against resistant strains.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ribosome Binding:</b> Like macrolides, telithromycin works by binding to the <b>50S ribosomal subunit</b> of the bacterial ribosome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The ribosome is the cellular machinery responsible for translating genetic information into proteins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dual Binding Sites:</b> A key distinction of telithromycin is its ability to bind simultaneously to two different domains of the 23S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) within the 50S subunit—domains II and V. Older macrolides typically bind strongly to only one of these domains.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual-binding action gives telithromycin a much higher affinity for the ribosome and makes it more effective against bacteria that have resistance mechanisms affecting a single site.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Peptide Synthesis:</b> By binding to these sites, telithromycin prevents the ribosome from elongating the growing polypeptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This blocks protein synthesis, which ultimately stops the bacteria from growing and multiplying.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bactericidal and Bacteriostatic Effect:</b> Telithromycin's effect can be either bacteriostatic (inhibiting growth) or bactericidal (killing the bacteria) depending on the concentration of the drug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In addition to its antibacterial action, telithromycin has a unique side-effect mechanism. It has been shown to act as an antagonist on cholinergic receptors in the neuromuscular junction, which is believed to be the cause of the severe respiratory failure reported in some patients with myasthenia gravis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Abwo
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clindamycin, typically formulated as Clindamycin Phosphate, is an antibiotic belonging to the lincosamide class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Clindamycin phosphate is a "prodrug," meaning it is an inactive compound that is rapidly converted to the active form, clindamycin, within the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is effective against a narrow spectrum of bacteria, primarily Gram-positive cocci and anaerobic bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including oral capsules, intravenous injection, and topical formulations (gels, lotions, creams) for dermatological use.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clindamycin is a versatile antibiotic used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anaerobic Infections:</b> It is particularly effective against anaerobic bacteria, making it a key treatment for infections in areas with low oxygen levels, such as deep-seated abscesses, intra-abdominal infections, and certain gynecological infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and Soft Tissue Infections:</b> It is used for conditions like cellulitis, abscesses, and infected wounds, including those caused by susceptible strains of methicillin-resistant <i>Staphylococcus aureus</i> (MRSA).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dental Infections:</b> It is a common choice for dental infections like abscesses and periodontitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory Infections:</b> It is used to treat infections such as pneumonia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acne Vulgaris:</b> The topical formulation is a widely used treatment for acne, targeting the bacteria (<i>Cutibacterium acnes</i>) that contribute to breakouts.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Infections:</b> It can also be used for bone and joint infections, pelvic inflammatory disease, and, in combination with other drugs, for parasitic infections like malaria and toxoplasmosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clindamycin works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis, a process that is essential for bacterial growth and survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ribosome Binding:</b> Clindamycin works by binding to the <b>50S ribosomal subunit</b> of the bacterial ribosome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The ribosome is the cellular machinery responsible for translating messenger RNA (mRNA) into a polypeptide chain (protein).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Peptide Bond Formation:</b> By binding to the 50S subunit, clindamycin prevents the ribosome from elongating the growing protein chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It does this by inhibiting ribosomal translocation, a step where the ribosome moves along the mRNA molecule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It also interferes with peptide bond formation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition of protein synthesis halts the growth and reproduction of bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The drug is primarily <b>bacteriostatic</b> (it inhibits bacterial growth), but it can be bactericidal (it kills bacteria) at higher concentrations or against very sensitive organisms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A unique feature of clindamycin is its ability to inhibit the production of bacterial toxins, which is why it is used in conditions like toxic shock syndrome.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Abwo 150mg
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clindamycin, typically formulated as Clindamycin Hydrochloride, is an antibiotic belonging to the lincosamide class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the oral form of clindamycin and is readily absorbed by the gastrointestinal tract. Clindamycin is effective against a narrow spectrum of bacteria, primarily Gram-positive cocci and anaerobic bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral capsules and is used for a variety of systemic infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clindamycin is a versatile antibiotic used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anaerobic Infections:</b> It is particularly effective against anaerobic bacteria, making it a key treatment for infections in areas with low oxygen levels, such as deep-seated abscesses, intra-abdominal infections, and certain gynecological infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and Soft Tissue Infections:</b> It is used for conditions like cellulitis, abscesses, and infected wounds, including those caused by susceptible strains of methicillin-resistant <i>Staphylococcus aureus</i> (MRSA).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dental Infections:</b> It is a common choice for dental infections like abscesses and periodontitis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory Infections:</b> It is used to treat infections such as pneumonia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bone and Joint Infections:</b> It can be used for infections like osteomyelitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Infections:</b> It can also be used for pelvic inflammatory disease and, in combination with other drugs, for parasitic infections like malaria and toxoplasmosis.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clindamycin works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis, a process that is essential for bacterial growth and survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ribosome Binding:</b> Clindamycin works by binding to the <b>50S ribosomal subunit</b> of the bacterial ribosome. The ribosome is the cellular machinery responsible for translating messenger RNA (mRNA) into a polypeptide chain (protein).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Peptide Bond Formation:</b> By binding to the 50S subunit, clindamycin prevents the ribosome from elongating the growing protein chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It does this by inhibiting ribosomal translocation, a step where the ribosome moves along the mRNA molecule. It also interferes with peptide bond formation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition of protein synthesis halts the growth and reproduction of bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The drug is primarily <b>bacteriostatic</b> (it inhibits bacterial growth), but it can be bactericidal (it kills bacteria) at higher concentrations or against very sensitive organisms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A unique feature of clindamycin is its ability to inhibit the production of bacterial toxins, which is why it is used in conditions like toxic shock syndrome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The difference between Clindamycin Hydrochloride and Clindamycin Phosphate is the salt form. Clindamycin Phosphate is typically used for IV injections or topical applications, while the hydrochloride salt is used for oral capsules. They both contain the same active drug, clindamycin, and therefore have the same mechanism of action.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Stoxil
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefadroxil is a first-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It belongs to the beta-lactam class of antibiotics, which are known for their broad-spectrum activity against various bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cefadroxil is a semi-synthetic drug that is administered orally.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A key characteristic of cefadroxil is its long duration of action, which allows for once or twice-daily dosing, improving patient adherence compared to other antibiotics with more frequent dosing schedules.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefadroxil is used to treat a variety of mild to moderate bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs):</b> Such as cystitis, pyelonephritis, and urethritis, caused by susceptible strains of bacteria.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and Soft Tissue Infections:</b> Such as impetigo, cellulitis, and erysipelas, caused by susceptible strains of <i>Staphylococcus aureus</i> and <i>Streptococcus pyogenes</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pharyngitis and Tonsillitis:</b> Infections of the throat and tonsils, caused by susceptible strains of <i>Streptococcus pyogenes</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other infections:</b> In some cases, it may be used to treat other infections like otitis media (ear infections).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefadroxil, like other beta-lactam antibiotics, works by inhibiting bacterial cell wall synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism of action is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bacterial Cell Wall Synthesis:</b> Bacteria require a rigid cell wall, made of a polymer called peptidoglycan, for structural integrity and survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The final stage of this synthesis involves a cross-linking process carried out by enzymes called <b>penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-Lactam Ring:</b> The key feature of cefadroxil is its beta-lactam ring.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This ring has a similar structure to the D-alanyl-D-alanine portion of the peptidoglycan precursor.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of PBPs:</b> Cefadroxil acts by binding to and inhibiting these PBPs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to the active site of the enzymes, it prevents them from cross-linking the peptidoglycan strands.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to the formation of a weak and defective bacterial cell wall. The weakened wall cannot withstand the internal osmotic pressure of the bacterium, causing the cell to swell and burst, a process known as lysis. This leads to bacterial cell death, making cefadroxil a bactericidal (bacteria-killing) antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefaclor, like other beta-lactam antibiotics, works by inhibiting bacterial cell wall synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism of action is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bacterial Cell Wall Synthesis:</b> Bacteria require a rigid cell wall, made of a polymer called peptidoglycan, for structural integrity and survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The final stage of this synthesis involves a cross-linking process carried out by enzymes called <b>penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-Lactam Ring:</b> The key feature of cefaclor is its beta-lactam ring. This ring has a similar structure to the D-alanyl-D-alanine portion of the peptidoglycan precursor.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of PBPs:</b> Cefaclor acts by binding to and inhibiting these PBPs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to the active site of the enzymes, it prevents them from cross-linking the peptidoglycan strands.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c448761968="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c448761968="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Resulting Effect:</b> This inhibition leads to the formation of a weak and defective bacterial cell wall. The weakened wall cannot withstand the internal osmotic pressure of the bacterium, causing the cell to swell and burst, a process known as lysis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to bacterial cell death, making cefaclor a bactericidal (bacteria-killing) antibiotic.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
