PRODUCT: Clomipramine Hcl
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Clomipramine HCl (hydrochloride), also known by its brand name Anafranil, is a tricyclic antidepressant (TCA). It is a dibenzazepine derivative that comes in capsule form for oral administration. Clomipramine is an antidepressant and antiobsessional drug.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Clomipramine is a medication primarily used to treat obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD) in adults and children 10 years of age and older. It was the first drug approved by the FDA specifically for this purpose.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It is also used off-label for a number of other conditions, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Major depressive disorder</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Panic disorder</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Chronic pain syndromes</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Narcolepsy-associated cataplexy</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Trichotillomania (hair-pulling disorder)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Body dysmorphic disorder</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Premature ejaculation</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The therapeutic effect of clomipramine is not fully understood, but it is believed to work by influencing neurotransmission in the brain. Its primary mechanism is the potent inhibition of the reuptake of two key neurotransmitters: serotonin (5-HT) and norepinephrine (NE).</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Serotonin and Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibition:</b> Clomipramine blocks the reabsorption of serotonin and norepinephrine back into the presynaptic nerve terminals. This action increases the concentration of these neurotransmitters in the synaptic cleft (the space between neurons), enhancing their availability to bind with postsynaptic receptors. This increased neurotransmission is thought to be responsible for its antidepressant and antiobsessional effects.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Role of Active Metabolites:</b> Clomipramine is extensively metabolized in the liver to form an active metabolite called desmethylclomipramine. While clomipramine itself has a stronger affinity for the serotonin transporter, its metabolite, desmethylclomipramine, is a more potent inhibitor of norepinephrine reuptake. This dual action on both serotonin and norepinephrine pathways contributes to its overall therapeutic effect.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Other Receptor Interactions:</b> Clomipramine also interacts with and blocks several other receptors, including histamine H1 receptors, muscarinic acetylcholine receptors, and alpha-adrenergic receptors. These interactions are not related to its primary therapeutic effects but are responsible for many of its common side effects, such as sedation, dry mouth, constipation, and dizziness.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Fylone
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levofloxacin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic belonging to the fluoroquinolone class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the L-isomer of the antibiotic ofloxacin and is known for its high potency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Levofloxacin is a bactericidal agent, meaning it kills bacteria directly.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including oral tablets, oral solution, and intravenous (IV) solution.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is effective against a wide range of bacteria, including both Gram-positive and Gram-negative organisms, and is often used to treat more severe infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Like other fluoroquinolones, it is associated with a risk of serious side effects and is typically reserved for use when other, less potent antibiotics are not effective or appropriate.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levofloxacin is used to treat a variety offections, particularly those that are serious, complicated, or caused by organisms resistant to other antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lower Respiratory Tract Infections:</b> Such as community-acquired pneumonia, nosocomial pneumonia, and acute exacerbations of chronic bronchitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs):</b> Including complicated UTIs, acute uncomplicated pyelonephritis (kidney infection), and chronic bacterial prostatitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and Soft Tissue Infections:</b> Such as cellulitis, abscesses, and other uncomplicated skin infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sinusitis:</b> Acute bacterial sinusitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anthrax:</b> It is approved for the treatment of inhalational anthrax and post-exposure prophylaxis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Plague:</b> It is used for the treatment of plague.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bone and Joint Infections:</b> Such as osteomyelitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Levofloxacin works by inhibiting two key bacterial enzymes, DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, which are essential for DNA replication, transcription, and repair. This is a unique mechanism of action that differentiates it from other antibiotic classes like beta-lactams. The process involves:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of DNA Gyrase:</b> Levofloxacin binds to and inhibits DNA gyrase, an enzyme responsible for unwinding and introducing negative supercoils into bacterial DNA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process is necessary to relieve the torsional stress on the DNA helix during replication. By inhibiting DNA gyrase, levofloxacin causes DNA breaks and prevents proper DNA replication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Topoisomerase IV:</b> Levofloxacin also binds to and inhibits topoisomerase IV.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is crucial for separating the intertwined daughter DNA strands after the DNA has been replicated. Inhibition of topoisomerase IV prevents the final division of the bacterial cell.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bactericidal Effect:</b> By disrupting both DNA replication and the separation of new DNA strands, levofloxacin leads to widespread DNA damage and fragmentation, which rapidly kills the bacterial cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This makes it a highly potent bactericidal agent.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Zoledronic Acid
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Zoledronic acid is a potent medication that belongs to a class of drugs called bisphosphonates. It is administered intravenously (IV) and works primarily on the bones. It is also known by the brand names Reclast and Zometa.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Zoledronic acid is used to treat a variety of metabolic bone disorders, including both malignant and benign diseases. Key indications include:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Osteoporosis:</b> Treatment and prevention of osteoporosis in postmenopausal women and men, as well as glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Paget's Disease of Bone:</b> Treatment of this chronic condition characterized by abnormal bone remodeling.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hypercalcemia of Malignancy:</b> Treatment of high blood calcium levels caused by certain types of cancer.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Bone Metastases:</b> Used in conjunction with other cancer therapies to prevent skeletal-related events (such as fractures or the need for radiation/surgery to the bone) in patients with bone lesions from multiple myeloma or other solid tumors.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Zoledronic acid works by targeting and inhibiting osteoclasts, which are the cells responsible for breaking down bone tissue. The mechanism is as follows:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>High Affinity for Bone:</b> Zoledronic acid has a very high affinity for bone mineral, specifically hydroxyapatite crystals, and preferentially binds to sites of active bone remodeling.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Osteoclast Absorption:</b> When osteoclasts begin the process of bone resorption, they release the bone-bound zoledronic acid. The drug is then absorbed by the osteoclasts.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Inhibition of Farnesyl Pyrophosphate Synthase (FPPS):</b> Once inside the osteoclast, zoledronic acid inhibits a key enzyme called farnesyl pyrophosphate synthase (FPPS), which is part of the mevalonate pathway.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Disruption of Osteoclast Function:</b> By blocking FPPS, zoledronic acid prevents the synthesis of important lipids needed for the proper function and survival of osteoclasts. This disrupts their ability to attach to the bone surface and carry out bone breakdown.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Induction of Apoptosis:</b> The disruption of the mevalonate pathway ultimately leads to the programmed cell death (apoptosis) of the osteoclasts, which further reduces bone resorption.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">In essence, by inhibiting osteoclast activity and promoting their death, zoledronic acid slows down the rate of bone breakdown. This shifts the balance in favor of bone formation by osteoblasts, leading to an increase in bone mass and density.</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Mofoxin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Moxifloxacin is a potent, broad-spectrum antibiotic that belongs to the fourth-generation fluoroquinolone class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a bactericidal agent, meaning it works by directly killing bacteria rather than just inhibiting their growth.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including oral tablets, intravenous (IV) solution, and ophthalmic (eye) drops. Compared to earlier fluoroquinolones like ciprofloxacin, moxifloxacin has enhanced activity against certain Gram-positive bacteria, particularly <i>Streptococcus pneumoniae</i>, which is a common cause of respiratory infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also effective against a wide range of Gram-negative bacteria and atypical pathogens.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Moxifloxacin is used to treat a variety of bacterial infections, especially those that are moderate to severe.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its use is often reserved for situations where other antibiotics are not effective due to resistance or other factors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The indications for moxifloxacin include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory Tract Infections:</b> Such as community-acquired pneumonia (CAP), acute bacterial sinusitis, and acute exacerbations of chronic bronchitis (AECB).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and Soft Tissue Infections:</b> Including complicated and uncomplicated infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intra-abdominal Infections:</b> For complicated infections, often used in combination with other antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bacterial Conjunctivitis:</b> When administered as eye drops.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Infections:</b> It is also used to treat and prevent plague and anthrax, and is sometimes used off-label for other conditions like tuberculosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Moxifloxacin's mechanism of action is unique among antibiotic classes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by inhibiting the function of two critical enzymes involved in bacterial DNA replication and repair: DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of DNA Gyrase (Topoisomerase II):</b> This enzyme is essential for unwinding and supercoiling bacterial DNA, a process necessary to relieve torsional stress during DNA replication and transcription.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Moxifloxacin binds to and inhibits DNA gyrase, causing DNA breaks and preventing the bacterium from replicating its genetic material.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Topoisomerase IV:</b> This enzyme is crucial for separating the intertwined DNA strands after they have been replicated. By inhibiting topoisomerase IV, moxifloxacin prevents the final step of bacterial cell division, effectively halting the bacterium's ability to multiply.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bactericidal Effect:</b> The simultaneous inhibition of both DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV leads to widespread DNA damage and fragmentation, which rapidly and effectively kills the bacterial cell.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual-targeting mechanism contributes to moxifloxacin's broad spectrum of activity and high potency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Aprepitant
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Aprepitant is an antiemetic medication, meaning it is used to prevent nausea and vomiting. It belongs to a class of drugs known as neurokinin-1 (NK-1) receptor antagonists. It is available in both oral (capsule and suspension) and intravenous forms.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Aprepitant is primarily used to prevent:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting (CINV):</b> It is used in combination with other antiemetic drugs (like 5-HT3 receptor antagonists and corticosteroids) for patients receiving highly and moderately emetogenic chemotherapy. It is effective for both acute (within 24 hours of chemotherapy) and delayed (more than 24 hours after chemotherapy) CINV.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV):</b> It can also be used to prevent nausea and vomiting that occur after surgery.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Aprepitant works by targeting a specific pathway in the body that leads to the vomiting reflex. The key steps of its mechanism are:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Substance P and NK-1 Receptors:</b> Nausea and vomiting are often triggered by a neuropeptide called Substance P. This substance acts as a neurotransmitter and is found in high concentrations in the "vomiting center" of the brain, as well as in the gastrointestinal tract. When Substance P is released (for example, due to chemotherapy), it binds to and activates specific receptors called neurokinin-1 (NK-1) receptors.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Blocking the Receptor:</b> Aprepitant is a selective and high-affinity antagonist of the NK-1 receptor. This means it binds to the NK-1 receptors and blocks them, preventing Substance P from binding and activating them.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Interrupting the Signal:</b> By blocking the NK-1 receptors, aprepitant interrupts the signal that would otherwise be sent to the brain's vomiting center. This action effectively prevents the initiation of the vomiting reflex.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Complementary Action:</b> A significant feature of aprepitant is its unique mechanism of action, which is different from other common antiemetics like serotonin (5-HT3) receptor antagonists (e.g., ondansetron) or corticosteroids (e.g., dexamethasone). This allows for a more comprehensive and powerful antiemetic effect when used in combination with these other drugs, as it targets a separate pathway in the emetic process.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Tegaserod
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tegaserod is a medication that belongs to a class of drugs called serotonin agonists. It was initially approved by the FDA in 2002 for the treatment of irritable bowel syndrome with constipation (IBS-C) in women. It was later withdrawn from the market in 2007 due to concerns about an increased risk of serious cardiovascular events, such as heart attack and stroke. It has since been reintroduced under a restricted-access program for a specific population.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tegaserod is indicated for the treatment of Irritable Bowel Syndrome with Constipation (IBS-C) in adult women under 65 years of age who have no history of cardiovascular ischemic events (e.g., heart attack, stroke, transient ischemic attack, or angina). Its use is limited and requires a doctor's prescription due to its restricted availability. The safety and effectiveness of tegaserod in men have not been established.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tegaserod is a selective partial agonist of the serotonin-4 (5−HT4​) receptor, which is found in the gastrointestinal tract, primarily in the enteric nervous system. By activating these receptors, tegaserod triggers the release of neurotransmitters, such as acetylcholine. This action has several effects on the gut:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Stimulates Gastrointestinal Motility:</b> It enhances the peristaltic reflex and increases intestinal secretion, which helps to accelerate the movement of stool through the bowels.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reduces Visceral Sensitivity:</b> It moderates the sensitivity of the gut, which can help to alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain and discomfort.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">In addition to being a 5−HT4​ receptor agonist, tegaserod is also a 5−HT2B​ receptor antagonist, which may contribute to its overall effects on the gastrointestinal system.</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Floxiwin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ofloxacin is a synthetic, broad-spectrum antibiotic that belongs to the fluoroquinolone class of drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a bactericidal agent, meaning it kills bacteria rather than just inhibiting their growth.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including oral tablets, eye drops, and ear drops.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ofloxacin is used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections caused by susceptible organisms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its use for some infections, like gonorrhea, is no longer recommended due to widespread bacterial resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often reserved for infections where other, safer antibiotics are not suitable.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Common indications for ofloxacin include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory tract infections:</b> Bronchitis and pneumonia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary tract infections (UTIs):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Uncomplicated and complicated UTIs, including cystitis and prostatitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and soft tissue infections.</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sexually transmitted diseases:</b> Chlamydia and certain types of cervicitis and urethritis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Eye infections:</b> Bacterial conjunctivitis and corneal ulcers (as ophthalmic drops).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> Otitis externa and chronic suppurative otitis media with a perforated eardrum (as otic drops)</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ofloxacin's mechanism of action is based on its ability to interfere with bacterial DNA replication, transcription, and repair.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It achieves this by inhibiting two key bacterial enzymes:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>DNA Gyrase (Topoisomerase II):</b> This enzyme is essential for the supercoiling and unwinding of bacterial DNA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> DNA gyrase relaxes the supercoiled DNA, a necessary step for replication. Ofloxacin binds to the DNA-gyrase complex, stabilizing it in a state that prevents the enzyme from functioning.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This halts the replication process and leads to the accumulation of DNA breaks.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Topoisomerase IV:</b> This enzyme is crucial for the final stages of bacterial cell division, as it separates the replicated daughter chromosomes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Ofloxacin similarly inhibits this enzyme, preventing the successful segregation of DNA strands.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By inhibiting both of these essential enzymes, ofloxacin disrupts bacterial cell division and ultimately causes bacterial cell death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A significant aspect of its effectiveness is its selective toxicity; ofloxacin has a much higher affinity for these bacterial enzymes than for the analogous enzymes in human cells (e.g., human topoisomerase II), which minimizes harm to the patient's own cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------
