</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> Otitis externa and chronic suppurative otitis media with a perforated eardrum (as otic drops).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ofloxacin's mechanism of action is based on its ability to interfere with bacterial DNA replication, transcription, and repair.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It achieves this by inhibiting two key bacterial enzymes:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>DNA Gyrase (Topoisomerase II):</b> This enzyme is essential for the supercoiling and unwinding of bacterial DNA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> DNA gyrase relaxes the supercoiled DNA, a necessary step for replication. Ofloxacin binds to the DNA-gyrase complex, stabilizing it in a state that prevents the enzyme from functioning.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This halts the replication process and leads to the accumulation of DNA breaks.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Topoisomerase IV:</b> This enzyme is crucial for the final stages of bacterial cell division, as it separates the replicated daughter chromosomes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Ofloxacin similarly inhibits this enzyme, preventing the successful segregation of DNA strands.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By inhibiting both of these essential enzymes, ofloxacin disrupts bacterial cell division and ultimately causes bacterial cell death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A significant aspect of its effectiveness is its selective toxicity; ofloxacin has a much higher affinity for these bacterial enzymes than for the analogous enzymes in human cells (e.g., human topoisomerase II), which minimizes harm to the patient's own cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Etifoxine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Etifoxine, sold under brand names like Stresam, is a non-benzodiazepine anxiolytic and anticonvulsant medication. It is a benzoxazine derivative, meaning its chemical structure is distinct from that of benzodiazepines, even though it shares some similar anxiolytic effects. A key difference is that etifoxine is known for having a lower risk of sedation, dependence, and withdrawal symptoms compared to traditional benzodiazepines.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Etifoxine is primarily indicated for the short-term management of anxiety disorders, particularly those related to a stressful event or situation, also known as adjustment disorder with anxiety. It is used to alleviate a range of symptoms, including emotional and physical reactions to stress such as tension, lack of concentration, and some autonomic nervous system disorders (e.g., heart palpitations).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It's also being studied for its potential in promoting peripheral nerve healing and treating chemotherapy-induced pain, but its primary established use is for anxiety.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Etifoxine has a unique and dual mechanism of action, which is what distinguishes it from other anxiolytic drugs.</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Direct Allosteric Modulation of GABAA​ Receptors:</b> Similar to benzodiazepines, etifoxine acts as a positive allosteric modulator of the GABAA​ receptor. This means it binds to the receptor at a site different from where the neurotransmitter GABA binds, enhancing the inhibitory effects of GABA. This leads to an increase in chloride ion flow into the neuron, which hyperpolarizes the cell and makes it less likely to fire an action potential, thus producing a calming effect. However, unlike benzodiazepines, etifoxine binds to the β2​ or β3​ subunits of the GABAA​ receptor complex, not the classical benzodiazepine site. This difference in binding site is one reason why its effects are not completely reversed by the benzodiazepine antagonist flumazenil.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indirect Action through Translocator Protein (TSPO):</b> Etifoxine's second mechanism involves binding to the 18 kDa mitochondrial translocator protein (TSPO), previously known as the peripheral benzodiazepine receptor. This binding stimulates the synthesis of neurosteroids, such as allopregnanolone, in both the central and peripheral nervous systems. These neurosteroids are themselves positive allosteric modulators of the GABAA​ receptor, further contributing to the drug's anxiolytic and neuroprotective effects.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Glamix
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Gemifloxacin is a synthetic, broad-spectrum antibiotic that belongs to the fourth-generation fluoroquinolone class of drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available as an oral tablet and is considered a potent bactericidal agent, which means it kills bacteria directly.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Gemifloxacin is primarily indicated for the treatment of specific respiratory tract infections in adults (18 years of age or older) caused by susceptible bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These indications are often limited to cases where other treatment options are not suitable.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute bacterial exacerbation of chronic bronchitis (ABECB):</b> Caused by bacteria such as <i>Streptococcus pneumoniae</i>, <i>Haemophilus influenzae</i>, and <i>Moraxella catarrhalis</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Community-acquired pneumonia (CAP):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Used for mild to moderate cases caused by a range of bacteria, including <i>Streptococcus pneumoniae</i> (including multi-drug resistant strains), <i>Haemophilus influenzae</i>, <i>Moraxella catarrhalis</i>, <i>Mycoplasma pneumoniae</i>, <i>Chlamydia pneumoniae</i>, and <i>Klebsiella pneumoniae</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It's important to note that due to concerns about serious side effects, regulatory agencies often recommend reserving fluoroquinolones like gemifloxacin for infections where other, safer antibiotics are not effective or cannot be used.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Gemifloxacin's bactericidal action is achieved by interfering with the essential processes of bacterial DNA replication, transcription, and repair.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It accomplishes this by specifically inhibiting two key bacterial enzymes:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>DNA Gyrase (Topoisomerase II):</b> This enzyme is critical for maintaining the proper supercoiling of bacterial DNA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> DNA gyrase unwinds and re-coils the DNA, a process necessary for replication and transcription. Gemifloxacin binds to this enzyme, preventing it from functioning and leading to double-strand breaks in the bacterial DNA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Topoisomerase IV:</b> This enzyme is essential for separating the replicated chromosomes into two distinct daughter cells during bacterial cell division.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting topoisomerase IV, gemifloxacin prevents the completion of cell division, leading to cell death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Gemifloxacin is a fourth-generation fluoroquinolone, and a key feature of its mechanism is its "dual-targeting" ability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It has a high affinity for both DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual action is particularly effective against bacteria that may have developed resistance to earlier-generation fluoroquinolones through mutations in one of these enzymes. By targeting both enzymes, gemifloxacin makes it more difficult for the bacteria to acquire high-level resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Tukol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clarithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic that is a semi-synthetic derivative of erythromycin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a broad-spectrum antibiotic that works by inhibiting bacterial growth.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Clarithromycin is more acid-stable than erythromycin, which results in better absorption and less gastrointestinal distress.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including tablets, extended-release tablets, and a liquid suspension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clarithromycin is used to treat a variety of bacterial infections in different parts of the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory tract infections:</b> It is commonly used for infections like pneumonia, bronchitis, sinusitis, pharyngitis (strep throat), and tonsillitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is effective against both typical and "atypical" pneumonia-causing bacteria, such as <i>Mycoplasma pneumoniae</i> and <i>Chlamydia pneumoniae</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and soft tissue infections:</b> It can be used to treat mild to moderate skin infections like cellulitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> It is used for acute otitis media (ear infections).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Treatment of <i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (H. pylori) infection:</b> Clarithromycin is a key component of multi-drug "triple therapy" regimens used to eradicate <i>H.</i><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><i> pylori</i>, a bacterium that causes peptic and duodenal ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Treatment and prevention of <i>Mycobacterium avium</i> complex (MAC) infection:</b> It is used to treat and prevent this infection, which often affects individuals with HIV/AIDS.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Penicillin allergy:</b> It is often used as an alternative to penicillin-based antibiotics in patients who have a penicillin allergy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clarithromycin's mechanism of action is based on its ability to stop bacterial protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It does this by binding to the bacterial ribosome in a very specific way:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to the 50S Ribosomal Subunit:</b> Clarithromycin reversibly binds to the 23S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) of the 50S subunit of the bacterial ribosome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The ribosome is the cellular machinery responsible for translating genetic code from messenger RNA (mRNA) into proteins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Translocation:</b> By binding to this site, clarithromycin blocks the "translocation" step of protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Translocation is the movement of the ribosome along the mRNA molecule, which is necessary to add new amino acids to the growing peptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Protein Synthesis Cessation:</b> This interruption prevents the ribosome from moving, effectively halting the elongation of the protein chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Without the ability to create essential proteins, the bacteria cannot grow, reproduce, or perform basic functions, which ultimately leads to their death or prevents their proliferation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">While clarithromycin is generally considered a bacteriostatic agent (inhibits growth), it can be bactericidal (kills bacteria) at higher concentrations or against certain susceptible organisms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A unique feature of clarithromycin is that it is metabolized in the liver to form an active metabolite, 14-hydroxyclarithromycin, which also has antibacterial activity and works synergistically with the parent compound to enhance the overall therapeutic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ezetimibe + Atorvastatin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ezetimibe and Atorvastatin is a combination medication used to treat high cholesterol and triglyceride levels in the blood. It is often prescribed as an adjunct to diet and exercise when those lifestyle changes alone are insufficient, or when a patient is not adequately controlled with a statin alone. This combination is particularly effective because the two drugs have complementary mechanisms of action, addressing cholesterol from different angles.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This medication is indicated for:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Primary (heterozygous familial and non-familial) hypercholesterolemia:</b> To reduce elevated levels of total cholesterol (Total-C), low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), apolipoprotein B (Apo B), and triglycerides (TG), and to increase high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mixed hyperlipidemia:</b> For patients with this condition, often in combination with fenofibrate.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Homozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (HoFH):</b> Used as an adjunct to diet and other treatments (e.g., LDL apheresis) to reduce elevated Total-C and LDL-C levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Prevention of cardiovascular events:</b> It is indicated to reduce the risk of events like heart attack and stroke in patients with coronary heart disease (CHD) and a history of acute coronary syndrome (ACS).</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination of Ezetimibe and Atorvastatin works through two distinct and complementary pathways to lower cholesterol:</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>1. Ezetimibe</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Ezetimibe is a <b>cholesterol absorption inhibitor</b>. It acts on the brush border of the small intestine by selectively blocking the Niemann-Pick C1-Like 1 (NPC1L1) protein. This protein is responsible for the intestinal absorption of cholesterol from both dietary sources and bile.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Effect:</b> By blocking NPC1L1, ezetimibe prevents cholesterol from being absorbed into the body. This leads to a decrease in the delivery of cholesterol from the intestine to the liver. As a result, the liver's cholesterol stores are reduced, which triggers an increase in the clearance of cholesterol from the blood.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>2. Atorvastatin</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Atorvastatin is an <b>HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor</b>, a class of drugs commonly known as statins. It works by inhibiting the enzyme HMG-CoA reductase, which is the rate-limiting step in the body's own cholesterol synthesis pathway.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Effect:</b> By inhibiting this enzyme, atorvastatin reduces the liver's production of cholesterol. This decrease in intracellular cholesterol causes the liver to increase the number of LDL receptors on the surface of its cells. These receptors then capture and remove more LDL ("bad") cholesterol from the bloodstream, leading to a significant reduction in circulating LDL-C.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">By combining these two drugs, the medication provides dual inhibition of both cholesterol absorption from the gut and cholesterol synthesis in the liver, leading to a more pronounced and effective reduction in lipid levels than either drug alone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Tamoxifen
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tamoxifen, often sold under the brand name Nolvadex, is a non-steroidal medication that belongs to a class of drugs called Selective Estrogen Receptor Modulators (SERMs). It is a type of hormonal therapy used primarily in the treatment and prevention of breast cancer. It can be administered as a tablet or liquid and is effective in both pre- and post-menopausal women, as well as men with breast cancer.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tamoxifen is primarily indicated for the treatment and prevention of breast cancer, specifically in cases where the cancer is "estrogen receptor-positive" (ER+). Its key indications include:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Treatment of Breast Cancer:</b> It is used to treat both early-stage and advanced (metastatic) ER+ breast cancer in men and women.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Adjuvant Therapy:</b> As an add-on treatment after surgery and radiation therapy to reduce the risk of cancer recurrence.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Risk Reduction:</b> For women at high risk of developing breast cancer.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS):</b> To reduce the risk of developing invasive breast cancer in women who have had surgery and radiation for DCIS.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It also has some off-label uses, such as treating anovulatory infertility and gynecomastia.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tamoxifen's mechanism of action is complex and is the reason it is categorized as a SERM. Its effect varies depending on the tissue it is acting on.</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Antagonistic Effect (Anti-Estrogenic):</b> In breast tissue, tamoxifen acts as an estrogen receptor antagonist. It works by competitively binding to estrogen receptors on breast cancer cells. This prevents the body's natural estrogen from binding to these receptors. Since many breast cancers are fueled by estrogen, blocking these receptors stops the growth signals that estrogen sends to the cancer cells, thereby inhibiting their proliferation.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Agonistic Effect (Estrogenic):</b> In other tissues, such as the uterus and bone, tamoxifen can act as a weak estrogen receptor agonist. This means it mimics the effects of estrogen, which can be both beneficial and harmful.</span></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Beneficial:</b> Its estrogenic effect on bone tissue helps to maintain bone density, which can be a positive side effect in postmenopausal women at risk of osteoporosis.</span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Harmful:</b> The estrogenic effect on the uterine lining can increase the risk of developing endometrial changes, including hyperplasia, polyps, and in rare cases, uterine cancer.</span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: J-zit 200mg
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic that is a semi-synthetic derivative of erythromycin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a broad-spectrum, bactericidal agent known for its ability to penetrate and accumulate in body tissues, particularly in immune cells like macrophages.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This characteristic, combined with its long half-life, allows for shorter treatment courses (e.g., a one-time dose or a 5-day course) compared to many other antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including tablets, capsules, and oral suspension.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin is used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its long-lasting effect and ability to target specific types of bacteria make it a popular choice for many conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Key indications for azithromycin include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory tract infections:</b> This includes community-acquired pneumonia, bronchitis, sinusitis, tonsillitis, and pharyngitis (strep throat).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sexually transmitted infections (STIs):</b> It is highly effective as a single dose for treating uncomplicated urethritis and cervicitis caused by <i>Chlamydia trachomatis</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also used to treat chancroid and sometimes for gonorrhea.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and soft tissue infections:</b> It is used for uncomplicated infections caused by susceptible organisms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> It is a common treatment for acute otitis media.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention and treatment of <i>Mycobacterium avium</i> complex (MAC):</b> It is used in patients with advanced HIV/AIDS for both the treatment and prophylaxis of this type of lung infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other uses:</b> It can also be used for conditions like traveler's diarrhea, Lyme disease, and pertussis (whooping cough).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin works by inhibiting the synthesis of essential proteins within bacterial cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is similar to other macrolide antibiotics and involves a specific interaction with the bacterial ribosome:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to the 50S Ribosomal Subunit:</b> Azithromycin binds reversibly to the 23S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) within the 50S subunit of the bacterial ribosome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is the part of the bacterial protein-making machinery that assembles amino acids into a polypeptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Translocation:</b> By binding to this specific site, azithromycin blocks the "translocation" step of protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Translocation is the process where the ribosome moves along the messenger RNA (mRNA) molecule to add a new amino acid.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Protein Synthesis:</b> This blockage prevents the elongation of the growing peptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Without the ability to synthesize new proteins, the bacteria cannot grow, reproduce, or repair themselves, which ultimately leads to their death or prevents their proliferation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin's effectiveness is also enhanced by its unique pharmacokinetic properties. It is highly concentrated in white blood cells, such as phagocytes, which then transport the drug directly to the site of infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This "targeted delivery" mechanism allows the drug to accumulate in infected tissues at concentrations much higher than in the blood, leading to a potent and prolonged antibacterial effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

</button></p>

PRODUCT: Calcium Dabeslate
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Calcium dobesilate, a synthetic drug, is the calcium salt of dobesilic acid. It is classified as a <b>vasoprotective agent</b> because it works to protect and strengthen blood vessel walls, particularly those of the microcirculation (the smallest blood vessels, such as capillaries).</span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Calcium dobesilate is primarily indicated for conditions related to vascular fragility and altered capillary permeability, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Chronic venous insufficiency</b>: A condition where veins have difficulty returning blood from the legs to the heart. This can lead to symptoms like pain, swelling, and cramping in the lower limbs.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Diabetic retinopathy</b>: A microvascular complication of diabetes where high blood sugar levels damage the blood vessels in the retina, leading to fluid leakage and vision problems.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hemorrhoids</b>: Swollen and inflamed veins in the rectum and anus.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The mechanism of action of calcium dobesilate is multifaceted, meaning it works through several pathways to improve vascular health:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reduces Capillary Permeability</b>: It reduces the leakage of fluid and proteins from capillaries into surrounding tissues by stabilizing the basement membrane of the capillary walls. This action is crucial in preventing edema and swelling.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Improves Blood Rheology</b>: It makes the blood thinner and less viscous, and also improves the flexibility of red blood cells. This helps blood flow more smoothly through the microcirculation, especially in areas where it is compromised.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Antioxidant Properties</b>: It acts as an antioxidant, scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which can damage the endothelium (the inner lining of blood vessels). This protects the vascular walls from degeneration.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Anti-inflammatory Effects</b>: It helps reduce inflammation in the vascular system by modulating the release of inflammatory mediators.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Inhibits Platelet Aggregation</b>: The drug can inhibit platelets from clumping together, which helps prevent blood clots from forming.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Azobit
