</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> It is a common treatment for acute otitis media.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention and treatment of <i>Mycobacterium avium</i> complex (MAC):</b> It is used in patients with advanced HIV/AIDS for both the treatment and prophylaxis of this type of lung infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other uses:</b> It can also be used for conditions like traveler's diarrhea, Lyme disease, and pertussis (whooping cough).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin works by inhibiting the synthesis of essential proteins within bacterial cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism is similar to other macrolide antibiotics and involves a specific interaction with the bacterial ribosome:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to the 50S Ribosomal Subunit:</b> Azithromycin binds reversibly to the 23S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) within the 50S subunit of the bacterial ribosome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is the part of the bacterial protein-making machinery that assembles amino acids into a polypeptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Translocation:</b> By binding to this specific site, azithromycin blocks the "translocation" step of protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Translocation is the process where the ribosome moves along the messenger RNA (mRNA) molecule to add a new amino acid.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Protein Synthesis:</b> This blockage prevents the elongation of the growing peptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Without the ability to synthesize new proteins, the bacteria cannot grow, reproduce, or repair themselves, which ultimately leads to their death or prevents their proliferation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin's effectiveness is also enhanced by its unique pharmacokinetic properties. It is highly concentrated in white blood cells, such as phagocytes, which then transport the drug directly to the site of infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This "targeted delivery" mechanism allows the drug to accumulate in infected tissues at concentrations much higher than in the blood, leading to a potent and prolonged antibacterial effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Terlipressin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Terlipressin is a synthetic analogue of the hormone vasopressin. It is a prodrug, meaning it is an inactive substance that is metabolized in the body into its active form, lypressin (also known as lysine vasopressin). It is a potent vasoconstrictor, with a long-lasting and more selective effect than vasopressin. Terlipressin is administered intravenously.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Terlipressin is used to treat conditions characterized by significant vasodilation, particularly in the splanchnic circulation (the blood supply to the abdominal organs). Its primary indications are:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hepatorenal syndrome (HRS):</b> A life-threatening condition in people with severe liver disease where the kidneys suddenly fail. Terlipressin is used to improve kidney function in adults with this condition.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Bleeding esophageal varices:</b> This is a severe complication of liver disease where enlarged veins in the esophagus rupture and bleed. Terlipressin is used to control this bleeding.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Terlipressin's mechanism of action is based on its role as a vasopressin receptor agonist. Here is a breakdown of how it works:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Prodrug conversion:</b> After administration, terlipressin is slowly converted by enzymes in the body into its active metabolite, lypressin. This gradual conversion contributes to its sustained effect.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vasopressin receptor binding:</b> Lypressin primarily binds to and activates V1 vasopressin receptors, which are found on the smooth muscle of blood vessels. It has a higher selectivity for V1 receptors than V2 receptors.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vasoconstriction:</b> The activation of V1 receptors causes powerful vasoconstriction (narrowing of blood vessels). This effect is most pronounced in the splanchnic circulation, which is abnormally dilated in conditions like hepatorenal syndrome and portal hypertension.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Blood flow redistribution:</b> By constricting the splanchnic blood vessels, terlipressin reduces blood flow to the portal venous system. This, in turn, decreases the pressure within the portal vein and esophageal varices, helping to control bleeding.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Improved kidney perfusion:</b> In hepatorenal syndrome, the widespread vasodilation in the splanchnic circulation leads to a decrease in the effective arterial blood volume, which triggers a compensatory constriction of blood vessels to the kidneys. By reversing the splanchnic vasodilation, terlipressin increases mean arterial pressure and redistributes blood flow, improving perfusion to the kidneys and restoring their function.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Cutexia
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefixime is a semisynthetic, third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a broad-spectrum, bactericidal agent that is administered orally.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cefixime, like other third-generation cephalosporins, is known for its effectiveness against a wide range of gram-negative bacteria, and it also maintains good activity against some gram-positive organisms.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefixime is used to treat various bacterial infections, particularly those affecting the respiratory and urinary systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also used for certain sexually transmitted infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary tract infections (UTIs):</b> Uncomplicated UTIs caused by susceptible bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> Acute otitis media.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory tract infections:</b> Pharyngitis, tonsillitis, and acute bacterial exacerbations of chronic bronchitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gonorrhea:</b> It is used as a second-line treatment for uncomplicated gonorrhea, particularly when first-line options like ceftriaxone are unavailable.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often administered in a single dose for this purpose.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefixime's mechanism of action is based on its ability to interfere with the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall, which is a vital structure for the survival of bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its action is similar to that of other beta-lactam antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-Lactam Ring:</b> Cefixime's structure contains a beta-lactam ring, a characteristic feature of its drug class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Penicillin-Binding Proteins (PBPs):</b> Cefixime works by binding to and inhibiting essential enzymes called penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These enzymes are located in the bacterial cell membrane and are crucial for the final stages of bacterial cell wall synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Specifically, PBPs are responsible for cross-linking the peptidoglycan chains, which forms the rigid, protective meshwork of the cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cell Wall Lysis:</b> When cefixime binds to PBPs, it inactivates them, preventing the cross-linking process.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The bacterial cell wall becomes weak and unstable, unable to withstand the internal osmotic pressure. This leads to the swelling and rupture (lysis) of the bacterial cell, ultimately causing cell death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key advantage of third-generation cephalosporins like cefixime is their enhanced stability against certain bacterial enzymes called beta-lactamases.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Many bacteria produce these enzymes to degrade antibiotics with beta-lactam rings, conferring resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cefixime's stability to these enzymes allows it to remain effective against many bacteria that would be resistant to older penicillins and cephalosporins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Cinfos
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fosfomycin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic that belongs to a unique class of antibiotics, the phosphonic acid derivatives.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Fosfomycin calcium is a specific salt form of the drug, often formulated for oral administration.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a bactericidal agent, meaning it kills bacteria, and is effective against a wide range of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The primary indication for Fosfomycin Calcium is the treatment of <b>uncomplicated urinary tract infections (UTIs)</b>, specifically acute cystitis in adult women, caused by susceptible strains of <i>Escherichia coli</i> and <i>Enterococcus faecalis</i>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Due to its high concentration in the urinary tract, it is particularly well-suited for this purpose.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">While fosfomycin has a broad spectrum of activity, its use in many regions is limited to uncomplicated UTIs. This is because other forms of fosfomycin (such as the tromethamine or disodium salts) are also available, and the different salts have varying pharmacokinetic properties, including bioavailability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Fosfomycin calcium is less bioavailable than the tromethamine salt, which may influence its use and dosing recommendations. It is not indicated for more complex infections like pyelonephritis or perinephric abscesses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fosfomycin's mechanism of action is unique and different from other common antibiotic classes, which contributes to its low rate of cross-resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by inhibiting a crucial early step in the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting MurA Enzyme:</b> Fosfomycin is a structural analog of phosphoenolpyruvate (PEP).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by irreversibly inhibiting the enzyme <b>UDP-N-acetylglucosamine enolpyruvyl transferase</b>, commonly known as <b>MurA</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Inhibition:</b> The drug forms a covalent bond with a cysteine residue in the active site of the MurA enzyme, which permanently inactivates it.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Cell Wall Synthesis:</b> The MurA enzyme is responsible for catalyzing the first committed step in the synthesis of peptidoglycan, the primary structural component of the bacterial cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting MurA, fosfomycin prevents the formation of N-acetylmuramic acid, a vital precursor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cell Lysis:</b> Without a properly formed peptidoglycan layer, the bacterial cell wall becomes weak and unstable.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This makes the bacterium highly susceptible to osmotic pressure, leading to cell swelling and eventual rupture (lysis).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fosfomycin enters bacterial cells through specific transport systems, primarily the L-alpha-glycerophosphate and hexose-6-phosphate transporter systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This unique uptake mechanism and its early-stage inhibition of cell wall synthesis make it an effective agent against a wide variety of bacteria, including some that are multi-drug resistant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Fluphenazine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Fluphenazine is a high-potency, first-generation antipsychotic medication. It's part of the phenothiazine class of drugs and is used to treat chronic psychotic disorders, particularly schizophrenia. It is available in various forms, including oral tablets, oral liquid, and long-acting injectable formulations, which are often used for patients who have trouble adhering to their daily medication schedule.</span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The primary indication for fluphenazine is the management of psychotic disorders, most notably <b>schizophrenia</b>. It is effective in managing the "positive symptoms" of schizophrenia, which are symptoms that are added to a person's life, such as:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Hallucinations (seeing or hearing things that aren't there)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Delusions (firmly held false beliefs)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Disorganized thought and speech</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It may also be used off-label for other conditions like chronic tic disorders and Huntington's disease to control abnormal movements.</span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Fluphenazine's primary mechanism of action is the <b>antagonism of postsynaptic dopamine D2 receptors</b> in the brain.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>&nbsp; Dopamine Overactivity: Psychotic disorders like schizophrenia are associated with an overactivity of the dopamine system in certain brain regions, specifically the mesolimbic pathway. This excess dopamine signaling is thought to contribute to the positive symptoms of psychosis.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>&nbsp; Receptor Blockade: By blocking the D2 receptors, fluphenazine reduces the effect of dopamine in these areas, thereby helping to control psychotic symptoms.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>&nbsp; Other Receptor Interactions: Fluphenazine also has antagonistic effects on other receptors, including alpha-1 adrenergic receptors, muscarinic-1 receptors, and histamine-1 receptors. These additional actions contribute to both its therapeutic effects and its side effect profile, which can include sedation, dry mouth, and low blood pressure.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Azrowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin Dihydrate is a salt form of the active drug azithromycin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The term "dihydrate" indicates that each molecule of azithromycin is associated with two molecules of water (H2​O). This is a common way to formulate drugs for stability, manufacturing, and absorption. From a therapeutic standpoint, azithromycin dihydrate is the active pharmaceutical ingredient found in many azithromycin products (e.g., tablets and oral suspensions) and has the same clinical effects, indications, and mechanism of action as azithromycin. It is a macrolide antibiotic belonging to the azalide subclass, characterized by a 15-membered ring.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Azithromycin dihydrate is used to treat a broad range of bacterial infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key features—such as its long half-life, high concentration in tissues, and ability to be administered in shorter courses—make it a common choice for various conditions. Indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Respiratory tract infections:</b> Including community-acquired pneumonia, bronchitis, sinusitis, tonsillitis, and pharyngitis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sexually transmitted infections (STIs):</b> A single dose is highly effective for uncomplicated urethritis and cervicitis caused by <i>Chlamydia trachomatis</i>. It is also used for chancroid and sometimes for uncomplicated gonorrhea.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and soft tissue infections:</b> Used for mild to moderate infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ear infections:</b> A common treatment for acute otitis media.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention and treatment of <i>Mycobacterium avium</i> complex (MAC):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Used in patients with advanced HIV/AIDS for both the treatment and prophylaxis of this type of lung infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other uses:</b> Can also be used for conditions like traveler's diarrhea and pertussis (whooping cough).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action for azithromycin dihydrate is identical to that of azithromycin. It is a bactericidal agent that works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It does this by specifically targeting the bacterial ribosome, which is the cellular machinery responsible for creating new proteins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to the 50S Ribosomal Subunit:</b> Azithromycin dihydrate binds reversibly to the 23S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) of the 50S ribosomal subunit.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Translocation:</b> By binding to this site, the drug blocks the process of "translocation," which is the movement of the ribosome along the messenger RNA (mRNA) molecule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This movement is essential for adding new amino acids to the growing protein chain.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Protein Synthesis:</b> By preventing translocation, azithromycin dihydrate halts the elongation of the peptide chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Without the ability to synthesize essential proteins, the bacteria cannot grow, reproduce, or perform basic functions, leading to their death or preventing their proliferation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The drug's effectiveness is further enhanced by its unique pharmacokinetic properties. It has a high affinity for and is actively absorbed by certain cells, particularly white blood cells like phagocytes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These cells then transport the drug to the site of infection, where it is released in high concentrations, leading to a potent and prolonged antibacterial effect.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Vactrox
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vancomycin is a glycopeptide antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a large, complex molecule that is used to treat serious bacterial infections, particularly those caused by Gram-positive bacteria that are resistant to other drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "HCl" in its name refers to the hydrochloride salt form, which is used for stability and solubility. Vancomycin is not absorbed well from the gut, so it's administered differently depending on the infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It's typically given intravenously for systemic infections and orally for infections confined to the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button><hr< source-inline-chip=""><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></source-inline-chip><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></source-inline-chip> size=3 width="100%" align=center&gt; </hr<></span></span></span></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vancomycin is a crucial "last-resort" antibiotic for many serious infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its use is generally restricted to prevent the development of widespread bacterial resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intravenous (IV) Administration:</b> The primary use of IV vancomycin is to treat severe, systemic infections caused by <b>Gram-positive bacteria</b>, especially those resistant to other antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This includes:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA)</b> infections, such as those causing septicemia (blood poisoning), endocarditis (infection of the heart lining), bone and joint infections, and complicated skin and soft tissue infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Serious infections in patients with a serious allergy to penicillin.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Infections caused by other multi-drug resistant Gram-positive organisms, such as <b>Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus (VRE)</b>, though this use has limitations as VRE has developed resistance to vancomycin.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Oral Administration:</b> Because vancomycin is poorly absorbed from the gut, oral administration is only effective for treating infections within the intestines.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indication is for <b>Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea (C. diff)</b>, also known as pseudomembranous colitis, and staphylococcal enterocolitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button><hr< source-inline-chip=""><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></source-inline-chip> size=3 width="100%" align=center&gt; </hr<></span></span></span></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vancomycin's mechanism of action is unique, making it effective against bacteria that have developed resistance to beta-lactam antibiotics like penicillin. It works by inhibiting a crucial step in the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall, a structure essential for the bacteria's survival.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to Peptidoglycan Precursors:</b> Vancomycin does not directly inhibit an enzyme, but instead acts like a "cap" by binding with high affinity to the terminal <b>D-alanyl-D-alanine</b> portion of the peptidoglycan precursors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Peptidoglycan is the main component of the bacterial cell wall.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Transglycosylation and Transpeptidation:</b> The binding of vancomycin to these precursors sterically hinders the enzymes (transglycosylases and transpeptidases, also known as penicillin-binding proteins or PBPs) from adding new units and cross-linking the peptidoglycan chains.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Weakening the Cell Wall and Causing Lysis:</b> By preventing the formation of a strong, cross-linked cell wall, vancomycin causes the existing cell wall to become unstable.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The bacterial cell, unable to withstand the internal osmotic pressure, swells and eventually ruptures (lyses), leading to cell death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This specific binding site is the reason vancomycin is effective against resistant bacteria, and it's also the basis for how some bacteria, like VRE, have become resistant: they have a mutation that changes the D-alanyl-D-alanine terminal to D-alanyl-D-lactate, which significantly reduces vancomycin's binding affinity.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Pioglitazone + Glimepride
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><b>Description</b></h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Pioglitazone and Glimepride is a combination medication used to treat Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. It combines two different classes of oral anti-diabetic agents: a thiazolidinedione (Pioglitazone) and a sulfonylurea (Glimepride). This dual-action approach helps manage high blood sugar levels in adults when a single medication is not sufficient.<button _ngcontent-ng-c2920471647="" cdkoverlayorigin="" data-hveid="0" data-ved="0CAAQvoAQahgKEwjxoO30voWQAxUAAAAAHQAAAAAQ5wE" decode-data-ved="1" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<p><b>Indicatio</b></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>This combination therapy is indicated for the management of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus as an adjunct to diet and exercise. It is typically prescribed for patients who:<button _ngcontent-ng-c2920471647="" cdkoverlayorigin="" data-hveid="0" data-ved="0CAAQvoAQahgKEwjxoO30voWQAxUAAAAAHQAAAAAQ6gE" decode-data-ved="1" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ul>
	<li>Are already being treated with a combination of pioglitazone and a sulfonylurea.</li>
	<li>Have inadequate glycemic control on a sulfonylurea alone.</li>
	<li>Have initially responded to pioglitazone alone but require additional glycemic control</li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><b>Mechanism of Action</b></h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>The effectiveness of this combination lies in its synergistic effect, addressing both insulin resistance and insulin secretion.<button _ngcontent-ng-c2920471647="" cdkoverlayorigin="" data-hveid="0" data-ved="0CAAQvoAQahgKEwjxoO30voWQAxUAAAAAHQAAAAAQ7gE" decode-data-ved="1" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"></button></p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Pioglitazone (Thiazolidinedione):</b> Pioglitazone is a potent and selective agonist for the peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-gamma (PPARγ). By activating this nuclear receptor, it influences the transcription of genes involved in glucose and lipid metabolism. This action primarily enhances the body's sensitivity to insulin in key tissues like muscle, fat, and the liver, leading to improved glucose uptake and utilization. It works to combat insulin resistance, which is a key characteristic of Type 2 diabetes.</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Glimepride (Sulfonylurea):</b> Glimepride's primary mechanism is to stimulate the release of insulin from the functioning beta cells in the pancreas. It works by binding to ATP-sensitive potassium channels on the surface of these beta cells, which leads to their depolarization and the subsequent influx of calcium. This increase in intracellular calcium triggers the exocytosis of insulin granules, increasing the amount of insulin in the bloodstream and helping to lower blood glucose levels.</p>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Cetzelex
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefprozil is a semi-synthetic, second-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It belongs to the beta-lactam class of drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cefprozil is available in oral forms, such as tablets and suspension, and is effective against a wide range of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefprozil is prescribed for the treatment of mild to moderate bacterial infections in adults and children.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Upper Respiratory Tract Infections:</b> Pharyngitis, tonsillitis, and acute sinusitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lower Respiratory Tract Infections:</b> Acute bacterial exacerbation of chronic bronchitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin and Skin Structure Infections:</b> Uncomplicated infections caused by susceptible bacteria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Otitis Media:</b> Acute ear infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary Tract Infections:</b> Uncomplicated urinary tract infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button><hr< source-inline-chip=""> size=3 width="100%" align=center&gt; </hr<></span></span></span></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cefprozil, like other beta-lactam antibiotics, works by inhibiting the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This mechanism is crucial for the drug's bactericidal (bacteria-killing) effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting Penicillin-Binding Proteins (PBPs):</b> Cefprozil's molecular structure contains a <b>beta-lactam ring</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This ring is able to bind to and inactivate enzymes called <b>penicillin-binding proteins</b> (PBPs), which are located in the bacterial cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibiting Cell Wall Synthesis:</b> PBPs are essential for the final step of bacterial cell wall synthesis—the <b>cross-linking of peptidoglycan chains</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This cross-linking process is what gives the bacterial cell wall its rigidity and strength.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Causing Cell Lysis:</b> By inhibiting PBPs, cefprozil prevents the cell wall from being properly formed and maintained.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The weakened cell wall is then unable to withstand the high internal osmotic pressure of the bacterium.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the cell swelling and rupturing, which ultimately causes the bacterium to die.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A notable feature of second-generation cephalosporins is their enhanced stability against some beta-lactamase enzymes produced by bacteria, which are responsible for breaking down antibiotics. This stability gives cefprozil a broader spectrum of activity against certain resistant bacterial strains compared to first-generation cephalosporins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Prasugrel
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Prasugrel is an antiplatelet medication. It is a thienopyridine prodrug, meaning it is converted into its active form in the body. It works by preventing platelets (a type of blood cell) from clumping together and forming blood clots.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Indication:</b> Prasugrel is used in conjunction with aspirin to reduce the risk of thrombotic cardiovascular events, such as heart attack and stroke, in patients with Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) who are undergoing a heart procedure called percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Prasugrel is a selective and irreversible antagonist of the P2Y$_{12}$ class of ADP receptors on the surface of platelets. When its active metabolite binds to these receptors, it blocks the binding of adenosine diphosphate (ADP), which is a key substance that promotes platelet activation and aggregation. This irreversible binding prevents platelets from activating and aggregating for their entire lifespan (about 7-10 days).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Risedronate Sodium
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Risedronate Sodium is a bisphosphonate, a class of non-hormonal drugs that inhibit bone breakdown. It is a crystalline powder that is soluble in water. It is available in various tablet strengths for oral administration.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Indication:</b> Risedronate Sodium is primarily used for the treatment and prevention of osteoporosis, a condition that causes bones to become thin, weak, and susceptible to fractures. It is also used to treat Paget's disease of bone, a condition where the body's bone-building process is disrupted. The drug is prescribed for postmenopausal women and men with osteoporosis, as well as for men and women who are taking glucocorticoid medications that can lead to bone loss.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Risedronate Sodium works by binding to hydroxyapatite crystals in the bone, where it acts as an antiresorptive agent. At a cellular level, it inhibits the activity of osteoclasts, which are the cells responsible for breaking down bone tissue. By reducing bone resorption, it slows down the rate of bone turnover and helps to increase bone density, ultimately decreasing the risk of fractures.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Bupivacaine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Bupivacaine is a potent, long-acting local anesthetic that belongs to the amide class of anesthetics. It is typically administered via injection to numb a specific area of the body. It causes a reversible loss of sensation and prevents pain without causing a loss of consciousness.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Bupivacaine is indicated for the production of local or regional anesthesia or analgesia for a variety of purposes, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Surgical procedures</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Dental and oral surgery</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Diagnostic and therapeutic procedures</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Obstetrical procedures (e.g., labor pain relief)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Post-operative pain relief</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Bupivacaine works by blocking nerve impulses. It does this by reversibly binding to and blocking the voltage-gated sodium channels in the neuronal membrane. This action prevents the influx of sodium ions, which is essential for the initiation and conduction of nerve impulses. By stabilizing the nerve cell membrane it inhibits the transmission of pain signals and other sensations.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
