PRODUCT: Glucita
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Phloroglucinol is an organic compound that acts as an <b>antispasmodic</b> drug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It's often used in combination with its derivative, trimethylphloroglucinol, to enhance its muscle-relaxing effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination, sometimes referred to as "hydrated phloroglucinol + trimethylphloroglucinol," is commonly formulated into suppositories, tablets, and solutions for injection.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This drug combination is used for the symptomatic treatment of pain caused by <b>spasms of smooth muscles</b> in various organs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Digestive disorders:</b> Pain associated with functional disorders of the gastrointestinal and biliary tracts.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary tract spasms:</b> Acute painful episodes like renal colic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gynecological conditions:</b> Painful spasms of the uterus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pregnancy contractions:</b> As an adjuvant therapy, along with rest.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Phloroglucinol and trimethylphloroglucinol work by directly relaxing smooth muscles, particularly in the digestive and urinary systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Their mechanism of action is non-anticholinergic, meaning they don't produce side effects like dry mouth or drowsiness.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The primary mechanism is the <b>inhibition of phosphodiesterase enzymes</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This inhibition leads to an increase in intracellular levels of cyclic AMP (cAMP) and cyclic GMP (cGMP), which are secondary messengers crucial for cellular signaling.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Elevated levels of these molecules cause a reduction in the influx of calcium ions into the smooth muscle cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Since calcium is essential for muscle contraction, its reduced availability leads to muscle relaxation and relief from spasms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Additionally, the combination has been shown to:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Directly <b>inhibit voltage-dependent calcium channels</b>, further decreasing the calcium concentration inside the cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibit the <b>catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) enzyme</b>, which breaks down catecholamines in smooth muscle tissue.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting this enzyme, the combination increases sympathetic tone, which also contributes to an antispasmodic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Olmesartan + Amlodipine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination of Olmesartan and Amlodipine is an oral medication that combines two different types of drugs used to treat hypertension (high blood pressure). It is available as a single tablet, which helps to simplify treatment and improve patient adherence.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This drug is indicated for the treatment of essential hypertension. The combination is particularly useful for patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled by either Olmesartan or Amlodipine alone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This medication works through a dual mechanism to lower blood pressure:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Olmesartan:</b> This is an Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker (ARB). It selectively blocks the binding of angiotensin II to the AT1 receptor in vascular smooth muscle, which prevents the vasoconstrictive (narrowing of blood vessels) effects of angiotensin II. This leads to vasodilation (widening of blood vessels) and a reduction in blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Amlodipine:</b> This is a dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker (CCB). It inhibits the influx of calcium ions into vascular smooth muscle and cardiac muscle. By reducing the calcium concentration in smooth muscle cells, it causes peripheral arterial vasodilation, which lowers blood pressure. Amlodipine's effect on heart rate is minimal at therapeutic doses.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination of these two mechanisms provides a more effective and complementary approach to lowering blood pressure than either drug alone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Rivastigmine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Rivastigmine is a medication used to treat mild to moderate dementia in individuals with Alzheimer's disease or Parkinson's disease. It is a type of drug known as a cholinesterase inhibitor. Rivastigmine is available in oral forms (capsules, solution) and as a transdermal patch.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Rivastigmine is indicated for the symptomatic treatment of:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Mild to moderately severe dementia associated with Alzheimer's disease.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Mild to moderate dementia in patients with Parkinson's disease.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The primary function of rivastigmine is to increase the levels of acetylcholine in the brain. Acetylcholine is a neurotransmitter crucial for memory, attention, and other cognitive functions. In Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease, there is a deficiency of acetylcholine due to the degeneration of cholinergic neurons.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Rivastigmine works by inhibiting two key enzymes responsible for breaking down acetylcholine:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Acetylcholinesterase (AChE)</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Butyrylcholinesterase (BuChE)</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">By inhibiting these enzymes, rivastigmine slows the degradation of acetylcholine, allowing it to remain in the synaptic clefts for a longer period. This increased concentration of acetylcholine helps to improve communication between nerve cells, which in turn can lead to an improvement in cognitive symptoms like memory loss and confusion. Rivastigmine is a pseudo-irreversible inhibitor, meaning its binding to the enzymes lasts longer than with a reversible inhibitor, providing a sustained therapeutic effect.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Myrozik
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Phloroglucinol is an organic compound that acts as an <b>antispasmodic</b> drug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It's often used in combination with its derivative, trimethylphloroglucinol, to enhance its muscle-relaxing effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination, sometimes referred to as "hydrated phloroglucinol + trimethylphloroglucinol," is commonly formulated into suppositories, tablets, and solutions for injection.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This drug combination is used for the symptomatic treatment of pain caused by <b>spasms of smooth muscles</b> in various organs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Digestive disorders:</b> Pain associated with functional disorders of the gastrointestinal and biliary tracts.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary tract spasms:</b> Acute painful episodes like renal colic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gynecological conditions:</b> Painful spasms of the uterus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pregnancy contractions:</b> As an adjuvant therapy, along with rest.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Phloroglucinol and trimethylphloroglucinol work by directly relaxing smooth muscles, particularly in the digestive and urinary systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Their mechanism of action is non-anticholinergic, meaning they don't produce side effects like dry mouth or drowsiness.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The primary mechanism is the <b>inhibition of phosphodiesterase enzymes</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This inhibition leads to an increase in intracellular levels of cyclic AMP (cAMP) and cyclic GMP (cGMP), which are secondary messengers crucial for cellular signaling.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Elevated levels of these molecules cause a reduction in the influx of calcium ions into the smooth muscle cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Since calcium is essential for muscle contraction, its reduced availability leads to muscle relaxation and relief from spasms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Additionally, the combination has been shown to:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Directly <b>inhibit voltage-dependent calcium channels</b>, further decreasing the calcium concentration inside the cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibit the <b>catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) enzyme</b>, which breaks down catecholamines in smooth muscle tissue.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting this enzyme, the combination increases sympathetic tone, which also contributes to an antispasmodic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Granisetron
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Granisetron is a selective serotonin 5-HT3 receptor antagonist. It is a type of antiemetic, a drug used to prevent nausea and vomiting. It is available in various forms, including tablets, oral solutions, and injectable formulations.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Granisetron is primarily used to prevent nausea and vomiting caused by cancer chemotherapy and radiation therapy. It is also used to prevent and treat postoperative nausea and vomiting.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Chemotherapy and radiation therapy can cause damage to cells in the gastrointestinal tract, which leads to the release of a natural substance called serotonin (5-HT). This serotonin then binds to 5-HT3 receptors located on the vagal nerve terminals in the gut and in the chemoreceptor trigger zone in the brain, which in turn sends signals to the brain's vomiting center, triggering the vomiting reflex. Granisetron works by selectively and competitively blocking these 5-HT3 receptors. By doing so, it prevents serotonin from binding to them and effectively inhibits the signals that cause nausea and vomiting.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Lercanidipine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Lercanidipine is an antihypertensive drug that belongs to a class of medicines known as dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers. It is available as tablets and is used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension). It is known for its high vascular selectivity and has a prolonged antihypertensive effect despite a relatively short half-life, which is due to its high affinity for lipid membranes.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Lercanidipine is indicated for the treatment of mild to moderate essential hypertension in adults.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Lercanidipine works by blocking the L-type calcium channels in the smooth muscle cells of blood vessels. This action inhibits the influx of calcium ions into the cells, which is necessary for muscle contraction. By preventing these contractions, the drug causes the blood vessels to relax and widen (vasodilation). This vasodilation lowers the total peripheral resistance, which in turn reduces blood pressure and the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Esowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) that works by reducing the amount of acid produced in the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the S-isomer of omeprazole, a racemic mixture, which is the reason for its name ("S-omeprazole").<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This specific isomer formulation leads to a more predictable metabolism and a higher bioavailability, which can result in more effective acid suppression. Esomeprazole is available in various forms, including delayed-release capsules, tablets, and oral suspensions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole is used to treat a range of conditions caused by excessive stomach acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It is used for the treatment of GERD, a chronic condition where stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing heartburn and potential damage.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Erosive Esophagitis:</b> A more severe form of GERD where stomach acid has caused damage and inflammation to the lining of the esophagus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Esomeprazole is used for both the short-term healing and long-term maintenance of this condition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</b> A rare condition where a tumor causes the stomach to produce an excessive amount of acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stomach and Duodenal Ulcers:</b> Used to treat and prevent gastric and duodenal ulcers, especially those caused by an infection with the bacterium <i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (H. pylori) or by the long-term use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When used for H. pylori, it is typically part of a multi-drug regimen with antibiotics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the enzyme responsible for the final step of gastric acid production.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the Proton Pump:</b> Esomeprazole is a prodrug, meaning it is inactive until it is activated. It is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the <b>parietal cells</b> in the stomach, which are responsible for secreting acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In the highly acidic environment of these cells, esomeprazole is converted into its active form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Binding:</b> The activated form of esomeprazole then irreversibly binds to and inactivates the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Acid Secretion:</b> The proton pump is the final step in the process of gastric acid secretion, pumping hydrogen ions (H+) into the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By irreversibly blocking this pump, esomeprazole prevents both basal (resting) and stimulated acid secretion, regardless of the stimulus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Because this binding is irreversible, the drug's effect lasts much longer than its half-life in the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The stomach cannot produce acid until new proton pump enzymes are synthesized, which takes approximately 24 hours. This is why a once-daily dose of esomeprazole is sufficient to provide a sustained reduction in stomach acid.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Eszopiclone
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Eszopiclone is a non-benzodiazepine hypnotic agent that is used for the treatment of insomnia. It is a cyclopyrrolone derivative and is the active stereoisomer of zopiclone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Eszopiclone is indicated for the treatment of insomnia, specifically for sleep maintenance (staying asleep) and sleep onset (falling asleep). It is typically prescribed for short-term use.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Eszopiclone is a positive allosteric modulator of the GABA-A receptor. It binds to a site on the GABA-A receptor complex, which is distinct from the benzodiazepine binding site, and enhances the effects of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA). GABA is the main inhibitory neurotransmitter in the central nervous system. By enhancing GABA's inhibitory effects, eszopiclone increases chloride ion influx into neurons, leading to hyperpolarization and a reduction in neuronal excitability. This results in sedative, hypnotic, anxiolytic, and muscle-relaxant effects, promoting sleep.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Zorolyx

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the enzyme responsible for the final step of gastric acid production.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the Proton Pump:</b> Esomeprazole is a prodrug, meaning it is inactive until it is activated. It is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the <b>parietal cells</b> in the stomach, which are responsible for secreting acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In the highly acidic environment of these cells, esomeprazole is converted into its active form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Binding:</b> The activated form of esomeprazole then irreversibly binds to and inactivates the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Acid Secretion:</b> The proton pump is the final step in the process of gastric acid secretion, pumping hydrogen ions (H+) into the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By irreversibly blocking this pump, esomeprazole prevents both basal (resting) and stimulated acid secretion, regardless of the stimulus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Because this binding is irreversible, the drug's effect lasts much longer than its half-life in the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The stomach cannot produce acid until new proton pump enzymes are synthesized, which takes approximately 24 hours. This is why a once-daily dose of esomeprazole is sufficient to provide a sustained reduction in stomach acid.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Cefoprozil: Respiratory &amp; Skin Infections Treatment
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Cefprozil is a second-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. It is a broad-spectrum antibiotic that is effective against a wide range of bacteria.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications:</b> It is used to treat various bacterial infections, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Pharyngitis and tonsillitis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Otitis media (ear infections)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Sinusitis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Lower respiratory tract infections, such as bronchitis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Skin and skin structure infections</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Cefprozil works by inhibiting the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall. It binds to and inactivates penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located on the inner membrane of the bacterial cell wall. This disrupts the peptidoglycan synthesis, leading to the lysis of the cell wall and the death of the bacterium.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Mycophenolate Mofetil
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Mycophenolate Mofetil (MMF) is an ester prodrug of mycophenolic acid (MPA). This means that it is an inactive compound that is converted into the active drug (MPA) in the body. It is a potent immunosuppressive agent used to prevent the immune system from attacking a transplanted organ.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The primary indication for Mycophenolate Mofetil is to prevent organ rejection in patients who have received a kidney, heart, or liver transplant. It is typically used in combination with other medications like cyclosporine or corticosteroids. It is also used "off-label" to treat various autoimmune and inflammatory conditions, such as lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, and psoriasis.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Mycophenolate Mofetil is converted into its active metabolite, mycophenolic acid (MPA). MPA is a potent, selective, and reversible inhibitor of inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase (IMPDH). This enzyme is crucial for the <i>de novo</i> pathway of guanosine nucleotide synthesis.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The key to its mechanism is that B and T lymphocytes, which are the immune cells responsible for transplant rejection, are highly dependent on this <i>de novo</i> pathway for their proliferation. By inhibiting IMPDH, MPA effectively blocks the replication of these lymphocytes, thereby suppressing the immune response and preventing the rejection of the transplanted organ.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Navix
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole Sodium, Lyophilized Powder is a sterile, freeze-dried form of the active drug esomeprazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "lyophilized powder" formulation is used for parenteral administration, specifically for intravenous (IV) infusion or injection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This formulation is chosen because esomeprazole is unstable in solution and would degrade quickly.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Converting it into a stable powder and then reconstituting it just before use allows for its safe and effective delivery directly into the bloodstream.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This injectable form of esomeprazole is primarily used in situations where a patient cannot take the medication orally.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is common in hospital settings for patients who are critically ill, have difficulty swallowing, or are unconscious. The main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastric antisecretory treatment:</b> This is for short-term use in adults and children with conditions like <b>gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD)</b> with erosive esophagitis when oral therapy is not possible.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Risk reduction of rebleeding from gastric or duodenal ulcers:</b> Following a therapeutic endoscopy for acute bleeding, this form of esomeprazole is used to provide continuous acid suppression, which is crucial for preventing a re-bleed.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often administered as a bolus followed by a continuous infusion for a period of up to 72 hours.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of gastric and duodenal ulcers:</b> In at-risk patients who are unable to take oral medication, particularly those on long-term nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action for esomeprazole sodium is identical to the oral form of esomeprazole. It is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) that works by irreversibly blocking the enzyme responsible for gastric acid production.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation:</b> Esomeprazole is an inactive prodrug that is administered into the body. After being absorbed, it travels to the <b>parietal cells</b> in the stomach lining.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> In the highly acidic environment of these cells, the esomeprazole is converted into its active sulfenamide form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Inhibition of the Proton Pump:</b> This activated form then binds irreversibly to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The proton pump is the final step in the acid secretion process, responsible for pumping hydrogen ions (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>H+) into the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sustained Acid Suppression:</b> By irreversibly blocking the proton pump, esomeprazole effectively prevents both resting and stimulated acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The acid-suppressing effect persists much longer than the drug's half-life in the bloodstream because the parietal cell cannot resume acid production until it synthesizes new proton pumps, a process that takes about 24 hours.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Cilostazol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Cilostazol is a quinolinone derivative that functions as a phosphodiesterase 3 (PDE3) inhibitor. It works by inhibiting platelet aggregation (clumping) and by acting as a vasodilator, meaning it widens blood vessels. This dual action helps to improve blood flow.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> The primary indication for Cilostazol is the treatment of symptoms associated with intermittent claudication. This is a condition caused by peripheral artery disease (PAD), where narrowed blood vessels in the legs lead to pain, cramping, or numbness during walking or exercise. Cilostazol is used to reduce these symptoms and increase the patient's walking distance. It is typically a second-line treatment for patients who have not responded to lifestyle modifications like exercise and smoking cessation.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Cilostazol's mechanism of action involves inhibiting the enzyme phosphodiesterase III (PDE3). This inhibition prevents the breakdown of cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) inside cells. The resulting increase in cAMP levels leads to two key effects:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Antiplatelet effect:</b> The increased cAMP in platelets inhibits their activation and aggregation, reducing the risk of blood clots.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vasodilatory effect:</b> The increased cAMP in the smooth muscle cells of blood vessels causes them to relax and widen (vasodilation), improving blood flow to the legs.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Navix sachet
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole Enteric Coated Pellets is a pharmaceutical formulation of the proton pump inhibitor (PPI) drug, esomeprazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The active ingredient, esomeprazole, is an acid-labile compound, meaning it is quickly degraded by the highly acidic environment of the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> To prevent this degradation and ensure the drug reaches its site of action, it is manufactured as tiny pellets (micro-pellets or granules) that are coated with a special <b>enteric coating</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This coating is designed to resist dissolution in the acidic stomach environment. The pellets remain intact as they pass through the stomach and only dissolve in the more alkaline environment of the small intestine, where the drug can be safely absorbed into the bloodstream. These pellets are then typically filled into capsules or compressed into tablets.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Esomeprazole enteric coated pellets are indicated for the same range of acid-related conditions as other formulations of esomeprazole. The use of this specific formulation is to ensure the drug's stability and effectiveness. Key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> For the treatment of GERD symptoms like heartburn and acid regurgitation, and for the healing and long-term maintenance of erosive esophagitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Healing of Stomach and Duodenal Ulcers:</b> Used to treat and prevent ulcers, particularly those caused by the long-term use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) or an infection with <i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (<i>H.</i><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><i> pylori</i>).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</b> For the long-term treatment of this pathological hypersecretory condition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of esomeprazole enteric coated pellets is a combination of the drug's pharmacological effect and its unique delivery system.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enteric-Coating for Protection:</b> The enteric coating is the first crucial step in the drug's mechanism. It protects the acid-sensitive esomeprazole from degradation in the stomach. The coating is a pH-sensitive polymer that remains undissolved below a pH of approximately 5.5.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Absorption in the Small Intestine:</b> Once the pellets leave the stomach and enter the small intestine, where the pH is higher, the enteric coating dissolves, releasing the esomeprazole. The drug is then absorbed into the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the Proton Pump:</b> The absorbed esomeprazole is an inactive prodrug. It travels through the bloodstream and accumulates in the highly acidic secretory canaliculi of the gastric parietal cells.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Activation and Irreversible Inhibition:</b> In this acidic environment, esomeprazole is converted into its active form, a sulfenamide. This active form then <b>irreversibly binds</b> to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, which is the "proton pump" responsible for the final step of gastric acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Acid Production:</b> By permanently inactivating the proton pump, esomeprazole effectively blocks both basal (resting) and stimulated acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because the binding is irreversible, acid production is suppressed until the parietal cells synthesize new proton pumps, which ensures a sustained and powerful acid-suppressing effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Exemestane
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Exemestane is a medication used primarily in postmenopausal women. It is a steroidal aromatase inhibitor, meaning it is structurally similar to the natural steroid hormones in the body. It is often used after a patient has already been treated with another medication, such as tamoxifen.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Exemestane is used to treat:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Early breast cancer</b> in postmenopausal women who have already been treated with tamoxifen for 2 to 3 years and are switching to Exemestane to complete a total of 5 years of hormonal therapy.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Advanced or metastatic breast cancer</b> in postmenopausal women whose cancer has progressed despite treatment with tamoxifen.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The main source of estrogen in postmenopausal women is the conversion of androgens (male hormones) into estrogens (female hormones) by an enzyme called aromatase. Many breast cancers are "hormone-sensitive" or "estrogen receptor-positive," meaning they need estrogen to grow. Exemestane works by binding irreversibly to and inactivating the aromatase enzyme. This is sometimes referred to as "suicide inhibition." By blocking this enzyme, Exemestane significantly lowers the amount of estrogen in the body, which helps to slow or stop the growth of breast cancer cells.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Meprawin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Omeprazole is a synthetic, substituted benzimidazole drug that belongs to the class of medications known as proton pump inhibitors (PPIs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the first PPI developed and is a racemic mixture, meaning it contains equal parts of two mirror-image isomers, the S-isomer (esomeprazole) and the R-isomer.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Omeprazole is an acid-labile compound and is formulated as delayed-release capsules or tablets with an enteric coating to protect it from degradation in the stomach's acidic environment.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Omeprazole is widely used to treat and prevent a variety of conditions caused by excess stomach acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It is used for the short-term treatment of heartburn, acid regurgitation, and other symptoms of GERD, as well as for the healing and maintenance of erosive esophagitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stomach and Duodenal Ulcers:</b> It is used to treat existing ulcers and to prevent their recurrence.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a key component in multi-drug regimens for the eradication of <i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (<i>H.</i><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><i> pylori</i>) infection, which is a major cause of peptic ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</b> Omeprazole is used for the long-term management of this rare hypersecretory condition where the stomach produces an excessive amount of acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of NSAID-induced ulcers:</b> It is prescribed to patients who are at high risk of developing gastric ulcers from long-term use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Omeprazole's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the enzyme that pumps acid into the stomach.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation:</b> Omeprazole is a <b>prodrug</b>, meaning it is pharmacologically inactive in its original form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After being absorbed from the small intestine, it enters the bloodstream and travels to the parietal cells in the stomach lining.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to Active Form:</b> In the highly acidic environment of the parietal cells' secretory canaliculi, omeprazole undergoes an acid-catalyzed conversion into its active form, a sulfenamide.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Inhibition of the Proton Pump:</b> This activated form then <b>irreversibly binds</b> to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump." The proton pump is the final and most crucial step in the secretion of gastric acid, responsible for pumping hydrogen ions (H+) into the stomach.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Acid Production:</b> By permanently inactivating the proton pump, omeprazole effectively blocks the production of gastric acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This suppression affects both basal (resting) acid secretion and acid secretion stimulated by food or other factors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The effect lasts for an extended period (up to 24 hours or more) because new proton pumps must be synthesized by the cell to restore acid production. This irreversible binding is what makes PPIs so effective at providing sustained acid suppression.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Dienogest
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Dienogest is a synthetic progestin, which is a type of medication that mimics the effects of the natural female hormone progesterone in the body. It has a high affinity for progesterone receptors and also exhibits antiandrogenic properties.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Dienogest is primarily indicated for the following uses:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Treatment of endometriosis</b>: It is used to manage pelvic pain associated with endometriosis.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Contraception</b>: It is used in combination with ethinylestradiol as a component of birth control pills.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Heavy menstrual bleeding</b>: When combined with estradiol valerate, it is used to treat heavy menstrual bleeding (menorrhagia) in women who also choose oral contraception.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Menopausal symptoms</b>: In some countries, it is used in combination with estradiol valerate for menopausal hormone therapy.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Acne</b>: When combined with ethinylestradiol, it can be used to treat moderate acne in women who also choose oral contraception.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The mechanism of action of Dienogest is multifaceted:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Progestogenic effect</b>: Dienogest binds to and activates progesterone receptors, leading to changes in the uterine lining (endometrium). In endometriosis, this causes the endometrial tissue to atrophy (shrink), which helps to alleviate pain and reduce lesions.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Suppression of hormones</b>: Dienogest moderately suppresses the production of estradiol (a type of estrogen), which reduces the trophic (growth-promoting) effects of estrogen on both normal and ectopic (out-of-place) endometrial tissue.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Antiandrogenic effect</b>: Dienogest has antiandrogenic properties, meaning it can block the effects of androgens (male hormones). This is beneficial in treating androgen-related conditions like acne.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ovulation suppression</b>: When used for contraception, it helps prevent ovulation, makes the cervical mucus thicker to block sperm, and alters the uterine lining to prevent a fertilized egg from implanting.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Alfuzosin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Alfuzosin is a medication that belongs to the class of drugs known as alpha-blockers. It is available as an extended-release tablet for oral use.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Alfuzosin is indicated for the treatment of the signs and symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), also known as an enlarged prostate, in men. Symptoms of BPH can include:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Difficulty urinating (hesitancy, weak stream, or dribbling)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Incomplete bladder emptying</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Painful urination</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Urinary frequency and urgency</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Alfuzosin works as a selective alpha-1 adrenergic antagonist. It functions by selectively blocking alpha-1 receptors, which are found in the smooth muscles of the prostate, bladder base, bladder neck, and prostatic urethra. By blocking these receptors, Alfuzosin causes the smooth muscles in these areas to relax. This relaxation reduces the resistance to urine flow, which in turn helps to improve urinary flow and relieve the symptoms of BPH.</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------
