PRODUCT: Ozowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Omeprazole Na, Lyophilized Powder is a <b>proton pump inhibitor (PPI)</b> provided in a sterile, freeze-dried powder form for intravenous (IV) administration. This formulation is primarily for use in a hospital setting for patients who are unable to take oral medications. It contains omeprazole sodium as the active ingredient and must be reconstituted with a suitable solvent before it can be administered.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indication</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This form of omeprazole is used as an alternative to oral therapy for the short-term treatment of various conditions. Its main indications include:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Including the healing of erosive esophagitis.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Peptic Ulcer Disease:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> To treat duodenal and gastric ulcers.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> A condition that causes the stomach to produce too much acid.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Prevention of upper gastrointestinal bleeding</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> in critically ill patients.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Omeprazole belongs to a class of medications that suppress stomach acid secretion. Its mechanism is <b>highly specific</b> and <b>irreversible</b>:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Prodrug:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Omeprazole is a <b>prodrug</b>, which means it's inactive until it's activated in the body. It is a weak base that is concentrated and converted to its active form in the highly acidic environment of the parietal cells in the stomach lining.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibition:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Once activated, omeprazole <b>covalently</b> binds to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme system</b>, which is also known as the <b>"proton pump."</b> This enzyme is the final step in the production of gastric acid.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Result:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By irreversibly blocking this pump, omeprazole effectively suppresses both basal (resting) and stimulated acid secretion, regardless of the stimulus. This provides potent and long-lasting relief from acid-related symptoms and allows damaged tissues to heal.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Aliskiren + Hydrochlorothiazide
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Aliskiren + Hydrochlorothiazide is a combination medication used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension). It contains two different drugs that work together to lower blood pressure. Aliskiren is the first in a class of drugs called direct renin inhibitors, while hydrochlorothiazide is a thiazide diuretic, or "water pill."</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This product is indicated for the treatment of hypertension to lower blood pressure. Lowering blood pressure helps reduce the risk of strokes, heart attacks, and other cardiovascular events. It is often used in patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with a single medication or as initial therapy in patients likely to need multiple drugs to reach their blood pressure goals.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This medication has a dual mechanism of action due to its two components:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Aliskiren:</b> It is a direct renin inhibitor. Renin is an enzyme that plays a crucial role in the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS), which regulates blood pressure. Aliskiren directly binds to renin and prevents it from converting angiotensinogen to angiotensin I. This inhibits the entire cascade, leading to a reduction in angiotensin II, a powerful vasoconstrictor. As a result, blood vessels relax and blood pressure decreases.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hydrochlorothiazide:</b> It is a thiazide diuretic. It works by inhibiting the sodium chloride co-transporter in the distal convoluted tubules of the kidneys. This prevents the reabsorption of sodium and chloride, causing the body to excrete more water and salt in the urine. This reduces the total blood volume and, consequently, lowers blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Aliskiren
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Aliskiren is the first in a class of drugs known as direct renin inhibitors. It is an orally active medication that helps lower blood pressure. It is available as a stand-alone treatment or in combination with other medications.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications:</b> The primary indication for Aliskiren is the treatment of essential hypertension (high blood pressure) in adults. By lowering blood pressure, it helps to reduce the risk of cardiovascular events such as strokes and heart attacks.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Aliskiren works by targeting the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS), a hormonal system that regulates blood pressure.</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It acts as a direct renin inhibitor, meaning it binds to and blocks the activity of the enzyme renin.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Renin's normal function is to convert a protein called angiotensinogen into angiotensin I.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">By inhibiting renin, Aliskiren prevents the formation of angiotensin I and, consequently, the subsequent production of angiotensin II, a powerful chemical that causes blood vessels to constrict and blood pressure to rise.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This action leads to vasodilation (the widening of blood vessels) and reduced fluid retention, which collectively helps to lower blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: O-Carb
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Omeprazole and Sodium Bicarbonate is a unique pharmaceutical formulation designed to provide both rapid and sustained relief from acid-related symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a dual-action product that combines a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) with a fast-acting antacid.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Omeprazole:</b> The PPI component, which blocks the production of stomach acid.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sodium Bicarbonate:</b> An alkaline compound that neutralizes existing stomach acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is typically formulated as an immediate-release powder or capsule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The sodium bicarbonate serves a dual purpose: it acts as an antacid to neutralize stomach acid for immediate relief and, more importantly, it protects the acid-sensitive omeprazole from degradation in the stomach, allowing it to be absorbed quickly and begin its long-term acid-suppressing action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is specifically indicated for conditions where both immediate relief and long-term acid control are needed. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Heartburn:</b> It is used for the treatment of frequent heartburn (two or more days a week). The sodium bicarbonate provides quick relief, while the omeprazole provides sustained acid suppression over 24 hours.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It is used for the treatment and maintenance of GERD and associated symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Healing of Erosive Esophagitis:</b> For the short-term healing of damage to the esophagus caused by acid reflux.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Hypersecretory Conditions:</b> Similar to omeprazole alone, it can be used for conditions like Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, where there is an excessive production of stomach acid.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the synergistic action of its two components:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Immediate Action (Sodium Bicarbonate):</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sodium bicarbonate (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>NaHCO3​) is a potent, fast-acting antacid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Upon entering the stomach, it reacts with hydrochloric acid (HCl) to produce carbon dioxide, water, and a neutral salt (sodium chloride). This neutralization reaction is: HCl+NaHCO3​→NaCl+H2​O+CO2​</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This action rapidly increases the gastric pH, providing immediate relief from heartburn and indigestion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This effect is why the combination is considered "immediate-release."<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Delayed and Sustained Action (Omeprazole):</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The sodium bicarbonate's most critical role in this formulation is to protect the omeprazole. Without it, the omeprazole would be degraded by stomach acid.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By neutralizing the stomach acid, the sodium bicarbonate creates a higher pH environment, which allows the omeprazole to pass safely into the small intestine where it is absorbed.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Once absorbed, the omeprazole, which is a <b>prodrug</b>, travels to the parietal cells in the stomach.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In the acidic environment of the parietal cells' secretory canaliculi, omeprazole is activated and irreversibly binds to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b> (the proton pump).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This irreversible inhibition of the proton pump blocks the final step of gastric acid secretion, providing a powerful and sustained reduction in acid production for over 24 hours.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the sodium bicarbonate provides a rapid-onset buffering effect to relieve symptoms and, at the same time, ensures that enough omeprazole survives to be absorbed and provide long-term acid control.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Eletriptan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Description:</b> Eletriptan is a selective serotonin receptor agonist (triptan) used to treat acute migraine headaches in adults, with or without aura. It is not intended to prevent migraines. It is a second-generation triptan that is often available in oral tablet form.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indication:</b> Eletriptan is specifically indicated for the acute treatment of migraine attacks. It should be taken as soon as a migraine headache begins, and is not effective if taken beforehand to prevent an attack.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The exact mechanism is not fully understood, but it is believed to work by binding to serotonin receptors, specifically 5-HT$<i>{1B}$ and 5-HT$</i>{1D}$ receptors, which are located on intracranial blood vessels and nerve endings. By activating these receptors, Eletriptan causes the following effects:</p>

	<ul>
		<li>
		<p><b>Vasoconstriction:</b> It constricts the dilated cranial arteries and veins that are thought to contribute to the pain of a migraine attack.</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>Inhibition of Neuropeptide Release:</b> It inhibits the release of pro-inflammatory neuropeptides from nerve endings in the trigeminal system, which helps to reduce the pain and associated symptoms of a migraine.</p>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Fluvoxamine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Fluvoxamine is an antidepressant medication that falls under the class of selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs). It is a psychiatric drug primarily used to treat major depressive disorder and obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). It works by restoring the balance of a natural substance (serotonin) in the brain.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Major Depressive Disorder</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Social Anxiety Disorder</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Panic Disorder</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Fluvoxamine works by selectively inhibiting the reuptake of serotonin by neurons in the central nervous system. This increases the concentration of serotonin in the synaptic cleft, thereby enhancing serotonin's neurotransmission and leading to an improvement in mood and reduction of obsessive-compulsive symptoms.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Lansowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lansoprazole Sodium, Lyophilized Powder is a sterile, freeze-dried formulation of the proton pump inhibitor (PPI) drug, lansoprazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The term "lyophilized powder" indicates that the drug has been converted into a stable, dry powder through a freeze-drying process.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This formulation is specifically designed for intravenous (IV) administration.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Like other PPIs, lansoprazole is highly unstable in acidic environments.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Administering it as a lyophilized powder that is reconstituted just before use prevents its degradation and allows for its safe and effective delivery directly into the bloodstream, bypassing the oral route.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This injectable form of lansoprazole is used in clinical settings where patients are unable to take oral medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This often occurs in hospital settings for critically ill patients or those undergoing surgery. The main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Short-term treatment of GERD:</b> Used for patients with gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with or without erosive esophagitis when oral therapy is not feasible.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prophylaxis and treatment of stress ulcers:</b> In critically ill patients, it is used to prevent the development of stress-related mucosal bleeding and ulcers.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Maintenance of hemostasis in peptic ulcer bleeding:</b> It is used as an adjunct to therapeutic endoscopy to maintain a high gastric pH, which is crucial for clot stabilization and preventing re-bleeding from gastric or duodenal ulcers.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison syndrome:</b> It can be used to manage this condition in patients who cannot take oral lansoprazole.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of lansoprazole sodium is the same as that of oral lansoprazole. It works by irreversibly inhibiting the final step of gastric acid production.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation:</b> Lansoprazole is a <b>prodrug</b> that is inactive in its original form. After being administered intravenously, it travels through the bloodstream and accumulates in the highly acidic secretory canaliculi of the gastric parietal cells.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to Active Form:</b> In this acidic environment, lansoprazole undergoes a chemical transformation into its active sulfenamide form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Binding:</b> This activated form then forms a permanent, irreversible covalent bond with the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, which is also known as the "proton pump." The proton pump is the enzyme responsible for pumping hydrogen ions (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>H+) into the stomach lumen, a key step in acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Acid Production:</b> By irreversibly blocking the proton pump, lansoprazole effectively stops both basal (resting) and stimulated acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The acid-suppressing effect is sustained and powerful because the cell cannot resume acid production until new proton pumps are synthesized, a process that can take up to 24 hours.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This mechanism ensures that lansoprazole provides powerful and prolonged acid suppression, which is essential for the treatment and prevention of many acid-related diseases.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Fosinopril
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Fosinopril is an angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitor. It is a prodrug, meaning it is an inactive compound that is converted into its active form, fosinoprilat, in the body after it is administered.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Fosinopril is primarily used for the treatment of:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">High blood pressure (hypertension).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Heart failure, often in combination with other medications.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It may also be used off-label to slow the progression of kidney disease in people with diabetes.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Fosinoprilat, the active metabolite, works by competitively inhibiting ACE. This enzyme is responsible for converting angiotensin I to angiotensin II, a powerful vasoconstrictor. By inhibiting this conversion, Fosinopril causes blood vessels to relax and widen, which lowers blood pressure and makes it easier for the heart to pump blood. This action also reduces the secretion of aldosterone, which helps the body excrete excess sodium and water.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Finasteride
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Finasteride is a synthetic 4-azasteroid compound that is a specific inhibitor of steroid 5-alpha-reductase, an intracellular enzyme that converts androgen testosterone into 5-alpha-dihydrotestosterone (DHT).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Finasteride is indicated for the treatment of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) and male pattern hair loss (androgenetic alopecia) in men.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Finasteride works by inhibiting the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase. This enzyme is responsible for converting testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT). By blocking this conversion, finasteride reduces the level of DHT in the prostate and scalp, leading to a decrease in prostate size and an increase in hair growth.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Wiber
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rabeprazole is a synthetic, substituted benzimidazole that belongs to the class of medications known as proton pump inhibitors (PPIs).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Like other PPIs, it works by reducing the amount of acid produced in the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Rabeprazole is supplied as an enteric-coated tablet or capsule to protect the drug from degradation by stomach acid, ensuring it reaches the small intestine for absorption.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rabeprazole is used for the treatment of various acid-related conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It is used for the short-term treatment of symptoms of GERD and for the healing of erosive or ulcerative GERD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also used for the long-term maintenance of healing.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Duodenal Ulcers:</b> It is used for the treatment and healing of active duodenal ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b><i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (H. pylori) eradication:</b> Rabeprazole is a key component of multi-drug regimens, typically with two antibiotics, to eradicate <i>H.</i><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><i> pylori</i> infection, which is a major cause of peptic ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</b> It is used for the long-term management of this rare hypersecretory condition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rabeprazole's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the enzyme that pumps acid into the stomach.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works similarly to other PPIs but with a few key differences in its activation and metabolism.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation:</b> Rabeprazole is a <b>prodrug</b>, meaning it is inactive until it is activated.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After being absorbed from the small intestine, it enters the bloodstream and travels to the acid-secreting <b>parietal cells</b> in the stomach lining.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Conversion to Active Form:</b> In the highly acidic secretory canaliculi of these cells, rabeprazole is converted into its active form, a sulfenamide.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Inhibition:</b> This activated form then forms a permanent, irreversible covalent bond with the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This pump is the final and most crucial step in the secretion of gastric acid.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cessation of Acid Production:</b> By permanently blocking the proton pump, rabeprazole effectively prevents the production and secretion of gastric acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This suppression affects both basal (resting) acid secretion and acid secretion stimulated by food or other factors.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A notable feature of rabeprazole is its faster onset of action compared to some other PPIs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It has a higher pKa (a measure of acidity) than omeprazole and lansoprazole, which allows it to concentrate more readily in the acidic environment of the parietal cells, leading to faster activation and a more rapid onset of acid suppression. Additionally, its metabolism is less dependent on certain cytochrome P450 enzymes (like CYP2C19), which can result in more consistent and predictable drug levels across different patient populations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Amlodipine + Perindopril
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> This is a combination drug that contains two active ingredients: Amlodipine and Perindopril. It is used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension) and stable coronary artery disease. Amlodipine is a calcium channel blocker, and Perindopril is an angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitor.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is primarily indicated for the treatment of essential hypertension and stable coronary artery disease in patients who require treatment with both amlodipine and perindopril.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Amlodipine:</b> As a calcium channel blocker, it works by relaxing the smooth muscles of the heart and blood vessel walls. It dilates arteries, which lowers blood pressure and improves blood flow.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Perindopril:</b> As an ACE inhibitor, it prevents the conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor. This leads to the relaxation of blood vessels, which lowers blood pressure. It also reduces the secretion of aldosterone, which helps the body excrete sodium and water. The combined action of both drugs provides a synergistic effect in lowering blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Repp-40
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Pantoprazole sodium is a substituted benzimidazole and a member of the proton pump inhibitor (PPI) class of drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "sodium" (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>Na) in its name refers to the salt form of the drug, which is used to increase its solubility and stability, making it suitable for both oral and intravenous (IV) formulations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Like other PPIs, pantoprazole is a prodrug that is activated in the body to exert its effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Pantoprazole sodium is used for the treatment of various conditions caused by excessive stomach acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Erosive Esophagitis:</b> Used for the short-term healing and long-term maintenance of erosive esophagitis, which is damage to the esophagus caused by gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>GERD (Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Used for the treatment of GERD and associated symptoms like heartburn and acid regurgitation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</b> Used for the long-term management of this rare hypersecretory condition.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Risk reduction of rebleeding from gastric or duodenal ulcers:</b> The intravenous formulation is often used in a hospital setting for patients who have had a bleeding ulcer to maintain a high gastric pH and prevent rebleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prophylaxis of stress ulcers:</b> In critically ill patients, it is used to prevent the formation of stress-related gastric ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Pantoprazole's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the enzyme responsible for the final step of gastric acid production.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation:</b> Pantoprazole is an inactive <b>prodrug</b> when administered.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the acid-secreting <b>parietal cells</b> in the stomach lining. In the highly acidic secretory canaliculi of these cells, the pantoprazole molecule is converted into its active form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irreversible Inhibition:</b> The activated form of pantoprazole then forms a permanent, irreversible covalent bond with the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, which is also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Acid Secretion:</b> The proton pump is the final step in the acid secretion process, responsible for pumping hydrogen ions (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>H+) into the stomach lumen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By permanently inactivating the proton pump, pantoprazole effectively stops the production of gastric acid, regardless of the stimulus (e.g., food, histamine, or gastrin).<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sustained Effect:</b> The irreversible binding means that the acid-suppressing effect persists much longer than the drug's short half-life in the bloodstream. The body cannot resume acid production until it synthesizes new proton pumps, which takes approximately 24 hours. This is why a once-daily dose is sufficient to provide a powerful and prolonged reduction in stomach acid.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A key feature of pantoprazole is its high selectivity for the parietal cells' acidic environment.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It remains stable at a higher pH than some other PPIs, which minimizes its activation outside of the target cells and reduces the risk of drug interactions.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Cindra
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cinitapride is a substituted benzamide drug that acts as a gastroprokinetic agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a dual-action compound that works by stimulating gastrointestinal motility and is used to treat disorders related to slow gastric emptying.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Cinitapride is not available in all countries, with its use being more common in Europe and Latin America.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cinitapride is primarily indicated for the treatment of gastrointestinal motility disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main uses include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptomatic treatment of dyspepsia:</b> It is used to relieve symptoms like discomfort, fullness, and bloating in functional dyspepsia (non-ulcer dyspepsia), which is not caused by an ulcer or other obvious disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Delayed gastric emptying:</b> It is used to improve gastric emptying and reduce symptoms associated with slow stomach emptying.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It can be used as an adjunct treatment for GERD by improving esophageal clearance and gastric emptying, thereby reducing reflux episodes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cinitapride's mechanism of action is based on its dual agonist and antagonist effects on two key neurotransmitter receptors in the gastrointestinal tract: serotonin and dopamine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual action makes it a highly effective prokinetic agent.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Serotonin 5-HT4​ Receptor Agonism:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cinitapride is a partial agonist of the serotonin 5-HT4​ receptors, which are located on the smooth muscle cells and enteric neurons of the GI tract.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">When cinitapride binds to and activates these receptors, it stimulates the release of acetylcholine (ACh).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acetylcholine is a major neurotransmitter in the enteric nervous system that promotes muscle contraction.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The increased release of ACh enhances the peristaltic contractions of the stomach and intestines, leading to improved gastric emptying and faster transit of food through the GI tract.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dopamine D2 Receptor Antagonism:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cinitapride also acts as an antagonist of the dopamine D2 receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dopamine typically has an inhibitory effect on gastrointestinal motility.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking these D2 receptors, cinitapride removes this inhibitory influence.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This action further enhances the prokinetic effect by allowing acetylcholine to exert its full contractile effect on the smooth muscles of the GI tract.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the combined effect of stimulating pro-motility receptors (5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT4​) and blocking inhibitory receptors (D2) results in a potent and coordinated increase in GI motility, which helps to alleviate symptoms of dyspepsia and other motility disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
