PRODUCT: Ertugliflozin and Metformin Hydrochloride
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Ertugliflozin and Metformin Hydrochloride is a combination medicine that contains two different types of anti-diabetic drugs. It is used as part of a treatment plan with diet and exercise to help improve blood sugar control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This medication is indicated for adults with type 2 diabetes who are not adequately controlled on metformin alone, or who are already taking both ertugliflozin and metformin as separate tablets.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The drug works through the complementary actions of its two components:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ertugliflozin:</b> This is a sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor. It works by blocking the SGLT2 protein in the kidneys, which is responsible for reabsorbing most of the glucose from the blood back into the body. By inhibiting this protein, ertugliflozin causes the kidneys to excrete more glucose in the urine, thereby lowering blood sugar levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Metformin:</b> This is a biguanide-class drug. It works by decreasing the amount of glucose produced by the liver, reducing the absorption of glucose from the stomach, and helping the body use insulin more effectively.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Oseltamivir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Oseltamivir is an antiviral medication that belongs to a class of drugs called neuraminidase inhibitors. It is used to treat and prevent infections caused by the influenza virus (influenza A and influenza B), commonly known as the flu. It is available in capsule and oral suspension forms.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Oseltamivir is indicated for the treatment of acute, uncomplicated influenza in patients who have been symptomatic for no more than two days. It is also used for the prophylaxis (prevention) of influenza in certain populations, such as those who have been in close contact with an infected individual or during a community outbreak.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Oseltamivir is an inactive prodrug that is converted in the body to its active form, oseltamivir carboxylate. This active metabolite works by selectively inhibiting the neuraminidase enzyme, which is found on the surface of influenza viruses. This enzyme is essential for the virus to release new viral particles from infected host cells and to spread the infection throughout the body. By inhibiting this enzyme, oseltamivir prevents the virus from multiplying and spreading, allowing the body's immune system to fight off the infection.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Estradiol + Dydrogesterone
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Estradiol + Dydrogesterone is a combination medication that contains two female hormones: estradiol (an estrogen) and dydrogesterone (a progestogen). It is a form of hormone replacement therapy (HRT) designed to alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This combination drug is primarily indicated for hormone replacement therapy (HRT) to treat:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Symptoms of estrogen deficiency:</b> Hot flashes, night sweats, vaginal dryness, and other menopausal symptoms.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Prevention of osteoporosis:</b> It can be used to prevent postmenopausal osteoporosis in women at high risk of fractures who are intolerant to other osteoporosis medications.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Estradiol:</b> This is an estrogen that replaces the declining levels of natural estrogen in the body during and after menopause. It acts on estrogen receptors in various tissues, including the brain, blood vessels, and bone, to alleviate menopausal symptoms and prevent bone loss.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Dydrogesterone:</b> This is a progestogen that is included to protect the uterine lining (endometrium) from the proliferative effects of the estradiol. When estrogen is administered alone in women with an intact uterus, it can cause the endometrial lining to thicken, increasing the risk of endometrial cancer. Dydrogesterone counteracts this effect, causing the endometrium to shed, which significantly reduces the risk.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Dopamine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul>
	<li>
	<p><b>Description:</b> Dopamine is a catecholamine and a precursor to norepinephrine and epinephrine. It is a sympathomimetic agent, meaning it mimics the effects of sympathetic nervous system stimulation. It is available as a solution for intravenous injection.</p>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Indication:</b> Dopamine is used to treat various conditions, including:</p>

	<ul>
		<li>
		<p>Shock (septic shock, cardiogenic shock, and traumatic shock)</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p>Heart failure</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p>Hypotension (low blood pressure)</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p>Bradycardia (slow heart rate)</p>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Dopamine's effects are dose-dependent:</p>

	<ul>
		<li>
		<p><b>Low doses (0.5-2 mcg/kg/min):</b> Primarily stimulates dopaminergic receptors, leading to vasodilation in the renal, mesenteric, coronary, and cerebral arteries. This increases blood flow to the kidneys, which can increase urine output.</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>Intermediate doses (2-10 mcg/kg/min):</b> Stimulates beta-1 adrenergic receptors in the heart, leading to increased heart rate and myocardial contractility (the force of heart muscle contraction). This improves cardiac output.</p>
		</li>
		<li>
		<p><b>High doses (&gt;10 mcg/kg/min):</b> Primarily stimulates alpha-1 adrenergic receptors, causing vasoconstriction (narrowing of blood vessels) in the peripheral arteries. This increases systemic vascular resistance and blood pressure.</p>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li>
	<p>&nbsp;</p>
	</li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Galantamine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Galantamine is an acetylcholinesterase inhibitor (AChEI). It works in the brain by increasing the levels of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that is important for memory, learning, and other cognitive functions.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Galantamine is used for the treatment of mild to moderate dementia of the Alzheimer's type.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Galantamine works through a dual mechanism. First, it is a competitive and reversible inhibitor of acetylcholinesterase, the enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine in the synaptic cleft. By inhibiting this enzyme, it increases the concentration of acetylcholine available to bind to receptors. Second, it is an allosteric potentiating ligand (APL) at nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, meaning it binds to these receptors at a different site than acetylcholine, which enhances the receptor's response to acetylcholine.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Venospa
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> This product is a fixed-dose combination tablet containing low-dose aspirin and vonoprazan fumarate. It is designed to be a single medication for patients who need the antiplatelet effects of aspirin but are also at a high risk of developing gastric or duodenal ulcers, a common side effect of long-term aspirin use. Vonoprazan's inclusion aims to improve patient adherence by reducing the need for two separate prescriptions and to effectively prevent the recurrence of ulcers.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This drug is indicated for the prevention of thrombosis and embolism in patients with a history of or who are at risk for conditions such as:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Angina pectoris</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Myocardial infarction (heart attack)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ischemic cerebrovascular disease (e.g., transient ischemic attack, cerebral infarction)</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It is also used to prevent thrombosis/embolism after coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG) or percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA). A crucial aspect of its indication is its use in patients who have a history of peptic ulcers and require long-term low-dose aspirin therapy.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The product's effect is due to the combined action of its two active ingredients:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Aspirin:</b> Aspirin is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that works as an antiplatelet agent. Its mechanism involves the irreversible inhibition of the enzyme cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1) in platelets. By blocking COX-1, aspirin prevents the formation of thromboxane A2 (</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">TXA2​</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">), a powerful molecule that causes platelets to clump together and form blood clots. This action suppresses platelet aggregation and helps to prevent blood clots from forming.</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vonoprazan:</b> Vonoprazan is a first-in-class potassium-competitive acid blocker (P-CAB). It works by reversibly and competitively binding to the potassium (</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">K+</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">) binding site of the </span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">H+,K+</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">-ATPase (the "proton pump") in the stomach's parietal cells. This action effectively blocks gastric acid secretion, regardless of whether the pump is actively secreting acid. Unlike proton pump inhibitors (PPIs), Vonoprazan does not require an acidic environment to become active, allowing for a faster and more potent acid-suppressing effect. Its role in this combination product is to counteract the ulcer-causing effects of aspirin by significantly reducing stomach acid production.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Miralax 3350
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination product is a balanced electrolyte solution containing <b>Polyethylene Glycol (PEG)</b>, a non-absorbable polymer, along with key electrolytes: <b>Sodium Chloride (NaCl)</b>, <b>Potassium Chloride (KCl)</b>, and <b>Sodium Bicarbonate (NaHCO3​)</b>. This specific formulation is designed to be reconstituted with water to create an oral solution.</span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The primary component, PEG, is a potent osmotic laxative.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The added electrolytes are crucial for maintaining the body's fluid and electrolyte balance during the process of bowel evacuation, preventing dehydration and electrolyte imbalances.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is primarily used for two main purposes:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bowel Cleansing:</b> Its most common use is for complete bowel evacuation and cleansing before colonoscopy, surgery, or other medical procedures that require a clean colon.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Constipation:</b> It can also be used as a treatment for chronic constipation, particularly in cases that have not responded to a high-fiber diet or other laxatives.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct roles of its components, working together to cleanse the bowel while maintaining the body's physiological balance.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Polyethylene Glycol (PEG) - Osmotic Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">PEG is a long-chain polymer that is not absorbed by the intestines.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">When taken orally, it passes through the digestive tract, drawing large volumes of water into the lumen of the bowel via osmosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The increased volume of fluid in the intestines softens the stool and promotes a strong peristaltic reflex, which leads to a rapid and thorough evacuation of the bowel.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Electrolytes (NaCl, KCl, NaHCO3​) - Balancing Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">During rapid and extensive bowel movements, the body can lose significant amounts of electrolytes and water, which could lead to dehydration and imbalances.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Sodium Chloride, Potassium Chloride, and Sodium Bicarbonate in the solution is formulated to be iso-osmotic.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By providing these electrolytes in a balanced concentration, the solution ensures that the osmotic pressure inside the bowel is similar to that of the body's fluids.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This prevents the net absorption or secretion of electrolytes from the body into the bowel, thereby minimizing the risk of dehydration and electrolyte disturbances (such as low sodium or low potassium) during the cleansing process.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the PEG acts as the primary osmotic agent to induce a powerful laxative effect, while the electrolytes prevent the complications of dehydration and electrolyte imbalance that can arise from rapid fluid loss. This dual mechanism ensures that the bowel is effectively cleansed in a physiologically safe manner.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Rixawin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rifaximin is a synthetic, non-systemic antibiotic that belongs to the class of drugs known as rifamycins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike other rifamycins that are used for systemic infections (e.g., tuberculosis), rifaximin is unique because it is minimally absorbed from the gastrointestinal (GI) tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means that after oral administration, it remains highly concentrated within the gut lumen, where it exerts its antibacterial effects with minimal systemic exposure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Due to its poor systemic absorption, rifaximin is primarily indicated for the treatment and prevention of conditions caused by bacteria within the GI tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Traveler's Diarrhea:</b> It is used to treat traveler's diarrhea caused by non-invasive strains of <i>Escherichia coli</i> in adults and children 12 years of age and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irritable Bowel Syndrome with Diarrhea (IBS-D):</b> It is used for the treatment of IBS with diarrhea in adults.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its action on the gut flora helps to alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and urgency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hepatic Encephalopathy:</b> It is used to reduce the risk of overt hepatic encephalopathy (HE) recurrence in adults.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> HE is a decline in brain function that occurs as a result of severe liver disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Rifaximin works by reducing the production of ammonia and other neurotoxins by gut bacteria, which are implicated in the development of HE.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Rifaximin's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit bacterial protein synthesis, but its effectiveness is tied to its unique pharmacokinetic property.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting Bacterial RNA Polymerase:</b> Rifaximin works by binding to the beta-subunit of the bacterial <b>DNA-dependent RNA polymerase enzyme</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Transcription:</b> By binding to this enzyme, rifaximin blocks the process of <b>transcription</b>, which is the first step in bacterial protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action prevents the bacterial cell from creating essential proteins and enzymes, leading to the cessation of bacterial growth and proliferation (bacteriostatic effect) or, at higher concentrations, bacterial death (bactericidal effect).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Localized Action:</b> The most crucial aspect of rifaximin's mechanism is its <b>minimal systemic absorption</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After oral administration, over 99% of the dose remains in the gastrointestinal tract. This high luminal concentration allows the drug to effectively target and eliminate susceptible bacteria within the gut while having a very low risk of systemic side effects or contributing to widespread antibiotic resistance in other parts of the body.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Octite
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dioctahedral smectite is a type of natural, refined clay that is classified as an absorbent and adsorbent agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly purified form of montmorillonite, a mineral clay with a layered structure. When mixed with water, it forms a fine suspension with a high viscosity.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Due to its unique structure, it has a strong capacity to bind to and absorb various substances, including toxins, bacteria, and viruses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dioctahedral smectite is used for the symptomatic treatment of various gastrointestinal disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute and Chronic Diarrhea:</b> It is commonly used for the symptomatic treatment of acute diarrhea in both adults and children.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It helps to reduce the frequency and duration of diarrheal episodes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS):</b> It can be used to alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain and bloating in individuals with IBS, particularly the diarrhea-predominant type.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Esophagitis and Gastrointestinal Disorders:</b> It is sometimes used for the symptomatic treatment of pain associated with esophagitis, gastritis, and other gastrointestinal disorders, as it can form a protective layer over the inflamed mucosa.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of dioctahedral smectite is purely physical and mechanical. It does not act systemically or by interfering with biological processes in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Adsorbent and Absorbent Properties:</b> The key to its action is its unique layered, dioctahedral structure. This large surface area gives it a high capacity for adsorption (binding to surfaces) and absorption (taking in substances).</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding of Toxins and Pathogens:</b> In the digestive tract, dioctahedral smectite binds to and immobilizes various substances that can cause diarrhea, including:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Toxins:</b> It adsorbs bacterial toxins and other irritating substances.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Viruses and Bacteria:</b> It can bind to viruses and bacteria, preventing them from adhering to the intestinal lining and causing infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Water:</b> It absorbs a significant amount of water, which helps to increase stool consistency and reduce the fluidity of diarrheal stool.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Protective Barrier:</b> As it passes through the gastrointestinal tract, dioctahedral smectite forms a protective coating or barrier over the intestinal and esophageal mucosa. This barrier helps to protect the underlying mucous membrane from irritation and damage caused by stomach acid, toxins, and other inflammatory agents.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Systemic Absorption:</b> Because dioctahedral smectite is not absorbed into the bloodstream, it is considered safe for use in various populations, including children. It is eliminated from the body unchanged, along with the substances it has bound to.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, dioctahedral smectite works by physically binding to harmful agents and forming a protective barrier, thereby reducing the cause and symptoms of gastrointestinal irritation and diarrhea.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Aprewin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aprepitant is an antiemetic drug that belongs to a class of medications called <b>neurokinin-1 (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>NK1</b><b>​</b><b>) receptor antagonists</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a synthetic substance that works by blocking the action of a specific neurotransmitter in the central nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral capsules and as a prodrug for intravenous administration (fosaprepitant).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aprepitant is used in adults and children for the <b>prevention</b> of nausea and vomiting, particularly those associated with highly or moderately emetogenic (vomit-inducing) therapies.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not intended to treat existing nausea or vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting (CINV):</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Aprepitant is used in combination with other antiemetic drugs, such as a serotonin 5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT3​ receptor antagonist and a corticosteroid (e.g., dexamethasone), for the prevention of both acute (within 24 hours) and delayed (1-5 days after) nausea and vomiting associated with highly emetogenic cancer chemotherapy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV):</b> It is also used for the prevention of nausea and vomiting following surgery.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aprepitant's mechanism of action is based on its ability to block the effects of a specific neuropeptide called <b>Substance P</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the NK1​ Receptor:</b> Aprepitant is a selective antagonist of the <b>neurokinin-1 (NK1​) receptor</b>. These receptors are found in various parts of the body, including the central nervous system, where they play a key role in the emetic reflex.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Substance P:</b> The natural ligand (the substance that binds to and activates) for the <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>NK1​ receptor is Substance P. Substance P is a neuropeptide that transmits signals of nausea and vomiting to the brain, particularly in the brainstem's "vomiting center" and chemoreceptor trigger zone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of the Emetic Reflex:</b> By blocking the NK1​ receptors, aprepitant prevents Substance P from binding and activating them. This action effectively inhibits the nausea and vomiting signal transmission from the gut and brain to the vomiting center.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Synergistic Effect:</b> Aprepitant is particularly effective because it works on a different pathway from other common antiemetics like serotonin 5-HT3​ receptor antagonists (e.g., ondansetron) and corticosteroids. By combining these different mechanisms, the overall antiemetic effect is enhanced, providing more comprehensive control over both acute and delayed nausea and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, aprepitant's action is to block the Substance P/<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>NK1​ pathway, thereby interrupting a key signaling route for the emetic reflex, making it a powerful tool for preventing chemotherapy- and surgery-induced nausea and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon><br />

PRODUCT: Tributin SR
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Trimebutine Maleate is a synthetic drug that acts as a gastrointestinal motility regulator.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike other motility drugs that only stimulate or inhibit contractions, trimebutine has a unique ability to both increase and decrease muscle contractions, depending on the current state of the bowel. It is often referred to as a "prokinetic agent" or "antispasmodic" depending on the context, but its main function is to normalize gut motility.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Trimebutine Maleate is primarily used for the symptomatic treatment of various functional gastrointestinal disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS):</b> It is used to relieve a range of IBS symptoms, including abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea, and constipation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dyspepsia:</b> It can be used for the symptomatic treatment of functional dyspepsia, which is characterized by stomach discomfort, bloating, and early satiety.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-operative ileus:</b> It is sometimes used to accelerate the return of normal gut function after surgery.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other motility disorders:</b> It is also used to manage other conditions with abnormal gastrointestinal motility, such as non-cardiac chest pain of esophageal origin.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Trimebutine Maleate has a complex and unique mechanism of action that makes it a true motility regulator rather than a simple prokinetic or antispasmodic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary action is mediated through its effects on opioid receptors in the enteric nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Opioid Receptor Modulation:</b> Trimebutine is a non-selective agonist of peripheral <b>opioid receptors</b>, particularly the mu (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>μ), delta (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>δ), and kappa (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>κ) receptors located on enteric neurons and smooth muscle cells of the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dual Regulatory Effect:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In states of hypomotility (slow movement):</b> Trimebutine's agonist effect on these receptors can stimulate peristalsis, thereby increasing the speed of gut transit. This helps to alleviate symptoms of constipation.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In states of hypermotility (fast movement):</b> Trimebutine's effect on these receptors, particularly the delta and kappa receptors, can also reduce muscle contractions, thereby slowing down gut transit. This helps to relieve symptoms of diarrhea and abdominal spasms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Calcium Channel Modulation:</b> In addition to its opioid receptor activity, trimebutine has been shown to modulate calcium channels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By influencing the flow of calcium ions into smooth muscle cells, it can further regulate muscle contraction and relaxation, providing an additional layer to its antispasmodic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In essence, trimebutine works as a "gastrointestinal pacemaker," normalizing the disordered motility that is characteristic of functional bowel disorders. Its ability to act on both slow and fast gut movements, along with its lack of central nervous system effects, makes it a unique and valuable tool for managing a variety of gastrointestinal symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Attapulgite
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Attapulgite is a natural, hydrated magnesium aluminum phyllosilicate mineral that is a type of clay.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is classified as an <b>oral adsorbent</b> and <b>anti-diarrheal agent</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Due to its high surface area and porous, fibrous structure, it has a strong capacity to bind to and adsorb various substances.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Attapulgite is not absorbed from the gastrointestinal (GI) tract and is eliminated from the body unchanged.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Attapulgite is primarily indicated for the symptomatic treatment of acute diarrhea. It is used to:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduce the frequency of bowel movements</b> in cases of non-specific diarrhea, including traveler's diarrhea.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Improve stool consistency</b> and reduce the fluidity of loose stools.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Relieve abdominal cramps</b> and discomfort associated with diarrhea.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is important to note that attapulgite provides symptomatic relief and does not treat the underlying cause of the diarrhea, such as a bacterial or viral infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of attapulgite is purely physical and mechanical. It does not act systemically and is not a pharmacological agent. Its therapeutic effect is based on its adsorbent properties.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>High Adsorptive Capacity:</b> Attapulgite's unique layered and porous structure gives it a very large surface area.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This allows it to physically bind to (adsorb) various substances in the lumen of the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding of Toxins and Pathogens:</b> In the GI tract, attapulgite binds to bacterial toxins, viruses, and other irritants that can cause diarrhea.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By adsorbing these substances, it prevents them from acting on the intestinal lining.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding of Water:</b> Attapulgite has a high capacity to absorb water.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By soaking up excess water in the gut, it helps to solidify the stool and reduce the frequency and fluid volume of bowel movements.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Formation of a Protective Barrier:</b> The drug may also form a protective coating over the intestinal mucosa, shielding it from further irritation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, attapulgite works by physically binding to and removing harmful agents and excess water from the gastrointestinal tract, thereby providing symptomatic relief from diarrhea.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Since it is not absorbed, it does not cause systemic side effects.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Combo Kit
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Clarithromycin, Metronidazole, and Omeprazole is a well-established and highly effective treatment regimen known as <b>Triple Therapy</b> for the eradication of <i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (<i>H. pylori</i>) infection. This regimen combines two potent antibiotics with a proton pump inhibitor (PPI).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The three drugs work together synergistically to eliminate the bacteria and promote the healing of associated gastrointestinal ulcers. This combination is typically taken for a duration of 7 to 14 days.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is specifically indicated for the treatment of <b>gastric and duodenal ulcers</b> caused by an infection with <i>Helicobacter pylori</i>. Eradicating the <i>H. pylori</i> bacteria is crucial for the long-term healing of ulcers and for preventing their recurrence.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this combination relies on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of each of its three components:</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Omeprazole (Proton Pump Inhibitor)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Omeprazole's primary role is to inhibit the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b> (the proton pump), which is responsible for the final step of gastric acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Triple Therapy:</b> By suppressing stomach acid, omeprazole achieves two critical goals:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It creates a more favorable, less acidic environment for the antibiotics (Clarithromycin and Metronidazole) to function effectively. Many antibiotics are less stable and less effective in highly acidic conditions.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It promotes the healing of peptic ulcers that were caused by the <i>H. pylori</i> infection. A less acidic environment allows the damaged mucosal lining to repair itself.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Clarithromycin (Macrolide Antibiotic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Clarithromycin works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It binds to the <b>23S ribosomal RNA (rRNA)</b> of the <b>50S ribosomal subunit</b>, which prevents the ribosome from elongating the peptide chain and halts the production of essential bacterial proteins.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Triple Therapy:</b> Clarithromycin is a potent antibiotic against <i>H. pylori</i> and is a core component of the regimen.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">3. Metronidazole (Nitroimidazole Antibiotic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b><sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline>Mechanism:</b> Metronidazole is an antibiotic and antiprotozoal agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a prodrug that is reduced in anaerobic bacteria by bacterial enzymes. This reduction process creates a toxic compound that damages the bacteria's DNA, leading to cell death.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Triple Therapy:</b> Metronidazole is highly effective against the anaerobic environment where <i>H.</i><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><i> pylori</i> thrives. It provides a secondary antimicrobial action and is often used as an alternative to amoxicillin in patients who are allergic to penicillin.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, Omeprazole creates a less hostile environment for the bacteria and promotes ulcer healing, while Clarithromycin and Metronidazole work together to kill the bacteria from two different angles. The combined action of these three drugs dramatically increases the success rate of <i>H. pylori</i> eradication and prevents ulcer recurrence.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Windexx
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) that is the <b>R-enantiomer</b> of the racemic compound lansoprazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The term "EC Pellets" refers to its pharmaceutical formulation as enteric-coated (EC) pellets.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These pellets are designed with a unique <b>dual delayed-release</b> system: some pellets are coated to release the drug at a higher pH (e.g., in the proximal small intestine) and others are coated to release the drug at a lower pH (e.g., in the distal small intestine).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This formulation is designed to provide prolonged acid suppression over a 24-hour period.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole is used to treat various acid-related conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications are similar to other PPIs but its dual-release formulation makes it particularly effective for:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Healing of all grades of erosive esophagitis:</b> It is used for the short-term healing of damage to the esophagus caused by gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Maintenance of healed erosive esophagitis:</b> It is indicated for the long-term maintenance of healing in patients who have completed therapy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptomatic non-erosive GERD:</b> It is used to treat heartburn associated with GERD in patients without erosive esophagitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole's mechanism of action is two-fold: it involves the drug's inherent ability to inhibit acid production and the unique pharmacokinetic advantage of its dual-release formulation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of the Proton Pump:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole is a <b>prodrug</b> that is absorbed into the bloodstream from the small intestine.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It then travels to the parietal cells in the stomach lining, where it is activated in the acidic environment of the secretory canaliculi.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The activated form of dexlansoprazole <b>irreversibly binds</b> to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This pump is the final step in the gastric acid secretion process. By permanently blocking it, dexlansoprazole effectively stops the production of gastric acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dual Delayed-Release Formulation:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The drug's pellets are manufactured with two distinct enteric coatings.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The first coating dissolves at a higher pH, allowing for an initial release of the drug in the proximal small intestine within a few hours. This provides rapid acid suppression.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The second coating requires a lower pH to dissolve, resulting in a second, later release of the drug in the distal small intestine, approximately 4 to 5 hours after the first release.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual-release mechanism creates two separate plasma concentration peaks, which provides a longer duration of effective acid suppression and maintains a high gastric pH for a longer period compared to a single-release PPI.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This can be particularly beneficial for patients with nocturnal GERD or those with incomplete symptom control on a single-release PPI.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>
