HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole Dual Delayed Release Pellets is a specific pharmaceutical formulation of the proton pump inhibitor (PPI) dexlansoprazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The term "Eq: Dexlansoprazole" means that the pellets contain an amount of the drug equivalent to the stated dosage of dexlansoprazole. This formulation is characterized by its unique <b>dual delayed-release</b> delivery system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The pellets are engineered with two different enteric coatings, each designed to dissolve at a different pH level.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This allows for two separate releases of the drug in the small intestine, providing a sustained therapeutic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This specific formulation of dexlansoprazole is used to treat a range of acid-related conditions. Its primary indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Healing of Erosive Esophagitis (EE):</b> It is used for the short-term healing of all grades of EE, which is damage to the esophageal lining caused by gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Maintenance of Healed EE:</b> It is also used for the long-term maintenance of healed EE.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The dual-release mechanism helps to provide a consistent level of acid suppression, which is crucial for preventing a relapse.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptomatic Non-Erosive GERD:</b> It is used for the treatment of heartburn and other symptoms of GERD in patients who do not have erosive esophagitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The dual-release system can be particularly beneficial for patients with nocturnal or breakthrough symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of dexlansoprazole dual delayed release pellets is a combination of the drug's inherent pharmacological effect and its unique delivery system.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of the Proton Pump:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole is a <b>prodrug</b> that is absorbed into the bloodstream from the small intestine.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It then travels to the gastric parietal cells, where it is converted to its active form in the acidic environment of the secretory canaliculi.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The activated form of dexlansoprazole <b>irreversibly binds</b> to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This pump is responsible for the final step of gastric acid secretion. By permanently inactivating it, the drug effectively blocks both basal and stimulated acid production.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dual Delayed-Release Delivery:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This is the unique aspect of the formulation. The pellets within the capsule have two different coatings:</span></span></span>

		<ul style="list-style-type:square">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>First Release:</b> A portion of the pellets has a coating that dissolves at a relatively high pH, allowing for a rapid initial release of the drug in the proximal small intestine. This provides a quick onset of acid suppression.</span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Second Release:</b> The remaining pellets have a coating that requires a lower pH to dissolve, resulting in a second, later release of the drug in the distal small intestine, approximately 4 to 5 hours after the first release.</span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		</li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This two-stage release system creates two distinct peaks of drug concentration in the plasma.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This provides a longer duration of effective acid suppression over a 24-hour period, helping to manage symptoms throughout the day and night and improve healing rates.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Kexolan 60mg
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole Dual Delayed Release Pellets is a specific pharmaceutical formulation of the proton pump inhibitor (PPI) dexlansoprazole.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The term "Eq: Dexlansoprazole" means that the pellets contain an amount of the drug equivalent to the stated dosage of dexlansoprazole. This formulation is characterized by its unique <b>dual delayed-release</b> delivery system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The pellets are engineered with two different enteric coatings, each designed to dissolve at a different pH level.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This allows for two separate releases of the drug in the small intestine, providing a sustained therapeutic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This specific formulation of dexlansoprazole is used to treat a range of acid-related conditions. Its primary indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Healing of Erosive Esophagitis (EE):</b> It is used for the short-term healing of all grades of EE, which is damage to the esophageal lining caused by gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Maintenance of Healed EE:</b> It is also used for the long-term maintenance of healed EE.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The dual-release mechanism helps to provide a consistent level of acid suppression, which is crucial for preventing a relapse.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptomatic Non-Erosive GERD:</b> It is used for the treatment of heartburn and other symptoms of GERD in patients who do not have erosive esophagitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The dual-release system can be particularly beneficial for patients with nocturnal or breakthrough symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of dexlansoprazole dual delayed release pellets is a combination of the drug's inherent pharmacological effect and its unique delivery system.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of the Proton Pump:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexlansoprazole is a <b>prodrug</b> that is absorbed into the bloodstream from the small intestine.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It then travels to the gastric parietal cells, where it is converted to its active form in the acidic environment of the secretory canaliculi.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The activated form of dexlansoprazole <b>irreversibly binds</b> to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This pump is responsible for the final step of gastric acid secretion. By permanently inactivating it, the drug effectively blocks both basal and stimulated acid production.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dual Delayed-Release Delivery:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This is the unique aspect of the formulation. The pellets within the capsule have two different coatings:</span></span></span>

		<ul style="list-style-type:square">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>First Release:</b> A portion of the pellets has a coating that dissolves at a relatively high pH, allowing for a rapid initial release of the drug in the proximal small intestine. This provides a quick onset of acid suppression.</span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Second Release:</b> The remaining pellets have a coating that requires a lower pH to dissolve, resulting in a second, later release of the drug in the distal small intestine, approximately 4 to 5 hours after the first release.</span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		</li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This two-stage release system creates two distinct peaks of drug concentration in the plasma.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This provides a longer duration of effective acid suppression over a 24-hour period, helping to manage symptoms throughout the day and night and improve healing rates.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Vonprazan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vonoprazan Fumarate is a novel, oral anti-secretory drug that belongs to a class of medications called <b>Potassium-Competitive Acid Blockers (P-CABs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a newer alternative to proton pump inhibitors (PPIs) for acid suppression.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "Fumarate" refers to the specific salt form of the drug, which is used for stability and bioavailability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike PPIs, vonoprazan does not require an acidic environment for activation and works by a different, more direct mechanism to inhibit gastric acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vonoprazan is indicated for the treatment of various acid-related disorders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Erosive Esophagitis (EE):</b> It is used for the healing and maintenance of healing of all grades of erosive esophagitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> It is used for the treatment of GERD, providing rapid and potent acid suppression.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b><i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (<i>H. pylori</i>) Eradication:</b> It is a core component of combination regimens, along with antibiotics, for the eradication of <i>H.</i><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><i> pylori</i> infection.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastric and Duodenal Ulcers:</b> It is used for the treatment of these ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vonoprazan's mechanism of action is unique and different from that of PPIs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by inhibiting the final step of gastric acid secretion in a direct and reversible manner.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Potassium-Competitive Binding:</b> Vonoprazan works by competitively and reversibly binding to the potassium (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>K+) binding site of the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b>, also known as the "proton pump."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The proton pump is responsible for exchanging hydrogen ions (H+) for potassium ions (K+) in the final step of gastric acid secretion.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Acid Secretion:</b> By binding to the same site as potassium, vonoprazan blocks the pump's function.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action prevents the exchange of ions and effectively halts the flow of hydrogen ions into the stomach lumen, leading to a powerful inhibition of gastric acid secretion.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Key Differences from PPIs:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rapid Onset:</b> Unlike PPIs, vonoprazan does not require an acidic environment to be activated.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is active immediately upon reaching the parietal cells, leading to a much faster onset of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reversible Binding:</b> The binding of vonoprazan to the proton pump is reversible.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> However, due to its high binding affinity, it maintains a potent and prolonged acid-suppressive effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>pH-Independent Efficacy:</b> Because it does not rely on acid for activation, its effectiveness is not reduced after the first dose, and it can work consistently even when gastric acid secretion is low.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, vonoprazan is a P-CAB that provides rapid, potent, and sustained acid suppression by directly and reversibly blocking the proton pump's potassium binding site, making it a valuable alternative to traditional PPIs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p><br />

PRODUCT: Racwis
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Racecadotril is an oral drug that is classified as a <b>pro-drug</b> of the active compound thiorphan.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a unique anti-diarrheal agent that works by targeting the intestinal fluid secretion process.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike other common anti-diarrheal medications, such as loperamide, racecadotril does not affect gut motility and therefore does not cause constipation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Racecadotril is indicated for the symptomatic treatment of <b>acute watery diarrhea</b> in adults and children. It is always used as an adjunct to standard rehydration therapy (oral rehydration salts or solution).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not a substitute for rehydration, which is the most critical component of diarrhea management.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Racecadotril's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit an enzyme that breaks down natural substances in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Enkephalinase:</b> After oral administration, racecadotril is rapidly converted into its active metabolite, thiorphan.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Thiorphan is a highly selective and potent inhibitor of the enzyme <b>enkephalinase (or neprilysin)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Enkephalin Levels:</b> Enkephalinase is an enzyme that degrades naturally occurring substances called <b>enkephalins</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Enkephalins are endogenous opioids that have an antisecretory effect on the intestines, meaning they reduce the secretion of water and electrolytes into the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting enkephalinase, racecadotril allows the levels of endogenous enkephalins to increase.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antisecretory Effect:</b> The increased levels of enkephalins bind to opioid receptors in the intestinal wall. This binding leads to a reduction in the secretion of water and electrolytes from the intestinal cells into the gut lumen.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Effect on Motility:</b> Unlike opiate-based anti-diarrheal drugs, racecadotril does not affect the speed of intestinal transit or peristalsis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means that while it reduces the fluid content of the stool, it does not cause constipation or the other side effects associated with opioid receptor agonists in the central nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, racecadotril works by protecting the body's own anti-diarrheal substances (enkephalins) from being broken down, which allows them to reduce intestinal fluid secretion and alleviate the symptoms of watery diarrhea without affecting the natural movement of the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Trestex
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tropisetron is a synthetic antiemetic drug that belongs to the class of medications known as <b>serotonin 5-</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>HT3</b><b>​</b><b> receptor antagonists</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "HCL" in its name refers to the hydrochloride salt form, which is used to enhance its stability and solubility, making it suitable for both oral and intravenous administration.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Tropisetron is known for its high selectivity and potency in blocking serotonin receptors, which play a key role in the emetic (vomiting) reflex.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tropisetron is used for the prevention and treatment of nausea and vomiting, particularly those that are induced by certain medical treatments.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting (CINV):</b> It is a first-line treatment for both acute and delayed nausea and vomiting associated with cancer chemotherapy. It is particularly effective for highly emetogenic chemotherapeutic agents like cisplatin.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV):</b> It is also used for the prevention of nausea and vomiting following surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tropisetron's mechanism of action is based on its ability to block serotonin receptors, thereby interrupting a key signaling pathway that triggers nausea and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the 5-HT3​ Receptor:</b> Tropisetron is a highly selective and competitive antagonist of the <b>serotonin 5-HT3​ receptors</b>. These receptors are found in two critical locations involved in the emetic reflex:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In the Peripheral Nervous System:</b> They are located on the vagal nerve terminals in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Chemotherapeutic agents and other emetogenic stimuli can cause the release of serotonin from the enterochromaffin cells in the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This serotonin then activates the 5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT3​ receptors on the vagal nerve, sending a nausea signal to the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In the Central Nervous System:</b> The receptors are also present in the chemoreceptor trigger zone (CTZ) of the brain. The CTZ receives signals from the blood and cerebrospinal fluid and can independently trigger the vomiting reflex.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking the Emetic Reflex:</b> By binding to and blocking the 5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT3​ receptors, tropisetron prevents serotonin from activating them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action effectively stops the nausea and vomiting signals from reaching the brain and also directly inhibits the vomiting center.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual action in both the gut and the brain makes tropisetron a powerful antiemetic.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, tropisetron works by blocking the 5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT3​ serotonin receptors in both the gastrointestinal tract and the central nervous system, thereby interrupting the signaling pathways that lead to nausea and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In the Periphery:</b> They are located on the vagal nerve terminals in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. The CTZ can independently trigger the vomiting reflex in response to various stimuli in the blood and cerebrospinal fluid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

PRODUCT: Racecadotril
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Racecadotril is an oral enkephalinase inhibitor used for the treatment of acute diarrhea. It is a prodrug that is converted to its active metabolite, thiorphan, in the body. Unlike other antidiarrheal medications that slow down intestinal motility, Racecadotril has an antisecretory effect.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Racecadotril is indicated as a complementary symptomatic treatment for acute diarrhea in infants (over 3 months of age), children, and adults. It is used in conjunction with oral rehydration and other supportive measures, especially when these measures alone are not sufficient to control the clinical condition.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Racecadotril's active metabolite, thiorphan, works by inhibiting the enzyme neutral endopeptidase (also known as enkephalinase). This enzyme is located in the epithelium of the small intestine and is responsible for degrading endogenous enkephalins. By inhibiting this enzyme, Racecadotril prevents the breakdown of enkephalins, which are peptides that naturally have an anti-secretory effect. This action reduces the hypersecretion of water and electrolytes into the intestinal lumen, thereby reducing the symptoms of diarrhea without affecting intestinal motility.</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Nalotax
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Nalbuphine Hydrochloride is a synthetic opioid analgesic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent pain reliever that is structurally related to both the opioid antagonists naloxone and the potent opioid analgesic oxymorphone. A key characteristic of nalbuphine is its <b>mixed agonist-antagonist</b> activity at opioid receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means it acts as an agonist at some receptors and an antagonist at others, giving it a unique pharmacological profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Nalbuphine is used for the management of moderate to severe pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pain Management:</b> It is used to relieve moderate to severe pain, such as that experienced after surgery, from a myocardial infarction (heart attack), or from various chronic conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pre-operative and Post-operative Analgesia:</b> It is often used as a pre-operative medication to provide analgesia and sedation before surgery.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is also used post-operatively for pain control.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>As an Adjunct to Anesthesia:</b> It is used as an analgesic component in balanced anesthesia, helping to provide pain relief during surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Nalbuphine's therapeutic effects are a result of its unique dual action on different opioid receptors in the central nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Kappa (κ) Opioid Receptor Agonist:</b> Nalbuphine acts as a strong agonist at the kappa opioid receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When it binds to and activates these receptors, it produces a powerful <b>analgesic effect</b> (pain relief). The kappa receptor pathway is also responsible for some of the effects like sedation and dysphoria.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mu (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>μ</b><b>) Opioid Receptor Antagonist:</b> At the same time, nalbuphine acts as a weak antagonist at the mu opioid receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The mu receptors are the primary targets for most common opioid pain relievers (e.g., morphine, fentanyl). Mu receptor activation is responsible for the strong pain relief, but also for many of the serious side effects of opioids, including respiratory depression, euphoria, and physical dependence.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Clinical Implications of the Dual Action:</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pain Relief with Reduced Side Effects:</b> The kappa agonism provides significant pain relief, while the mu antagonism helps to reduce the risk of respiratory depression and other mu-related side effects compared to a pure mu agonist.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reversal of Opioid Effects:</b> In patients who are physically dependent on a mu opioid agonist, administering nalbuphine can precipitate a withdrawal syndrome because it will block the mu receptors that are being stimulated by the other opioid.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, nalbuphine provides pain relief primarily through its agonistic action on kappa opioid receptors, while its antagonistic action on mu opioid receptors mitigates some of the most serious and common side effects of opioids, making it a valuable tool in specific clinical settings.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Esomperazole + Naproxen Sodium
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> This is a combination product containing a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) and a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is used to relieve the signs and symptoms of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis while decreasing the risk of developing stomach ulcers in patients who are at risk of NSAID-associated gastric ulcers.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Esomeprazole:</b> It is a proton pump inhibitor that works by irreversibly blocking the H+/K+ ATPase enzyme system (the "proton pump") in the parietal cells of the stomach. This action inhibits the final step in acid production, thereby reducing stomach acid secretion.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Naproxen Sodium:</b> It is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug that works by inhibiting the cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes, specifically COX-1 and COX-2. This inhibition prevents the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are responsible for pain, inflammation, and fever.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Megesterol Acetate
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description: Megestrol Acetate is a synthetic progestin, a type of female hormone. It is a derivative of progesterone.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication: It is used to treat loss of appetite (anorexia), wasting (cachexia), and significant unexplained weight loss in patients with AIDS. It is also used to treat advanced breast cancer and endometrial cancer.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action: The exact mechanism for its effect on appetite and weight gain is not fully understood, but it is believed to be related to its effects on appetite-regulating hormones. In cancer treatment, it works by inhibiting the growth of hormone-sensitive tumors by modifying the effects of estrogen on the body.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Elagolix
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Description:</b> Elagolix is a non-peptide, small-molecule, oral medication. It is a gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) receptor antagonist used to manage moderate to severe pain associated with endometriosis.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is indicated for the management of moderate to severe pain associated with endometriosis in premenopausal women. Endometriosis is a condition where tissue similar to the lining of the uterus grows outside the uterus, causing pelvic pain and other symptoms.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action (MOA):</b> Elagolix works by acting as a competitive antagonist at the pituitary GnRH receptors. By binding to these receptors, it inhibits the release of gonadotropins, specifically luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH). The reduced levels of LH and FSH lead to a decrease in the production of ovarian sex hormones, primarily estradiol and progesterone. This dose-dependent suppression of estrogen helps to alleviate the pain and growth of endometrial tissue outside the uterus.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Deferasirox
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b> Deferasirox is an oral iron chelating agent. It is a medication used to treat chronic iron overload, which can occur from frequent blood transfusions (e.g., in patients with thalassemia or other chronic anemias) or other conditions that cause excess iron to accumulate in the body.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b> The primary indications for Deferasirox include:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Treatment of chronic iron overload</b> in patients with transfusional hemosiderosis (iron buildup due to repeated blood transfusions).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Treatment of chronic iron overload</b> in non-transfusion-dependent thalassemia syndromes.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Treatment of chronic iron overload</b> in patients with beta-thalassemia major who are at least 2 years of age.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b> Deferasirox works by <b>chelating iron</b>. Chelation is a process where a drug binds to a substance (in this case, iron) to form a complex. This complex is then excreted from the body, primarily through the feces. Deferasirox has a high affinity for iron (Fe3+), and by binding to it, the drug helps to remove the excess iron from the body's tissues and organs, preventing iron-related organ damage.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Brand Name</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Diclox
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a derivative of phenylacetic acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "sodium" in its name refers to the salt form, which is used to enhance its solubility and bioavailability, making it suitable for a wide range of formulations, including oral tablets, topical gels, and injectable solutions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is widely used to manage pain and inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium is indicated for the treatment of a variety of inflammatory and painful conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatic Diseases:</b> It is commonly used for the symptomatic treatment of inflammatory joint diseases, such as rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, and ankylosing spondylitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> It is effective in treating pain from sprains, strains, fractures, and other soft tissue injuries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative and Post-traumatic Pain:</b> It is used for the management of pain and inflammation following surgery or injury.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dysmenorrhea:</b> It is effective in relieving menstrual cramps and pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Migraine Headaches:</b> It can be used for the acute treatment of migraine attacks.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Diclofenac works by inhibiting the activity of two key enzymes: <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b>. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role of COX-1 and COX-2:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-1</b> is an enzyme that is expressed constitutively (all the time) and is involved in normal physiological functions, such as protecting the stomach lining and promoting platelet aggregation.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-2</b> is an inducible enzyme that is primarily expressed at the sites of inflammation in response to injury or infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is responsible for the production of prostaglandins that cause pain, swelling, and fever.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting both COX-1 and COX-2, diclofenac significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> accounts for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, diclofenac sodium is a non-selective NSAID that provides its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, thereby preventing the synthesis of prostaglandins that mediate pain and inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> accounts for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, diclofenac sodium is a non-selective NSAID that provides its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, thereby preventing the synthesis of prostaglandins that mediate pain and inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon><br />

PRODUCT: Linaglitpin + Metformin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Linagliptin + Metformin is a combination prescription medicine that contains two different medications to help control blood sugar levels in adults with type 2 diabetes. It is used as an adjunct to diet and exercise.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus to improve glycemic control. It is not for the treatment of type 1 diabetes or diabetic ketoacidosis.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> This product works through the complementary actions of its two components:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Linagliptin:</b> This is a DPP-4 inhibitor. It works by blocking the enzyme DPP-4, which normally breaks down "incretin" hormones like GLP-1 (glucagon-like peptide-1). By inhibiting this enzyme, Linagliptin increases the levels of these hormones, which in turn stimulates the pancreas to release more insulin and decreases the production of glucagon. This effect is glucose-dependent, meaning it primarily works when blood sugar levels are high.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Metformin:</b> This is a biguanide. Its primary mechanism is to decrease the production of glucose in the liver (hepatic gluconeogenesis). It also helps the body use insulin more effectively and reduces the absorption of glucose from the intestines.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Fentolit SR
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a derivative of phenylacetic acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "sodium" in its name refers to the salt form, which is used to enhance its solubility and bioavailability, making it suitable for a wide range of formulations, including oral tablets, topical gels, and injectable solutions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is widely used to manage pain and inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium is indicated for the treatment of a variety of inflammatory and painful conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatic Diseases:</b> It is commonly used for the symptomatic treatment of inflammatory joint diseases, such as rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, and ankylosing spondylitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> It is effective in treating pain from sprains, strains, fractures, and other soft tissue injuries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative and Post-traumatic Pain:</b> It is used for the management of pain and inflammation following surgery or injury.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dysmenorrhea:</b> It is effective in relieving menstrual cramps and pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Migraine Headaches:</b> It can be used for the acute treatment of migraine attacks.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Diclofenac works by inhibiting the activity of two key enzymes: <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b>. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role of COX-1 and COX-2:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-1</b> is an enzyme that is expressed constitutively (all the time) and is involved in normal physiological functions, such as protecting the stomach lining and promoting platelet aggregation.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-2</b> is an inducible enzyme that is primarily expressed at the sites of inflammation in response to injury or infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is responsible for the production of prostaglandins that cause pain, swelling, and fever.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting both COX-1 and COX-2, diclofenac significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> accounts for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, diclofenac sodium is a non-selective NSAID that provides its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, thereby preventing the synthesis of prostaglandins that mediate pain and inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
