PRODUCT: Azilsartan + Amlodipine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This is a fixed-dose combination medication containing <b>azilsartan</b> and <b>amlodipine</b>. Azilsartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB), and amlodipine is a calcium channel blocker (CCB). The combination is designed to provide a more effective and stable reduction in blood pressure than either drug used alone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This medication is indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension</b> (high blood pressure). It is typically used for patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled by a single medication.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The drug works through a dual mechanism to lower blood pressure:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Azilsartan</b> is an Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker (ARB). It works by blocking the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors. Angiotensin II is a powerful hormone that causes blood vessels to constrict and stimulates the release of aldosterone, which leads to sodium and water retention. By blocking the effects of angiotensin II, azilsartan causes blood vessels to relax and widen, reducing blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Amlodipine</b> is a Calcium Channel Blocker (CCB). It inhibits the influx of calcium ions into the smooth muscle cells of the blood vessels. This causes the blood vessels to relax and dilate, particularly the arteries, which reduces peripheral vascular resistance and lowers blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination of these two mechanisms provides a synergistic effect, offering a more significant reduction in blood pressure than either component alone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Rosuvastatin + Ezetimibe
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Rosuvastatin + Ezetimibe is a combination medication that contains two active ingredients: rosuvastatin, a statin, and ezetimibe, a cholesterol absorption inhibitor. This combination is designed to provide a more comprehensive approach to lowering cholesterol than either drug alone.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>This medication is indicated for the treatment of primary hypercholesterolemia, a condition characterized by high levels of LDL ("bad") cholesterol. It's used in patients who have not achieved their target cholesterol levels with a statin alone, and for those who require a more significant reduction in cholesterol.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Rosuvastatin works by inhibiting an enzyme called HMG-CoA reductase, which is a key enzyme in the liver responsible for producing cholesterol. By blocking this enzyme, rosuvastatin reduces the amount of cholesterol the liver makes, which in turn leads to a decrease in LDL cholesterol in the bloodstream.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ezetimibe works differently. It acts on the brush border of the small intestine to inhibit the absorption of cholesterol from the food you eat. By preventing cholesterol from entering the body, ezetimibe further helps to lower cholesterol levels.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>The combination of these two drugs provides a dual mechanism of action—one that reduces the body's production of cholesterol and another that reduces its absorption, leading to a more potent cholesterol-lowering effect.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Fentolit

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac sodium's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Diclofenac works by inhibiting the activity of two key enzymes: <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b>. </ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role of COX-1 and COX-2:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-1</b> is an enzyme that is expressed constitutively (all the time) and is involved in normal physiological functions, such as protecting the stomach lining and promoting platelet aggregation.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-2</b> is an inducible enzyme that is primarily expressed at the sites of inflammation in response to injury or infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is responsible for the production of prostaglandins that cause pain, swelling, and fever.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting both COX-1 and COX-2, diclofenac significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> accounts for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, diclofenac sodium is a non-selective NSAID that provides its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, thereby preventing the synthesis of prostaglandins that mediate pain and inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Lansoprazole + Aspirin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Lansoprazole</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Lansoprazole is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) that decreases the amount of acid produced in the stomach.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is used to treat and prevent stomach and duodenal ulcers, erosive esophagitis (damage to the esophagus from stomach acid), and other conditions involving excessive stomach acid such as Zollinger-Ellison syndrome. In this combination product, it primarily serves to reduce the risk of stomach ulcers that can be caused by the long-term use of aspirin.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Lansoprazole works by irreversibly blocking the proton pump (H+/K+ ATPase) in the gastric parietal cells. This pump is the final step in the production of stomach acid. By blocking it, lansoprazole effectively inhibits both basal and stimulated acid secretion.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Aspirin</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Aspirin is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) and an antiplatelet agent.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is used to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation. In the context of a combination drug with a PPI, it is primarily used for cardiovascular benefits, such as preventing heart attacks and strokes in high-risk patients.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Aspirin's antiplatelet effect is due to its irreversible inhibition of the cyclooxygenase (COX-1) enzyme in platelets. This enzyme is responsible for producing thromboxane A2, a molecule that promotes platelet aggregation and blood clot formation. By inhibiting this process, aspirin reduces the risk of blood clots. Its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects are also related to COX inhibition, but in this case, it inhibits both COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Pofac
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac Potassium is a synthetic nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) and a derivative of phenylacetic acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the potassium salt form of diclofenac.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The key difference between diclofenac potassium and diclofenac sodium lies in the salt form's effect on absorption.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The potassium salt is more soluble in water, which allows for faster absorption from the gastrointestinal tract and a quicker onset of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Due to its rapid absorption, diclofenac potassium is primarily indicated for the management of acute and painful conditions where a fast onset of pain relief is desired. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mild to Moderate Acute Pain:</b> Such as that following minor surgeries or dental procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Dysmenorrhea:</b> For the relief of menstrual cramps and pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Migraine Headaches:</b> For the acute treatment of migraine attacks.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fever:</b> It can be used as an antipyretic (fever reducer).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of diclofenac potassium is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This mechanism is identical to that of diclofenac sodium.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Diclofenac works by inhibiting the activity of two key enzymes: <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b>. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> reduces the production of prostaglandins that are responsible for inflammation, pain, and fever. This is the source of the drug's therapeutic effects.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> reduces the production of prostaglandins involved in normal physiological functions, such as protecting the stomach lining and promoting platelet aggregation. This inhibition is the cause of some of the drug's side effects, such as gastrointestinal irritation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, diclofenac potassium is a non-selective NSAID that provides its therapeutic effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary clinical advantage over the sodium salt is its faster absorption and quicker onset of action, making it particularly suitable for the management of acute pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p><br />
<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Omarigliptin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Omarigliptin is a potent, long-acting oral antidiabetic drug. It belongs to the class of medications known as Dipeptidyl Peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitors. It is unique among DPP-4 inhibitors for its once-weekly dosing schedule, which is made possible by its long half-life.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Omarigliptin is indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus in adults to help improve glycemic control, often used as an adjunct to diet and exercise.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The mechanism of action for Omarigliptin involves the selective inhibition of the enzyme dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4). This enzyme is responsible for the rapid breakdown of incretin hormones, such as glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) and glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">By inhibiting DPP-4, Omarigliptin increases the levels and prolongs the activity of these incretin hormones. This leads to two key effects:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Increased Insulin Secretion:</b> The elevated levels of GLP-1 and GIP stimulate the pancreas to release more insulin in a glucose-dependent manner (meaning it only works when blood sugar is high).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Decreased Glucagon Release:</b> The hormones also suppress the release of glucagon, a hormone that increases blood glucose levels by promoting glucose production in the liver.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This dual action helps to regulate blood glucose levels, particularly after meals, without a significant risk of hypoglycemia when used as a monotherapy.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Zolfenac
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Diclofenac Sodium and Misoprostol is a fixed-dose oral medication that combines a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) with a synthetic prostaglandin analog. This unique formulation is designed to provide the anti-inflammatory and analgesic benefits of diclofenac while simultaneously reducing the risk of developing NSAID-induced gastrointestinal ulcers and other complications.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for patients who require chronic NSAID therapy for conditions like <b>rheumatoid arthritis</b> and <b>osteoarthritis</b> and are at <b>high risk for developing gastric or duodenal ulcers</b>. High-risk patients typically include the elderly, those with a history of ulcers, or those on long-term corticosteroid therapy.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is important to note that due to the potential for serious side effects from misoprostol, particularly in women of child-bearing age (e.g., miscarriage), this drug should be used with caution and only when the benefits outweigh the risks.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diclofenac Sodium (NSAID Component):</span></span></b></span></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Diclofenac works by inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Action:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> This inhibition prevents the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of the drug are primarily due to its inhibition of COX-2 at the site of inflammation.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Side Effect:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> A major side effect of NSAIDs is that they also inhibit COX-1, which reduces the production of prostaglandins that are essential for protecting the stomach and intestinal lining. This is the reason NSAIDs can cause ulcers.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Misoprostol (Prostaglandin Analog Component):</span></span></b></span></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Misoprostol is a synthetic analog of prostaglandin E1​.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Action:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It works by acting on prostaglandin receptors in the gastrointestinal tract. Its primary functions are:</span></span></span></span></span></span>
		<ul style="list-style-type:square">
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibition of Acid Secretion:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It directly inhibits the secretion of gastric acid by the parietal cells in the stomach.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mucosal Protection:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It enhances the protective properties of the stomach lining. It stimulates the production of mucus and bicarbonate, which form a protective barrier against stomach acid.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		</li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Counteracting Side Effects:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By providing these protective effects, misoprostol directly counteracts the ulcerogenic effects of diclofenac, which result from the inhibition of natural prostaglandin synthesis.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the combination provides a dual-action therapy: diclofenac effectively manages the pain and inflammation of arthritis, while misoprostol protects the gastrointestinal lining from the damaging effects of diclofenac, making the treatment safer for high-risk patients.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Deferoxamine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Deferoxamine, also known by the brand name <b>Desferal</b>, is an iron-chelating agent. It's a compound that binds to iron, which helps the body to remove excess iron. While its primary use is for iron overload, its ability to chelate iron has also shown potential in inhibiting the replication of certain viruses that rely on iron for their proliferation.</span></span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The primary indications for Deferoxamine are:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Acute iron poisoning:</b> Used to treat patients who have ingested a toxic amount of iron.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Chronic iron overload:</b> A condition that can result from repeated blood transfusions (e.g., in patients with thalassemia) or certain diseases.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Aluminum toxicity:</b> Used to treat aluminum overload in patients with kidney failure.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" /></div>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Deferoxamine works as a <b>chelating agent</b>. It has a high affinity for ferric iron (Fe3+). When administered, it binds to free iron in the bloodstream and tissues, forming a stable, non-toxic complex called ferrioxamine. This complex is then excreted from the body, primarily through the urine, which reduces the overall iron load.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">For its potential antiviral effects, the mechanism is related to its iron-chelating properties. Viruses, like many other pathogens, require iron for their replication and survival. By sequestering and reducing the available iron, Deferoxamine can disrupt the viral life cycle and inhibit its proliferation.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Cinacalcet
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Cinacalcet is a medication in a class of drugs called calcimimetics. It is an oral drug that is used to manage disorders related to the parathyroid glands.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Cinacalcet is indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Secondary hyperparathyroidism in patients with chronic kidney disease who are on dialysis.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Hypercalcemia (high calcium levels in the blood) in patients with parathyroid carcinoma.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Severe hypercalcemia in patients with primary hyperparathyroidism who are unable to undergo surgery.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Cinacalcet works by increasing the sensitivity of the calcium-sensing receptors on the parathyroid glands to extracellular calcium. By mimicking the effects of calcium on these receptors, it suppresses the release of parathyroid hormone (PTH). The reduction in PTH levels leads to a decrease in serum calcium and phosphorus levels.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Tofacitinib
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Tofacitinib is a medication that belongs to a class of drugs called Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. It is an oral drug that works by slowing down an overactive immune system to decrease inflammation. It is often used when other medications, such as methotrexate or TNF blockers, have not been effective or cannot be tolerated by the patient.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Tofacitinib is used to treat a variety of autoimmune and inflammatory conditions, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Moderately to severely active rheumatoid arthritis (RA)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Active psoriatic arthritis (PsA)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Active ankylosing spondylitis (AS)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis (UC)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Active polyarticular course juvenile idiopathic arthritis (pcJIA) in patients 2 years of age and older</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Tofacitinib's primary mechanism of action is the inhibition of Janus kinases (JAKs). JAKs are intracellular enzymes that play a critical role in the signaling pathways of various cytokines and growth factors involved in the immune and inflammatory responses. Tofacitinib primarily inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, and to a lesser extent, JAK2. By blocking these JAKs, tofacitinib prevents the phosphorylation of signal transducers and activators of transcription (STATs). This disruption of the JAK-STAT signaling pathway hinders the movement of STATs to the cell nucleus, which in turn reduces the expression of genes that produce inflammatory mediators. This ultimately leads to a reduction in the inflammatory response and helps to control the symptoms of autoimmune diseases.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Clonidine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Clonidine is a centrally acting alpha-2 adrenergic agonist. It is typically available as an oral tablet or a transdermal patch.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hypertension (High Blood Pressure): Clonidine is most commonly prescribed to treat high blood pressure.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>ADHD (Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder): It can be used off-label or as an adjunct therapy for some individuals with ADHD.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Withdrawal Symptoms: It is sometimes used to manage withdrawal symptoms from opioids, alcohol, or nicotine.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vasomotor Symptoms: It can be used to treat hot flashes in menopausal women.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Other uses: Can also be used to treat certain types of pain and Tourette's syndrome.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Clonidine works by stimulating alpha-2 adrenergic receptors in the brain, particularly in the brainstem's vasomotor center. This stimulation has two main effects:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reduced Sympathetic Outflow: It decreases the release of norepinephrine (a vasoconstrictor) from sympathetic nerves.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Decreased Heart Rate and Blood Pressure: By reducing sympathetic activity, it leads to a decrease in heart rate and a relaxation of blood vessels, which in turn lowers blood pressure.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>This central action results in a reduction of peripheral resistance, a decrease in renal vascular resistance, and a decrease in heart rate, all contributing to a lower blood pressure.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Molnupiravir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description: Molnupiravir is an oral antiviral medication. It is a prodrug, meaning it is converted into its active form once inside the body. This active form is a ribonucleoside analog that is structurally similar to one of the building blocks of RNA.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication: Molnupiravir is indicated for the treatment of mild-to-moderate COVID-19 in adults who are at a high risk of progressing to severe illness, including hospitalization or death, and for whom other treatment options are not suitable. It should be taken as soon as possible after a diagnosis of COVID-19 and within five days of the onset of symptoms.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action: Molnupiravir works by a process called "lethal mutagenesis" or "viral error catastrophe." Once it is converted to its active form, it is incorporated into the viral RNA during replication by the virus's RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) enzyme. This incorporation introduces copying errors into the viral genome at a rate that is too high for the virus to survive, leading to the production of non-functional viral particles and effectively stopping the virus from replicating.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Celewin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Celecoxib is a synthetic nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that belongs to a class of drugs known as <b>selective cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike traditional NSAIDs, which inhibit both COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, celecoxib's unique chemical structure allows it to target the COX-2 enzyme with a high degree of specificity. This selectivity is the basis for its therapeutic effects and its more favorable gastrointestinal safety profile.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral capsules.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Celecoxib is primarily indicated for the treatment of inflammatory and painful conditions, particularly those requiring chronic management. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteoarthritis (OA):</b> For the symptomatic relief of pain and inflammation associated with OA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA):</b> For the symptomatic relief of RA, including the relief of signs and symptoms of juvenile rheumatoid arthritis in patients 2 years and older.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ankylosing Spondylitis:</b> For the symptomatic relief of pain and inflammation in this chronic inflammatory disease of the spine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Pain:</b> For the management of acute pain, such as that following dental surgery or other minor surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Dysmenorrhea:</b> For the relief of menstrual cramps and pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of celecoxib is based on its ability to selectively inhibit the enzyme cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), thereby reducing the production of inflammatory prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Selective Inhibition of COX-2:</b> Celecoxib works by specifically and potently inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzyme.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role of COX-1 vs. COX-2:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-1</b> is a constitutively expressed enzyme that is involved in normal physiological functions, such as maintaining the protective gastric mucosal lining, promoting platelet aggregation, and regulating renal blood flow.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>COX-2</b> is an inducible enzyme that is primarily expressed at the site of inflammation in response to cytokines and other inflammatory mediators.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is responsible for the production of prostaglandins that cause pain, swelling, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By selectively inhibiting COX-2, celecoxib blocks the production of the prostaglandins that mediate the symptoms of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Minimizing Gastrointestinal Side Effects:</b> Because celecoxib largely spares the COX-1 enzyme, it does not interfere with the production of the protective prostaglandins in the stomach. This selectivity is what provides its primary clinical advantage: a significantly lower risk of gastrointestinal side effects (e.g., ulcers, bleeding) compared to non-selective NSAIDs like ibuprofen or naproxen, which inhibit both COX-1 and COX-2.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, celecoxib is a selective COX-2 inhibitor that provides effective relief from pain and inflammation by targeting the enzymes responsible for these symptoms while largely sparing the enzyme that protects the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Acetazolamide
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Acetazolamide is a carbonic anhydrase inhibitor. It is a sulfonamide derivative but lacks significant antimicrobial activity.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is primarily used to treat glaucoma (to reduce intraocular pressure), edema due to congestive heart failure or drug-induced edema, and certain types of epilepsy (as an adjunct to other medications). It can also be used to prevent or treat acute mountain sickness (altitude sickness).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Acetazolamide works by inhibiting the enzyme carbonic anhydrase. In the eye, this reduces the formation of aqueous humor, thereby lowering intraocular pressure. In the kidneys, it increases the excretion of bicarbonate, sodium, potassium, and water, leading to diuresis (increased urine production). This action also helps to correct metabolic alkalosis. In the central nervous system, it may inhibit carbonic anhydrase in the brain, which may help to control seizures. The inhibition of carbonic anhydrase also helps to increase ventilation and oxygenation at high altitudes.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sumatriptan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Sumatriptan is a selective serotonin (5-HT) receptor agonist of the 5-HT$<i>{1B}$ and 5-HT$</i>{1D}$ subtypes. It is the first in a class of drugs called triptans. It is a vasoconstrictive agent that is effective in the acute treatment of migraine attacks.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Sumatriptan is indicated for the acute treatment of migraine attacks, with or without aura, in adults. It is not intended for the prophylactic (preventive) treatment of migraines. It can also be used for the acute treatment of cluster headache episodes.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Sumatriptan's therapeutic effect is believed to be due to its agonist action on 5-HT$_{1B/1D}$ receptors. This action leads to two main effects:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Cranial Vasoconstriction:</b> It causes vasoconstriction of the intracranial blood vessels, which are thought to be dilated during a migraine attack.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Inhibition of Neuropeptide Release:</b> It inhibits the release of pro-inflammatory neuropeptides, such as substance P and calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP), from trigeminal nerve endings. These neuropeptides contribute to the pain and inflammation associated with a migraine attack.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination of these two actions helps to relieve the headache and other symptoms of a migraine.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Citicoline
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description: Citicoline, also known as cytidine diphosphate-choline (CDP-choline), is an endogenous nucleoside. It is a psychostimulant and nootropic compound. It is a precursor to the essential phospholipid phosphatidylcholine, a major component of neuronal cell membranes.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Cognitive impairment and dementia</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ischemic stroke and head trauma</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Glaucoma</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Parkinson's disease</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action: Citicoline works by increasing the synthesis of phosphatidylcholine, a key component of nerve cell membranes. It also increases levels of several important neurotransmitters, including acetylcholine, norepinephrine, and dopamine. This helps to improve nerve signal transmission and protect neurons from damage. Additionally, citicoline enhances cerebral metabolism and blood flow.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Midodrine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Midodrine is a medication used to treat orthostatic hypotension, a condition where a person's blood pressure drops significantly upon standing up, which can cause symptoms like dizziness, lightheadedness, and fainting. It is an oral medication that works by increasing blood pressure.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The primary indication for Midodrine is the treatment of symptomatic orthostatic hypotension, especially in cases that are unresponsive to other treatments. This condition can be caused by various factors, including autonomic nervous system disorders (such as Bradbury-Eggleston or Shy-Drager syndromes), diabetes mellitus, or Parkinson's disease.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Midodrine is a prodrug, meaning it is converted into its active form, called desglymidodrine, once it is in the body. Desglymidodrine acts as a selective agonist of the alpha-1 adrenergic receptors located on the blood vessels. When it binds to these receptors, it causes the blood vessels to constrict (vasoconstriction), which increases peripheral vascular resistance and, as a result, raises blood pressure. This action helps prevent the pooling of blood in the lower extremities when a person stands up, thereby alleviating the symptoms of orthostatic hypotension.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Candesartan + Hydrochlorothiazide
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> This is a combination medication that contains two active ingredients: candesartan and hydrochlorothiazide. Candesartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB), and hydrochlorothiazide is a thiazide diuretic. The combination is used to treat hypertension (high blood pressure) when treatment with a single drug is not effective.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is indicated for the treatment of essential hypertension.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Candesartan:</b> It works by selectively blocking the binding of angiotensin II to the AT1 receptors found in many tissues, such as vascular smooth muscle and the adrenal gland. Angiotensin II is a powerful vasoconstrictor, so by blocking its effects, candesartan causes blood vessels to relax and widen, lowering blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hydrochlorothiazide:</b> It acts on the kidneys to increase the excretion of sodium and water, which reduces the total blood volume. This decrease in blood volume also contributes to a reduction in blood pressure. The combination of these two mechanisms provides a more potent blood pressure-lowering effect than either drug alone.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Candesartan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Candesartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB). It is a prodrug, meaning it is an inactive substance (candesartan cilexetil) that is converted into its active form (candesartan) by the body during absorption. It works by affecting the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS), which helps regulate blood pressure.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Candesartan is primarily used to treat:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>High blood pressure (Hypertension):</b> It is used alone or with other medications to lower blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Heart failure:</b> It is used to treat congestive heart failure and reduce the number of hospital visits for heart problems.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Candesartan works by blocking the angiotensin II receptor, subtype 1 (AT1​). By blocking this receptor, it prevents the effects of angiotensin II, a powerful hormone that causes blood vessels to constrict and triggers the release of aldosterone, which leads to sodium and water retention. The result of blocking these actions is:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vasodilation:</b> Blood vessels relax and widen, allowing blood to flow more smoothly.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Decreased fluid retention:</b> The body excretes excess water and sodium.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">These combined effects lead to a reduction in blood pressure and a decrease in the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sirolimus
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Sirolimus, also known as rapamycin, is an immunosuppressant drug. It is a macrolide produced by the bacterium <i>Streptomyces hygroscopicus</i>.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Renal Transplant:</b> Primarily used for the prophylaxis of organ rejection in patients receiving a kidney transplant.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM):</b> Approved to treat LAM, a rare, progressive lung disease.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Certain Tumors:</b> Investigated for use in some types of cancer.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Sirolimus works by inhibiting the mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR), a protein that regulates cell growth, proliferation, and survival. By blocking mTOR, sirolimus prevents the activation of T-cells and B-cells, which are essential components of the immune system responsible for attacking foreign substances and transplanted organs. This action suppresses the immune response, helping to prevent organ rejection.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Flurwin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flurbiprofen is a synthetic nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that is a derivative of propionic acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent inhibitor of prostaglandin synthesis and is used for its analgesic (pain-relieving), anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various forms, including oral tablets, topical gels, and throat lozenges.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flurbiprofen is indicated for the treatment of a range of inflammatory and painful conditions. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatic Diseases:</b> It is used for the symptomatic treatment of rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, and ankylosing spondylitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> It is effective in treating pain from sprains, strains, and other soft tissue injuries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dysmenorrhea:</b> It is used for the relief of primary dysmenorrhea (menstrual cramps).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ophthalmic Uses:</b> As an eye drop, it is used to inhibit miosis (pupil constriction) during surgical procedures and to control post-operative inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sore Throat:</b> Flurbiprofen lozenges are widely used for the symptomatic relief of a sore throat and its associated pain and swelling.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flurbiprofen's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Flurbiprofen works by non-selectively inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing the production of prostaglandins that cause inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Unique Local Action (Lozenges):</b> When used as a lozenge for a sore throat, flurbiprofen works locally at the site of inflammation in the throat. By inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis directly in the inflamed tissues, it provides rapid and effective relief from throat pain and swelling.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, flurbiprofen provides its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects by inhibiting the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, thereby blocking the synthesis of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its various formulations allow it to be used for both systemic and localized inflammatory conditions.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>
