PRODUCT: Tacrolimus
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Tacrolimus is a potent immunosuppressive drug. It is a macrolide that is used to reduce the activity of the body's immune system. It comes in various forms, including oral capsules, extended-release capsules, and a topical ointment.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Primary Indication:</b> Prophylaxis (prevention) of organ rejection in patients who have received an allogeneic (from another person) organ transplant, such as a kidney, liver, heart, or lung. It is typically used in combination with other immunosuppressive agents.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Other Uses:</b> Tacrolimus is also used as a topical ointment for the treatment of moderate to severe atopic dermatitis (eczema) in patients who have not responded to or cannot use other therapies. It is also sometimes used off-label for other autoimmune conditions.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Tacrolimus works by inhibiting the function of T-cells, which are a type of white blood cell that plays a key role in the immune system's response to foreign invaders, including transplanted organs.</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tacrolimus binds to an intracellular protein called FKBP-12.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This new complex of Tacrolimus and FKBP-12 then inhibits a protein phosphatase called calcineurin.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">By inhibiting calcineurin, the drug prevents the dephosphorylation of a nuclear factor known as NF-AT (nuclear factor of activated T-cells).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This action prevents NF-AT from entering the nucleus and activating the transcription of genes that are critical for T-cell activation and proliferation, such as those that produce interleukin-2 (IL-2).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ultimately, this process suppresses the T-cell-mediated immune response, preventing the body from rejecting the transplanted organ.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Figlex
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Piroxicam is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) of the oxicam class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A key characteristic of piroxicam is its exceptionally long half-life, which allows for once-daily dosing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is used for its analgesic (pain-relieving), anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various formulations, including oral capsules, tablets, and topical gels.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Due to its long half-life, piroxicam is primarily indicated for the long-term management of chronic inflammatory diseases. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatoid Arthritis:</b> For the symptomatic relief of signs and symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteoarthritis:</b> For the symptomatic treatment of osteoarthritis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ankylosing Spondylitis:</b> For the symptomatic treatment of this chronic inflammatory disease of the spine.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Inflammatory Conditions:</b> It is also used for the symptomatic treatment of other painful and inflammatory musculoskeletal conditions.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Piroxicam's primary mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Piroxicam works by non-selectively inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing the production of prostaglandins that cause inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Actions:</b> In addition to its COX inhibition, piroxicam also has other anti-inflammatory properties. It has been shown to inhibit neutrophil function, including their aggregation and the release of their lysosomal enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It also inhibits the synthesis of thromboxanes, which are involved in blood clotting.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, piroxicam is a non-selective NSAID that provides its therapeutic effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its unique advantage is its long half-life, which makes it a convenient option for the long-term management of chronic inflammatory conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p><br />

PRODUCT: Ceftazidime + AvibuctumCeftazidime + Avibuctum
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ceftazidime + Avibactam is a fixed-dose combination medication. It is composed of two active components:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ceftazidime:</b> A third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Avibactam:</b> A non-β-lactam β-lactamase inhibitor.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination of these two drugs is designed to treat serious bacterial infections, particularly those caused by resistant gram-negative bacteria.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ceftazidime + Avibactam is used to treat a range of infections caused by susceptible bacteria, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Complicated intra-abdominal infections (cIAI) (in combination with metronidazole).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Complicated urinary tract infections (cUTI), including pyelonephritis.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Hospital-acquired bacterial pneumonia (HABP) and ventilator-associated bacterial pneumonia (VABP).</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The mechanism of action for this medication involves the combined effects of its two components:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ceftazidime:</b> This component is a bactericidal antibiotic. It works by binding to essential penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) inside the bacteria. This binding inhibits the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall, leading to a loss of cell integrity and ultimately, bacterial cell death.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Avibactam:</b> This component is a β-lactamase inhibitor. Many bacteria produce enzymes called β-lactamases that can break down and inactivate ceftazidime and other β-lactam antibiotics. Avibactam works by binding to and inactivating these β-lactamase enzymes. By doing so, it protects ceftazidime from degradation, which allows the ceftazidime to effectively kill the bacteria. This combination is particularly effective against bacteria that have become resistant to other antibiotics due to the production of these enzymes.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Pexy
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Piroxicam as Beta Cyclodextrin is a specific pharmaceutical formulation where the active drug, piroxicam, is complexed with <b>beta-cyclodextrin</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Beta-cyclodextrin is a cyclic oligosaccharide that acts as a molecular "cage" or carrier.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This complexing process significantly improves the water solubility and dissolution rate of piroxicam, a drug that is otherwise poorly soluble.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enhanced solubility leads to faster absorption from the gastrointestinal tract and a quicker onset of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This formulation is used for the same range of painful and inflammatory conditions as regular piroxicam. However, due to its faster onset of action, it is particularly useful for the short-term treatment of <b>acute conditions</b> where rapid pain relief is a priority. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Pain:</b> Such as that following dental surgery, trauma, or sprains.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Gout:</b> For the symptomatic treatment of acute gouty arthritis attacks.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other acute inflammatory conditions:</b> Including acute flares of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It can also be used for the long-term management of chronic conditions like osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis, offering the convenience of once-daily dosing with the added benefit of faster absorption.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on two distinct components: the pharmacological action of piroxicam and the physical enhancement provided by beta-cyclodextrin.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Piroxicam's Pharmacological Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Piroxicam is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It works by non-selectively inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By blocking these enzymes, piroxicam prevents the conversion of arachidonic acid into prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-Cyclodextrin's Enhancing Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Beta-cyclodextrin itself has no pharmacological activity. Its role is purely as a delivery vehicle.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It forms an "inclusion complex" with piroxicam, where the hydrophobic piroxicam molecule is encapsulated within the hydrophilic core of the beta-cyclodextrin molecule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This complex improves the drug's water solubility and its dissolution rate in the gastrointestinal fluids.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This increased dissolution rate allows for a much faster and more complete absorption of the piroxicam into the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the beta-cyclodextrin complex accelerates the absorption of piroxicam, leading to a faster onset of pain relief, while the piroxicam component continues to provide its long-lasting anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects through its inhibition of prostaglandin synthesis.</span></span></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Beta-1

	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It works by non-selectively inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By blocking these enzymes, piroxicam prevents the conversion of arachidonic acid into prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

	</li>

	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-Cyclodextrin's Enhancing Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Beta-cyclodextrin itself has no pharmacological activity. Its role is purely as a delivery vehicle.</span></span></span></li>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This complex improves the drug's water solubility and its dissolution rate in the gastrointestinal fluids.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This increased dissolution rate allows for a much faster and more complete absorption of the piroxicam into the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the beta-cyclodextrin complex accelerates the absorption of piroxicam, leading to a faster onset of pain relief, while the piroxicam component continues to provide its long-lasting anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects through its inhibition of prostaglandin synthesis.</span></span></p>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Dapagliflozin + Metformin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Dapagliflozin and Metformin is a combination of two oral anti-diabetic medications used to manage type 2 diabetes. Dapagliflozin is a Sodium-Glucose Co-Transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor, while Metformin is a biguanide.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This combination medicine is used, along with diet and exercise, to improve blood sugar control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus. It is also indicated to reduce the risk of hospitalization for heart failure in adults with type 2 diabetes who also have cardiovascular disease.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The drug works through two complementary mechanisms:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Dapagliflozin:</b> It works in the kidneys by inhibiting the SGLT2 protein. This prevents the reabsorption of glucose back into the bloodstream, causing excess glucose to be excreted in the urine, thus lowering blood sugar levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Metformin:</b> It works primarily by decreasing glucose production by the liver, reducing the absorption of glucose from the intestines, and improving the body's sensitivity to insulin. This helps the body use glucose more effectively.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Dapagliflozin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Dapagliflozin is an oral medication that belongs to a class of drugs called sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitors. It is used to help manage blood sugar levels in people with Type 2 diabetes. It is also used to reduce the risk of hospitalization for heart failure and to slow the progression of chronic kidney disease.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Dapagliflozin is primarily indicated for:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Improving glycemic control as an adjunct to diet and exercise in adults and pediatric patients (10 years and older) with Type 2 diabetes mellitus.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Reducing the risk of hospitalization for heart failure in adults with Type 2 diabetes and established cardiovascular disease or multiple cardiovascular risk factors.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Reducing the risk of cardiovascular death and hospitalization for heart failure in adults with heart failure.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Reducing the risk of sustained eGFR decline, end-stage kidney disease, cardiovascular death, and hospitalization for heart failure in adults with chronic kidney disease.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Dapagliflozin works by inhibiting the SGLT2 protein, which is primarily found in the kidneys. This protein is responsible for reabsorbing most of the glucose that has been filtered from the blood back into the body. By blocking SGLT2, dapagliflozin prevents this reabsorption, causing more glucose to be excreted in the urine. This process lowers blood sugar levels. Additionally, it can reduce sodium reabsorption and increase the delivery of sodium to the distal tubule, which is thought to influence cardiovascular and renal effects.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Loraxo
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lornoxicam is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that belongs to the oxicam class.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a derivative of piroxicam but has a significantly shorter elimination half-life, which makes it suitable for treating both acute and chronic painful conditions. Lornoxicam is known for its strong analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-inflammatory properties and is available in oral tablets and injectable forms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lornoxicam is indicated for the symptomatic treatment of a variety of painful and inflammatory conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Pain:</b> It is highly effective for the management of acute pain, such as that following surgery, dental procedures, or traumatic injuries.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Pain:</b> It is also used for the symptomatic treatment of chronic inflammatory conditions, such as <b>rheumatoid arthritis</b> and <b>osteoarthritis</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sciatica and Lumbago:</b> It is often used for the short-term relief of pain associated with sciatica and lumbago.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lornoxicam's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Lornoxicam works by non-selectively inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting both COX-1 and COX-2, lornoxicam significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Anti-inflammatory Effects:</b> In addition to its COX inhibition, lornoxicam has been shown to have other anti-inflammatory effects. It can inhibit the release of oxygen free radicals from activated leukocytes (white blood cells) and interfere with the synthesis and release of various inflammatory cytokines.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, lornoxicam is a potent, non-selective NSAID that provides its therapeutic effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its unique advantage over other oxicams is its relatively short half-life, which allows for more flexible dosing and a quicker onset of action for acute pain, while still being effective for chronic conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Pirfenidone
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description: Pirfenidone is an orally administered synthetic small-molecule drug that has anti-fibrotic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. It is a pyridone derivative and is used to slow the progression of fibrosis in various tissues.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication: Pirfenidone is primarily indicated for the treatment of Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF) in adults. IPF is a chronic, progressive, and often fatal lung disease characterized by the formation of scar tissue in the lungs, which makes it difficult to breathe. Pirfenidone is not a cure for IPF but can help slow the decline in lung function and progression of the disease. It is also being studied for its potential use in other fibrotic conditions.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action: The exact mechanism of action is not fully understood, but it is believed to involve multiple pathways:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Anti-fibrotic effects: Pirfenidone is thought to work by modulating and downregulating the production of certain growth factors and procollagens (like TGF-β, or transforming growth factor-beta) that are crucial in the development of scar tissue (fibrosis). By inhibiting these factors, it reduces fibroblast proliferation, which is the process of cells forming and depositing the fibrotic material.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Anti-inflammatory effects: It also has a role in reducing inflammation by inhibiting the production of certain pro-inflammatory cytokines and inflammatory cells.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Antioxidant effects: Pirfenidone can help reduce oxidative stress, which is also a contributing factor to fibrosis.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ertugliflozin + Sitagliptin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ertugliflozin + Sitagliptin is a combination oral medication used to manage type 2 diabetes. It combines two different classes of drugs to improve blood sugar control. <b>Ertugliflozin</b> is a sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor, and <b>Sitagliptin</b> is a dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This medication is indicated as an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with <b>type 2 diabetes mellitus</b>. It is not used for treating type 1 diabetes or for treating diabetic ketoacidosis.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ertugliflozin (SGLT2 inhibitor):</b> Ertugliflozin works by blocking the SGLT2 protein in the kidneys. This protein is responsible for reabsorbing glucose from the urine back into the bloodstream. By inhibiting it, Ertugliflozin causes the kidneys to excrete more glucose in the urine, thereby lowering blood glucose levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Sitagliptin (DPP-4 inhibitor):</b> Sitagliptin works by inhibiting the enzyme DPP-4. This enzyme normally breaks down incretin hormones, such as GLP-1 and GIP, which are released after a meal. By blocking DPP-4, Sitagliptin increases the levels of these hormones, which in turn leads to increased insulin secretion and decreased glucagon secretion from the pancreas. The combined effect is a reduction in blood glucose levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Dexiwin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexibuprofen is a synthetic nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that is the pharmacologically active <b>S(+) enantiomer</b> of the racemic mixture, ibuprofen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This means that while standard ibuprofen contains equal parts of the S(+) and R(-) enantiomers, dexibuprofen is composed solely of the S(+) form, which is responsible for the drug's therapeutic effects. This single-isomer formulation allows it to provide the same analgesic and anti-inflammatory benefits as ibuprofen, often at a lower dose.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexibuprofen is indicated for the symptomatic treatment of various inflammatory and painful conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteoarthritis:</b> For the symptomatic relief of pain and inflammation in osteoarthritis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dysmenorrhea:</b> For the relief of menstrual cramps and pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Pain:</b> For the management of mild to moderate acute pain, such as that following dental surgery or from musculo-skeletal injuries.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Headaches:</b> It can be used for the symptomatic relief of headaches.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Dexibuprofen's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This mechanism is identical to that of its parent compound, ibuprofen.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Dexibuprofen works by non-selectively inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting both COX-1 and COX-2, dexibuprofen significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation and an increased risk of ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Single-Isomer Advantage:</b> The R(-) enantiomer of ibuprofen is largely inactive and is partially converted to the active S(+) form in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By administering only the active S(+) enantiomer as dexibuprofen, a similar therapeutic effect can be achieved with a lower dose, which may potentially lead to a lower incidence of side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, dexibuprofen is a potent, non-selective NSAID that provides its therapeutic effects by blocking the COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes. Its formulation as a single, active isomer allows it to be an effective alternative to racemic ibuprofen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Ketilac
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ketorolac Tromethamine is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "tromethamine" in its name refers to the salt form of the drug, which is used to increase its solubility and stability, making it suitable for a variety of formulations, including oral tablets, injectable solutions, and ophthalmic drops.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Ketorolac is primarily known for its powerful analgesic (pain-relieving) effect, which is often comparable to that of opioid analgesics, but without the risk of physical dependence.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ketorolac is specifically indicated for the <b>short-term (up to 5 days)</b> management of moderately severe acute pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not recommended for chronic use due to the risk of serious side effects. Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative Pain:</b> It is widely used for the management of pain following surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> It can be used for the short-term relief of acute musculoskeletal pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ophthalmic Use:</b> As an eye drop, it is used for the relief of ocular itching caused by seasonal allergic conjunctivitis and for the management of pain and inflammation following cataract surgery.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Renal Colic:</b> In some regions, it is used for the short-term treatment of pain from kidney stones.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ketorolac's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation and pain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Non-selective COX Inhibition:</b> Ketorolac works by non-selectively inhibiting the activity of both <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting these enzymes, ketorolac significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: powerful analgesia and anti-inflammatory action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common and potentially serious side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal bleeding and kidney problems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Opioid Receptor Interaction:</b> Unlike opioids, ketorolac's analgesic effect is not mediated through opioid receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This makes it an effective alternative for patients who cannot take or wish to avoid opioid analgesics, as it does not carry the risk of physical dependence or tolerance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, ketorolac tromethamine is a potent, non-selective NSAID that provides powerful, short-term pain relief by blocking the synthesis of prostaglandins.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its use is limited to acute pain management due to the risk of gastrointestinal, renal, and cardiovascular side effects associated with its non-selective COX inhibition.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Alendronate + Cholecalciferol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> This is a combination product containing Alendronate and Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3).</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Alendronate</b> is a bisphosphonate medication.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Cholecalciferol</b> is a form of Vitamin D.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This medication is primarily used to treat and prevent osteoporosis in postmenopausal women. It is also used to increase bone mass in men with osteoporosis and to treat glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis in men and women. The combination of Alendronate and Cholecalciferol helps to both strengthen bones and ensure adequate calcium absorption.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Alendronate:</b> It works by inhibiting osteoclast-mediated bone resorption. Osteoclasts are the cells that break down bone tissue. By reducing their activity, Alendronate slows bone loss and allows the body to build more bone mass, thereby increasing bone mineral density and reducing the risk of fractures.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3):</b> This component helps the body absorb calcium from the diet. Adequate Vitamin D levels are crucial for the proper mineralization of bone and for maintaining strong bones.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Strolac
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

Ketorolac is primarily known for its powerful analgesic (pain-relieving) effect, which is often comparable to that of opioid analgesics but without the risk of physical dependence.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Sitagliptin + Metformin XR
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Description:</b> Sitagliptin + Metformin XR is a combination medication that contains two active ingredients: sitagliptin, a dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor, and metformin, a biguanide. The "XR" stands for extended-release, meaning the metformin component is released slowly over time. This combination is used to improve blood sugar control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This medication is indicated as an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus when treatment with both sitagliptin and metformin is appropriate. It is not for the treatment of type 1 diabetes or diabetic ketoacidosis.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Sitagliptin:</b> This drug works by inhibiting the enzyme DPP-4. This inhibition increases the levels of incretin hormones (GLP-1 and GIP) in the body. Incretins are released after a meal and stimulate the pancreas to produce more insulin and decrease the release of glucagon. This helps to lower blood glucose levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Metformin:</b> This drug primarily works by reducing glucose production in the liver (hepatic gluconeogenesis) and by increasing the sensitivity of muscle cells to insulin, which helps them take up more glucose from the blood. It also reduces glucose absorption from the intestine.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Iron Ferric Carboxy Maltose
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Iron Ferric Carboxy Maltose is an iron (III)-hydroxide carbohydrate complex. It's an injectable, non-dextran intravenous iron preparation designed to replenish iron stores in the body. Unlike oral iron supplements, it is administered directly into a vein, bypassing the gastrointestinal tract and minimizing side effects like constipation and nausea.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications:</b> This medication is used for the treatment of iron deficiency anemia in adults and children (typically ages 1 and older). It is particularly indicated for patients who:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Have had an unsatisfactory response to oral iron supplements.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Cannot tolerate oral iron due to side effects.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Have non-dialysis-dependent chronic kidney disease.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Have iron deficiency with mild-to-moderate heart failure to improve exercise capacity.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Iron Ferric Carboxy Maltose works by delivering iron directly into the bloodstream. The carbohydrate shell of the complex protects the iron from being released too quickly, preventing free iron toxicity. The iron is then taken up by the cells of the reticuloendothelial system (RES) in the liver, spleen, and bone marrow. From there, it is used to make hemoglobin, a protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen, and is also stored in the body as ferritin, an iron-storage protein. This process efficiently replenishes the body's iron stores and increases hemoglobin levels.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Wintro
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tramadol Hydrochloride is a synthetic opioid analgesic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a centrally acting pain reliever that is structurally related to codeine and morphine. The "HCL" in its name refers to the hydrochloride salt form, which is used for stability and bioavailability. Tramadol is notable for having a dual mechanism of action, which provides its analgesic effect through both opioid and non-opioid pathways.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tramadol is indicated for the management of <b>moderate to moderately severe pain</b> in adults.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It can be used for both acute and chronic pain conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative Pain:</b> For the management of pain following surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> For the relief of pain from conditions like osteoarthritis or fibromyalgia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Neuropathic Pain:</b> It is sometimes used off-label for the treatment of neuropathic pain due to its effect on monoamines.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Painful Conditions:</b> It is used for a wide range of other acute and chronic painful conditions when other analgesics are not sufficient.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tramadol's analgesic effect is a result of its unique dual mechanism of action, which involves both a weak opioid effect and an effect on central neurotransmitters.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Weak Opioid Agonism:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tramadol and its primary active metabolite, O-desmethyltramadol (M1), act as weak agonists at the <b>mu (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>μ</b><b>) opioid receptors</b> in the central nervous system.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Activation of these receptors reduces the perception of pain. While tramadol itself has a low affinity for the mu receptor, its M1 metabolite has a much higher affinity and is responsible for a significant portion of the drug's analgesic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Monoamine Reuptake Inhibition (Non-opioid Action):</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tramadol's second mechanism involves its action on central monoamines.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It inhibits the neuronal reuptake of both <b>norepinephrine</b> and <b>serotonin</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By increasing the concentration of these neurotransmitters in the synaptic cleft, tramadol enhances the descending inhibitory pain pathways.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These pathways act as a "brake" on pain signals, thereby reducing the transmission of pain impulses from the spinal cord to the brain.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the combined effect of a weak opioid action and an enhanced monoamine effect provides tramadol with its synergistic analgesic properties. This dual mechanism contributes to its effectiveness in treating a wide range of pain conditions.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Anastrozol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Anastrozole is a non-steroidal drug that belongs to a class of medications called aromatase inhibitors. It is used as a first-line treatment for hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer in postmenopausal women.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Indication:</b> It is indicated for the treatment of hormone-receptor-positive early breast cancer in postmenopausal women. It is also used to treat advanced or metastatic hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer in postmenopausal women.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Anastrozole works by blocking the enzyme aromatase, which is responsible for converting androgens (male hormones) into estrogens (female hormones) in the body's tissues, particularly in the adrenal glands and fatty tissue. By inhibiting aromatase, anastrozole significantly reduces the amount of estrogen circulating in the body. In hormone-receptor-positive breast cancers, estrogen can stimulate the growth of cancer cells. By lowering estrogen levels, anastrozole helps to slow or stop the growth of these cancer cells.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Taclip
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Tramadol Hydrochloride and Acetaminophen is a fixed-dose oral medication that combines two distinct analgesics: a synthetic opioid (<b>Tramadol</b>) and a non-opioid pain reliever (<b>Acetaminophen</b>, also known as paracetamol). This combination is designed to provide synergistic pain relief by targeting different pain pathways in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is often used to manage pain when a single analgesic is insufficient.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the management of <b>moderate to moderately severe acute pain</b>. It is not typically recommended for long-term chronic pain, and its use is limited to situations where the pain is not adequately relieved by other analgesics.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>
