<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, working together to provide a more comprehensive and potent analgesic effect than either drug alone.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Tramadol Hydrochloride</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Tramadol has a dual mechanism of action:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Weak Opioid Agonism:</b> It acts as a weak agonist at the <b>mu (μ) opioid receptors</b> in the central nervous system.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Monoamine Reuptake Inhibition:</b> It also inhibits the neuronal reuptake of <b>norepinephrine</b> and <b>serotonin</b>, which enhances the body's descending inhibitory pain pathways.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> Tramadol provides its analgesic effect through both opioid and non-opioid pathways, helping to reduce the perception of pain and inhibit pain signals.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Acetaminophen</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> The exact mechanism of acetaminophen is not fully understood, but it is believed to work primarily in the central nervous system.</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prostaglandin Inhibition:</b> It is a weak inhibitor of <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, particularly in the central nervous system. This reduces the production of prostaglandins that are involved in pain and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Central Action:</b> It may also act on other descending pain pathways, possibly involving serotonin, to enhance pain relief.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> Acetaminophen provides a non-opioid analgesic effect that is distinct from tramadol's.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Tramadol and Acetaminophen provides a powerful synergistic analgesic effect.</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tramadol's opioid and monoamine effects are complemented by Acetaminophen's central analgesic action.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By targeting different pain pathways, the combination provides a multi-modal approach to pain relief, often allowing for a lower dose of each component to be used while achieving greater overall efficacy.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This approach can be particularly beneficial as it allows the clinician to use a lower dose of the opioid component (tramadol), which may help to reduce the risk of opioid-related side effects.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Tamsulosin + Solifenacin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Tamsulosin + Solifenacin is a combination medication that includes two different active ingredients. Tamsulosin is an alpha-blocker, while Solifenacin is an anticholinergic (or antimuscarinic) agent. The product is a modified-release tablet designed to provide both a quick-acting and a prolonged effect.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This combination is primarily indicated for the treatment of moderate to severe Lower Urinary Tract Symptoms (LUTS) in men. These symptoms are often associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), also known as an enlarged prostate. It is used in men who have not responded adequately to monotherapy (treatment with a single drug). The symptoms treated include both "voiding symptoms" (difficulty starting urination, weak stream, incomplete emptying) and "storage symptoms" (urinary urgency, increased frequency of urination).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination works by targeting two different mechanisms to relieve LUTS associated with BPH:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Tamsulosin:</b> Tamsulosin is a selective alpha-1A and alpha-1D adrenoceptor antagonist. It works by relaxing the smooth muscles in the prostate and bladder neck. By blocking these receptors, it reduces the obstruction of the urethra caused by the enlarged prostate, which improves urine flow and alleviates voiding symptoms.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Solifenacin:</b> Solifenacin is a competitive and selective antagonist of muscarinic receptors, with a high affinity for M3 receptors in the bladder. It works by blocking the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that causes bladder muscle contractions. By inhibiting these involuntary contractions of the detrusor muscle, solifenacin increases the bladder's capacity and reduces the symptoms of urgency and frequency.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Levocarnitine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Levocarnitine, also known as L-carnitine, is an amino acid derivative that is naturally produced by the body. It is a vital substance required for energy metabolism. It is a white, crystalline powder that is soluble in water.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Levocarnitine is primarily indicated for the treatment of carnitine deficiency, which can be caused by certain genetic disorders, inborn errors of metabolism, or certain medical conditions like end-stage renal disease (ESRD) in patients on dialysis. Carnitine deficiency can lead to various symptoms, including muscle weakness, cardiomyopathy (a weakened and enlarged heart), and hypoketotic hypoglycemia (low blood sugar). It is also used to prevent and treat carnitine deficiency in people whose bodies cannot properly use carnitine from their diet.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Levocarnitine's primary function is to facilitate the transport of long-chain fatty acids into the mitochondria, which are the "powerhouses" of the cell. Fatty acids are a major source of energy for the body, but they cannot enter the mitochondria on their own. Levocarnitine acts as a carrier molecule, forming a complex with fatty acids that allows them to cross the inner mitochondrial membrane. Once inside, the fatty acids are broken down through a process called beta-oxidation to produce energy. By enabling this process, levocarnitine helps the body generate energy, particularly in tissues with high-energy demands like the heart and skeletal muscles. It also helps to remove excess acyl groups and toxic metabolites from the cells.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ace-Fenac Care
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aceclofenac is a synthetic nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that is a derivative of phenylacetic acid. It is closely related to diclofenac but is considered to be better tolerated in the gastrointestinal tract in some studies. Aceclofenac is known for its potent anti-inflammatory, analgesic (pain-relieving), and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties. It is available in oral tablets and other formulations.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aceclofenac is primarily indicated for the management of pain and inflammation associated with a variety of chronic and acute conditions. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteoarthritis:</b> For the symptomatic relief of pain and inflammation in osteoarthritis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatoid Arthritis:</b> For the symptomatic relief of signs and symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ankylosing Spondylitis:</b> For the symptomatic treatment of this chronic inflammatory disease of the spine.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Painful Conditions:</b> It is also used for the management of other painful conditions, such as lumbago, dental pain, and dysmenorrhea (menstrual cramps).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Aceclofenac's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Non-selective COX Inhibition:</b> Aceclofenac works by inhibiting the activity of both <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By inhibiting these enzymes, aceclofenac significantly reduces the production of prostaglandins.</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation and an increased risk of ulcers.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cartilage-Protective Effect:</b> A unique feature of aceclofenac is its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines like interleukin-1 beta (IL-1β) and tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α) in cartilage cells. It has also been shown in some studies to stimulate the synthesis of glycosaminoglycans, which are crucial components of cartilage matrix. This chondroprotective (cartilage-protecting) effect is a unique advantage of aceclofenac over some other NSAIDs, making it a particularly suitable choice for the treatment of osteoarthritis.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, aceclofenac is a potent, non-selective NSAID that provides its therapeutic effects by blocking the synthesis of prostaglandins. Its unique ability to inhibit inflammatory cytokines and potentially promote cartilage synthesis makes it a valuable option for the long-term management of joint diseases.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sofosbuvir+ Velpatasvir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description: Sofosbuvir and velpatasvir are two different antiviral medications combined into a single tablet. This fixed-dose combination is used to treat chronic hepatitis C (HCV), which is a viral infection that causes liver damage. Sofosbuvir is a nucleotide hepatitis C virus (HCV) NS5B polymerase inhibitor, and velpatasvir is an HCV NS5A replication complex inhibitor.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication: This combination is used to treat chronic hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection in adults and children aged 3 years and older. It is effective against all six major HCV genotypes (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6), with or without cirrhosis. In some cases, particularly for patients with decompensated cirrhosis, it may be used in combination with another drug, ribavirin.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action: The combination works by targeting two different proteins essential for the hepatitis C virus to replicate and spread within the body.</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Sofosbuvir: It is a prodrug that is converted into an active form within liver cells. This active form mimics a building block of RNA and works as a "chain terminator," effectively stopping the virus's RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (NS5B) from synthesizing new viral RNA.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Velpatasvir: It inhibits the non-structural protein 5A (NS5A), which is a crucial protein for HCV RNA replication and the assembly of new virus particles. By inhibiting NS5A, velpatasvir prevents the formation of the viral replication complex.</b></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>The use of two different mechanisms of action makes this combination highly effective and helps to prevent the development of drug resistance.</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Linagliptin + Empagliflozin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This product is a combination medication containing two active ingredients: <b>linagliptin</b> and <b>empagliflozin</b>. It is used to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Linagliptin + Empagliflozin is indicated as an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve blood sugar control in adults with <b>type 2 diabetes mellitus</b>. It is often prescribed when a single medication isn't enough to manage blood sugar levels.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The combination works through two distinct, yet complementary, mechanisms:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Linagliptin</b> is a <b>dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor</b>. It works by preventing the breakdown of certain gut hormones called incretins, such as GLP-1 and GIP. These hormones are released after eating and stimulate the pancreas to produce more insulin and decrease the amount of glucose produced by the liver. By preserving these hormones, linagliptin helps to lower blood sugar levels, especially after meals.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Empagliflozin</b> is a <b>sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor</b>. It works by blocking the SGLT2 protein in the kidneys. This protein is responsible for reabsorbing glucose from the urine back into the bloodstream. By blocking this protein, empagliflozin causes the kidneys to excrete more glucose in the urine, thereby lowering blood sugar levels.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Etoricoxib
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Etoricoxib is a synthetic, nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that is a selective inhibitor of the enzyme cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2). It has analgesic (pain-relieving), anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Etoricoxib is used for the symptomatic relief of various painful and inflammatory conditions. This includes:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Osteoarthritis (OA)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Rheumatoid arthritis (RA)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ankylosing spondylitis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Pain and signs of inflammation associated with acute gouty arthritis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Short-term treatment of moderate pain after dental surgery</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Chronic low back pain</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Etoricoxib works by selectively inhibiting the enzyme cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2). The COX-2 enzyme is primarily responsible for producing prostaglandins at sites of inflammation. By blocking COX-2, Etoricoxib prevents the synthesis of these prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that cause pain, swelling, and inflammation. This selective action is intended to provide the pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory benefits of NSAIDs while reducing the risk of gastrointestinal side effects associated with non-selective NSAIDs that also inhibit the COX-1 enzyme (which is important for protecting the stomach lining).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Dystem Plus Tab
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Methocarbamol and Paracetamol (also known as Acetaminophen) is a fixed-dose oral medication that combines a muscle relaxant with a non-opioid analgesic. It is designed to provide dual action: Methocarbamol addresses muscle spasms, and Paracetamol relieves the associated pain and fever.</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Methocarbamol:</b> A centrally acting skeletal muscle relaxant.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Paracetamol:</b> A non-opioid analgesic and antipyretic.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is used to treat a wide range of painful musculoskeletal conditions.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the symptomatic relief of <b>pain and discomfort associated with acute musculoskeletal conditions</b>. These conditions include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Muscle spasms</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strains and sprains</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Low back pain</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Torticollis (wry neck)</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Myalgia (muscle pain)</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is used as an adjunct to rest, physical therapy, and other measures to manage these conditions.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, working together to provide comprehensive relief from pain and muscle spasms.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Methocarbamol (Skeletal Muscle Relaxant)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Methocarbamol does not act directly on skeletal muscles. Its action is primarily in the <b>central nervous system (CNS)</b>. It is believed to act by depressing the CNS, which leads to a generalized reduction in muscle hyper-excitability and spasticity.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> It reduces muscle spasms by calming the CNS. This action helps to break the cycle of pain, where pain from an injury leads to muscle spasms, and the spasms, in turn, intensify the pain.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Direct Muscle Relaxation:</b> Unlike some other drugs, methocarbamol does not directly relax the muscle fibers themselves. Its effect is a result of its sedative properties on the brain and spinal cord.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Paracetamol (Analgesic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> The exact mechanism of paracetamol is not fully understood, but its effects are believed to be primarily central.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> It is a weak inhibitor of <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, particularly in the central nervous system. This reduces the production of prostaglandins in the brain and spinal cord, which are responsible for pain and fever signals.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Anti-inflammatory Effect:</b> Unlike NSAIDs, paracetamol has a very limited anti-inflammatory effect at therapeutic doses because it is a poor inhibitor of COX enzymes in the periphery (at the site of inflammation).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, Methocarbamol relieves the muscle spasms by depressing the CNS, while Paracetamol provides pain relief by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis in the brain. The combination provides a multi-modal approach to treating painful musculoskeletal conditions, with each drug addressing a different component of the symptoms.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Apixaban
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Apixaban is an oral anticoagulant medication, often referred to as a "blood thinner." It is a member of the class of drugs known as direct oral anticoagulants (DOACs) and specifically, it is a direct Factor Xa inhibitor.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Apixaban is used to prevent and treat blood clots in various cardiovascular conditions. This includes:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Reducing the risk of stroke and systemic embolism in patients with non-valvular atrial fibrillation (an irregular heart rhythm).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Preventing deep vein thrombosis (DVT) and pulmonary embolism (PE) in patients who have undergone hip or knee replacement surgery.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Treating existing DVT and PE, and reducing the risk of them happening again.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The process of blood clotting involves a complex series of steps known as the coagulation cascade. A key player in this cascade is an enzyme called Factor Xa. Apixaban works by reversibly and selectively inhibiting Factor Xa. By blocking Factor Xa, apixaban prevents the final step of the cascade—the conversion of prothrombin into thrombin—which is essential for the formation of a blood clot. This action effectively reduces the blood's ability to clot, thereby preventing the formation of new clots and the growth of existing ones.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Aceclofenac Plus Tab
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Aceclofenac, Chlorzoxazone, and Paracetamol is a fixed-dose oral medication that provides a multi-modal approach to treating painful musculoskeletal conditions. This formulation brings together a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID), a centrally acting muscle relaxant, and a non-opioid analgesic.</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Aceclofenac:</b> A potent NSAID with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chlorzoxazone:</b> A centrally acting skeletal muscle relaxant.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Paracetamol (Acetaminophen):</b> A non-opioid analgesic and antipyretic.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is designed to address the three key components of musculoskeletal pain: inflammation, pain, and muscle spasm.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the symptomatic relief of <b>pain, inflammation, and muscle spasms</b> associated with acute musculoskeletal disorders. Its common uses include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Muscle spasms and cramps</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acute low back pain</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strains and sprains</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Torticollis (wry neck)</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fibromyalgia and myalgia</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is used as an adjunct to rest, physical therapy, and other measures.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this three-drug combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of each of its components:</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Aceclofenac (Anti-inflammatory and Analgesic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Aceclofenac is a non-selective NSAID that works by inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By inhibiting COX-2, it significantly reduces the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are the primary mediators of inflammation and pain at the site of injury. This action directly addresses the inflammatory component of the condition.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Chlorzoxazone (Skeletal Muscle Relaxant)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Chlorzoxazone's action is primarily in the <b>central nervous system (CNS)</b>. It is believed to act by depressing polysynaptic reflexes in the spinal cord and subcortical areas of the brain.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By depressing the CNS, Chlorzoxazone effectively reduces muscle hyperactivity and spasticity. It helps to break the cycle of pain-spasm-pain, where pain from an injury leads to muscle spasms, which in turn exacerbate the pain. Its effect is on the nervous system's control of muscle tone, rather than a direct effect on the muscle itself.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">3. Paracetamol (Analgesic and Antipyretic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> The exact mechanism of Paracetamol is not fully understood, but its effects are believed to be primarily central. It is a weak inhibitor of <b>COX enzymes</b> in the central nervous system.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> Paracetamol provides a powerful analgesic and antipyretic effect that is distinct from the anti-inflammatory action of aceclofenac. It targets a different pain pathway, complementing the pain relief provided by aceclofenac and reducing fever if present.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, this combination provides a comprehensive therapeutic effect: Aceclofenac reduces inflammation and pain at the source, Chlorzoxazone relieves the associated muscle spasms by acting on the CNS, and Paracetamol provides additional pain relief through its central analgesic action.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sevelamer carbonate
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Description:</b> Sevelamer carbonate is a polymer drug that is not absorbed by the body. It is a non-calcium, metal-free phosphate-binding agent.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is used to control high blood levels of phosphorus (hyperphosphatemia) in adult patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD), particularly those who are on dialysis. It can also be used in patients with CKD who are not on dialysis but have high serum phosphorus levels.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Mechanism of Action:</b> When taken with meals, Sevelamer carbonate works in the gastrointestinal tract. Its positively charged amine groups bind to the negatively charged phosphate ions from food. This binding prevents the phosphate from being absorbed into the bloodstream. The sevelamer-phosphate complex is then eliminated from the body through the feces. By preventing phosphorus absorption, it helps lower and control serum phosphate levels in the blood.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Prucalopride
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b> Prucalopride is a medication used to treat chronic idiopathic constipation (CIC). It is a first-in-class dihydrobenzofuran-carboxamide compound. It is a selective and high-affinity serotonin 5-HT4 receptor agonist.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b> Prucalopride is indicated for the symptomatic treatment of chronic constipation in adults, particularly in cases where laxatives have failed to provide adequate relief.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b> Prucalopride acts as a selective agonist for serotonin type 4 (5-HT4) receptors. By binding to and activating these receptors in the gastrointestinal tract, it stimulates colonic motility. This leads to the release of acetylcholine, an excitatory neurotransmitter that causes the contraction of the muscle layers of the colon. This action enhances the natural propulsive movements of the colon, known as peristalsis, which increases bowel motility and facilitates defecation.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Myfamic
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mefenamic Acid</b> is a <b>nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID)</b> of the fenamate class. It's used for its <b>analgesic</b> (pain-relieving), <b>anti-inflammatory</b>, and <b>antipyretic</b> (fever-reducing) properties. Unlike many other NSAIDs, it's particularly known for its effectiveness in treating pain related to the female reproductive system. It is commonly available in oral capsule or tablet form.</span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mefenamic Acid is primarily indicated for the short-term treatment of mild to moderate pain. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Primary Dysmenorrhea:</b> It's one of the most common and effective treatments for menstrual cramps and pain.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Musculoskeletal Pain:</b> For the relief of pain associated with conditions like sprains, strains, and other injuries.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Painful Conditions:</b> It can also be used for the short-term management of mild to moderate pain from various causes.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mefenamic Acid's mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of <b>prostaglandins</b>, which are fatty acid compounds that act as mediators of pain, inflammation, and fever.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Mefenamic Acid works by inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b> By blocking these enzymes, mefenamic acid reduces the production of prostaglandins throughout the body. The inhibition of <b>COX-2</b> is primarily responsible for its therapeutic effects, such as reducing inflammation and pain. The inhibition of <b>COX-1</b> can lead to some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, like gastrointestinal upset.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Unique Antagonism:</b> In addition to its COX inhibition, mefenamic acid is unique because it may also have an antagonistic effect on prostaglandin receptors. This means it not only prevents the production of prostaglandins but also blocks their ability to cause pain and muscle contractions, which is particularly relevant for its efficacy in treating menstrual cramps.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sacubitril +Valsartan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Sacubitril + Valsartan is a combination medication that includes two different active ingredients. Sacubitril is a neprilysin inhibitor, and Valsartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This drug is primarily indicated for the treatment of chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) in adults. It is used to reduce the risk of cardiovascular death and hospitalization for heart failure.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Sacubitril:</b> This component inhibits the enzyme neprilysin. Neprilysin is responsible for breaking down natriuretic peptides, which are hormones that help to regulate blood pressure and fluid balance. By inhibiting neprilysin, sacubitril increases the levels of these peptides, leading to vasodilation (widening of blood vessels), reduced blood pressure, and increased sodium excretion by the kidneys.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Valsartan:</b> This component blocks the effects of angiotensin II, a powerful hormone that constricts blood vessels and increases blood pressure. By blocking the angiotensin II receptors, valsartan causes blood vessels to relax and widen, lowering blood pressure and reducing the workload on the heart.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ticagrelor
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Ticagrelor is an oral antiplatelet agent. It is a blood thinner that prevents platelets from clumping together to form dangerous blood clots that can lead to a heart attack or stroke. Unlike some other antiplatelet drugs, Ticagrelor is a direct-acting drug that does not need to be metabolized to be active. It is often used in combination with aspirin.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Ticagrelor is indicated to reduce the risk of cardiovascular death, myocardial infarction (heart attack), and stroke in patients with:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) or a history of myocardial infarction.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Coronary Artery Disease (CAD) who are at high risk for a first heart attack or stroke.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Acute ischemic stroke or high-risk transient ischemic attack (TIA).</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Ticagrelor is a selective and reversible P2Y12 receptor antagonist. The P2Y12 receptor is located on the surface of platelets. When adenosine diphosphate (ADP) binds to this receptor, it causes platelets to activate and aggregate, leading to the formation of a blood clot. Ticagrelor works by binding to this receptor, preventing ADP from binding and thus inhibiting platelet activation and aggregation. It also weakly inhibits the uptake of adenosine, which further contributes to its antiplatelet effect.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Osteowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diacerin is a synthetic drug that is classified as a <b>slow-acting symptom-modifying drug</b> for osteoarthritis. It is a pro-drug of the active metabolite <b>rhein</b>. Unlike nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) that provide immediate symptomatic relief, diacerin works by a different mechanism to inhibit inflammation and cartilage degradation over a longer period. It is available in oral capsules.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diacerin is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>osteoarthritis</b>, a degenerative joint disease. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptomatic relief of osteoarthritis:</b> It is used for the management of pain and discomfort in patients with osteoarthritis, particularly of the hip and knee.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Long-term treatment:</b> Due to its slow-acting nature, it is intended for long-term use, and its effects on symptoms may not be noticeable for several weeks.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Diacerin's mechanism of action is unique as it does not inhibit cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes like NSAIDs. Instead, its therapeutic effects are mediated through the inhibition of key inflammatory cytokines and cartilage-degrading enzymes.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Interleukin-1 Beta (IL-1β):</b> This is the primary mechanism of diacerin. After oral administration, diacerin is rapidly metabolized into its active form, <b>rhein</b>. Rhein inhibits the synthesis and activity of the pro-inflammatory cytokine <b>interleukin-1 beta (IL-1β)</b>. IL-1β is a key mediator of inflammation and is a major driver of cartilage destruction in osteoarthritis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Modulation of Matrix-Degrading Enzymes:</b> By inhibiting IL-1β, diacerin indirectly reduces the production and activity of enzymes that degrade cartilage, such as matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) and aggrecanases. This helps to slow down the progressive damage to the joint's cartilage.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cartilage Synthesis Promotion:</b> Diacerin has also been shown to promote the synthesis of cartilage components, such as glycosaminoglycans and hyaluronic acid, in some studies. This contributes to its potential to not only slow down cartilage degradation but also help in its repair.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Prostaglandin Inhibition:</b> Because it does not inhibit COX enzymes, diacerin has no effect on prostaglandin synthesis. This is why it does not provide immediate pain relief like NSAIDs, but it also means it avoids the gastrointestinal side effects associated with them.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, diacerin works by inhibiting the pro-inflammatory cytokine IL-1β, which slows down the degradation of cartilage and has a beneficial effect on the long-term progression of osteoarthritis. Its slow-acting and non-NSAID mechanism makes it a valuable alternative for long-term therapy.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Vortioxetine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;<b>Description:</b> Vortioxetine is an antidepressant medication used to treat Major Depressive Disorder (MDD). It is classified as a "serotonin modulator and stimulator" (SMS) because it has a unique, multimodal mechanism of action.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Vortioxetine is primarily indicated for the treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) in adults.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The exact mechanism is not fully understood, but it is believed to work by enhancing serotonergic activity in the central nervous system. It has a multimodal action, which means it works in more than one way:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It inhibits the reuptake of serotonin by blocking the serotonin transporter (SERT).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It also acts directly on several serotonin receptors: it is an agonist of the 5-HT1A receptor, a partial agonist of the 5-HT1B receptor, and an antagonist of the 5-HT3, 5-HT1D, and 5-HT7 receptors.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This combined action is thought to increase the extracellular concentration of serotonin and other neurotransmitters like norepinephrine and dopamine</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Mirabegron
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Mirabegron is a prescription medication used to treat overactive bladder (OAB) symptoms. It comes in an extended-release tablet or oral suspension form.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Mirabegron is indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Overactive bladder (OAB) with symptoms of urge urinary incontinence, urgency, and urinary frequency. It can be used as a monotherapy or in combination with other medications like solifenacin.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Neurogenic detrusor overactivity (NDO), a bladder dysfunction caused by neurological impairment, in pediatric patients aged 3 years and older.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Mirabegron is a selective beta-3 adrenergic receptor agonist. It works by activating the beta-3 adrenergic receptors in the bladder. This activation causes the detrusor smooth muscle, which surrounds the bladder, to relax during the storage phase of the urinary fill-void cycle. By relaxing this muscle, the bladder's capacity to store urine increases, which in turn reduces the symptoms of urgency, frequency, and incontinence.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Anonox
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tenoxicam is a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that belongs to the oxicam class. It is a derivative of thienothiazine and is used for its powerful analgesic (pain-relieving), anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties. Like other oxicams, it has a very long elimination half-life, which allows for convenient once-daily dosing. It is available in oral tablets, injections, and topical gels.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Due to its long half-life, tenoxicam is primarily indicated for the long-term management of chronic inflammatory diseases. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rheumatic Diseases:</b> For the symptomatic relief of signs and symptoms of <b>rheumatoid arthritis</b>, <b>osteoarthritis</b>, and <b>ankylosing spondylitis</b>.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Musculoskeletal Disorders:</b> It is also used for the short-term treatment of painful conditions like tendinitis, bursitis, and sprains.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gout:</b> For the symptomatic treatment of acute gouty arthritis attacks.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tenoxicam's primary mechanism of action is based on its ability to inhibit the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Cyclooxygenase (COX) Enzymes:</b> Tenoxicam works by non-selectively inhibiting the activity of the <b>cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)</b> and <b>cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2)</b> enzymes. These enzymes are responsible for converting arachidonic acid into prostaglandins.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking Prostaglandin Synthesis:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-2</b> is responsible for the drug's primary therapeutic effects: reducing inflammation, pain, and fever.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The <b>inhibition of COX-1</b> is responsible for some of the common side effects of NSAIDs, such as gastrointestinal irritation, ulcer formation, and a potential for bleeding.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Other Anti-inflammatory Effects:</b> In addition to its COX inhibition, tenoxicam also has other anti-inflammatory properties. It has been shown to inhibit the release of oxygen free radicals from activated leukocytes (white blood cells) and inhibit the migration of leukocytes into inflamed tissues. It can also inhibit the synthesis and release of certain pro-inflammatory cytokines.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, tenoxicam is a potent, non-selective NSAID that provides its therapeutic effects by blocking the synthesis of prostaglandins. Its long half-life and additional anti-inflammatory properties make it a valuable option for the long-term management of chronic inflammatory conditions.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Azilsartan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Azilsartan is a medication that belongs to a class of drugs called angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs). It is used to treat high blood pressure, also known as hypertension. It is available as a prodrug, azilsartan medoxomil, which is converted to its active form (azilsartan) in the body.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Azilsartan is primarily indicated for the treatment of hypertension in adults. It may be used alone or in combination with other blood pressure-lowering agents. By lowering blood pressure, it helps to reduce the risk of cardiovascular events such as strokes and heart attacks.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Azilsartan works by selectively blocking the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors. Angiotensin II is a powerful hormone that causes blood vessels to narrow and constrict, which increases blood pressure. By blocking the AT1 receptors, azilsartan prevents angiotensin II from binding to them. This leads to the relaxation and widening of blood vessels, which in turn lowers blood pressure and improves blood flow.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Nebivolol + Valsartan
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> This product is a combination medication that contains two active ingredients: Nebivolol and Valsartan. Nebivolol is a beta-blocker, and Valsartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB). The combination works synergistically to lower blood pressure.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> It is indicated for the treatment of essential hypertension (high blood pressure). The combination is used when treatment with a single drug is not sufficient to control blood pressure.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Nebivolol:</b> It is a highly selective beta-1 adrenergic receptor antagonist. It blocks the effects of adrenaline on the heart, leading to a decrease in heart rate and contractility, which reduces blood pressure. It also has a unique mechanism of action that promotes vasodilation (widening of blood vessels) by stimulating the release of nitric oxide.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Valsartan:</b> It works by blocking the angiotensin II receptor. Angiotensin II is a powerful vasoconstrictor (narrows blood vessels) and also stimulates the release of aldosterone, which causes the body to retain sodium and water. By blocking its effects, Valsartan causes blood vessels to relax and widen, leading to a decrease in blood pressure.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Ulipristal Acetate
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ulipristal acetate is a synthetic steroid derivative of 19-norprogesterone. It is a selective progesterone receptor modulator (SPRM) with both antagonistic and partial agonistic effects on the progesterone receptor. This means it can either block or mimic the effects of progesterone in different tissues.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ulipristal acetate is primarily indicated for two main uses:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Emergency Contraception:</b> It is used to prevent pregnancy after unprotected sexual intercourse or a known or suspected contraceptive failure. It is effective for up to 120 hours (5 days) after the event.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Uterine Fibroids:</b> In a different formulation, it is used for the pre-operative treatment of moderate to severe symptoms of uterine fibroids in adult women of reproductive age.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The mechanism of action of ulipristal acetate depends on its use:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>For Emergency Contraception:</b> Its primary mechanism is the inhibition or delay of ovulation. By binding to progesterone receptors, it prevents or postpones the release of an egg from the ovary, even when the luteinizing hormone (LH) surge has already begun. This makes it more effective than other emergency contraceptives in some cases. It may also alter the endometrial lining, making it less receptive to a fertilized egg.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>For Uterine Fibroids:</b> It acts as an antagonist on the progesterone receptors in the uterine fibroid tissue. This inhibits cell proliferation and induces programmed cell death (apoptosis) in the fibroid cells, leading to a reduction in their size and volume.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Samdix
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Sumatriptan Succinate and Naproxen Sodium is a fixed-dose oral medication designed for the acute treatment of migraine headaches. It brings together two different classes of drugs to address the complex pathology of migraines: a <b>triptan</b> (Sumatriptan) and a <b>nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID)</b> (Naproxen Sodium). This formulation is designed to provide dual-action relief by targeting both the vascular and inflammatory components of a migraine attack.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the <b>acute treatment of migraine attacks, with or without aura</b>, in adults. It is important to note that it is <b>not</b> intended for the prophylactic (preventive) treatment of migraines or for the management of other types of headaches. It should be taken at the first sign of a migraine attack.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, working together to provide a more comprehensive and potent effect on migraine symptoms than either drug alone.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Sumatriptan Succinate (Triptan Component)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Sumatriptan acts as a selective agonist of <b>serotonin receptors, specifically the 5-</b><b>HT1B</b><b>​</b><b> and 5-</b><b>HT1D</b><b>​</b><b> receptor subtypes</b>. These receptors are found on cranial blood vessels and on nerve endings in the central nervous system.</span></span></span></li>
