	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> When a migraine attack occurs, there is a vasodilation (widening) of the cranial blood vessels and a release of pro-inflammatory neuropeptides. Sumatriptan's action directly counters these effects:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vasoconstriction:</b> By activating the 5-HT1B​ receptors on the intracranial blood vessels, sumatriptan causes them to constrict, which helps to normalize blood flow and relieve the throbbing pain of a migraine.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Neuropeptide Release:</b> By activating the 5-HT1D​ receptors on nerve endings, sumatriptan inhibits the release of pro-inflammatory neuropeptides like Substance P and calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP), thereby reducing the neurogenic inflammation that contributes to the migraine pain.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Naproxen Sodium (NSAID Component)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Naproxen Sodium is a non-selective NSAID that works by inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> In the context of a migraine, the inflammatory cascade is a key driver of pain and other symptoms. By inhibiting COX enzymes, naproxen sodium prevents the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are potent inflammatory mediators. This action helps to reduce the underlying inflammation and associated pain.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Sumatriptan and Naproxen Sodium provides a synergistic effect by targeting different aspects of the migraine pathology. Sumatriptan addresses the vascular and neuronal components, while Naproxen Sodium addresses the inflammatory component. This multi-modal approach leads to faster, more complete, and more sustained pain relief compared to either drug used alone.</span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Vinamol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Paracetamol, also known as Acetaminophen, is a widely used and well-tolerated drug that acts as a non-opioid analgesic (pain reliever) and antipyretic (fever reducer). It is a central-acting drug, meaning its effects are primarily mediated in the central nervous system (CNS). Unlike nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) like ibuprofen or aspirin, paracetamol has very little anti-inflammatory effect in the periphery.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Paracetamol is indicated for the symptomatic relief of a wide range of conditions. Its key indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pain Relief:</b> It is used for the treatment of mild to moderate pain, such as headaches, muscle aches, toothaches, and pain from colds and flu.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fever Reduction:</b> It is a highly effective antipyretic for reducing fever in adults and children.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is often the first-line treatment for pain and fever, especially in children and for individuals who cannot tolerate NSAIDs due to gastrointestinal issues or other conditions.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The exact mechanism of action of paracetamol is not fully understood, but its effects are believed to be primarily central and distinct from those of traditional NSAIDs.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Central COX Inhibition:</b> Paracetamol is a weak inhibitor of <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, particularly in the brain and spinal cord (the CNS). By inhibiting COX in the CNS, it reduces the synthesis of prostaglandins.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reducing Pain Signals:</b> In the CNS, prostaglandins play a key role in sensitizing nerve endings and transmitting pain signals. By reducing their synthesis, paracetamol raises the pain threshold, thereby providing its analgesic effect.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antipyretic Effect:</b> Fever is caused by the action of pyrogens on the hypothalamus in the brain, which leads to an increase in prostaglandin E2​ synthesis. Paracetamol inhibits this synthesis in the hypothalamus, which causes vasodilation (widening of blood vessels) and sweating, allowing the body to dissipate heat and lower the fever.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lack of Peripheral Action:</b> Unlike NSAIDs, paracetamol is a poor inhibitor of COX enzymes in the periphery (at the site of inflammation). This is why it has very little anti-inflammatory effect and does not cause the common gastrointestinal side effects associated with NSAIDs, such as stomach upset or ulcers.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, paracetamol provides its pain-relieving and fever-reducing effects by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis primarily in the central nervous system, without a significant anti-inflammatory effect in the periphery.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Klyrovi DR Tablet
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This unique combination of Diclofenac Sodium and Misoprostol is a fixed-dose oral medication with a specialized, multi-layered tablet structure. The tablet is engineered with a <b>gastro-resistant core</b> containing <b>Diclofenac Sodium</b> and an <b>immediate-release outer mantle</b> containing <b>Misoprostol</b>. This innovative formulation is designed to provide the therapeutic benefits of a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) while simultaneously and immediately protecting the gastrointestinal lining from its damaging effects.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for patients who require long-term NSAID therapy for conditions like <b>osteoarthritis</b> and <b>rheumatoid arthritis</b> but are at a <b>high risk for developing gastric or duodenal ulcers</b>. This risk is typically seen in patients with a history of gastrointestinal bleeding or ulcers, the elderly, or those on concomitant therapies that increase the risk of GI complications.</span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The unique formulation ensures that the gastroprotective agent (misoprostol) is released first, offering immediate protection, while the diclofenac is released later in the small intestine, bypassing the stomach and further reducing gastric irritation.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components and the strategic timing of their release.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Misoprostol (Immediate-Release Outer Mantle):</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Misoprostol is a synthetic analog of prostaglandin E1​.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role:</b> The immediate-release outer mantle of misoprostol dissolves quickly in the stomach. Its main actions are to:</span></span></span>
		<ul style="list-style-type:square">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibit Gastric Acid Secretion:</b> It directly inhibits the secretion of stomach acid, which is a key factor in ulcer formation.</span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enhance Mucosal Protection:</b> It stimulates the production of mucus and bicarbonate, which form a protective barrier over the stomach lining.</span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		</li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Timing:</b> The immediate release of misoprostol ensures that the stomach is protected from the very beginning of the digestive process, anticipating the arrival of the diclofenac.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diclofenac Sodium (Gastro-Resistant Core):</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Diclofenac works by inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role:</b> The gastro-resistant core prevents the diclofenac from dissolving in the acidic environment of the stomach. It remains intact until it reaches the more alkaline environment of the small intestine. This delayed release minimizes local irritation to the stomach lining, which can be a direct cause of gastritis. Once released, it inhibits prostaglandin synthesis, providing the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects necessary for managing arthritis.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect:</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This formulation's design provides a synchronized and synergistic effect. Misoprostol provides immediate gastroprotection, mitigating the ulcerogenic effects of NSAIDs, while the delayed release of diclofenac provides effective pain and inflammation relief. This multi-layered approach offers a safer long-term NSAID therapy for at-risk patients.</span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Redcitta
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Paracetamol (also known as Acetaminophen) and Orphenadrine Citrate is a fixed-dose oral medication that combines a non-opioid analgesic with a centrally acting muscle relaxant. This formulation is designed to provide dual-action relief: Paracetamol addresses the pain, while Orphenadrine Citrate addresses the associated muscle spasms and discomfort.</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Paracetamol:</b> A non-opioid analgesic and antipyretic.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Orphenadrine Citrate:</b> A centrally acting skeletal muscle relaxant with mild anticholinergic and antihistaminic properties.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is used to treat a wide range of painful musculoskeletal conditions where muscle spasms are a significant component.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the symptomatic relief of <b>painful musculoskeletal conditions associated with muscle spasms</b>. Its common uses include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Muscle strains and sprains</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acute back pain</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fibromyalgia and myalgia</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Torticollis (wry neck)</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is used as an adjunct to rest, physical therapy, and other measures to manage these conditions.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, working together to provide comprehensive relief from pain and muscle spasms.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Paracetamol (Analgesic)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> The exact mechanism of paracetamol is not fully understood, but its effects are believed to be primarily central.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> It is a weak inhibitor of <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, particularly in the central nervous system. This reduces the production of prostaglandins in the brain and spinal cord, which are responsible for pain and fever signals.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>No Anti-inflammatory Effect:</b> Unlike NSAIDs, paracetamol has a very limited anti-inflammatory effect at therapeutic doses because it is a poor inhibitor of COX enzymes in the periphery.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Orphenadrine Citrate (Skeletal Muscle Relaxant)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Orphenadrine's action is primarily in the <b>central nervous system (CNS)</b>. It does not act directly on the muscles themselves but rather on the brain and spinal cord.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> It is believed to act by depressing polysynaptic reflexes in the spinal cord and subcortical areas of the brain, thereby reducing muscle hyperactivity and spasticity. It also has mild anticholinergic (muscarinic receptor antagonist) and antihistaminic properties, which may contribute to its muscle-relaxing effects and sedation.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By reducing the CNS-mediated reflex that causes muscle spasms, orphenadrine helps to break the cycle of pain and spasm.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, Paracetamol provides pain relief by acting on central pain pathways, while Orphenadrine Citrate relieves the associated muscle spasms by depressing the central nervous system. The combination provides a multi-modal approach, with each drug addressing a different component of the symptoms of musculoskeletal conditions.</span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Eznepo
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Naproxen Sodium and Esomeprazole is a fixed-dose oral medication that combines a potent nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) with a proton pump inhibitor (PPI). This unique formulation is designed to provide the anti-inflammatory and analgesic benefits of naproxen while simultaneously protecting the stomach and small intestine from the risk of developing NSAID-induced ulcers and erosions.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for patients who require chronic NSAID therapy for conditions like <b>osteoarthritis</b>, <b>rheumatoid arthritis</b>, and <b>ankylosing spondylitis</b> and are at <b>high risk for developing gastric ulcers</b>. High-risk patients typically include those with a history of ulcers, the elderly, or those on long-term corticosteroid therapy.</span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination allows for the long-term use of naproxen while mitigating its most common and serious side effect: gastrointestinal damage.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Naproxen Sodium (NSAID Component):</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Naproxen is a non-selective NSAID that works by inhibiting the <b>cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes</b>, specifically COX-1 and COX-2.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> This inhibition prevents the synthesis of prostaglandins, which are key mediators of inflammation, pain, and fever. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of the drug are due to its inhibition of COX-2 at the site of inflammation.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Side Effect:</b> A major side effect of NSAIDs is that they also inhibit COX-1, which reduces the production of prostaglandins that are essential for protecting the stomach lining. This is a primary reason NSAIDs can cause ulcers.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Esomeprazole (Proton Pump Inhibitor Component):</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Esomeprazole is a proton pump inhibitor. It is a prodrug that is converted to its active form in the acid-secreting parietal cells of the stomach. The activated form then <b>irreversibly binds</b> to the <b>H+/K+-ATPase enzyme</b> (the proton pump).</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Action:</b> By irreversibly blocking the proton pump, esomeprazole effectively inhibits the final step of gastric acid secretion, leading to a profound and prolonged reduction in stomach acid.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Counteracting Side Effects:</b> The acid-suppressing action of esomeprazole counteracts the ulcerogenic effects of naproxen. By reducing the acidity of the stomach, it allows the stomach lining to heal and reduces the risk of new ulcers forming.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, the combination provides a dual-action therapy: naproxen effectively manages the pain and inflammation of chronic conditions, while esomeprazole provides potent gastroprotection, making the treatment safer for at-risk patients who need long-term NSAID therapy.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Redzi
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Remdesivir (brand name Veklury) is a broad-spectrum antiviral medication. It is a prodrug, meaning it is an inactive compound that is metabolized into its active form within the body. It is administered intravenously (into a vein) by a healthcare professional.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Remdesivir is indicated for the treatment of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) in adults and pediatric patients who meet certain criteria. This includes hospitalized patients, as well as non-hospitalized patients who have mild-to-moderate COVID-19 and are at high risk for progression to severe disease, including hospitalization or death.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Remdesivir is a nucleoside analog. Once it enters a cell, it is metabolized into its active form, remdesivir triphosphate. This active metabolite acts as a fake building block (an analog of adenosine triphosphate) for the virus's genetic material (RNA). The virus, SARS-CoV-2, uses an enzyme called RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) to replicate its RNA. Remdesivir triphosphate competes with the natural building blocks and is incorporated into the new viral RNA strand. This incorporation causes delayed chain termination, which disrupts the viral replication process and prevents the virus from spreading.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Molvir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Molvir is the brand name for the generic drug molnupiravir. It is an oral, antiviral medication that is a prodrug of N4-hydroxycytidine (NHC). This means it is a substance that is metabolized within the body to become its active form, NHC. It is an orally active drug, developed to treat certain RNA viruses.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Molvir (molnupiravir) is indicated for the treatment of mild to moderate COVID-19 in adults who have a positive SARS-CoV-2 diagnostic test and are at a high risk for developing severe illness, including hospitalization or death. It is used in non-hospitalized patients and must be initiated within 5 days of symptom onset. It is not a substitute for COVID-19 vaccination.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Molnupiravir works by a unique mechanism known as "lethal mutagenesis" or "viral error catastrophe." The process is as follows:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">After being ingested, molnupiravir is converted into its active form, NHC, which is then phosphorylated to its active 5'-triphosphate form (NHC-TP).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">NHC-TP is a ribonucleoside analog that mimics both cytidine (C) and uridine (U), which are natural building blocks of RNA.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">During viral replication, the SARS-CoV-2 virus's RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) enzyme mistakenly incorporates NHC-TP into the newly made RNA strand instead of the correct C or U.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Because NHC-TP can mimic both C and U, it causes a high rate of mutations when the new viral RNA is transcribed and copied.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">These mutations accumulate to the point where the viral genome becomes too flawed to function, effectively stopping the virus from replicating and leading to its extinction within the host's body.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Fozipa
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Dapagliflozin is an oral medication that belongs to a class of drugs called sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitors. It is available in tablet form and is used to treat a variety of conditions, including type 2 diabetes, heart failure, and chronic kidney disease.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Dapagliflozin is indicated for:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Improving glycemic control</b> in adults with type 2 diabetes, along with diet and exercise. It can be used as a monotherapy or in combination with other anti-diabetic medications.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reducing the risk of cardiovascular death and hospitalization for heart failure</b> in adults with heart failure.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reducing the risk of kidney disease worsening, end-stage kidney disease, cardiovascular death, and hospitalization for heart failure</b> in adults with chronic kidney disease.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Dapagliflozin works by selectively and reversibly inhibiting the SGLT2 protein, which is primarily located in the kidneys' proximal convoluted tubules. The SGLT2 protein is responsible for reabsorbing about 90% of the glucose that is filtered by the kidneys back into the bloodstream. By blocking this protein, dapagliflozin prevents glucose from being reabsorbed and causes it to be excreted in the urine. This action lowers blood sugar levels in people with diabetes.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">In addition to its effects on glucose, dapagliflozin's mechanism of action also involves reducing sodium reabsorption in the kidneys. This leads to a number of beneficial effects on the cardiovascular system and kidneys, including a reduction in blood volume, blood pressure, and pressure within the kidneys, which helps to explain its effectiveness in treating heart failure and chronic kidney disease.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Paxkol

It is derived from a compound initially found in the Pacific yew tree and is administered intravenously. It is available in different formulations, including an albumin-bound version.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp; <b>Indication:</b> It is used to treat a range of cancers, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ovarian cancer</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Breast cancer</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Non-small cell lung cancer</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Kaposi's sarcoma (a cancer commonly found in patients with AIDS)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Cervical cancer</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Pancreatic cancer</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Esophageal and gastric cancers</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Head and neck cancer</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp; <b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Paclitaxel is a mitotic inhibitor that works by interfering with the normal function of microtubules, which are essential structures for cell division. Specifically, it binds to the beta-tubulin subunits of microtubules and stabilizes them, preventing them from disassembling. This "freezes" the microtubules in place, disrupting the formation of the mitotic spindle, which is required to separate chromosomes during cell division (mitosis). As a result, the cancer cells cannot divide and replicate, leading to their death. This mechanism makes it a powerful anticancer agent.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Thermafane
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Artemether and Lumefantrine is a fixed-dose oral medication used for the treatment of malaria. It is a type of <b>Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT)</b>, which is the current global standard of care for treating uncomplicated malaria. This combination leverages the distinct properties of its two components: Artemether provides a rapid, potent action to quickly reduce parasite load, while Lumefantrine provides a longer-acting effect to clear the remaining parasites and prevent a relapse.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the treatment of <b>uncomplicated <i>Plasmodium falciparum</i> malaria</b> in regions where there is a high risk of parasite resistance to other antimalarial drugs. It can be used in both adults and children.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, which target the malarial parasite at different stages of its life cycle in the red blood cells.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Artemether (Artemisinin Derivative)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Artemether is a potent and fast-acting antimalarial. Its mechanism is believed to involve the cleavage of the endoperoxide bridge within its molecular structure by the parasite's own iron (heme) within the red blood cells. This process generates highly reactive and toxic <b>free radicals</b> and other cytotoxic compounds.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> These free radicals quickly and non-selectively damage essential parasite proteins and membranes. This action leads to a rapid and significant reduction in the number of parasites in the patient's blood, providing a swift clinical response and resolving symptoms like fever and chills. Artemether has a short half-life and is rapidly cleared from the body.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Lumefantrine (Aryl-alcohol)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Lumefantrine has a longer half-life and works by a different mechanism. It is believed to inhibit the detoxification of heme, a toxic byproduct of the parasite's digestion of hemoglobin. In a normal process, the parasite would convert heme into a non-toxic compound called hemozoin.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By inhibiting this detoxification, lumefantrine allows the toxic heme to build up within the parasite. This heme damages the parasite's membranes and eventually leads to its death. Lumefantrine's long half-life ensures that drug concentrations are maintained over time, clearing any remaining parasites and preventing recrudescence (a relapse of the infection).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination is highly effective because it provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Artemether</b> provides a rapid "knockdown" of the parasite biomass, quickly alleviating the patient's symptoms.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lumefantrine</b> follows up with a prolonged action, eliminating any parasites that may have survived the initial treatment and preventing the development of resistance. This dual approach is crucial for achieving a high cure rate and delaying the emergence of drug resistance.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Winart
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Artemether is a fast-acting synthetic antimalarial drug derived from <b>artemisinin</b>, a compound isolated from the sweet wormwood plant (<i>Artemisia annua</i>). It is an oil-soluble derivative, which allows it to be formulated for oral, intramuscular, and suppository administration. Artemether is known for its powerful and rapid effect against the blood-stage of the malarial parasite, leading to a quick reduction of the parasite load and a fast resolution of symptoms.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Artemether is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>uncomplicated <i>Plasmodium falciparum</i> malaria</b>. Due to the rapid development of drug resistance, artemether is almost exclusively used in combination with another longer-acting antimalarial drug, such as lumefantrine. This strategy, known as Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT), is the standard of care for treating malaria and is crucial for achieving a high cure rate and preventing the emergence of resistance.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of artemether is unique and involves the parasite's own metabolic processes to generate its lethal effect.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Activation by Heme:</b> The malarial parasite digests hemoglobin from red blood cells to obtain amino acids. This process produces <b>heme</b>, a highly reactive and toxic iron-containing compound.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Free Radical Generation:</b> The defining feature of artemether is the <b>endoperoxide bridge</b> within its molecular structure. When artemether is taken up by the parasite, the heme iron cleaves this endoperoxide bridge. This cleavage generates highly reactive and toxic <b>free radicals</b> and other cytotoxic compounds.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Protein and Membrane Damage:</b> These free radicals quickly and indiscriminately damage essential parasite proteins, enzymes, and membranes. This widespread damage leads to the rapid death of the parasite within the red blood cell.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rapid Action:</b> This mechanism allows artemether to kill the malarial parasites very quickly, leading to a rapid reduction in the total parasite biomass. This action is responsible for the fast clinical response observed in patients, with fever and other symptoms resolving rapidly.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, artemether works by being activated by the parasite's own metabolism, which leads to the generation of free radicals that cause widespread damage and kill the parasite. Its potent and rapid action is why it is a cornerstone of modern malaria treatment, especially in combination with a drug that has a longer half-life to ensure complete eradication.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Calcium Dobesilate is a synthetic vasoprotective agent. It is the calcium salt of dobesilic acid and is known for its ability to regulate impaired capillary function.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Calcium Dobesilate Monohydrate is primarily used to treat conditions involving disorders of the blood vessels, particularly the microcirculation (the network of the smallest blood vessels). Its key indications include:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Chronic Venous Insufficiency (CVI):</b> A condition where veins, particularly in the legs, have difficulty returning blood to the heart, leading to symptoms like pain, swelling (edema), heaviness, and cramps.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Diabetic Retinopathy:</b> A complication of diabetes that damages the blood vessels in the retina, potentially leading to vision loss.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hemorrhoidal Syndrome:</b> The symptoms associated with hemorrhoids, which are swollen and inflamed veins in the rectum and anus.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Microcirculation Disorders:</b> Various conditions where blood flow in the smallest vessels is compromised.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The mechanism of action of Calcium Dobesilate Monohydrate is multifaceted, primarily focusing on improving the health and function of blood vessels, especially the capillaries. It is believed to work through several pathways:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Regulation of Capillary Permeability:</b> It acts on the capillary walls to reduce their fragility and permeability, which prevents the leakage of fluid and proteins into surrounding tissues. This helps to reduce edema (swelling) and other symptoms of vascular disorders.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Improvement of Blood Rheology:</b> It helps to improve blood flow by decreasing blood hyperviscosity (thickness) and inhibiting platelet aggregation.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Anti-inflammatory and Antioxidant Effects:</b> The drug has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the inflammatory response in the vascular system. It also acts as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals that can damage blood vessel walls.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Vascular Endothelial Protection:</b> It helps to stabilize and protect the endothelial cells that line blood vessels, which is crucial for maintaining proper vascular function.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Mexiletine is a Class Ib antiarrhythmic drug. It is an oral medication structurally similar to lidocaine and is used to treat serious abnormal heart rhythms.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications:</b> Mexiletine is primarily used to treat documented life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, such as sustained ventricular tachycardia. It is also used in some cases to treat chronic pain, including diabetic neuropathy, and muscle stiffness associated with myotonic disorders.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Mexiletine works by blocking fast inward sodium channels in the heart's nerve cells. This action inhibits the inward sodium current, which is responsible for the initial rapid rise (Phase 0) of the action potential. By doing so, it stabilizes the heart's electrical signals and helps to correct irregular heartbeats. This drug has fast onset and offset kinetics, meaning its effect is more pronounced at faster heart rates.</span></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Winart
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Artemether and Lumefantrine is a fixed-dose oral medication used for the treatment of malaria. It is a type of <b>Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT)</b>, which is the current global standard of care for treating uncomplicated malaria. This combination leverages the distinct properties of its two components: Artemether provides a rapid, potent action to quickly reduce parasite load, while Lumefantrine provides a longer-acting effect to clear the remaining parasites and prevent a relapse.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the treatment of <b>uncomplicated <i>Plasmodium falciparum</i> malaria</b> in regions where there is a high risk of parasite resistance to other antimalarial drugs. It can be used in both adults and children.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, which target the malarial parasite at different stages of its life cycle in the red blood cells.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Artemether (Artemisinin Derivative)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Artemether is a potent and fast-acting antimalarial. Its mechanism is believed to involve the cleavage of the endoperoxide bridge within its molecular structure by the parasite's own iron (heme) within the red blood cells. This process generates highly reactive and toxic <b>free radicals</b> and other cytotoxic compounds.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> These free radicals quickly and non-selectively damage essential parasite proteins and membranes. This action leads to a rapid and significant reduction in the number of parasites in the patient's blood, providing a swift clinical response and resolving symptoms like fever and chills. Artemether has a short half-life and is rapidly cleared from the body.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Lumefantrine (Aryl-alcohol)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Lumefantrine has a longer half-life and works by a different mechanism. It is believed to inhibit the detoxification of heme, a toxic byproduct of the parasite's digestion of hemoglobin. In a normal process, the parasite would convert heme into a non-toxic compound called hemozoin.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By inhibiting this detoxification, lumefantrine allows the toxic heme to build up within the parasite. This heme damages the parasite's membranes and eventually leads to its death. Lumefantrine's long half-life ensures that drug concentrations are maintained over time, clearing any remaining parasites and preventing recrudescence (a relapse of the infection).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination is highly effective because it provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Artemether</b> provides a rapid "knockdown" of the parasite biomass, quickly alleviating the patient's symptoms.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lumefantrine</b> follows up with a prolonged action, eliminating any parasites that may have survived the initial treatment and preventing the development of resistance. This dual approach is crucial for achieving a high cure rate and delaying the emergence of drug resistance.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p class="query-text-line"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Please provide description, indication ,mechanism of action of the Artemether </span></span></p>

This process produces <b>heme</b>, a highly reactive and toxic iron-containing compound.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Free Radical Generation:</b> The defining feature of artemether is the <b>endoperoxide bridge</b> within its molecular structure. <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Parawin works by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins in the central nervous system, particularly in the brain. Prostaglandins are chemical messengers that are responsible for signaling pain and causing fever. By blocking their release, Parawin helps to raise the body's pain threshold, making a person feel less pain, and it acts on the hypothalamus (the brain's temperature control center) to lower body temperature and reduce fever.</span></span></span></p>

	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Nasal congestion, stuffy/runny nose</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Sneezing</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Watery eyes</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Sinus headaches</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The combined action of the three ingredients provides comprehensive relief:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Paracetamol:</b> Works by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins in the brain, which are chemical messengers responsible for causing pain and fever.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Phenylephrine:</b> Acts as a decongestant by shrinking the blood vessels in the nasal passages, which reduces congestion and excess mucus production.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Chlorpheniramine:</b> A first-generation antihistamine that works by blocking the action of histamine, a substance released by the body during allergic reactions. This helps to alleviate symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, and watery eyes.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp;</span></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Peproxin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Dihydroartemisinin and Piperaquine Phosphate is a fixed-dose oral medication used for the treatment of malaria. It is a type of <b>Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT)</b>, which is the current global standard of care for treating uncomplicated malaria. Dihydroartemisinin is a potent, rapid-acting metabolite of artemisinin, while Piperaquine is a long-acting antimalarial of the quinoline class. The combination leverages the distinct properties of these two drugs to provide a powerful and sustained effect against the malarial parasite.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the treatment of <b>uncomplicated <i>Plasmodium falciparum</i> malaria</b>. It is also effective against other malaria species like <i>P. vivax</i>, <i>P. ovale</i>, and <i>P. malariae</i>. It is a crucial therapeutic option in regions where there is a high risk of drug resistance to other antimalarials.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, which target the malarial parasite at different stages of its life cycle in the red blood cells.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Dihydroartemisinin (Artemisinin Derivative)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Dihydroartemisinin is a highly potent and fast-acting antimalarial. Like other artemisinin derivatives, its mechanism involves the cleavage of its endoperoxide bridge by the parasite's own iron (heme) within the red blood cells. This process generates highly reactive and toxic <b>free radicals</b> and other cytotoxic compounds.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> These free radicals quickly and indiscriminately damage essential parasite proteins, membranes, and other cellular components. This action leads to a rapid and significant reduction in the number of parasites in the patient's blood, providing a swift clinical response and resolving symptoms like fever and chills. Dihydroartemisinin has a very short half-life and is rapidly cleared from the body.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Piperaquine Phosphate (Quinoline Derivative)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Piperaquine has a very long half-life and works by a different mechanism. It is a quinoline derivative, similar to chloroquine, and it is believed to inhibit the detoxification of heme, a toxic byproduct of the parasite's digestion of hemoglobin. In a normal process, the parasite would convert heme into a non-toxic compound called hemozoin.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By inhibiting this detoxification, piperaquine allows the toxic heme to build up within the parasite. This heme damages the parasite's membranes and eventually leads to its death. Piperaquine's long half-life ensures that drug concentrations are maintained over a prolonged period (up to several weeks), clearing any remaining parasites and providing post-treatment prophylaxis to prevent recrudescence (a relapse of the infection).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination is highly effective because it provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dihydroartemisinin</b> provides a rapid "knockdown" of the parasite biomass, quickly alleviating the patient's symptoms.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Piperaquine</b> follows up with a prolonged action, eliminating any parasites that may have survived the initial treatment and preventing the development of drug resistance.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual approach is crucial for achieving a high cure rate and ensuring long-term efficacy against malaria.</span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Arme
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Artesunate and Mefloquine is a fixed-dose oral medication used for the treatment of malaria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a type of <b>Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT)</b>, which is the current global standard of care for treating uncomplicated malaria.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination brings together two powerful antimalarials with different properties: Artesunate is a potent, rapid-acting artemisinin derivative, while Mefloquine is a long-acting antimalarial of the quinoline-methanol class. This dual-drug approach is designed to provide a rapid cure and prevent the emergence of drug resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination drug is specifically indicated for the treatment of <b>uncomplicated <i>Plasmodium falciparum</i> malaria</b>, particularly in areas where resistance to other antimalarial drugs is a concern. The combination is a crucial tool in the fight against malaria, offering high cure rates.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic efficacy of this combination is based on the distinct yet complementary mechanisms of its two components, which target the malarial parasite at different stages of its life cycle in the red blood cells.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Artesunate (Artemisinin Derivative)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Artesunate is a potent and fast-acting antimalarial. Like other artemisinin derivatives, its mechanism involves the cleavage of its unique endoperoxide bridge by the parasite's own iron (heme) within the red blood cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process generates highly reactive and toxic <b>free radicals</b> and other cytotoxic compounds.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> These free radicals quickly and indiscriminately damage essential parasite proteins, enzymes, and membranes. This action leads to a rapid and significant reduction in the number of parasites in the patient's blood, providing a swift clinical response and resolving symptoms like fever and chills. Artesunate has a short half-life and is rapidly cleared from the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Mefloquine (Quinoline-Methanol Antimalarial)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism:</b> Mefloquine has a very long half-life and works by a different mechanism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is believed to act by inhibiting the detoxification of heme, a toxic byproduct of the parasite's digestion of hemoglobin. In a normal process, the parasite would convert heme into a non-toxic compound called hemozoin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Role in Combination:</b> By inhibiting this detoxification, mefloquine allows the toxic heme to accumulate within the parasite, which damages the parasite's membranes and eventually leads to its death. Mefloquine's long half-life ensures that drug concentrations are maintained over a prolonged period (up to several weeks), clearing any remaining parasites and providing post-treatment prophylaxis to prevent recrudescence (a relapse of the infection).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination is highly effective because it provides a synergistic effect:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Artesunate</b> provides a rapid "knockdown" of the parasite biomass, quickly alleviating the patient's symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mefloquine</b> follows up with a prolonged action, eliminating any parasites that may have survived the initial treatment. This dual approach is crucial for achieving a high cure rate and delaying the emergence of drug resistance.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>
