PRODUCT: Solwin 0.9%
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b> Solwin 0.9% is a sterile, nonpyrogenic solution of sodium chloride in water for injection. It contains 900 mg of sodium chloride per 100 mL, making it an isotonic solution, meaning its salt concentration is similar to that of human blood. It is intended for intravenous administration.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b> This solution is used for a variety of purposes, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Parenteral replenishment of fluid and electrolytes, especially in cases of dehydration, hypovolemia, or mild sodium depletion.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Serving as a vehicle or diluent for the intravenous administration of other compatible drugs.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Acting as a priming solution for medical procedures like hemodialysis.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Initiating and terminating blood transfusions.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b> When administered, the solution provides a source of water and electrolytes, specifically sodium (Na+) and chloride (Cl−) ions.</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Sodium (Na+):</b> This is the principal cation of the extracellular fluid and plays a major role in controlling water distribution, fluid balance, and osmotic pressure in the body.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Chloride (Cl−):</b> This is the major extracellular anion and works with sodium to maintain the acid-base balance.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The isotonic nature of the solution allows it to expand the intravascular volume without causing significant shifts of fluid between the intracellular and extracellular spaces. The distribution and excretion of both ions are primarily managed by the kidneys, which maintain a balance between intake and output.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Vizeka
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telbivudine (trade names Sebivo in Europe and Tyzeka in the United States) is an oral antiviral medication used for the treatment of chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a synthetic nucleoside analogue of thymidine, which is a building block of DNA.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Telbivudine is known for its potent and specific activity against HBV.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telbivudine is indicated for the treatment of chronic hepatitis B in adult patients with compensated liver disease and evidence of viral replication. This includes patients who show persistently elevated serum alanine aminotransferase (ALT) levels or have histological evidence of active liver inflammation and/or fibrosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is important to note that while telbivudine was a significant treatment option, it has a relatively lower genetic barrier to resistance compared to other newer HBV drugs like entecavir and tenofovir. For this reason, its use is often recommended when alternative agents with a higher genetic barrier are not available or appropriate.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of telbivudine is based on its ability to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis B virus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process occurs in three main steps:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Phosphorylation:</b> After oral administration, telbivudine is taken up by the host cells and is phosphorylated by cellular enzymes (kinases) into its active form, <b>telbivudine triphosphate</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Competitive Inhibition:</b> Telbivudine triphosphate acts as a competitive inhibitor of the HBV DNA polymerase (reverse transcriptase).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It competes with the natural substrate of the enzyme, deoxythymidine triphosphate (dTTP), for binding to the active site.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>DNA Chain Termination:</b> Once incorporated into the growing viral DNA chain, telbivudine triphosphate acts as a DNA chain terminator.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is because it lacks a 3'-hydroxyl group, which is required for the formation of the next phosphodiester bond.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This premature termination of the DNA chain effectively stops the synthesis of new viral DNA, thereby halting viral replication and reducing the viral load.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Telbivudine has been shown to inhibit both first- and second-strand DNA synthesis by the HBV polymerase, with a preference for inhibiting second-strand synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: WinWater
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">subcutaneous injection. It is a novel drug that targets a specific signaling pathway involved in the progression of pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Winrevair is indicated for the treatment of adults with pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH, WHO Group 1) to:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Increase exercise capacity</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Improve WHO functional class (FC)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Reduce the risk of clinical worsening events</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Winrevair is an activin signaling inhibitor. It works by binding to activin A and other members of the transforming growth factor-β (TGF-β) superfamily. By doing this, it modulates the signaling balance in the pulmonary arteries. This action helps to reverse the structural tissue changes that occur in PAH, such as vascular proliferation, and reduces inflammation, leading to a decrease in pulmonary artery pressure.</span></span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Ursowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ursowin is a medication that contains the active ingredient ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA), a naturally occurring bile acid. It is primarily used to treat certain liver and gallbladder disorders.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ursowin is indicated for the following conditions:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Gallstone dissolution:</b> It is used to dissolve radiolucent (non-calcified) gallstones, which are mainly composed of cholesterol, in patients who are not suitable for surgery.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Primary biliary cholangitis (PBC):</b> This is a chronic autoimmune liver disease where the bile ducts are gradually destroyed. Ursowin helps to improve liver function and can prevent or delay liver damage in people with this condition.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Liver diseases associated with cystic fibrosis:</b> It can be used to manage liver and biliary diseases in children with cystic fibrosis.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) works through several mechanisms:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reduces cholesterol saturation in bile:</b> UDCA decreases the amount of cholesterol produced by the liver and absorbed by the intestines. This leads to a reduction in cholesterol in the bile, which helps dissolve existing cholesterol gallstones and prevents new ones from forming.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Displaces toxic bile acids:</b> UDCA is a hydrophilic (water-loving) bile acid. It increases the proportion of hydrophilic bile acids in the bile pool, displacing the more hydrophobic (water-repelling) and toxic bile acids that can cause damage to liver cells.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Protects liver cells:</b> By displacing toxic bile acids and improving bile flow, UDCA protects liver cells (hepatocytes) and bile duct cells (cholangiocytes) from damage.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Immunomodulatory effect:</b> It has been shown to have an effect on the immune system, which is believed to reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, a key component in diseases like primary biliary cholangitis.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Lignowin 2%
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Lignowin 2% contains the active ingredient Lidocaine (also known as Lignocaine). It is a local anesthetic used to numb a specific area of the body. It can come in various forms such as an injection or a gel. As an injection, it's a sterile solution, while as a gel, it's a viscous substance often used as a lubricant for medical procedures.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Lignowin 2% is primarily indicated for:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Local Anesthesia:</b> To numb a specific area of the body before minor surgical, dental, or diagnostic procedures.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Ventricular Arrhythmia:</b> As a Class Ib antiarrhythmic agent to treat irregular heart rhythms, particularly after a heart attack.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Pain and Discomfort Relief:</b> When in gel or topical form, it's used to relieve pain and discomfort from conditions like skin irritation, burns, insect bites, or in procedures such as catheterization or intubation.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Lidocaine, the active ingredient in Lignowin 2%, is an amide-type local anesthetic. Its mechanism of action involves:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Blocking Sodium Channels:</b> It works by blocking the voltage-gated sodium channels in the membranes of nerve cells.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Inhibiting Nerve Impulses:</b> By blocking these channels, it prevents the influx of sodium ions, which is essential for the initiation and conduction of nerve impulses.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Preventing Pain Signals:</b> This action stabilizes the neuronal membrane, preventing the nerve from firing and sending pain signals to the brain, which results in temporary numbness and pain relief in the affected area.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Letnova
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lopinavir/ritonavir (trade name Kaletra) is a fixed-dose combination of two antiviral drugs, lopinavir and ritonavir.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly effective treatment for HIV-1 infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Lopinavir is the primary active component, and it belongs to the class of drugs known as protease inhibitors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Ritonavir, also a protease inhibitor, is included in this combination at a low dose not for its own antiviral effect, but as a "pharmacokinetic booster."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lopinavir/ritonavir is indicated for the treatment of HIV-1 infection in adults and pediatric patients (typically 6 months of age and older).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is used in combination with other antiretroviral agents as part of a highly active antiretroviral therapy (HAART) regimen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination is effective for both treatment-naïve patients and those who have previously been treated with other antiretroviral drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">While it was once a cornerstone of first-line therapy, its use has shifted in recent years due to the availability of newer, often more convenient and better-tolerated drugs. However, it remains an important option, particularly in patients with pre-existing resistance to other protease inhibitors.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of lopinavir/ritonavir involves the inhibition of the HIV protease enzyme, a critical component of the virus's life cycle.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The two components work together to achieve this effect:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Lopinavir's Antiviral Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lopinavir is the primary antiviral agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a protease inhibitor that specifically targets the <b>HIV-1 protease</b> enzyme.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This enzyme is essential for the virus to mature and become infectious.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After the virus's genes are transcribed into a long polypeptide chain (known as a "polyprotein"), HIV protease is responsible for "cleaving" or cutting this long chain into smaller, functional proteins (like Gag, Pol, and others).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lopinavir binds to the active site of the HIV protease, preventing it from performing this cleavage.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">As a result, the virus produces only immature, defective, and non-infectious viral particles, which cannot go on to infect new cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ritonavir's Boosting Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ritonavir is included at a low dose for a completely different purpose.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a potent inhibitor of a specific human liver enzyme called <b>cytochrome P450 3A4 (CYP3A4)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This enzyme is responsible for metabolizing and clearing many drugs, including lopinavir, from the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By inhibiting CYP3A4, ritonavir significantly slows the metabolism of lopinavir.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This "boosting" effect results in a much higher and more sustained concentration of lopinavir in the bloodstream, allowing it to exert its full antiviral effect while reducing the required dosage and frequency of administration.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is crucial because lopinavir, when administered alone, has poor oral bioavailability and is rapidly cleared from the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Tigowin 50mg
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp; <b>Description:</b> Tigowin is an antibiotic containing the active ingredient tigecycline. It is a member of the glycylcycline class of antibiotics and is typically administered as an intravenous infusion. It is used to treat serious bacterial infections and is often reserved for critically ill patients or when other antibiotics are not suitable.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp; <b>Indication:</b> Tigowin (tigecycline) is indicated for the treatment of various bacterial infections, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Complicated skin and skin structure infections.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Complicated intra-abdominal infections.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Community-acquired bacterial pneumonia.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It is important to note that it is not effective against viral infections like the common cold or flu.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">&nbsp; <b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Tigecycline works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis. It binds to the 30S ribosomal subunit of the bacteria, which prevents amino-acyl tRNA molecules from entering the A site of the ribosome. This action stops the growth of the peptide chain, effectively halting the bacteria's ability to produce the essential proteins it needs to survive and multiply.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Bartex
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Bartex (busulfan) is a cytotoxic or anti-cancer medication. It is an alkylating agent, which works by damaging the genetic material of cells. It is typically a potent and highly toxic medicine used in specific cancer treatments.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The primary indication for Bartex (busulfan) is the treatment of certain blood cancers, specifically <b>chronic myeloid leukemia (CML)</b>. It is also used in combination with other cancer medicines during <b>hematopoietic progenitor cell transplantation (HPCT)</b>, also known as a stem cell or bone marrow transplant.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Busulfan, the active ingredient in Bartex, is an alkylating agent. Its mechanism of action involves damaging the genetic material (DNA and RNA) of cancer cells. It introduces alkyl groups into the DNA, which causes breaks and cross-linking in the DNA strands. This prevents the cancer cells from growing and multiplying, leading to their death. This action is not selective to cancer cells, which is why it has serious side effects on healthy, rapidly dividing cells in the body, such as those in bone marrow.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Osimavir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Oseltamivir phosphate (trade name Tamiflu) is an oral antiviral prodrug used to treat and prevent influenza A and B viruses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> As a prodrug, it is inactive until it is converted by the body into its active form, <b>oseltamivir carboxylate</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It belongs to a class of antiviral drugs known as <b>neuraminidase inhibitors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Oseltamivir phosphate is indicated for:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Treatment of Influenza:</b> It is used to treat acute, uncomplicated influenza in patients (typically 2 weeks of age and older) who have been symptomatic for no more than 48 hours.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When started early, it can shorten the duration of flu symptoms and may reduce the risk of complications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Influenza (Prophylaxis):</b> It can be used to prevent influenza in individuals who are at least 1 year of age and have been in close contact with an infected person.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is not a substitute for the annual flu vaccine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Prophylaxis is generally reserved for high-risk individuals or during a community outbreak.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Oseltamivir's antiviral effect is achieved by targeting a key enzyme on the surface of the influenza virus called <b>neuraminidase (NA)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The mechanism of action involves the following steps:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Conversion:</b> After oral administration, oseltamivir phosphate is rapidly absorbed and converted by an enzyme in the liver (hepatic esterases) into its active form, oseltamivir carboxylate.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Neuraminidase:</b> Oseltamivir carboxylate is a potent and selective inhibitor of the influenza virus neuraminidase enzyme.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Neuraminidase is a glycoprotein on the viral surface that plays a critical role in the final stage of the viral replication cycle.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Viral Release:</b> The primary function of neuraminidase is to cleave sialic acid residues on the surface of infected host cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This cleavage is necessary for newly formed viral particles to be released from the infected cell and to spread to other cells. By inhibiting neuraminidase, oseltamivir carboxylate prevents the release of these new viruses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The new viral particles become "stuck" to the surface of the infected cell, preventing them from infecting other cells and halting the spread of the infection within the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This mechanism effectively reduces the viral load and the severity and duration of influenza symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The effectiveness of oseltamivir is highly dependent on timely administration, as it works best when started within 48 hours of the onset of symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Midiven Care 0.1 % Cream
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Midiven Care 0.1 % Cream (also known as Mediven Cream) is a topical medication containing the active ingredient betamethasone valerate. It is a medium-strength corticosteroid. It is a cream intended for external use only.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This cream is used to treat a variety of inflammatory skin conditions, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Eczema (atopic dermatitis)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Psoriasis (dry, scaly patches on the skin)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Other forms of dermatitis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Skin irritation, redness, and itching caused by allergies or other conditions.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The active ingredient, betamethasone valerate, is a synthetic glucocorticoid. Its mechanism of action involves:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Binding to cytoplasmic receptors:</b> It binds to specific receptors within the cells of the skin.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Inhibition of inflammatory mediators:</b> This binding action suppresses the release of certain chemicals in the body, such as prostaglandins, that are responsible for causing inflammation, swelling, redness, and itching.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reducing immune response:</b> It also interferes with the functioning of inflammatory response mediators and inhibits the entry of white blood cells (leukocytes) at the site of inflammation.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effect:</b> By performing these actions, it reduces the overall inflammatory and allergic response in the skin, leading to a decrease in symptoms.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sofowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir (trade name Sovaldi) is a direct-acting antiviral (DAA) medication used for the treatment of chronic hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a nucleotide analog and a prodrug, meaning it is inactive until it is metabolized in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Sofosbuvir has been a game-changer in the treatment of hepatitis C, offering a highly effective, well-tolerated, and short-duration cure for many patients. It is not used as a monotherapy but is always part of a combination regimen with other antivirals to prevent resistance and maximize efficacy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir is indicated for the treatment of chronic hepatitis C virus infection in adults and children (typically 3 years of age and older).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its specific use depends on the HCV genotype and the patient's condition. It is used as a component of a combination antiviral treatment regimen for:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>HCV Genotypes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6:</b> Sofosbuvir is effective against all major HCV genotypes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Various Patient Populations:</b> It is used in both treatment-naïve patients and those who have failed previous therapies (e.g., interferon-based regimens).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Patients with Liver Disease:</b> It is used in patients with compensated liver disease, including cirrhosis, and in some cases, with decompensated cirrhosis in combination with other drugs like ribavirin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Co-infected Patients:</b> It is also used to treat patients co-infected with HIV-1 and HCV.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir is most commonly seen in fixed-dose combination tablets with other DAAs, such as ledipasvir (in Harvoni) or velpatasvir (in Epclusa), which simplifies the treatment and boosts cure rates to over 90% in most cases.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir's mechanism of action is highly specific and targets a crucial enzyme in the hepatitis C virus's life cycle.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation:</b> After oral administration, sofosbuvir is absorbed and undergoes a series of metabolic steps inside the liver cells (hepatocytes).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is converted into its active form, <b>uridine analog triphosphate</b> (GS-461203).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the HCV Polymerase:</b> The active form, GS-461203, is a defective substrate for the <b>HCV RNA-dependent RNA polymerase</b>, known as <b>NS5B</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is the catalytic subunit of the viral replication complex and is essential for transcribing the viral RNA and replicating the viral genome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>DNA Chain Termination:</b> As a uridine analog, GS-461203 is incorporated into the new viral RNA strand during replication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Because of its specific chemical structure, once it is incorporated, it prevents the addition of any further nucleotides.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This causes premature termination of the viral RNA chain.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Viral Replication:</b> By halting the synthesis of the viral RNA, sofosbuvir effectively stops the virus from replicating, leading to a rapid and dramatic decrease in the viral load and, in most cases, the complete eradication of the virus from the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This targeted mechanism is a key reason for its high efficacy, favorable safety profile, and high barrier to resistance.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: Velpovir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir + Velpatasvir (trade name Epclusa) is a fixed-dose combination tablet containing two direct-acting antiviral (DAA) medications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly effective, one-pill, once-daily regimen for the treatment of chronic hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The combination therapy targets two different viral proteins, providing a powerful synergistic effect that leads to very high cure rates.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir/velpatasvir is a <b>pangenotypic</b> treatment, meaning it is effective against all six major genotypes of the hepatitis C virus (genotypes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This simplifies treatment decisions by eliminating the need for complex genotype testing for many patients.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination is indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Adults and pediatric patients (typically 3 years of age and older) with chronic hepatitis C without cirrhosis or with compensated cirrhosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Patients with decompensated cirrhosis, for whom the regimen is used in combination with ribavirin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Patients who have been previously treated for HCV and those who are treatment-naïve.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The strength of this combination lies in its dual mechanism of action, which targets two distinct and essential viral proteins, making it very difficult for the virus to develop resistance.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sofosbuvir:</b> As a nucleotide analog, sofosbuvir is a prodrug that is converted to its active triphosphate form within the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This active form, <b>GS-461203</b>, acts as a competitive inhibitor of the <b>HCV RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (NS5B)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is crucial for the replication of the viral genome. By being incorporated into the new viral RNA strand, sofosbuvir acts as a chain terminator, stopping the replication process.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Velpatasvir:</b> Velpatasvir is an inhibitor of the <b>HCV non-structural protein 5A (NS5A)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> NS5A is a non-enzymatic protein that plays a key role in both viral RNA replication and the assembly of new viral particles.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to NS5A, velpatasvir disrupts the formation of the viral replication complex and prevents the assembly of infectious virions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By combining these two drugs, the treatment regimen attacks the hepatitis C virus at two different stages of its life cycle, leading to a sustained virologic response (SVR), which is considered a cure, in over 90% of patients.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Alovir
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tenofovir alafenamide (TAF) is a newer oral prodrug of the nucleotide analogue tenofovir, often prescribed as its fumarate salt, tenofovir alafenamide fumarate (TAF).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly effective antiviral agent used to treat chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection and, in combination with other drugs, HIV-1 infection.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Compared to its predecessor, tenofovir disoproxil fumarate (TDF), TAF is designed to be a more efficient and targeted delivery system for the active drug, tenofovir. TAF is more stable in the bloodstream, which allows it to be administered at a much lower dose (about one-tenth of TDF's dose).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This results in significantly lower concentrations of tenofovir in the plasma and a more favorable safety profile, particularly regarding kidney function and bone mineral density.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tenofovir alafenamide is indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) Infection:</b> It is approved for use in adults and pediatric patients (typically 6 years of age and older) with compensated liver disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>HIV-1 Infection:</b> It is a component of several fixed-dose combination tablets (e.g., Biktarvy, Descovy, Genvoya) used in combination with other antiretroviral agents for the treatment of HIV-1 in adults and pediatric patients. It is not used as a single agent for HIV.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Before starting TAF for HBV, patients should be tested for HIV-1 to ensure an appropriate and complete treatment regimen is administered for any co-infection.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of tenofovir alafenamide is an optimized version of the mechanism of tenofovir. The goal is to efficiently deliver the drug to the target cells (hepatocytes for HBV and lymphocytes for HIV) while minimizing systemic exposure.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Activation and Targeted Delivery:</b> After oral administration, TAF is absorbed and enters the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike TDF, which breaks down into tenofovir in the plasma, TAF is highly stable in plasma.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This stability allows it to be more efficiently taken up by target cells. Once inside the cells, TAF is hydrolyzed (broken down) by intracellular enzymes like cathepsin A (in lymphocytes) and carboxylesterase-1 (in hepatocytes) to release its active parent compound, <b>tenofovir</b>.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Phosphorylation to Active Form:</b> The newly released tenofovir is then phosphorylated by cellular kinases to its active, diphosphate form, <b>tenofovir diphosphate</b> (tenofovir-DP).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Viral Reverse Transcriptase/DNA Polymerase:</b> Tenofovir diphosphate is a potent competitive inhibitor of the viral enzymes responsible for replication:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>HBV DNA Polymerase:</b> It interferes with the synthesis of the HBV DNA genome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>HIV Reverse Transcriptase:</b> It prevents the conversion of HIV's RNA genome into DNA.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>DNA Chain Termination:</b> As an analogue of deoxyadenosine triphosphate (dATP), tenofovir diphosphate is incorporated into the growing viral DNA chain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> However, its structure lacks a necessary 3'-hydroxyl group, which is essential for further chain elongation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The incorporation of tenofovir-DP therefore leads to premature termination of the DNA chain, effectively stopping viral replication.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Sofoled
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir + ledipasvir (trade name Harvoni) is a fixed-dose combination of two direct-acting antiviral (DAA) medications.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly effective, one-pill, once-daily regimen used to treat chronic hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The combination of these two drugs, which target different stages of the viral life cycle, has revolutionized HCV treatment by achieving high cure rates with a short duration of therapy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sofosbuvir/ledipasvir is indicated for the treatment of chronic hepatitis C virus infection in adults and pediatric patients (typically 3 years of age and older).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is particularly effective for certain genotypes and patient populations:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>HCV Genotypes 1, 4, 5, and 6:</b> The combination is highly effective against these genotypes, with cure rates typically exceeding 90%.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Treatment-naïve and Treatment-experienced Patients:</b> It is used in both patients who have never been treated for HCV and those who have failed previous therapies, including interferon-based regimens.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Patients with Liver Disease:</b> The regimen is used in patients with compensated cirrhosis and can be used in combination with ribavirin for those with decompensated cirrhosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Co-infected Patients:</b> It is also effective for treating patients who are co-infected with HIV and HCV.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The fixed-dose combination simplifies the treatment process and reduces the risk of non-compliance, which is crucial for achieving a sustained virologic response (SVR), or a cure.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The power of sofosbuvir/ledipasvir lies in its dual mechanism of action, which targets two distinct and essential viral proteins, making it highly effective and less prone to viral resistance.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Sofosbuvir:</b> This is a nucleotide analog prodrug.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> After oral administration, it is metabolized in the body into its active form, <b>uridine analog triphosphate</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This active metabolite acts as a competitive inhibitor of the <b>HCV RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (NS5B)</b>, a key viral enzyme responsible for replicating the viral RNA genome.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> When the active metabolite is incorporated into the newly synthesized viral RNA chain, it acts as a chain terminator, halting the replication process.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ledipasvir:</b> This is an inhibitor of the <b>HCV non-structural protein 5A (NS5A)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> NS5A is a non-enzymatic protein that is crucial for both viral RNA replication and the assembly of new viral particles.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to NS5A, ledipasvir disrupts the formation of the viral replication complex and prevents the assembly of infectious virions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By targeting both NS5B and NS5A, the combination attacks the virus at two different, critical stages of its life cycle, leading to a profound reduction in viral load and a high likelihood of achieving a cure.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

<h3><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></h3>
