PRODUCT: Iconyx
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itraconazole is a synthetic broad-spectrum triazole antifungal medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a member of the azole class of drugs, which are widely used to treat various fungal infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in different oral formulations (capsules, tablets, and oral solution) and an intravenous formulation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a lipophilic drug that is highly protein-bound and extensively metabolized in the liver.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itraconazole is indicated for the treatment of a wide range of fungal infections, including systemic mycoses and superficial mycoses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Systemic Fungal Infections:</b> It is a drug of choice for treating certain serious systemic mycoses, such as:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blastomycosis</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Histoplasmosis</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Aspergillosis</b> (in patients who are intolerant of or refractory to amphotericin B)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Superficial Fungal Infections:</b> It is used to treat infections of the skin, nails, and mucous membranes, such as:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Onychomycosis</b> (fungal nail infections)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Candidiasis</b> (e.g., oropharyngeal and esophageal candidiasis)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prophylaxis:</b> It may be used to prevent fungal infections in immunocompromised patients, such as those with HIV/AIDS or those undergoing chemotherapy or organ transplantation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itraconazole's mechanism of action is primarily fungistatic (it slows fungal growth) and, at higher concentrations, fungicidal (it kills the fungus).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The core mechanism of action is the inhibition of ergosterol synthesis, a vital component of the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process can be broken down into these steps:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of 14-α-demethylase:</b> Itraconazole inhibits the fungal cytochrome P450-dependent enzyme called <b>lanosterol 14-alpha-demethylase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is crucial for converting lanosterol to ergosterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Cell Membrane Integrity:</b> By blocking this key step, itraconazole prevents the synthesis of ergosterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the accumulation of toxic intermediate sterols (such as 14-alpha-methyl sterols) in the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Permeability and Cell Death:</b> The defective cell membrane has altered permeability and function.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It becomes "leaky," leading to the leakage of essential cellular components.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This disruption of membrane integrity and function ultimately leads to the inhibition of fungal growth and, at higher concentrations, cell death.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Itraconazole's antifungal activity is based on its high affinity for fungal cytochrome P450 enzymes, which is much greater than its affinity for the equivalent mammalian enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This selective action minimizes harm to human cells while effectively targeting the fungus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Vagitab
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clotrimazole is a synthetic broad-spectrum antifungal medication belonging to the imidazole class of azole drugs.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is primarily used topically to treat a variety of fungal infections of the skin, mouth, and vagina.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in various formulations, including creams, lotions, powders, and vaginal tablets.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clotrimazole is a widely used over-the-counter and prescription medication for treating superficial fungal infections caused by yeasts and dermatophytes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vaginal Yeast Infections:</b> It is commonly used to treat vulvovaginal candidiasis (yeast infections).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Oral Thrush:</b> It is used in lozenge or troche form to treat oropharyngeal candidiasis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Skin Infections:</b> It is indicated for a range of fungal skin infections, including:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Tinea pedis (athlete's foot)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Tinea cruris (jock itch)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Tinea corporis (ringworm)</b></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cutaneous candidiasis</b></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clotrimazole, like other azole antifungals, works by inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, a vital component of the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process can be broken down into these steps:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of 14-α-demethylase:</b> Clotrimazole inhibits the fungal cytochrome P450-dependent enzyme called <b>lanosterol 14-alpha-demethylase</b>. This enzyme is essential for converting lanosterol to ergosterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Cell Membrane Integrity:</b> By blocking this key enzymatic step, clotrimazole prevents the synthesis of ergosterol, which is crucial for the structural integrity and function of the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the accumulation of toxic intermediate sterols in the cell membrane.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased Permeability and Cell Death:</b> The accumulation of defective sterols and the lack of ergosterol cause the fungal cell membrane to become more permeable and "leaky." This leads to the disruption of essential membrane functions and the loss of intracellular components, ultimately causing the inhibition of fungal growth and, at higher concentrations, cell death.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The fungistatic and fungicidal properties of clotrimazole are due to its selective action on fungal cytochrome P450 enzymes, with little to no effect on human cytochrome P450 enzymes. This selectivity is the basis for its therapeutic effect with minimal toxicity to the host.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: NT Funga
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluconazole is a synthetic triazole antifungal medication, part of the larger azole class of drugs. It is a highly water-soluble compound that can be administered orally (as tablets, capsules, or oral suspension) and intravenously. Fluconazole is distinguished from other azoles by its high bioavailability and excellent penetration into the central nervous system, making it a valuable treatment for certain systemic and central nervous system (CNS) fungal infections.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indication</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluconazole is indicated for the treatment and prophylaxis of a wide range of fungal infections caused by susceptible fungi, primarily <i>Candida</i> species. Its main indications include:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Candidiasis:</span></span></b></span></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Oropharyngeal and esophageal candidiasis (thrush):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Often used as a first-line treatment.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vulvovaginal candidiasis (yeast infections):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> A single-dose oral regimen is a common treatment.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Systemic candidiasis (candidemia):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Used to treat serious bloodstream infections caused by <i>Candida</i> species.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cryptococcal Meningitis:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Due to its excellent penetration into the cerebrospinal fluid, it is a key drug for the treatment and maintenance therapy of cryptococcal meningitis, especially in patients with HIV/AIDS.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Prophylaxis:</span></span></b></span></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is used to prevent candidiasis in patients undergoing bone marrow transplantation who are at high risk of developing a fungal infection.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is also used to prevent cryptococcal disease in patients with advanced HIV infection.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluconazole's mechanism of action is fungistatic (it inhibits fungal growth) by targeting the synthesis of ergosterol, a crucial component of the fungal cell membrane. The process can be broken down into these steps:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibition of 14-α-demethylase:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Fluconazole specifically inhibits the fungal cytochrome P450-dependent enzyme called <b>lanosterol 14-alpha-demethylase</b>. This enzyme is essential for converting lanosterol to ergosterol, which is a key step in the synthesis of the fungal cell membrane.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Disruption of Cell Membrane Integrity:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By blocking this enzymatic step, fluconazole prevents the production of ergosterol. This leads to the accumulation of toxic intermediate sterols (such as 14-alpha-methyl sterols) within the fungal cell membrane.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Increased Permeability and Cell Death:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> The defective cell membrane has altered structure and function, leading to increased permeability. The membrane becomes "leaky," causing the loss of essential cellular components and inhibiting the growth and replication of the fungus. This ultimately leads to cell death at higher concentrations.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The selective toxicity of fluconazole for fungi is due to its much higher affinity for fungal cytochrome P450 enzymes compared to human P450 enzymes, minimizing adverse effects on host cells.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Acnetin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Acnetin (tretinoin) is a topical medication derived from Vitamin A, belonging to a class of drugs called retinoids. It is available in various forms, such as creams and gels, and is used to treat skin conditions.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Acnetin (tretinoin) is primarily indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Acne vulgaris:</b> It is used to treat mild, moderate, and severe acne, including comedones, papules, and pustules.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Fine facial wrinkles and skin roughness:</b> Some formulations are also approved as an adjunctive therapy to reduce the appearance of fine wrinkles and improve skin texture caused by sun damage.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hyperpigmentation:</b> It can help to lighten dark spots and improve uneven skin tone.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The exact mechanism of action is not fully understood, but its effects are believed to be mediated through the following actions:</span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Regulates cell turnover:</b> Tretinoin stimulates the renewal of skin cells, causing them to divide and shed more quickly. This helps to unclog pores and prevent the formation of new acne lesions.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Reduces comedone formation:</b> It promotes the detachment of cornified cells and enhances the shedding of dead skin cells, which helps to expel the contents of comedones (blackheads and whiteheads).</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Decreases sebum production:</b> While the effect is not as dramatic as with oral isotretinoin, topical tretinoin can help regulate sebum (oil) production, making the skin environment less favorable for acne-causing bacteria.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Anti-inflammatory effects:</b> Tretinoin has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with acne.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Terbilet / Terbilet Cream 1
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Terbinafine hydrochloride is a synthetic allylamine antifungal medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral and topical formulations (creams, sprays, gels), with the oral form primarily used for more extensive and difficult-to-treat infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Terbinafine is highly lipophilic and has a strong affinity for keratinous tissues, such as skin, hair, and nails, where it accumulates to provide a sustained antifungal effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Terbinafine HCl is indicated for the treatment of various fungal infections caused by dermatophytes (a type of fungus that lives on the skin).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The indication varies depending on the formulation:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Oral Terbinafine:</b> This is primarily used for deep-seated or extensive infections, especially those of the nails and scalp.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Onychomycosis (fungal nail infection):</b> Terbinafine is considered one of the most effective oral treatments for toenail and fingernail fungal infections caused by dermatophytes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Tinea Capitis (scalp ringworm):</b> It is a common and effective treatment for this condition, particularly when caused by <i>Trichophyton</i> species.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Severe Tinea Infections:</b> It may be used for severe cases of tinea corporis (ringworm), tinea cruris (jock itch), and tinea pedis (athlete's foot) that have not responded to topical therapy.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Topical Terbinafine:</b> This is used for superficial fungal infections of the skin, such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and ringworm.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Terbinafine's mechanism of action is primarily fungicidal (it kills the fungus) and is different from the azole antifungals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It works by targeting an early step in the fungal ergosterol synthesis pathway.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Squalene Epoxidase:</b> Terbinafine is a non-competitive inhibitor of the fungal enzyme <b>squalene epoxidase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is crucial for converting squalene to squalene epoxide, an early step in the biosynthesis of ergosterol.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dual Effect:</b> The inhibition of squalene epoxidase leads to two simultaneous effects that are lethal to the fungus:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Ergosterol Deficiency:</b> By blocking the synthesis pathway, terbinafine causes a severe depletion of ergosterol, which is essential for the structural integrity and function of the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Toxic Squalene Accumulation:</b> The inhibition of the enzyme also causes a toxic accumulation of squalene inside the fungal cell.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The buildup of squalene is believed to be directly toxic to the cell, further disrupting membrane function and leading to cell death.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual mechanism makes terbinafine a highly effective antifungal agent, particularly against dermatophytes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The drug's selectivity for fungal squalene epoxidase over the mammalian enzyme ensures minimal effects on human cholesterol synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

This helps to maintain mental balance and also inhibits pain signals from traveling to the brain.</span></span></span></span></li>

	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Valproic acid works to treat seizures through multiple mechanisms. It is thought to increase the amount of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which is a neurotransmitter that helps to calm nerve activity. It may also work by blocking voltage-gated sodium channels and T-type calcium channels, which are involved in nerve signal transmission.</span></span></span></span></li>

PRODUCT: Isoplex Gel
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Isoplex Gel is a topical medication that contains the active ingredient isotretinoin, which is a retinoid. It is a derivative of vitamin A and is used to treat acne. The gel is applied to the skin to help manage and prevent various forms of acne.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Isoplex Gel is primarily indicated for the topical treatment of acne, including blackheads, whiteheads, and inflamed spots. It is used to reduce the production of oil (sebum) in the skin, unblock pores, and lower the number of red, inflamed acne spots. It may also be used to improve overall skin texture.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> While the exact mechanism of action is not fully understood, it is believed that isotretinoin works by:</span></span></span></span>

	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Ometo
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Based on the provided list of categories, "Ometo" falls under the <b>GIT Drugs Segment</b> (Gastrointestinal Tract Drugs Segment).</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">"Ometo" is a brand name for a drug containing <b>omeprazole</b>. In some cases, it may also be a combination drug, like Ometo-D, which contains both omeprazole and domperidone.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Here is a breakdown of the product's details:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b></span></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ometo is a medication containing omeprazole, which is a type of proton pump inhibitor (PPI).</span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">When the drug also contains domperidone (e.g., Ometo-D), it is a combination medication that includes both a PPI and a prokinetic agent (domperidone).</span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b></span></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Ometo is primarily used to treat conditions caused by excess stomach acid.</span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Common indications include:</span></span></span></span>
		<ul style="list-style-type:square">
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD):</b> A chronic condition where stomach acid flows back into the food pipe (esophagus), causing symptoms like heartburn.</span></span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Peptic Ulcers:</b> Sores that form on the lining of the stomach or small intestine.</span></span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome:</b> A rare condition that causes the stomach to produce too much acid.</span></span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Healing of erosive esophagitis.</span></span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">In some cases, it is used in combination with antibiotics to treat <i>Helicobacter pylori</i> (H. pylori) infection, which can cause ulcers.</span></span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		</li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The domperidone component in combination products helps to relieve symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and bloating by increasing the movement of the stomach and intestines.</span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b></span></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Omeprazole:</b> It works by irreversibly blocking the <b>H+/K+ ATPase enzyme system</b>, also known as the "proton pump," in the parietal cells of the stomach. This pump is the final step in the production of gastric acid. By inhibiting this pump, omeprazole effectively reduces both the basal (normal) and stimulated production of stomach acid.</span></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Domperidone (if present):</b> It acts as a dopamine antagonist. It works on the upper digestive tract to increase the movement and contractions of the stomach and intestines, helping food move through the digestive system more easily and preventing reflux.</span></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Fusia-B

It is formulated as a cream for external, topical use.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> This medication is used to treat a variety of skin infections caused by bacteria, particularly when inflammation and itching are also present. Common conditions it is prescribed for include:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Inflamed bacterial skin infections</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Infected eczema</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Dermatitis accompanied by inflammation and itching</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The dual action of Fusia-B is due to its two components:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Fusidic Acid:</b> This is an antibiotic. It works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis. Specifically, it prevents a crucial step in protein production by binding to a part of the bacterial ribosome, which prevents the bacteria from growing and multiplying. This effectively helps to clear the bacterial infection.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Beclomethasone:</b> This is a potent corticosteroid. It works by blocking the production of certain chemical messengers in the body, such as prostaglandins, that cause the skin to become red, swollen, and itchy. This provides relief from the inflammation and discomfort associated with the infection.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Fusia-H
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Fusia-H (also known as Fucidin H, Fudic-H, or Fusac-H) is a topical cream that contains a combination of two active ingredients: fusidic acid and hydrocortisone. Fusidic acid is an antibiotic, and hydrocortisone is a corticosteroid. The product is formulated to provide both antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Fusia-H is used to treat skin conditions that are both inflamed (such as eczema or dermatitis) and infected with bacteria. It is prescribed for conditions like atopic dermatitis, allergic dermatitis, and seborrheic dermatitis that have a secondary bacterial infection.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Fusidic Acid:</b> This is a bacteriostatic antibiotic, which means it prevents bacteria from multiplying, allowing the body's immune system to clear the infection. It works by interfering with bacterial protein synthesis. Specifically, it prevents the turnover of elongation factor G (EF-G) from the ribosome, which is a crucial step in the process of building new proteins. Without this process, the bacteria cannot grow or reproduce.</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Hydrocortisone:</b> This is a mild corticosteroid. Its primary mechanism is to reduce inflammation. It does this by preventing skin cells from releasing substances that cause inflammation, such as redness, swelling, and itching.</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Fusia
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b> Fusia is a brand name for a topical antibiotic containing the active ingredient fusidic acid. It is typically available as a cream, ointment, or gel for external use. Fusidic acid is a bacteriostatic antibiotic, which means it prevents bacteria from growing rather than killing them outright, allowing the body's immune system to clear the infection.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication</b> Fusia (fusidic acid) is used to treat a variety of bacterial skin infections, including:</span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Impetigo</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Folliculitis</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Boils</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Infected dermatitis (such as eczema)</span></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Infected cuts, scrapes, and grazes</span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">It is effective against a range of susceptible bacteria, particularly <i>Staphylococcus aureus</i>, which is a common cause of skin infections.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b> Fusidic acid works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis. Specifically, it binds to and prevents the translocation of elongation factor G (EF-G) from the ribosome. EF-G is a crucial protein that facilitates the movement of the ribosome along the messenger RNA (mRNA) strand, which is a necessary step for building new proteins. By blocking this process, fusidic acid stops the bacteria from producing the proteins they need to grow and multiply. This action effectively halts the infection's spread and allows the host's immune system to clear the remaining bacteria.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Baztron
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bambuterol is an oral, long-acting beta-2 adrenergic receptor agonist.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a prodrug of terbutaline, a well-known short-acting beta-2 agonist.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> As a prodrug, bambuterol is inactive until it is metabolized in the body. It is designed to be slowly and continuously converted to terbutaline, providing a sustained therapeutic effect over 24 hours.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bambuterol is indicated for the treatment of chronic bronchial asthma and other conditions where a prolonged bronchodilator effect is required, such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is typically used for long-term maintenance therapy and is not suitable for the immediate relief of acute asthma attacks. For acute symptom relief, a fast-acting bronchodilator is needed.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bambuterol's mechanism of action is based on its conversion to the active drug, terbutaline, which then acts on beta-2 adrenergic receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prodrug Conversion:</b> After oral administration, bambuterol is slowly and incompletely absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is then metabolized in the liver and intestinal mucosa by cholinesterase enzymes, a process that converts it into its active form, <b>terbutaline</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This slow conversion rate is responsible for its long duration of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Beta-2 Adrenergic Receptor Agonism:</b> The active metabolite, terbutaline, acts as a selective agonist on beta-2 adrenergic receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are located primarily on the smooth muscle cells of the bronchioles in the lungs.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bronchodilation:</b> When terbutaline binds to and activates these receptors, it stimulates the enzyme <b>adenylyl cyclase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This stimulation increases the intracellular concentration of cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The elevated cAMP levels lead to the relaxation of the smooth muscles in the airways. This process is known as <b>bronchodilation</b>.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Mast Cell Mediator Release:</b> Beta-2 receptor activation also has an effect on mast cells, inhibiting the release of inflammatory mediators such as histamine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This helps to reduce airway inflammation and hypersensitivity.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By promoting sustained bronchodilation, bambuterol helps to prevent bronchospasm, reduce airway resistance, and improve airflow, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with asthma and COPD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Adlowin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Adapalene is a synthetic topical retinoid, which is a derivative of vitamin A. It is a third-generation retinoid and is often used in the form of a gel, cream, or lotion for application on the skin. It is known for its stability and effectiveness with a generally milder side-effect profile compared to some other retinoids.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Adlowin (adapalene) is primarily used for the topical treatment of acne vulgaris, which includes blackheads, whiteheads, and pimples. It is also used in some cases for other skin conditions such as keratosis pilaris and to help with fine lines, wrinkles, and post-acne scarring.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Adapalene works by binding to specific nuclear retinoic acid receptors (RAR-beta and RAR-gamma) in the skin. This action modulates cellular differentiation, keratinization, and inflammatory processes. In simpler terms, it helps to normalize the growth of skin cells, preventing them from clogging pores and forming new acne lesions. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with acne.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Fabulas
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description:</b> Fabulas is a medication used to treat chronic gout, a painful form of arthritis caused by the buildup of uric acid in the joints. The active ingredient is febuxostat. It is an anti-gout agent that works by lowering the level of uric acid in the blood. It is often prescribed for long-term management of the condition, especially for patients who cannot tolerate or do not respond well to other common gout medications like allopurinol.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indication:</b> Fabulas is indicated for the chronic management of hyperuricemia (elevated uric acid levels in the blood) in adult patients with gout. It is used to prevent gout attacks and the formation of uric acid crystals in and around the joints, which cause pain, swelling, and inflammation. It is not intended for the treatment of asymptomatic hyperuricemia.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="line-height:115%"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> Fabulas belongs to a class of drugs called xanthine oxidase inhibitors. It works by selectively and potently inhibiting the enzyme xanthine oxidase. This enzyme is responsible for the final two steps in the metabolic pathway of converting purines to uric acid. By blocking xanthine oxidase, Fabulas effectively reduces the production of uric acid in the body, which lowers the overall concentration of uric acid in the blood and helps to prevent the formation of urate crystals that cause gout.</span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Telwin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fexofenadine hydrochloride is a second-generation antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms. It's the active metabolite of terfenadine but, unlike its predecessor, it doesn't cause cardiotoxic side effects. Fexofenadine is a non-sedating antihistamine because it does not cross the blood-brain barrier. It is available as oral tablets and suspensions.</span></span></p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fexofenadine is primarily indicated for the treatment of symptoms associated with:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Seasonal Allergic Rhinitis:</b> It effectively relieves symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, itchy or watery eyes, and itching of the nose or throat caused by hay fever and other seasonal allergies.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Idiopathic Urticaria:</b> It is also used to treat and relieve the itching and hives (urticaria) associated with this skin condition.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fexofenadine's mechanism of action involves selectively blocking the effects of histamine, a chemical released by the body during an allergic reaction. The process is as follows:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Histamine Release:</b> When an allergen enters the body, the immune system releases <b>histamine</b> from mast cells. Histamine then binds to and activates <b>H1 receptors</b> on cells throughout the body, triggering the characteristic symptoms of an allergic reaction.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>H1 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Fexofenadine acts as a potent and selective <b>peripheral H1 receptor antagonist</b>. This means it binds to the H1 receptors on the surface of cells, effectively blocking histamine from binding to them.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptom Relief:</b> By preventing histamine from activating H1 receptors, fexofenadine prevents the cascade of events that cause allergy symptoms like vasodilation, increased vascular permeability (leading to swelling and a runny nose), and itching. This action provides relief without causing significant drowsiness, as it does not affect histamine receptors in the central nervous system.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------
