PRODUCT: Welzinc
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Zinc Sulphate Monohydrate is a common salt of the essential trace element zinc. It is a source of elemental zinc and is used primarily as a dietary supplement and a medication. Zinc is a vital mineral that plays a crucial role in numerous biological processes, including enzymatic function, protein synthesis, cell growth, and immune system function.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Zinc Sulphate Monohydrate is indicated for the treatment and prevention of zinc deficiency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Zinc Deficiency:</b> It is used to correct a zinc deficiency, which can result from inadequate dietary intake, malabsorption syndromes (e.g., Crohn's disease, celiac disease), chronic alcoholism, or other conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Symptoms of zinc deficiency can include stunted growth, hair loss, diarrhea, immune dysfunction, and poor wound healing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Diarrhea:</b> The World Health Organization (WHO) and UNICEF recommend zinc supplementation as an essential component of the treatment for acute or persistent diarrhea in children.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Zinc has been shown to reduce the duration, severity, and recurrence of diarrheal episodes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acrodermatitis Enteropathica:</b> This is a rare, inherited disorder that results in zinc deficiency and is treated with lifelong zinc supplementation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Wound Healing:</b> Zinc is important for tissue repair and cell proliferation, so it is sometimes used as an adjunct in treating wounds, burns, and ulcers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic and physiological mechanisms of Zinc Sulphate Monohydrate are directly related to the functions of the zinc ion in the body.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enzymatic Cofactor:</b> Zinc is a crucial cofactor for over 300 enzymes involved in major metabolic pathways, including DNA and RNA synthesis, protein synthesis, and carbohydrate metabolism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By providing the zinc ion, the supplement ensures that these enzymes can function correctly.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cell Growth and Proliferation:</b> Zinc is essential for cell division and growth.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It plays a key role in DNA replication, transcription, and protein synthesis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Correcting a zinc deficiency restores these fundamental processes, which is why zinc is vital for growth in children and wound healing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Immune System Function:</b> Zinc is critical for the development and function of immune cells, including T-lymphocytes and natural killer cells.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It helps to regulate the immune response and plays a role in reducing inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is a key reason for its effectiveness in treating diarrhea, where it helps to restore gut integrity and immune function.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism in Diarrhea:</b> The specific mechanism of action in treating diarrhea is thought to be multifaceted:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intestinal Repair:</b> Zinc helps to regenerate the intestinal mucosa (lining), which is often damaged by diarrhea-causing pathogens.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fluid and Electrolyte Absorption:</b> It promotes the absorption of water and electrolytes, helping to correct dehydration.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Immune Response:</b> It strengthens the local immune response in the gut, helping the body fight off the infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antioxidant Effects:</b> Zinc is a component of the antioxidant enzyme superoxide dismutase (SOD), which helps to protect cells from oxidative stress.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Delta D3
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cholecalciferol, also known as Vitamin <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>D3​, is a fat-soluble vitamin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a secosteroid produced in the skin upon exposure to ultraviolet B (UVB) sunlight.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It can also be obtained from dietary sources (e.g., fatty fish, fortified foods) or supplements.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The 200,000 IU dosage is a very high-potency formulation, typically administered as a single dose or a limited number of doses, and is used for therapeutic rather than daily maintenance purposes.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The 200,000 IU dose of cholecalciferol is indicated for the treatment of severe vitamin D deficiency. This high-dose therapy is used to rapidly replenish the body's vitamin D stores and is typically prescribed when:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Severe Vitamin D Deficiency:</b> Blood levels of 25-hydroxyvitamin D are critically low, often below 12 ng/mL (30 nmol/L).</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rickets:</b> In children, severe vitamin D deficiency can cause rickets, a condition leading to bone softening and skeletal deformities.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteomalacia:</b> In adults, severe deficiency can cause osteomalacia, which results in bone pain and muscle weakness.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Malabsorption Syndromes:</b> Patients with conditions that impair fat absorption (e.g., cystic fibrosis, celiac disease, bariatric surgery) often require high-dose therapy to correct their deficiency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Renal and Liver Failure:</b> While other forms of vitamin D might be needed, high-dose cholecalciferol may be used in certain stages of these diseases to build up stores.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This high-dose regimen is not intended for long-term daily use and should be administered under medical supervision to avoid the risk of vitamin D toxicity.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cholecalciferol itself is biologically inert.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its mechanism of action relies on a two-step metabolic activation process that occurs in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>First Hydroxylation (in the Liver):</b> After absorption, cholecalciferol is transported to the liver. There, an enzyme called 25-hydroxylase converts it into <b>calcidiol</b> (25-hydroxyvitamin <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>D3​).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is the major circulating form of vitamin D, and its blood level is used as the primary indicator of a person's vitamin D status.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Calcidiol is also a storage form of the vitamin.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Second Hydroxylation (in the Kidneys):</b> Calcidiol is then transported to the kidneys. A second enzyme, 1-alpha-hydroxylase, converts it into the biologically active hormonal form, <b>calcitriol</b> (1,25-dihydroxyvitamin <source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>D3​).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is the final, most potent form of vitamin D.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hormonal Action of Calcitriol:</b> Calcitriol acts by binding to the <b>vitamin D receptor (VDR)</b> in various cells, initiating a range of actions to regulate calcium and phosphate homeostasis:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intestinal Absorption:</b> Calcitriol is the primary hormone that promotes the absorption of dietary calcium and phosphate from the small intestine, which is crucial for maintaining proper blood levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bone Metabolism:</b> It works in concert with parathyroid hormone (PTH) to regulate bone remodeling, promoting bone mineralization and, when necessary, mobilizing calcium from bone to maintain blood calcium levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Renal Reabsorption:</b> It increases the reabsorption of calcium and phosphate in the kidneys, conserving these minerals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By providing a large dose of cholecalciferol, the body is given a substantial amount of substrate to convert into these active metabolites, thereby rapidly correcting the deficiency and restoring normal calcium and phosphate balance.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Citrocid
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Sodium Bicarbonate, Sodium Citrate, Citric Acid, and Tartaric Acid is a multi-component, effervescent granular powder. This formulation is designed to be dissolved in water before consumption, creating a bubbly solution. The core components work together as a systemic alkalinizing agent and a topical antacid. Sodium Bicarbonate is an alkaline salt, while Citric and Tartaric Acids provide the acidic component for the effervescence. Sodium Citrate serves as a buffer and the primary agent for systemic alkalinization.</span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is indicated for two primary therapeutic purposes:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antacid and Symptomatic Relief:</b> It is used for the symptomatic relief of conditions related to excess stomach acidity, such as:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dyspepsia (indigestion)</b></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Heartburn</b></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastric discomfort</b></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary and Systemic Alkalinizer:</b> This is its more significant and frequent use. It is indicated for:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary Alkalinization:</b> To increase the pH of the urine to prevent the formation of and aid in the dissolution of certain types of kidney stones, specifically uric acid and cystine stones.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gout:</b> To help prevent the crystallization of uric acid in the kidneys and joints.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Supportive Therapy:</b> As an adjunct therapy in the treatment of certain drug overdoses (e.g., salicylates, phenobarbital) to enhance their renal excretion by making the urine more alkaline.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effects of this combination are based on two distinct mechanisms:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antacid Mechanism:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">When the powder is dissolved in water, the sodium bicarbonate reacts with the citric acid and tartaric acid, releasing carbon dioxide gas, which creates the effervescence.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">When ingested, the remaining sodium bicarbonate directly neutralizes the hydrochloric acid (HCl) in the stomach.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The chemical reaction is: NaHCO3​+HCl→NaCl+H2​O+CO2​.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This direct neutralization rapidly reduces stomach acidity, providing immediate relief from heartburn and indigestion.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary Alkalinizing Mechanism:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This is the more systemic effect, primarily driven by the <b>citrate</b> component (from both Sodium Citrate and the metabolism of Citric Acid).</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">When absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract, citrate ions are metabolized by the liver into bicarbonate (HCO3−​).</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The liver's metabolic process consumes a proton (H+) for every molecule of citrate metabolized, resulting in a net gain of bicarbonate in the bloodstream.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The kidneys then excrete this newly generated bicarbonate.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The presence of bicarbonate in the urine increases the urine's pH (makes it more alkaline).</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This alkalinization is crucial for:</span></span></span>
		<ul style="list-style-type:square">
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Kidney Stone Prevention:</b> Uric acid and cystine are less soluble in acidic urine and tend to crystallize.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By increasing the urinary pH, their solubility is increased, preventing the formation of new stones and helping to dissolve existing ones.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
			<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enhanced Drug Excretion:</b> The excretion of certain acidic drugs (e.g., salicylates) is increased in alkaline urine, a principle known as "ion trapping."</span></span></span></li>
		</ul>
		</li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>

PRODUCT: CXT-3
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of Calcium Lactate, Ascorbic Acid, and Calcium Carbonate is a compound supplement designed to provide a comprehensive source of calcium and vitamin C. Calcium Lactate and Calcium Carbonate are two different calcium salts, each with a varying amount of elemental calcium and absorption characteristics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Ascorbic Acid is the chemical name for Vitamin C. This combination is typically available in effervescent tablets or chewable tablets, making it easy to consume.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is indicated for the treatment and prevention of deficiencies in both calcium and vitamin C. Its primary uses include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteoporosis and Osteopenia:</b> As a supplement to increase bone mineral density and reduce the risk of fractures, particularly in postmenopausal women and the elderly.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hypocalcemia:</b> To treat or prevent low blood calcium levels caused by various conditions.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Nutritional Supplementation:</b> For individuals with insufficient dietary intake of calcium and/or vitamin C, such as growing adolescents, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and those with specific dietary restrictions.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Scurvy and other Vitamin C Deficiency Symptoms:</b> To prevent or treat symptoms of vitamin C deficiency, such as fatigue, gum disease, and impaired wound healing.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Adjunct Therapy:</b> Used alongside other treatments for conditions where calcium and vitamin C play a supportive role, such as certain cases of rickets or bone fractures.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is a result of the individual and synergistic mechanisms of its components.</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Calcium's Mechanism (from Calcium Lactate and Calcium Carbonate)</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Absorption:</b> Both calcium lactate and calcium carbonate release calcium ions (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>Ca2+) in the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Calcium is absorbed through a combination of active transport (which is vitamin D-dependent) and passive diffusion. Calcium carbonate, being less soluble, requires stomach acid for optimal absorption, whereas calcium lactate is more readily absorbed regardless of stomach acid levels.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Physiological Roles:</b> Once absorbed, the calcium ions are crucial for a multitude of biological processes:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bone Health:</b> Calcium is the primary mineral component of bone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It combines with phosphate to form hydroxyapatite crystals, which give bones their strength and rigidity.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Supplementation increases the available calcium for bone mineralization, helping to prevent bone loss and promote bone formation.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Nerve and Muscle Function:</b> Calcium ions are essential for nerve impulse transmission and muscle contraction, including the function of the heart muscle.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blood Clotting:</b> Calcium is a vital cofactor in several steps of the blood coagulation cascade.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C)'s Mechanism</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enzyme Cofactor:</b> Ascorbic acid is a water-soluble vitamin that acts as a cofactor for several enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its most critical role is in the synthesis of <b>collagen</b>, a protein that forms the structural framework of bones, cartilage, skin, and blood vessels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is essential for the hydroxylation of proline and lysine residues, which is a key step in creating strong, stable collagen fibers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Antioxidant:</b> As a powerful antioxidant, ascorbic acid protects the body from oxidative damage caused by free radicals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Enhancing Calcium Absorption:</b> While vitamin D is the primary facilitator, vitamin C can also help enhance the absorption of minerals like calcium.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It contributes to the overall health of the intestinal lining and may help maintain an acidic environment in the stomach, which can aid in the absorption of calcium carbonate.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">3. Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The components work together to enhance each other's effects. The calcium provides the building blocks for bone, while the ascorbic acid is essential for synthesizing the collagen matrix upon which the calcium is deposited. Without adequate vitamin C, the collagen framework of the bone would be weak, even with sufficient calcium.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination ensures that both the mineral and the structural protein for healthy bones are available.</span></span></p>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Eucalmin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mecobalamin, also known as methylcobalamin, is a naturally occurring and active form of vitamin B12.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a vital water-soluble vitamin that plays a key role in various metabolic processes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike other forms of vitamin B12 (like cyanocobalamin) that need to be converted to their active coenzyme forms in the body, mecobalamin is already in its active state. This makes it readily available for use in the body's metabolic pathways.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mecobalamin is primarily indicated for the treatment of vitamin B12 deficiency and its associated conditions. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Vitamin B12 Deficiency:</b> It is used to correct a deficiency caused by poor dietary intake, malabsorption (e.g., in pernicious anemia, Crohn's disease), or other medical conditions.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Peripheral Neuropathy:</b> It is a drug of choice for treating various types of peripheral neuropathy, including diabetic neuropathy and drug-induced neuropathy. It helps to regenerate damaged nerves and improve nerve function.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Megaloblastic Anemia:</b> It is used in the treatment of megaloblastic anemia caused by vitamin B12 deficiency.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS):</b> It is being studied for its potential neuroprotective effects in patients with ALS, although its efficacy in this context is still a subject of ongoing research.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mecobalamin's therapeutic effects are a direct result of its role as an essential coenzyme in two critical metabolic pathways within the human body.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Homocysteine Metabolism:</b> Mecobalamin is a cofactor for the enzyme <b>methionine synthase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is responsible for converting the amino acid <b>homocysteine</b> into <b>methionine</b>. Methionine is then used to synthesize S-adenosylmethionine (SAMe), a universal methyl donor essential for numerous biological processes, including the synthesis of DNA, RNA, and proteins, and the methylation of myelin sheaths that insulate nerve fibers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In Vitamin B12 deficiency,</b> this pathway is impaired, leading to a buildup of homocysteine, which is toxic to the nervous system and is a risk factor for cardiovascular disease. Mecobalamin supplementation restores this process, reducing homocysteine levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Methylmalonyl-CoA Metabolism:</b> The second key pathway involves the enzyme <b>L-methylmalonyl-CoA mutase</b>, which requires another form of vitamin B12 (adenosylcobalamin).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> While mecobalamin's primary role is in the methionine pathway, its presence ensures the overall metabolic health that underpins this process. The breakdown of certain amino acids and fatty acids is dependent on this pathway, and a deficiency can lead to the accumulation of toxic methylmalonic acid, which also contributes to neurological symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Neuroprotective and Regenerative Effects:</b> The unique role of mecobalamin in the methionine synthase pathway makes it crucial for the health of the nervous system. The synthesis of SAMe, a product of this pathway, is essential for the formation and maintenance of <b>myelin sheaths</b>. Myelin is a fatty substance that insulates nerve fibers and allows for rapid nerve impulse transmission.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Mecobalamin helps in the repair of damaged nerves and promotes the regeneration of axons, which is why it is effective in treating peripheral neuropathy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: WinPlus
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Polymaltose Complex (IPC) is a non-ionic, water-soluble macromolecule of ferric hydroxide and polymaltose.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike traditional iron salts (e.g., ferrous sulfate), IPC is a stable complex, which means it does not release a large amount of free iron ions in the gastrointestinal tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This unique structure gives it a different absorption profile and leads to a significantly lower incidence of gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, constipation, and stomach upset.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Polymaltose Complex is indicated for the treatment and prevention of iron deficiency anemia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a preferred option for patients who:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cannot tolerate traditional iron supplements:</b> Patients who experience significant gastrointestinal side effects from ferrous salts.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Have iron deficiency anemia:</b> IPC is used to replenish the body's iron stores and restore hemoglobin levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Are pregnant or breastfeeding:</b> To meet the increased iron requirements during pregnancy and lactation.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Are children or adolescents:</b> To prevent or treat iron deficiency due to rapid growth.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Have chronic kidney disease:</b> As an oral option for iron supplementation.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of Iron Polymaltose Complex is based on its stable, non-ionic structure and a controlled absorption process.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stable Complex:</b> The iron in IPC is in the <b>ferric (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>Fe3+</b><b>)</b> state and is tightly bound within the polymaltose complex.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This prevents the release of free iron ions into the gastrointestinal tract. By avoiding the presence of free iron, IPC bypasses the need for the body to manage a large influx of iron that can be irritating to the intestinal lining.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Active Absorption:</b> The IPC is absorbed intact by a specific active transport mechanism in the small intestine. It is not absorbed via the same diffusion channels as ferrous salts. The intact complex is taken up by the mucosal cells of the duodenum.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intracellular Processing:</b> Once inside the mucosal cells, the iron is released from the polymaltose complex. This iron is then either:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stored as Ferritin:</b> The iron can be stored in the intestinal cells as <b>ferritin</b>, a storage protein, until the body needs it.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Transported to the Bloodstream:</b> The iron can be transported out of the cell into the bloodstream. It binds to <b>transferrin</b>, the primary iron-transport protein in the body.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Utilization for Hemoglobin Synthesis:</b> The transferrin-bound iron is then transported to the bone marrow, where it is used as a crucial building block for the synthesis of <b>hemoglobin</b>, the protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen. By providing a steady and well-tolerated supply of iron, IPC helps to correct anemia and restore normal hemoglobin levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The controlled, active absorption mechanism of Iron Polymaltose Complex is what distinguishes it from traditional iron salts and accounts for its superior tolerability.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Hifero
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Polymaltose Complex with Folic Acid is a combination supplement used to treat and prevent deficiencies of both iron and folic acid. It contains Iron Polymaltose Complex (IPC), a stable, non-ionic form of iron that is well-tolerated and less likely to cause gastrointestinal side effects compared to traditional iron salts. Folic acid (or folate) is a B-vitamin that is essential for cell growth and metabolism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This combination is particularly useful in situations where a dual deficiency of these nutrients is present.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This combination is indicated for the treatment and prevention of iron deficiency anemia and/or folic acid deficiency anemia. Its main uses include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anemia in Pregnancy:</b> It is commonly prescribed to pregnant women to meet the increased demands for both iron and folic acid, which are crucial for the health of both the mother and the developing fetus. Folic acid is especially important for preventing neural tube defects in the baby.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Megaloblastic Anemia with Coexisting Iron Deficiency:</b> This formulation is used when a patient has a type of anemia caused by folate deficiency (megaloblastic anemia) that is also accompanied by iron deficiency.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Malnutrition and Malabsorption:</b> For individuals with nutritional deficiencies or conditions that impair nutrient absorption.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-surgical or Blood Loss Anemia:</b> To help rebuild iron and folate stores after significant blood loss.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effect of this combination is based on the individual and synergistic actions of its two components:</span></span></p>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">1. Iron Polymaltose Complex (IPC) Mechanism</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Absorption:</b> The iron in IPC is in a non-ionic ferric (Fe3+) state and is absorbed by a specific active transport mechanism in the small intestine. This bypasses the need for the free iron ions that cause gastrointestinal irritation with traditional iron salts.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Metabolism:</b> Once inside the intestinal cells, the iron is released from the complex and either stored as <b>ferritin</b> or transported into the bloodstream by binding to <b>transferrin</b>.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hemoglobin Synthesis:</b> The transferrin-bound iron is then transported to the bone marrow, where it is used as a critical component for the synthesis of <b>hemoglobin</b>. Hemoglobin is the protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen to tissues.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By providing a well-tolerated source of iron, IPC helps to correct iron deficiency and restore normal hemoglobin levels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">2. Folic Acid Mechanism</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Nucleotide Synthesis:</b> Folic acid is an essential cofactor for enzymes involved in the synthesis of purines and pyrimidines, the building blocks of <b>DNA</b> and <b>RNA</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process is vital for cell division and growth.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Red Blood Cell Maturation:</b> A key role of folic acid is in the maturation of red blood cells in the bone marrow.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> A deficiency leads to <b>megaloblastic anemia</b>, where red blood cells are large and immature and cannot function properly. Folic acid supplementation restores normal red blood cell development.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Amino Acid Metabolism:</b> Folic acid is also involved in the metabolism of certain amino acids, such as homocysteine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h4 style="margin-top:3px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:107%"><span style="font-family:&quot;Calibri Light&quot;,sans-serif"><span style="color:#2e74b5"><span style="font-weight:normal"><span style="font-style:italic">3. Synergistic Effect</span></span></span></span></span></span></h4>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination is synergistic because both iron and folic acid are essential for the production of healthy red blood cells. Iron provides the core component for hemoglobin, while folic acid provides the building blocks for the DNA needed for red blood cell maturation. A deficiency in either can lead to anemia. By providing both, the combination ensures that the body has all the necessary components to restore red blood cell production and correct the anemia.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Hemosol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Sucrose Complex is an intravenous iron preparation used to treat iron deficiency anemia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It consists of a colloidal solution of polynuclear iron(III)-hydroxide in a sucrose complex.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This formulation is a stable compound that allows for the direct delivery of iron into the bloodstream, bypassing the gastrointestinal tract. This makes it particularly useful for patients who cannot tolerate or absorb oral iron supplements, and it provides a rapid and effective way to replenish iron stores.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Sucrose Complex is indicated for the treatment of iron deficiency anemia in adults and children under specific circumstances. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD):</b> It is a standard treatment for iron deficiency anemia in patients with CKD, both those who are on dialysis and those who are not, as these patients often have impaired iron absorption and increased iron requirements.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intolerance or Failure of Oral Iron:</b> It is used in patients who have a confirmed iron deficiency but are unable to tolerate oral iron supplements due to severe gastrointestinal side effects.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Non-response to Oral Iron:</b> It is indicated for patients who have not responded adequately to a course of oral iron therapy.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Situations Requiring Rapid Iron Replenishment:</b> In cases where a rapid increase in hemoglobin levels is necessary, such as before major surgery or in severe anemia.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD):</b> Patients with IBD often have chronic inflammation that can lead to impaired iron absorption and is a common cause of iron deficiency anemia.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of Iron Sucrose Complex is based on its direct delivery and subsequent controlled release of iron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Direct Intravenous Delivery:</b> The iron sucrose complex is administered intravenously, which means it bypasses the gastrointestinal tract entirely. This eliminates the gastrointestinal side effects commonly associated with oral iron.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Uptake by the Reticuloendothelial System (RES):</b> Once in the bloodstream, the iron sucrose complex is rapidly taken up by the cells of the <b>reticuloendothelial system (RES)</b>, particularly macrophages in the liver, spleen, and bone marrow.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Controlled Release of Ferric Iron:</b> Within the macrophages, the sucrose component is separated from the iron. The iron is then released in a controlled manner as <b>ferric iron (</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>Fe3+</b><b>)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This controlled process is crucial, as it prevents the release of a large amount of free iron, which can be toxic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to Transferrin and Storage as Ferritin:</b> The released ferric iron is then either:</span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stored:</b> It can be stored within the cells as <b>ferritin</b>, a crucial protein for long-term iron storage.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Transported:</b> It can be transported to the bone marrow by binding to <b>transferrin</b>, the body's primary iron-transport protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Utilization for Erythropoiesis:</b> The transferrin-bound iron is delivered to the bone marrow, where it is used as a vital component for the synthesis of <b>hemoglobin</b>. Hemoglobin is the protein in red blood cells that is responsible for carrying oxygen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By directly providing iron to the bone marrow, Iron Sucrose Complex rapidly increases hemoglobin levels and corrects iron deficiency anemia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Malasto
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Isomaltoside is a modern, high-dose intravenous iron preparation used to treat iron deficiency anemia.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It consists of a stable complex of ferric hydroxide and a carbohydrate, isomaltoside.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This non-dextran complex is designed to be administered at a high dose, often in a single infusion, providing a rapid and efficient method for replenishing the body's iron stores. Its stable structure and low immunogenicity reduce the risk of allergic reactions compared to older intravenous iron formulations.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Iron Isomaltoside is indicated for the treatment of iron deficiency anemia in adults and children who meet specific criteria. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Intolerance or Failure of Oral Iron:</b> It is used in patients who cannot tolerate or do not respond adequately to oral iron supplements due to severe gastrointestinal side effects or poor absorption.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Rapid Iron Repletion:</b> In situations where a very rapid increase in hemoglobin and iron stores is required, such as before major surgery, in severe symptomatic anemia, or in the pre-dialysis stage of chronic kidney disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD):</b> Patients with IBD often have chronic inflammation and impaired iron absorption, making intravenous iron a preferred treatment.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-bariatric Surgery:</b> Patients who have undergone bariatric surgery are at high risk for iron malabsorption and may require intravenous iron.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of Iron Isomaltoside is based on its direct intravenous delivery and its controlled, stable breakdown within the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Direct Intravenous Delivery:</b> Iron Isomaltoside is administered directly into the bloodstream. This bypasses the gastrointestinal tract, completely avoiding the gastrointestinal side effects (e.g., nausea, constipation) that are common with oral iron supplements.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Uptake by Macrophages:</b> The stable Iron Isomaltoside complex is taken up by the cells of the <b>reticuloendothelial system (RES)</b>, particularly the macrophages in the liver, spleen, and bone marrow. These macrophages recognize the complex and internalize it.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Controlled Release of Iron:</b> Inside the macrophages, the complex is gradually broken down in a controlled manner.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process releases the <b>ferric iron (Fe3+)</b> in a slow and safe way. The controlled release is a key advantage, as it prevents the sudden release of a large amount of free iron, which can be toxic.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to Transferrin and Storage as Ferritin:</b> The released ferric iron is then either:</span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stored:</b> It is stored within the macrophages as <b>ferritin</b>, the body's primary iron storage protein.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This replenishes the body's iron reserves.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Transported:</b> It is released from the macrophages into the bloodstream, where it binds to <b>transferrin</b>, the transport protein that carries iron to where it is needed.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Utilization for Erythropoiesis:</b> The transferrin-bound iron is delivered to the bone marrow, the primary site of red blood cell production. Here, the iron is used as a crucial building block for the synthesis of <b>hemoglobin</b>, the protein responsible for oxygen transport.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This process, known as erythropoiesis, is stimulated, leading to a rapid and effective correction of iron deficiency anemia.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>
