PRODUCT: Renelate
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strontium Ranelate is a medication used for the treatment of osteoporosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a salt of strontium and ranelic acid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its unique pharmacological profile is due to its active component, the strontium ion, which is absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract and incorporates into the bone crystal lattice. Strontium Ranelate is distinguished from other osteoporosis treatments by its dual mechanism of action.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available as an oral granular powder that is dissolved in water.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strontium Ranelate is indicated for the treatment of severe osteoporosis. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postmenopausal Osteoporosis:</b> It is used to reduce the risk of vertebral and hip fractures in postmenopausal women with severe osteoporosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Osteoporosis in Men:</b> It is also indicated for men at increased risk of fracture.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Due to a small risk of serious cardiovascular events, its use is now generally restricted to patients with severe osteoporosis who cannot be treated with other therapies, such as bisphosphonates. It is contraindicated in patients with a history of cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, or ischemic heart disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strontium Ranelate's mechanism of action is unique in that it has a <b>dual effect on bone metabolism</b>, simultaneously promoting bone formation and inhibiting bone resorption.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Stimulation of Bone Formation:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The strontium ion, which is chemically similar to calcium, is incorporated into the bone crystal lattice at a ratio that mimics the natural bone mineral.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strontium stimulates the proliferation and differentiation of <b>osteoblasts</b>, the cells responsible for building new bone tissue.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It also promotes the synthesis of collagen, the protein matrix of bone, upon which the bone mineral is deposited.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By stimulating osteoblast activity, strontium ranelate increases the rate of bone formation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Bone Resorption:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Strontium also has an inhibitory effect on <b>osteoclasts</b>, the cells that break down bone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is believed to inhibit the differentiation of osteoclast precursors into mature, bone-resorbing osteoclasts.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It also reduces the resorptive activity of mature osteoclasts by disrupting their cellular processes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By inhibiting osteoclast activity, strontium ranelate reduces the rate of bone breakdown.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The net effect of these two actions is a shift in the balance of bone remodeling towards bone formation, leading to a significant increase in bone mineral density and a reduction in fracture risk.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike bisphosphonates, which primarily inhibit resorption, strontium ranelate both builds and strengthens bone by its dual mechanism.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span>&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Colwincine
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Colchicine is an alkaloid derived from the autumn crocus plant (<i>Colchicum autumnale</i>).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is an oral medication that has been used for centuries for its potent anti-inflammatory effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Colchicine is not an analgesic and does not affect serum uric acid levels. Its therapeutic effects are due to its unique mechanism of action on inflammatory cells, which makes it particularly effective in treating gout and other inflammatory conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Colchicine is indicated for the treatment and prevention of certain inflammatory conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gout:</b> It is a first-line treatment for the prevention of acute gout flares and for the treatment of an acute gout attack.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Familial Mediterranean Fever (FMF):</b> It is the cornerstone of treatment for FMF, a genetic inflammatory disorder, to prevent acute attacks and amyloidosis.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Pericarditis:</b> It is used as a treatment for recurrent pericarditis, an inflammation of the sac-like tissue surrounding the heart.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Colchicine's mechanism of action is distinct from other anti-inflammatory drugs like NSAIDs or corticosteroids. Its anti-inflammatory effect is primarily achieved by disrupting the function of inflammatory cells, particularly neutrophils, which are key players in acute inflammatory responses like a gout attack.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Binding to Tubulin:</b> The primary action of colchicine is to bind to a protein called <b>tubulin</b>. Tubulin is a building block for <b>microtubules</b>, which are essential components of the cellular cytoskeleton.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Microtubule Polymerization:</b> By binding to tubulin, colchicine prevents it from polymerizing into functional microtubules.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This has a profound effect on cellular processes that depend on microtubules, such as cell division, motility, and intracellular transport.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Disruption of Neutrophil Function:</b> In the context of gout, the inflammatory response is triggered by the deposition of uric acid crystals in the joints.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This triggers a massive influx of neutrophils to the joint. By disrupting microtubule formation, colchicine interferes with several critical functions of neutrophils:<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Chemotaxis:</b> It prevents neutrophils from migrating to the site of inflammation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Phagocytosis:</b> It impairs the ability of neutrophils to engulf the uric acid crystals.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Degranulation:</b> It prevents neutrophils from releasing pro-inflammatory mediators that would otherwise amplify the inflammatory response.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduction of Inflammation:</b> By crippling the inflammatory response of neutrophils, colchicine effectively reduces the pain, swelling, and redness associated with the gout attack.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its action is to break the inflammatory cycle, not to directly affect uric acid levels.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Laxove SR
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tamsulosin hydrochloride is a medication belonging to the class of drugs known as alpha-1 adrenergic receptor antagonists.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The Sustained-Release (SR) pellets formulation is designed to release the active drug slowly and consistently over a 24-hour period, allowing for convenient once-daily dosing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Tamsulosin is distinguished from older alpha-blockers by its high selectivity for the alpha-1A and alpha-1D subtypes of the receptor, which are predominantly found in the lower urinary tract.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tamsulosin HCl SR pellets are indicated for the treatment of the signs and symptoms of <b>benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> BPH is a non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland that can cause bothersome urinary symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Tamsulosin helps to improve these symptoms, which include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Weak urinary stream</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary urgency</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hesitancy</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Incomplete bladder emptying</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Nocturia (frequent nighttime urination)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Tamsulosin treats the dynamic component of BPH symptoms by relaxing the smooth muscles of the urinary tract and does not reduce the size of the enlarged prostate itself.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of Tamsulosin is based on its selective antagonism of alpha-1 adrenergic receptors, which are responsible for smooth muscle contraction in the prostate and bladder neck.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Selective Alpha-1A and Alpha-1D Receptor Antagonism:</b> Tamsulosin has a high affinity for <b>alpha-1A</b> and <b>alpha-1D adrenergic receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are the primary subtype found in the smooth muscles of the prostate, bladder neck, and prostatic urethra. By selectively blocking these receptors, tamsulosin minimizes its effect on other alpha-1 receptors, such as the alpha-1B subtype found in blood vessels, which helps to reduce the risk of orthostatic hypotension (a drop in blood pressure upon standing).<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Smooth Muscle Relaxation:</b> The activation of alpha-1 adrenergic receptors normally leads to the contraction of the smooth muscles of the prostate and bladder neck.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This contraction increases the resistance to urine flow, leading to the obstructive and irritative symptoms of BPH. By acting as an antagonist, tamsulosin binds to these receptors and prevents the naturally occurring neurotransmitter, norepinephrine, from binding to them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Improved Urine Flow:</b> The blockade of these receptors results in the relaxation of the smooth muscles in the prostate and bladder neck.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This relaxation reduces the dynamic obstruction to urine outflow, leading to an increase in urinary flow rate, a decrease in the feeling of incomplete bladder emptying, and an improvement in the overall symptoms of BPH.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Savelawin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sevelamer hydrochloride is a non-absorbable phosphate binder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a polymer of polyallylamine hydrochloride, which is a cationic (positively charged) resin.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Sevelamer is not metabolized or absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract and is a metal-free and calcium-free alternative to other phosphate binders.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is administered orally, typically in tablet or powder form, to be taken with meals.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sevelamer hydrochloride is indicated for the control of serum phosphorus (phosphate) levels in adult and pediatric patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD) who are on dialysis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its primary purpose is to manage <b>hyperphosphatemia</b>, a common and serious complication of CKD.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> While its primary indication is for dialysis patients, it may also be used in patients with CKD who are not yet on dialysis, where elevated phosphorus levels are a concern.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of Sevelamer hydrochloride is entirely localized to the gastrointestinal tract. It works by preventing the absorption of dietary phosphate.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The process is as follows:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cationic Polymer:</b> Sevelamer is a long-chain polymer with numerous amine groups.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> At physiological pH in the gastrointestinal tract, these amine groups become positively charged.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Phosphate Binding:</b> Phosphate ions (<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>PO43−​) from food are negatively charged.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> As food moves through the digestive system, the positively charged amine groups on the sevelamer molecule are able to bind to the negatively charged phosphate ions.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Insoluble Complex Formation:</b> This binding forms a stable, insoluble complex between the sevelamer and the phosphate.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Elimination in Feces:</b> Because the complex is insoluble and sevelamer itself is not absorbed, the entire complex is excreted from the body in the feces.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ol start="5">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Reduced Phosphorus Absorption:</b> By binding to dietary phosphate and preventing its absorption, sevelamer effectively lowers the amount of phosphorus that enters the bloodstream.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This helps to reduce high serum phosphorus levels (hyperphosphatemia), which is crucial for preventing serious complications associated with CKD, such as renal bone disease and cardiovascular calcification.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Phazorid
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Phenazopyridine hydrochloride is a medication that acts as a urinary tract analgesic.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is an azo dye that is excreted in the urine, where it exerts a local anesthetic effect on the lining of the urinary tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It provides symptomatic relief from the pain and discomfort of urinary tract irritation but does not have any antibacterial properties and does not treat the underlying cause of the irritation or infection.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Phenazopyridine is indicated for the symptomatic relief of pain, burning, urgency, and frequency that are the result of irritation of the lower urinary tract mucosa caused by infection, trauma, surgery, endoscopic procedures, or the insertion of catheters.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is important to note that phenazopyridine should be used as an adjunct therapy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It should not be used for more than two days when used in conjunction with an antibacterial agent to treat a urinary tract infection, as its prolonged use could mask the symptoms while the infection is still present.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of phenazopyridine is a localized, topical effect on the urinary tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Excretion in the Urine:</b> After oral administration, phenazopyridine is rapidly absorbed and metabolized, with its metabolites and a large portion of the unchanged drug being excreted in the urine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Topical Analgesic Effect:</b> As the drug is excreted, it comes into direct contact with the mucosa (lining) of the urinary tract (bladder, ureters, urethra). It is believed to exert a direct, local anesthetic effect on the nerve endings of the mucosa.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptom Relief:</b> By desensitizing these nerve endings, phenazopyridine reduces the sensations of pain, burning, and urgency associated with inflammation and irritation.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Azo Dye Properties:</b> A key physical property of phenazopyridine is its nature as an azo dye.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It colors the urine a reddish-orange or brownish-red color. This is a normal and expected effect of the medication. This discoloration can also stain clothing or contact lenses.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<h3>&nbsp;</h3>

PRODUCT: Flexot
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flavoxate hydrochloride is a synthetic, tertiary amine that acts as a direct-acting smooth muscle relaxant.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a medication primarily used for its antispasmodic effects on the urinary tract.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral tablet form and is used to relieve the symptoms of bladder and urinary tract irritation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flavoxate hydrochloride is indicated for the symptomatic relief of various urinary symptoms associated with inflammatory conditions of the bladder and urinary tract. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Dysuria (painful urination)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary urgency</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary frequency</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Nocturia (frequent nighttime urination)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Suprapubic pain (pain above the pubic bone)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">These symptoms may be a result of conditions such as cystitis, prostatitis, urethritis, or urolithiasis (kidney stones). Flavoxate is not an antibiotic and does not treat the underlying cause of the condition; it only helps to relieve the associated symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flavoxate hydrochloride's mechanism of action is based on its direct action on the smooth muscles of the urinary tract. Its antispasmodic effect is believed to be due to two primary mechanisms:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Direct Myotropic Relaxant Action:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The primary mechanism is its direct relaxation of the smooth muscle cells of the bladder and urinary tract.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">It is thought to act by inhibiting the enzyme <b>phosphodiesterase</b>, which is responsible for breaking down cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) and cyclic guanosine monophosphate (cGMP).</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By inhibiting this enzyme, flavoxate leads to an increase in intracellular levels of cAMP and cGMP.</span></span></span></li>
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Elevated cAMP and cGMP levels cause the relaxation of smooth muscles, thereby reducing the spasms and involuntary contractions of the bladder that lead to urgency, frequency, and pain.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anticholinergic (Muscarinic) Properties:</b></span></span></span>
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Flavoxate also has a weak <b>anticholinergic</b> or antimuscarinic effect.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It acts as an antagonist at muscarinic acetylcholine receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acetylcholine is a neurotransmitter that promotes bladder contraction.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By weakly blocking these receptors, flavoxate further contributes to the relaxation of the smooth muscles in the bladder, reducing its spasticity and helping to relieve the associated symptoms.</span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">This dual mechanism—a primary direct myotropic effect combined with a weak anticholinergic action—makes flavoxate an effective antispasmodic for the urinary tract.</span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Sopret
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Solifenacin succinate is a medication belonging to the class of drugs known as muscarinic receptor antagonists, also commonly referred to as anticholinergics or antimuscarinics.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is an oral medication that works to relax the smooth muscles of the bladder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a highly selective antagonist, meaning it preferentially targets the specific receptors responsible for bladder contractions, which helps to reduce the side effects that can be associated with less selective agents.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Solifenacin succinate is indicated for the treatment of the symptoms of <b>overactive bladder (OAB)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> OAB is a condition characterized by a sudden, involuntary contraction of the bladder muscle, leading to a variety of urinary symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Solifenacin is used to relieve these symptoms, which include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary urgency:</b> A sudden, compelling need to urinate that is difficult to postpone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary frequency:</b> Urinating more often than usual.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urge incontinence:</b> The involuntary leakage of urine associated with a sudden urge to urinate.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of solifenacin is based on its ability to block muscarinic acetylcholine receptors in the bladder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Neural Control of Bladder Contraction:</b> The smooth muscle of the bladder (the detrusor muscle) is innervated by parasympathetic nerves. When these nerves are stimulated, they release the neurotransmitter <b>acetylcholine (ACh)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Acetylcholine then binds to and activates <b>muscarinic receptors</b>, primarily the <b>M3 subtype</b>, on the detrusor muscle cells, which causes the muscle to contract and leads to urination.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Muscarinic Receptor Antagonism:</b> Solifenacin acts as a potent and competitive <b>antagonist</b> at these muscarinic receptors.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It has a high affinity for the M3 subtype, which makes it particularly effective at targeting the bladder.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By binding to these receptors, solifenacin prevents acetylcholine from binding and activating them.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bladder Smooth Muscle Relaxation:</b> The blockade of the M3 receptors inhibits the signal for bladder muscle contraction.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This leads to the relaxation of the detrusor muscle and an increase in the bladder's capacity to store urine.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Symptom Relief:</b> By relaxing the bladder muscle, solifenacin helps to reduce the involuntary contractions and the feeling of urgency.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This, in turn, decreases the frequency of urination and the occurrence of urge incontinence.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Alzufo ER
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Alfuzosin hydrochloride is a medication belonging to the class of drugs known as alpha-1 adrenergic receptor antagonists.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is an oral medication primarily used to treat symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Alfuzosin is distinguished by its high selectivity for the alpha-1A subtype of adrenergic receptors, which are the main receptors in the lower urinary tract, and its long duration of action, allowing for once-daily dosing.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Alfuzosin HCl is indicated for the treatment of the signs and symptoms of <b>benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> BPH is a non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland that can cause bothersome urinary symptoms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Alfuzosin helps to improve these symptoms, which include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Weak urinary stream</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Urinary urgency</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hesitancy</b></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Incomplete bladder emptying</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Nocturia (frequent nighttime urination)</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Alfuzosin treats the dynamic component of BPH symptoms by relaxing the smooth muscles of the urinary tract and does not reduce the size of the prostate gland itself.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of Alfuzosin is based on its selective antagonism of alpha-1 adrenergic receptors, which are responsible for smooth muscle contraction in the prostate and bladder neck.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b><button _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" cdkoverlayorigin="" class="button ng-star-inserted" jslog="262206;track:generic_click,impression,attention"><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c3889421858="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate symbol gds-icon-s google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button>Selective Alpha-1 Receptor Antagonism:</b> Alfuzosin has a high affinity for <b>alpha-1A</b> and <b>alpha-1D adrenergic receptors</b>. These receptors are the predominant subtype found in the smooth muscles of the prostate, bladder neck, and prostatic urethra. By selectively blocking these receptors, alfuzosin targets the specific muscles responsible for urinary obstruction while having a lesser effect on alpha-1B receptors located in blood vessels. This selectivity helps to reduce the risk of orthostatic hypotension (a drop in blood pressure upon standing) compared to non-selective alpha-blockers.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Smooth Muscle Relaxation:</b> The activation of alpha-1 adrenergic receptors normally leads to the contraction of the smooth muscles of the prostate and bladder neck. This contraction increases the resistance to urine flow, leading to the obstructive and irritative symptoms of BPH. By acting as an antagonist, alfuzosin binds to these receptors and prevents the naturally occurring neurotransmitter, norepinephrine, from binding to them.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Improved Urine Flow:</b> The blockade of these receptors results in the relaxation of the smooth muscles in the prostate and bladder neck.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This relaxation reduces the dynamic obstruction to urine outflow, leading to an increase in urinary flow rate, a decrease in the feeling of incomplete bladder emptying, and an improvement in the overall symptoms of BPH.</span></span></span>&nbsp;<button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon><br />
	</button></li>
</ol>

<p><button _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" class="mdc-button mat-mdc-button-base legacy-sources-sidebar-button mdc-button--outlined mat-mdc-outlined-button mat-unthemed ng-star-inserted" jslog="262207;track:generic_click,impression,attention" mat-ripple-loader-class-name="mat-mdc-button-ripple" mat-ripple-loader-uninitialized="" mat-stroked-button=""><mat-icon _ngcontent-ng-c681899473="" aria-hidden="true" class="mat-icon notranslate legacy-sources-sidebar-button-icon google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" data-mat-icon-name="link" data-mat-icon-type="font" fonticon="link" role="img"></mat-icon></button></p>
