PRODUCT: Drotawns
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Drotaverine is a potent, non-anticholinergic spasmolytic (antispasmodic) agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a derivative of isoquinoline and is known for its direct action on the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal, biliary, urinary, and reproductive systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike some other antispasmodics, it does not act on the central nervous system or have significant anticholinergic side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral tablets and injectable forms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Drotaverine is indicated for the symptomatic relief of spasms and pain associated with smooth muscle contractions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Biliary Colic:</b> To relieve spasms associated with cholelithiasis (gallstones), cholecystitis, or cholangitis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Renal Colic:</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> To relieve pain and spasms associated with nephrolithiasis (kidney stones) and other urinary tract conditions.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gastrointestinal Spasms:</b> For spasms of the smooth muscles of the stomach, intestines, and colon, such as in irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or spastic constipation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Gynecological Spasms:</b> To relieve painful menstrual cramps (dysmenorrhea).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Headaches:</b> May be used for tension-type headaches that have a muscular component.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The mechanism of action of drotaverine is based on its direct relaxant effect on smooth muscles, which is mediated by its selective inhibition of a key enzyme.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Phosphodiesterase (PDE) Inhibition:</b> Drotaverine's primary mechanism is the potent and selective inhibition of the enzyme <b>phosphodiesterase type IV (PDE4)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> PDE4 is the predominant phosphodiesterase isozyme in smooth muscle tissue.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Increased cAMP Levels:</b> PDE4 is responsible for breaking down cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) into inactive AMP.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By inhibiting PDE4, drotaverine causes an increase in intracellular levels of <b>cAMP</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Inhibition of Muscle Contraction:</b> The increased level of cAMP triggers a series of intracellular events that lead to smooth muscle relaxation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Specifically, it activates an enzyme called protein kinase A (PKA). PKA then phosphorylates and inactivates <b>myosin light chain kinase (MLCK)</b>.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Smooth Muscle Relaxation:</b> MLCK is the enzyme responsible for phosphorylating the myosin light chain, a necessary step for smooth muscle contraction. By inhibiting MLCK, drotaverine prevents the phosphorylation of the myosin light chain. This disruption of the contractile process leads to the relaxation of the smooth muscle, effectively relieving spasms and associated pain.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The direct and specific action on smooth muscles, independent of the autonomic nervous system, makes drotaverine a targeted and effective antispasmodic.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Drotawns inj
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Drotaverine is a potent, non-anticholinergic spasmolytic (antispasmodic) agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a derivative of isoquinoline and is known for its direct action on the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal, biliary, urinary, and reproductive systems.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Unlike some other antispasmodics, it does not act on the central nervous system or have significant anticholinergic side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is available in oral tablets and injectable forms.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

</ul>

PKA then phosphorylates and inactivates <b>myosin light chain kinase (MLCK)</b>.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>

PRODUCT: Ormetril
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lynestrenol is a synthetic progestogen (progestin) belonging to the 19-nortestosterone group.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is an oral medication that serves as a <b>prodrug</b>; it is rapidly converted in the body to its pharmacologically active metabolite, <b>Norethisterone</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Lynestrenol is used for a wide range of gynecological and reproductive health purposes, primarily to treat conditions associated with hormonal imbalances.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indication</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lynestrenol is indicated for the treatment of various gynecological conditions where a progestational effect is desired. Its main indications include:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Contraception:</b> As a progestin-only oral contraceptive (the "mini-pill").<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Abnormal Uterine Bleeding:</b> To treat dysfunctional uterine bleeding (menorrhagia) by stabilizing the uterine lining.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Endometriosis:</b> To manage the pain and other symptoms of endometriosis by suppressing the growth of endometrial tissue outside the uterus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Amenorrhea:</b> To induce withdrawal bleeding in women with a lack of menstruation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Polymenorrhea:</b> To regulate menstrual cycles characterized by abnormally frequent periods.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT):</b> In combination with an estrogen, it is used in postmenopausal women with an intact uterus to prevent endometrial hyperplasia and reduce the risk of endometrial cancer.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effects of Lynestrenol are not due to the drug itself but are mediated through its active metabolite, Norethisterone, which acts as a potent agonist at progesterone receptors.</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Metabolic Conversion:</b> After oral administration, Lynestrenol is quickly and extensively metabolized in the liver to its active form, <b>Norethisterone</b>.</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Progesterone Receptor Agonism:</b> Norethisterone then binds with high affinity to <b>progesterone receptors</b> in the hypothalamus, pituitary gland, and reproductive organs (uterus, cervix, etc.). This binding initiates a series of physiological responses that are characteristic of progesterone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Suppression of Ovulation (Contraceptive Effect):</b> By acting on the hypothalamus and pituitary, Norethisterone inhibits the release of <b>gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH)</b>, which in turn suppresses the secretion of <b>follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH)</b> and <b>luteinizing hormone (LH)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The suppression of the LH surge is crucial for preventing ovulation.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Endometrial Effects:</b> Norethisterone causes the endometrium (the uterine lining) to become thin, atrophic, and unsuitable for the implantation of a fertilized egg.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action is key for treating abnormal bleeding and is a component of its contraceptive effect. In endometriosis, this action helps to suppress the growth of the ectopic endometrial tissue.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cervical Mucus Alteration:</b> It causes the cervical mucus to become thick and viscous, creating a barrier that is difficult for sperm to penetrate.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The therapeutic effects of Lynestrenol are essentially the same as those of its active metabolite, Norethisterone.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Telmirox–A
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Description</b></span></span></p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">Amlodipine Besylate Eq to Amlodipine + Telmisartan is a combination medication containing two active ingredients:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li class="MsoNoSpacing" style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Amlodipine:</b> A long-acting dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker (CCB).</span></span></span></li>
	<li class="MsoNoSpacing" style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Telmisartan:</b> An angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB).</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This medication is formulated to combine the antihypertensive effects of both drugs into a single tablet, providing a dual mechanism for blood pressure control.</span></span></p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">This combination drug is primarily indicated for the treatment of <b>hypertension (high blood pressure)</b>. It is typically prescribed for adult patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled with either amlodipine or telmisartan monotherapy. By using a single tablet, it simplifies the treatment regimen and may improve patient adherence.</span></span></p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b></span></span></p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif">The medication works through two distinct, yet complementary, mechanisms to lower blood pressure:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li class="MsoNoSpacing" style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Amlodipine:</b> As a calcium channel blocker, amlodipine inhibits the influx of calcium ions into vascular smooth muscle and cardiac muscle. This causes the peripheral arteries to relax and widen, a process known as vasodilation. The resulting reduction in peripheral resistance makes it easier for the heart to pump blood, thereby lowering overall blood pressure.</span></span></span></li>
	<li class="MsoNoSpacing" style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b>Telmisartan:</b> As an angiotensin II receptor blocker, telmisartan selectively blocks the binding of angiotensin II to its receptors in various tissues, including vascular smooth muscle. Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor (a substance that narrows blood vessels). By blocking its action, telmisartan prevents blood vessel constriction, leading to vasodilation and a decrease in blood pressure. It also reduces the secretion of aldosterone, which helps decrease salt and water retention.</span></span></span></li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Trixon (IM/ IV)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Description</span></span></h3>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ceftriaxone is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. It is a broad-spectrum, bactericidal drug, meaning it kills a wide range of bacteria. It is administered parenterally, either through intramuscular (IM) injection or intravenous (IV) infusion. Ceftriaxone is known for its long half-life, which allows for once-daily dosing in many cases. This is a significant advantage in treatment, particularly for outpatient therapy. It is also well-known for its ability to penetrate the central nervous system (CNS), making it highly effective for treating infections of the brain and spinal cord.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Indications</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ceftriaxone is a versatile antibiotic used to treat a wide array of serious bacterial infections, including:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Meningitis:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Its ability to cross the blood-brain barrier makes it a primary choice for treating bacterial meningitis, especially those caused by <i>Haemophilus influenzae</i>, <i>Neisseria meningitidis</i>, and <i>Streptococcus pneumoniae</i>.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lower Respiratory Tract Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Such as community-acquired pneumonia.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Genitourinary Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Including complicated urinary tract infections (UTIs) and gonorrhea. It is a standard first-line treatment for uncomplicated gonorrhea.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Skin and Soft Tissue Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is used to treat various bacterial infections of the skin and underlying tissues.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bone and Joint Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Such as osteomyelitis.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Sepsis or Septicemia:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Due to its broad-spectrum activity, it is often used as a first-line treatment for blood infections.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Intra-abdominal Infections:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Including peritonitis and other infections within the abdominal cavity.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Surgical Prophylaxis:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is commonly administered before certain surgical procedures to prevent post-operative infections.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Lyme disease:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> It is used for the treatment of disseminated Lyme borreliosis.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Mechanism of Action</span></span></b></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ceftriaxone is a beta-lactam antibiotic, and its mechanism of action is very similar to that of other drugs in this class, including cefotaxime and penicillins. It works by interfering with the synthesis of the bacterial cell wall, which is essential for the bacteria's survival. The key steps are:</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<ol style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Inhibition of Penicillin-Binding Proteins (PBPs):</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> Ceftriaxone binds to and inactivates a group of enzymes called penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located in the bacterial cell membrane.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="2" style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Disruption of Peptidoglycan Cross-linking:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> PBPs are responsible for the transpeptidation reaction, a critical final step in the formation of the bacterial cell wall. This reaction cross-links the peptidoglycan chains, providing structural integrity and rigidity to the cell wall.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="3" style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Osmotic Lysis:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> By inhibiting this cross-linking process, ceftriaxone leads to the formation of a defective, unstable cell wall. The weakened cell wall can no longer withstand the internal osmotic pressure of the bacterial cell, causing the cell to swell and burst (lyse).</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<ol start="4" style="margin-bottom:11px">
	<li style="margin-left:16px; margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bactericidal Effect:</span></span></b><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"> This process of cell lysis directly kills the bacteria, giving ceftriaxone its bactericidal effect.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px"><span style="font-size:11pt"><span style="line-height:normal"><span style="font-family:Calibri,sans-serif"><span style="font-size:12.0pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">A major advantage of ceftriaxone is its stability against many beta-lactamase enzymes produced by bacteria. This allows it to be effective against many bacteria that have developed resistance to earlier-generation antibiotics.</span></span></span></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: NT Fungal
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluconazole is a synthetic, broad-spectrum triazole antifungal medication.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is the first drug in its subclass and is available for oral and intravenous administration.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is well-absorbed after oral dosing, with bioavailability exceeding 90%.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Fluconazole can penetrate various body tissues and fluids, including cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), making it effective for systemic infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluconazole is used to prevent and treat a wide range of fungal and yeast infections.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c2317158386="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c2317158386="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c1218284679="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Candidiasis:</b></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Vaginal candidiasis (vaginal yeast infections)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">

		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Oropharyngeal candidiasis (oral thrush)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">

	</ul>
	</li>

<ul style="">

	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Cryptococcal meningitis</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Fungal prophylaxis:</b> It is used to prevent candidiasis in patients at high risk, such as those undergoing bone marrow transplantation or cytotoxic chemotherapy, and in premature infants.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>other infections:</b> It is also used to treat other fungal infections, including blastomycosis, coccidioidomycosis, and histoplasmosis.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Fluconazole exerts its fungistatic effect by selectively inhibiting a fungal enzyme called <b>lanosterol 14-alpha-demethylase</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This enzyme is a cytochrome P450-dependent protein that is essential for the synthesis of <b>ergosterol</b>, a crucial component of the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking this enzyme, fluconazole prevents ergosterol from being formed.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The inhibition of ergosterol synthesis leads to a buildup of toxic 14-alpha-methyl sterols within the fungal cell membrane.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This accumulation increases the permeability of the cell membrane, causing its contents to leak out and ultimately leading to the death of the fungal cell.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The mechanism is highly selective for fungal cells, as it has a much weaker inhibitory effect on mammalian P450 enzymes.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Frexolin
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Doxofylline is a xanthine derivative with bronchodilator properties, used for the management of bronchial asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is distinct from older xanthines like theophylline as it has fewer side effects.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Doxofylline acts primarily on the lungs to improve breathing.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Doxofylline is indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Bronchial Asthma:</b> To relieve bronchospasm and help manage the condition long-term.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD):</b> To improve airflow and reduce symptoms like shortness of breath.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Doxofylline primarily works as a <b>phosphodiesterase (PDE) inhibitor</b>. By blocking the action of this enzyme, Doxofylline leads to an increase in intracellular levels of <b>cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP)</b> in the smooth muscle cells of the bronchi.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2509403486="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><button _ngcontent-ng-c2982728716="" class="image-button ng-star-inserted" jslog="181501;track:generic_click,impression;BardVeMetadataKey:[[&quot;r_24b416477d8838b5&quot;,&quot;c_338f07363acebece&quot;,null,&quot;rc_18ccc2f12c9166bc&quot;,null,null,&quot;en&quot;,null,1,null,null,1,0]];mutable:true">The increased cAMP concentration results in the relaxation of the bronchial smooth muscles, causing the airways to widen. This process is known as <b>bronchodilation</b>, which makes it easier to breathe. PRODUCT: Cyper
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cyproterone Acetate is a synthetic steroid with potent anti-androgen and progestogenic properties.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is a derivative of progestin and acts as a competitive androgen receptor antagonist.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cyproterone Acetate is primarily indicated for the treatment of conditions in women that are dependent on male hormones (androgens).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These conditions include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1283834164="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1283834164="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c918966868="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Severe hirsutism (excessive hair growth)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Severe acne and seborrhea<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS)<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">in men, it is used to reduce sex drive in cases of sexual deviation and for the palliative treatment of prostate cancer.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Cyproterone Acetate works in two main ways:</span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Anti-androgen Effect:</b> It directly blocks androgen receptors in target cells. By competing with natural androgens like testosterone for these receptors, it prevents androgens from exerting their effects on the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This is how it helps reduce hair growth, acne, and other androgen-dependent symptoms.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1283834164="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1283834164="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c918966868="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Progestogenic Effect:</b> It also has a progestogenic effect, similar to natural progesterone.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action suppresses the release of gonadotropins from the pituitary gland, which in turn reduces the production of androgens in the ovaries and adrenal glands.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual action makes it highly effective in counteracting the effects of excess androgens in the body.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

PRODUCT: Atrena
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acitretin</b> is an oral retinoid drug, a synthetic derivative of <b>vitamin A</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It's primarily used for the treatment of severe skin conditions that involve abnormal keratinization, or the hardening of the outer layer of the skin. As a retinoid, it works by influencing cell growth and differentiation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">&nbsp;</div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acitretin is specifically indicated for the treatment of:</span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Severe psoriasis</b>, including erythrodermic and pustular types, which have not responded to other forms of therapy.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Other severe <b>keratinization disorders</b> like ichthyosis and lichen planus.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Psoriatic arthritis</b> as a treatment for the skin lesions.</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<div align="center" style="text-align:center; margin-bottom:11px">
<hr align="center" size="3" width="100%" /></div>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Acitretin's mechanism of action involves binding to <b>retinoic acid receptors (RARs)</b> and <b>retinoid X receptors (RXRs)</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are present in the nucleus of cells and play a crucial role in regulating gene expression, which controls cell growth, differentiation, and shedding.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c1283834164="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c1283834164="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c918966868="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">By activating these receptors, acitretin <b>normalizes the process of skin cell growth</b>. In conditions like psoriasis, skin cells multiply too quickly, leading to thick, scaly patches.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Acitretin helps to slow down this rapid cell turnover and promotes the proper shedding of old skin cells, reducing the scaling and thickness of the lesions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It also has <b>anti-inflammatory effects</b>, which help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with these skin conditions.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c1728693231="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p style="margin-left:48px">&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Bartex 24
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><b>Description</b></p>

<p>Bartex (busulfan) is a cytotoxic or anti-cancer medication. It is an alkylating agent, which works by damaging the genetic material of cells. It is typically a potent and highly toxic medicine used in specific cancer treatments.</p>

<p><b>Indication</b></p>

<p>The primary indication for Bartex (busulfan) is the treatment of certain blood cancers, specifically <b>chronic myeloid leukemia (CML)</b>. It is also used in combination with other cancer medicines during <b>hematopoietic progenitor cell transplantation (HPCT)</b>, also known as a stem cell or bone marrow transplant.</p>

<p><b>Mechanism of Action</b></p>

<p>Busulfan, the active ingredient in Bartex, is an alkylating agent. Its mechanism of action involves damaging the genetic material (DNA and RNA) of cancer cells. It introduces alkyl groups into the DNA, which causes breaks and cross-linking in the DNA strands. This prevents the cancer cells from growing and multiplying, leading to their death. This action is not selective to cancer cells, which is why it has serious side effects on healthy, rapidly dividing cells in the body, such as those in bone marrow.</p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Dapagliflozin + Metformin 100mg
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><b>Description:</b> Dapagliflozin and Metformin is a combination of two oral anti-diabetic medications used to manage type 2 diabetes. Dapagliflozin is a Sodium-Glucose Co-Transporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor, while Metformin is a biguanide.</p>

<p><b>Indication:</b> This combination medicine is used, along with diet and exercise, to improve blood sugar control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus. It is also indicated to reduce the risk of hospitalization for heart failure in adults with type 2 diabetes who also have cardiovascular disease.</p>

<p><b>Mechanism of Action:</b> The drug works through two complementary mechanisms:</p>

<ul>
	<li><b>Dapagliflozin:</b> It works in the kidneys by inhibiting the SGLT2 protein. This prevents the reabsorption of glucose back into the bloodstream, causing excess glucose to be excreted in the urine, thus lowering blood sugar levels.</li>
	<li><b>Metformin:</b> It works primarily by decreasing glucose production by the liver, reducing the absorption of glucose from the intestines, and improving the body's sensitivity to insulin. This helps the body use glucose more effectively.</li>
</ul>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

PRODUCT: Gestodine + Ethinylestradol
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><b>Description</b></p>

<p>Gestodine + Ethinylestradol is a combination oral contraceptive, often referred to as a "birth control pill." It is a synthetic hormonal medication that contains two main components: Gestodine, which is a third-generation synthetic progestin, and Ethinylestradol, a synthetic estrogen. This combination is designed to mimic the natural hormones of a woman's body to prevent pregnancy.</p>

<p><b>Indication</b></p>

<p>The primary indication for Gestodine + Ethinylestradol is the prevention of pregnancy. It is a highly effective form of birth control when used correctly and consistently.</p>

<p><b>Mechanism of Action</b></p>

<p>The contraceptive effect of Gestodine + Ethinylestradol is based on three primary mechanisms:</p>

<ol start="1">
	<li>
	<p><b>Suppression of Ovulation:</b> The estrogen and progestin in the tablet work synergistically to inhibit the release of gonadotropins (FSH and LH) from the pituitary gland. This suppression prevents the maturation of follicles and the subsequent surge of luteinizing hormone (LH), which is necessary for ovulation to occur. Without ovulation, there is no egg to be fertilized.</p>
	</li>
	<li><b>Alteration of Cervical Mucus:</b> The Gestodine component causes the cervical mucus to become thicker and less permeable. This change creates a physical barrier that makes it difficult for sperm to travel through the cervix and reach the egg.</li>
	<li>
	<p><b>Changes to the Endometrial Lining:</b> The hormones also cause changes to the endometrium (the lining of the uterus). They make the lining thinner and less receptive to implantation. If a fertilized egg were to somehow reach the uterus, the altered endometrial environment would be less likely to support its development.</p>
	</li>
</ol>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Orlistat
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><b>Description</b></h3>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<p>Orlistat is a lipase inhibitor medication used to treat obesity. It is designed to be used in conjunction with a reduced-calorie, low-fat diet and exercise. Unlike many weight-loss medications that act on the central nervous system, Orlistat works locally in the digestive system to prevent the absorption of dietary fat.</p>

<p><b>Indication</b></p>

<p>Orlistat is indicated for weight management, including weight loss and weight maintenance, in overweight and obese adults (those with a BMI of 27 kg/m² or greater with other risk factors or 30 kg/m² or greater). It is intended for long-term use and is most effective when combined with lifestyle changes.</p>

<p><b>Mechanism of Action</b></p>

<p>Orlistat works by irreversibly inhibiting gastrointestinal lipases, which are enzymes produced in the stomach and pancreas. These enzymes are responsible for breaking down dietary triglycerides (fat) into smaller, absorbable free fatty acids and monoglycerides.</p>

<p>By blocking the action of these lipases, Orlistat prevents the digestion of a significant portion of dietary fat (approximately 30%). The undigested fat is then eliminated from the body through bowel movements. This reduction in calorie absorption from fat helps to create a negative energy balance, which ultimately leads to weight loss. The medication does not affect appetite or metabolism.</p>
-------------------------------------------

PRODUCT: Laspay(Mebeverine HCL)
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Description</b> Laspay (Mebeverine HCl 200mg) is an antispasmodic medication used to treat the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal conditions. Mebeverine works directly on the smooth muscles of the gut to relieve painful spasms and cramps without affecting normal gut motility. It is a well-tolerated and effective treatment for symptoms such as abdominal pain, cramping, bloating, and diarrhea or constipation associated with IBS. Laspay is suitable for long-term use and is typically taken as directed by a healthcare professional.</span></span></p>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Indications</b></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Abdominal cramps and pain caused by gastrointestinal spasms</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Spastic colon</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Chronic functional bowel disorders</span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Bowel disturbances</span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Mechanism of Action</b> Mebeverine HCl is a musculotropic antispasmodic agent. It acts directly on the smooth muscle of the gastrointestinal tract, primarily in the colon, to relax it and relieve spasms. Unlike anticholinergic drugs, its action is not mediated through the autonomic nervous system. It directly blocks the voltage-dependent potassium channels (K+) and calcium channels (Ca2+) in the muscle cells, reducing muscle contraction and relieving painful cramps and spasms without inhibiting normal intestinal motility. This makes it effective at treating symptoms without causing significant side effects like dry mouth or blurred vision.</span></span></p>

PRODUCT: Hexla-AP
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clopidogrel bisulphate and Aspirin are a combination of two antiplatelet medicines, also commonly referred to as "blood thinners."<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This medication is typically available as an oral tablet or capsule.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The fixed-dose combination is used to prevent the formation of blood clots, which can lead to serious cardiovascular events.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">The combination of clopidogrel and aspirin is primarily used to prevent serious or life-threatening problems with the heart and blood vessels.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its main indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c4016470676="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c4016470676="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c448890458="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Prevention of Heart Attack and Stroke:</b> This is the primary use of the medication. It is prescribed to patients who are at high risk of a heart attack or stroke, including those who have a history of these events, severe chest pain (unstable angina), or peripheral arterial disease.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS):</b> It is a standard treatment for patients with ACS, a condition where blood flow to the heart is suddenly blocked.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Post-Surgical Prevention:</b> It is used in patients who have undergone certain heart procedures, such as percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), which may involve the placement of a coronary artery stent, or coronary artery bypass graft surgery (CABG).<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Clopidogrel bisulphate and Aspirin work synergistically to prevent blood clots.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> They both target different pathways of platelet aggregation, providing a more robust anti-clotting effect than either drug alone.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c4016470676="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c4016470676="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c448890458="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Aspirin:</b> Aspirin is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) and an antiplatelet agent.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> At low doses, it works by irreversibly inhibiting the enzyme cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1). This prevents the production of thromboxane A2 (TXA2​), a potent molecule that causes platelets to clump together and blood vessels to constrict. By inhibiting TXA2​, aspirin reduces platelet aggregation and clot formation.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c4016470676="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c4016470676="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c448890458="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Clopidogrel Bisulphate:</b> Clopidogrel is a prodrug, meaning it must be metabolized by the liver into its active form.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The active metabolite then irreversibly binds to a specific receptor on platelets called the P2Y12 adenosine diphosphate (ADP) receptor.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This binding prevents ADP from activating the glycoprotein GPIIb/IIIa complex, which is a crucial step for platelet aggregation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> By blocking this receptor, clopidogrel prevents platelets from sticking to each other, thereby inhibiting clot formation.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c3795881607="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

PRODUCT: Zopivet
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p><strong>Linezolid</strong> is a potent synthetic antibiotic from the oxazolidinone class, specifically designed to combat serious infections caused by multi-resistant Gram-positive bacteria.</p>

<p>It is highly effective against challenging pathogens, including:</p>

<ul>
	<li>Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA)</li>
	<li>Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus (VRE)</li>
	<li>Nosocomial pneumonia</li>
	<li>Complicated skin and soft tissue infections</li>
</ul>

<p>Linezolid works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis, a unique mechanism that helps prevent cross-resistance with other antibiotic classes. It is a critical medication in treating severe infections where other antibiotics may have failed.</p>

<p><em>This medication must be used under the strict guidance of a healthcare professional.</em></p>

HTML_DESCRIPTION: <p>Discover the vital role of Progesterone, a naturally occurring steroid hormone essential for women's health. Our high-quality Progesterone formulation is crucial for regulating the menstrual cycle and maintaining a healthy pregnancy. It plays a key part in preparing the uterus for implantation and supporting early embryonic development.</p>

<p><strong>Key Uses:</strong></p>

<ul>
	<li>Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT)</li>
	<li>Treating menstrual irregularities</li>
	<li>Supporting fertility treatments</li>
	<li>Preventing premature birth</li>
</ul>

<p>Trust in our product to help restore hormonal balance and support your overall well-being. Consult your healthcare provider for appropriate use.</p>

PRODUCT: Odensy injection
HTML_DESCRIPTION: <h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Description</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ondansetron is a synthetic antiemetic drug that belongs to the class of medications known as <b>serotonin 5-</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>HT3</b><b>​</b><b> receptor antagonists</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> The "HCL" in its name refers to the hydrochloride salt form, which is the most common formulation for oral, intravenous, and suppository preparations. Ondansetron is one of the most widely used and effective drugs for controlling nausea and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<p>&nbsp;</p>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Indications</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ondansetron is primarily used for the prevention and treatment of nausea and vomiting caused by certain medical procedures and treatments.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> Its key indications include:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></p>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting (CINV):</b> It is a cornerstone of antiemetic therapy for patients receiving cancer chemotherapy, particularly for highly emetogenic regimens.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> It is effective in preventing both acute (within 24 hours) and delayed (1-5 days after) CINV.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Radiotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting (RINV):</b> It is used to prevent nausea and vomiting in patients undergoing radiotherapy, especially when the radiation targets the abdomen.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<ul>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Postoperative Nausea and Vomiting (PONV):</b> It is widely used to prevent and treat nausea and vomiting following surgical procedures.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
</ul>

<h3><span style="font-size:13.5pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><span style="font-weight:bold">Mechanism of Action</span></span></span></h3>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">Ondansetron's mechanism of action is based on its ability to block serotonin receptors, thereby interrupting a key signaling pathway that triggers nausea and vomiting.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>

<ol>
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Targeting the 5-</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>HT3</b><b>​</b><b> Receptor:</b> Ondansetron is a selective and competitive antagonist of the <b>serotonin 5-</b><source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote><b>HT3</b><b>​</b><b> receptors</b>.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> These receptors are found in two critical locations involved in the emetic reflex:<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span>

	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In the Periphery:</b> They are located on the vagal nerve terminals in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. When chemotherapeutic agents, radiation, or other emetogenic stimuli are introduced, they can cause the release of serotonin from enterochromaffin cells in the gut.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This serotonin then activates the 5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT3​ receptors on the vagal nerve, sending a nausea signal to the brain.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ol>

<ul style="">
	<li style="list-style-type:none">
	<ul style="list-style-type:circle">
		<li style="margin-left:16px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list 1.0in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>In the Central Nervous System:</b> The receptors are also present in the chemoreceptor trigger zone (CTZ) of the brain. The CTZ can independently trigger the vomiting reflex in response to various stimuli in the blood and cerebrospinal fluid.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></span></li>
	</ul>
	</li>
</ul>

<ol start="2">
	<li style="margin-left:8px"><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="tab-stops:list .5in"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif"><b>Blocking the Emetic Reflex:</b> By binding to and blocking the 5-<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote>HT3​ receptors, ondansetron prevents serotonin from activating them.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This action effectively stops the nausea and vomiting signals from being transmitted from the gut to the brain. It also directly inhibits the vomiting center by blocking the receptors in the CTZ.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote> This dual action in both the periphery and the brain makes ondansetron a very effective antiemetic.<sources-carousel-inline _nghost-ng-c530466506="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"><source-inline-chips _ngcontent-ng-c530466506="" _nghost-ng-c786323692="" class="ng-star-inserted"><source-inline-chip _ngcontent-ng-c786323692="" _nghost-ng-c3889421858="" class="ng-star-inserted"></source-inline-chip></source-inline-chips></sources-carousel-inline></span></span></span></li>
</ol>

<p><span style="font-size:12pt"><span style="font-family:&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif">In summary, ondansetron works by interrupting the signaling pathway of serotonin, a key mediator of the emetic reflex, thereby preventing nausea and vomiting from both peripheral (gut) and central (brain) triggers.<source-footnote _nghost-ng-c2077508474="" ng-version="0.0.0-PLACEHOLDER"></source-footnote></span></span></p>
